segments.s655-g73: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. آگاه شد is a past-tense compound meaning 'became aware' (آگاه = aware; شد = became). زان سخن is a prepositional phrase 'from that speech / because of that speech' (زان = از آن, 'from that'; سخن = 'speech, words') and provides the source of his awareness.
segments.s655-g74: که introduces a subordinate content clause ('that / which') giving what he learned. خاقان چینی is a noun phrase 'the Chinese khaghan' (خاقان = khaghan/ruler; چینی = Chinese). چه is an interrogative/indefinite 'what' (here 'what ... had done'). افگند is past 'threw/sent/caused to fall' and completes the verb; بن here is a nominal element (literal 'base/root') used after افگند — taken together literally as 'what the khaghan of China had cast (at / on) the base', but more generally 'what he had sent/fired/loosed.'
segments.s655-g75: سپاهی ... برگزید is a simple past clause 'he chose/raised an army' (سپاهی = army/force; برگزید = chose/selected). ز هیتالیان is the source phrase 'from the Hitalians' — poetic use of ز = از ('from'). The subject ('he') is omitted as common in Persian narrative.
segments.s655-g76: که here links a following result/clause ('and/so that'): گشت is past of گشتن meaning 'became' (intransitive change of state). آفتاب ازجهان ناپدید is a verbless result phrase 'the sun became unseen/vanished from the world' (آفتاب = sun; ازجهان = from the world; ناپدید = vanished/hidden), describing the awe or darkness caused by that army.
segments.s655-g77: This is a list of place-names introduced by the preposition ز (from). In poetic Persian 'ز' may combine with the coordinating و, appearing here as 'وز', functioning as 'and from' between items. The sequence names regions or peoples (زبلخ = Zabul; شگنان, آموی, زم are other local names/regions).
segments.s655-g78: سلیح وسپه خواست is 'he demanded/sought weapons and an army/host' (سلیح = weapons/armament; وسپه = and host/army; خواست = wanted/sought, past). و گنج درم adds an additional object 'and treasure and dirhams' (گنج = treasure; درم = dirham/coins). The verb خواست governs the entire coordinated object list.
segments.s655-g79: Another place-list introduced by ز ('from'): ز سومان وز ترمذ و ویسه گرد literally 'from Suman, from Termez, and Viseh around'. As before, وز is a poetic fusion of و + ز ('and from'). گرد at the end of the group is part of the spatial phrase indicating 'around/about' or 'surrounding'.
segments.s655-g80: سپاهی برآمد زهرسوی گرد is the main clause: سپاهی برآمد = 'an army arose/issued forth' (سپاهی = force; برآمد = rose/issued forth). زهرسوی گرد is a poetic compounding meaning 'from every side / from every quarter' (here written together but analyzable as ز هر سوی گرد). The verb is simple past/perfective describing the sudden appearance of the host.
segments.s655-g1: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' placed first for emphasis; گفت is the past 3rd-person verb 'said' (here used in the simple past); the noun phrase پرمایه دهقان پیر follows the verb (verb–subject order is common in classical Persian). پرمایه is an epithet before دهقان, and پیر is a post‑positive epithet ‘old/elder’ attached to the noun.
segments.s655-g2: سخن 'words/speech' is the theme; هرچ 'whatever' quantifies it. ز + او is contracted in the line as زو meaning 'from him' (z + pronoun), so the preposition 'ز' introduces the source. بشنوی is 2nd‑person singular present/subjunctive ('you hear' / 'if you hear'), and یادگیر is an imperative 'learn / remember' addressed to the same 'you'.
segments.s655-g3: که introduces a relative clause ('who/which/that'). از نامداران is a partitive/origin phrase 'from/of the renowned'; با governs the following pair of nouns, so با فر و داد means 'with farr (royal glory) and justice' — فر and داد are coordinated by و.
segments.s655-g4: ز introduces origin again ('from / of'): ز مردان جنگی = 'from the warlike men' (مردان followed by the adjectival جنگی). به here marks relation/character ('in/regarding'), so به فر ونژاد ties the person to 'glory and lineage' (فر and ونژاد coordinated).
segments.s655-g5: چوخاقان is an archaic/formulaic word here functioning as a comparative/epithet ('like a khaghan' / 'as a khaghan'). چینی is a noun 'Chinese (person)' used as the item being negated by نبود, the past negative 'was not' (نبود = 'was not / there was not'). از مهان ('from/among the guests') uses از to indicate inclusion/partitive sense.
segments.s655-g6: گذشته is a verbal adjective/participle 'having gone/past' ('having left'); ز کسری 'from Khosrow' gives the point of departure. بگرد is a poetic/imperative or optative form of گردیدن ('to go about/turn'); in narrative poetry it often functions as a simple past/continuous sense 'he went about' with an implied subject. جهان is the object/place 'the world'.
segments.s655-g7: همان 'that/the same' points back to the person; تا is 'as far as/until' and introduces a limit: تا لب رود جیحون = 'as far as the bank (لب) of the river (رود) Jeyhûn (جیحون)'. ز چین 'from China' indicates origin or route; sequence of nouns in poetry omits linking particles but the syntactic relations are the same (لبِ رودِ جیحون).
segments.s655-g8: برو is an imperative/root-form 'go' or a narrative present 'went' depending on context (here part of a narrative cluster). خواندندی is an archaic past‑plural form (‑ندی marking 3pl past) meaning 'they sang/called/read aloud'. بداد آفرین is a compound/exclamation: بداد (part of the compound here, not 'oppression' in sense) plus آفرین ('acclamation'/'bravo'); together used as an interjective cry. The exact syntactic relation is verbal: 'they went and cried “Bedād-āfarin.”'
segments.s655-g1745: چنین داد پاسخ queuing the clause: Literally 'thus gave reply that ...'. دادن is past simple here (داد = 'gave') and precedes the subordinate clause introduced by که. چنین is a discourse adverb ('thus/so') modifying the whole reply; پاسخ is the noun 'reply' governed by داد. The که that follows introduces the content of that reply.
segments.s655-g1746: ستیهیدن مردم بی‌گناه: ستیهیدن is the infinitive/masdar form (a verbal noun) meaning 'hostility/persecuting' and functions as the content of the reply introduced by که. مردم is the object/affected group 'people' and بی‌گناه modifies them ('innocent'). In this construction the masdar names the action ('the persecuting of innocent people').
segments.s655-g1747: توانگر که تنگی کند در خورش: همان توانگر is the head noun 'the rich/wealthy man' and که introduces a relative clause. تنگی is a noun meaning 'meagerness/shortage' (here 'making scanty'), and کند is the present/simple verb 'does/makes'. در خورش is a prepositional phrase 'in (his) food' (the possessive is implied), so the clause says he 'makes his food meagre' or is stingy with his food.
segments.s655-g1748: دریغ آیدش پوشش و پرورش: دریغ آید (literally 'it comes as reluctance/pity') is an impersonal construction with the dative clitic -ش = 'to him' (آیدش = 'it comes to him' → 'he feels reluctance'). پوشش و پرورش are the things he is reluctant to provide ('clothing and upbringing'). The verb is present/simple descriptive of his attitude.
segments.s655-g1749: زنانی که ایشان ندارند شرم: زنانی is the head noun 'women' and که introduces the relative clause. ایشان is a resumptive/third‑person pronoun referring back to those women; ندارند is the negative present 'they do not have', and شرم ('modesty/shame') is the noun they lack. The clause thus means 'women who do not possess modesty.'
segments.s655-g1750: بگفتن ندارند آواز نرم: بگفتن is the infinitive 'to speak' used as the item related to ندارند ('they do not have'); آواز نرم ('a soft/gentle voice') is what they lack in speaking. Grammatically this is the pattern 'ندارند + بَ + noun/infinitive' to state the lack of a quality or habit (they do not have a soft voice when speaking).
segments.s655-g1751: همان نیک‌مردان که تندی کنند: همان 'those very' (emphatic) + نیک‌مردان 'virtuous men'. که introduces the relative clause; تندی (fierceness/harshness) with کنند ('they do') says these very good men sometimes display harshness. The verb کنَند is the present/gnomic form describing such behavior.
segments.s655-g1752: وگر تنگ‌دستان بلندی کنند: وگر (here 'but if' / 'and yet if') introduces a contrastive conditional about the poor (تنگ‌دستان). بلندی کند/کنند is a poetic use meaning 'to rise up/show loftiness or pride' (an intransitive expression of becoming high/lofty); کنند is the present plural. The clause contrasts the behavior of the needy with that of the noble.
segments.s655-g33: بفرمود is a past, honorific/polite 3rd-person verb here meaning 'he ordered/said.' تا introduces a subordinate/result clause (purpose/jussive) — 'that ...'. پیش اوشد is an idiomatic, somewhat archaic sequence: پیش ('in the presence of/before') + اوشد (an older poetic form meaning 'came to be / be brought'), so together 'that a secretary might come before him.' دبیر is the noun 'secretary' (the expected complement of اوشد).
segments.s655-g34: This is a noun phrase giving what the secretary carries: ز marks source ('from the Khagan'), یکی is an indefinite 'a certain / a', نامه‌ای is the indefinite 'a letter' (note the -ای suffix), and برحریر is an old fused prepositional phrase 'on silk' (بر + حریر) meaning 'written on silk.' Word order is source → indefinite determiner → noun → material.
segments.s655-g35: نبشتند is the main past plural verb 'they wrote.' What follows (برسان ارژنگ چین) is an unmarked complement clause expressing the content of what they wrote — برسان is the jussive/imperative-inflected form of رساندن used here as a reported command 'deliver/bring,' followed by the name ارژنگ and چین (here part of the name or an epithet meaning 'the Chinese'). The clause is thus 'they wrote: "Bring Arzhang Chin."'
segments.s655-g36: سوی شاه is a prepositional phrase 'to/toward the king' marking destination/recipient. با introduces accompaniment/means ('with'), صد هزار is a hyperbolic numeric phrase 'a hundred thousand,' and آفرین here is a noun 'acclamation/praise' (not the verb stem). Together: 'to the king with a hundred thousand acclamations.'
segments.s655-g37: گذر ('passage, crossing') is the head noun; مرد را uses را to mark the definite/ topicalized noun phrase 'the man's' (here functioning like a possessive/topic marker). سوی هیتال 'toward Hital' is the directional complement, and بود is the past copula 'was' — so the literal order is 'the man's passage (was) toward Hital.' Note the slightly marked use of را with what is essentially a subject/topic.
segments.s655-g38: همه ره 'the whole road' is the subject phrase (همه = whole/entire, ره = way). پر + از + N is the adjectival construction 'full of ...' (پر = full, از = of), so پر از تیغ و کوپال بود = 'was full of swords and Koopal.' کوپال is a proper name of a weapon or force here. بود is again the past copula.
segments.s655-g39: This initial phrase gives the extent/limits of the deployment: ز ('from') + سغد اندرون ('within Sogdiana') then تا ('as far as') + به جیحون ('to the Jeyhun/Amu Darya'). The final noun of the segment سپاه ('army') is the head that these range phrases modify; تا here marks the span 'from ... to ...'.
segments.s655-g40: کشیده is a past participle/adjectival form 'drawn/stretched' (here 'drawn up'), and رده is 'rank/row' — together 'drawn-up ranks.' پیش هیتال شاه places this formation spatially 'before King Hital' (پیش = in the presence of/before). The clause thus describes the army (سپاه) as 'drawn up in ranks before King Hital' with the copula understood or implied from context.
segments.s655-g1513: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; here it introduces the source of the question ('he asked from the wise'). بپرسید is a past-form verb 'asked' (classical/poetic word order places the verb before the subject). پس = 'then' or 'thereupon', and دادگر is the subject placed after the verb for emphasis: 'the just man.'
segments.s655-g1514: که introduces an indirect question ('whether/that'). فرهنگ = 'culture/learning' is the theme; بهتر is the comparative 'better/preferable'. بود‌گر (classical fused form) expresses an irrealis/conditional sense 'if it were' or 'would be' — here marking the hypothetical/comparative clause — and گهر = 'jewel.' The line thus asks whether culture would be better than a gem.
segments.s655-g1515: چنین ('thus') foregrounds the reply. داد here is the verb 'gave' (object follows), and پاسخ is the direct object 'answer.' بدو is the indirect-object clitic/phrase 'to him,' and رهنمون ('guiding' / 'guidance') apposes or qualifies 'پاسخ' — 'the guiding answer.' Word order (verb + object + indirect object) is characteristic of classical diction.
segments.s655-g1516: که again introduces reported content ('that'). فرهنگ is the subject/topic. باشد is the present/subjunctive/copulative 'be' (here expressing the proposition or desirability: 'should be / is to be'). ز is the poetic comparative 'than/from' and گوهر 'gem' is the thing compared; فزون 'more/more abundant' is the comparative predicate. Together: 'that culture should be more (valuable) than a gem.'
segments.s655-g1517: This is a nominal subject with predicative adjectives: 'گهر بی‌هنر' ('a gem lacking art/skill') is followed by a string of predicates. زار = 'pitiable/wretched.' The sequence 'و خوارست و سست' reads as 'and is despised and weak' where خوارست is the classical fused form 'خوار است' ('is low/despised') and سست = 'weak/unstable.' The copula 'است' is implied/partly fused into 'خوارست.'
segments.s655-g1518: به + noun here marks instrument/means ('by/through culture'). باشد is the copular/subjunctive 'is/should be' linking the clause, and روان تندرست is the predicate nominal 'the spirit (is) healthy.' In short: 'By culture the spirit is hale.'
segments.s655-g1519: بدو = 'to him' (indirect object). گفت here is the speech verb 'said' (the provided lemma senses list does not include the simple past 'he said,' so the standard sense 'said' is intended). جان را زدودن is an infinitival clause literally 'to cleanse/remove (from) the soul' with را marking جان as definite object; بچیست is the interrogative 'what is it?' — together: 'He said to him: What is cleansing the soul?'
segments.s655-g1520: هنرهای تن = 'the arts/skills of the body' (construct/genitive). را again marks the object. ستودن is the infinitive 'to praise' used in parallel to زدودن, and بچیست repeats the interrogative 'what is it?' — 'and what is praising bodily arts?' The two infinitival questions form a rhetorical parallel.
segments.s655-g1649: بپرسید و گفتش is a sequence of past‑tense actions: بپرسید (he asked) + و (and) + گفتش (he said to him — گفت + enclitic object ش). که introduces the content clause that follows, and از آرزوی is an ablative/prepositional phrase 'from/of desire' that begins that subordinate clause.
segments.s655-g1650: چه is the interrogative 'what'; بیشست is an archaic/perfective form meaning 'remained/was left' (classical past sense) and here marks what is left of the wish; پیدا کن is a two‑part imperative 'find out' (پیدا + کن), and ای نیک‌خوی is a vocative 'O good‑natured one' addressing the addressee.
segments.s655-g1651: بدو is the prepositional phrase 'to him' (بد + ـو). گفت is the reporting verb 'said' (past); چون functions as a subordinating/comparative 'when/as', and سرفرازی is the predicate noun 'pride, honour' governed by the copula بود that follows.
segments.s655-g1652: همه آرزو is the subject phrase 'all desire' (همه = all; آرزو = desire). بی‌نیازی is the predicate noun/adjective 'self‑sufficiency / freedom from want', and بود is the past copula 'was', so the clause states that all desire amounted to self‑sufficiency.
segments.s655-g1653: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'; ازبی‌نیازی is an ablative phrase 'being free from want' (از + بی‌نیازی) describing the state; بود is the past copula 'was'; تندرست is the predicate adjective 'healthy'. The clause states a condition/state: 'when [he] was free of need, [he] was healthy.'
segments.s655-g1654: نباید is an impersonal negative modal 'one should not / ought not' (here conveying 'there is no need'); جز = 'except'; از کام دل = 'from/of the heart's desire' (از + کام + دل); چیز جست = verb‑final poetic order where جست (here 'to seek') is the verbal action applying to چیز ('thing'). Together: 'one should seek nothing except what comes from the heart's desire.'
segments.s655-g1655: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that' (از + آن + پس). چنین گفت is a fixed expression 'thus he said'; با رهنمون is a prepositional phrase 'with (his) guide' or 'accompanied by guidance' (با + رهنمون). The whole segment frames the following reported question/remark.
segments.s655-g1656: که introduces the content clause. بردل is a locative/dir‑phrase 'into/in the heart' (بـ + دل). چه اندیشه is 'what thought' (interrogative + noun). آید is the present/habitual verb 'comes' (آیَد/آید) and فزون is an adverb/adjective 'increasing/abundant' modifying the coming — 'what thought comes more abundantly into the heart.'
segments.s655-g945: 'ز دیبا بیاراست مهدی به زر' is a compact, slightly inverted poetic clause: بیاراست is a past tense verb 'adorned', followed by its patient/target مهدی (Mahdi) — poetry allows verb-before-subject word order. ز + noun (ز دیبا) here gives the material or source ('from/of brocade') and به زر uses به in an instrumental sense ('with/by gold').
segments.s655-g946: 'به مهد اندرون نابسوده گهر' places something into/within the cradle: به + مهد (towards/into the cradle) plus اندرون 'inside', then the adjective نابسوده 'untouched' modifies گهر 'jewel'. Word order is noun phrase-final (adjective before noun) as is common in older/poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g947: چو introduces a temporal clause ('when'); 'سیصد پرستار' is the numeral + noun ('three hundred attendants/attendants numbering three hundred'), and با ماهروی is a prepositional phrase 'with moon-faced (ones)' describing accompaniment or appearance. The noun پرستار here reads as attendant/caretaker.
segments.s655-g948: برفتند is the simple past plural 'they departed/went off'. شادان‌دل is a compound adjective 'cheerful-hearted'; و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; راه‌جوی is an agent noun 'way‑seeker' or 'traveller', describing their disposition as eager for the road.
segments.s655-g949: فرستاد is a simple past transitive verb 'he sent'. فرزند را is the direct object with the postposed object marker را (noun + را = 'the/that son' as object). نزد شاه places the beneficiary/location after the object: نزد ('in the presence of / to') + شاه ('king'). Word order is Verb - Object - (prepositional phrase) here because the verb appears initially for narrative emphasis.
segments.s655-g950: سپاهی is the subject meaning 'a soldier/one of the troops.' همی‌رفت is the classical literary imperfective/past-continuous form (همی‑ + رفت) best rendered 'was going' or 'went along' — common in epic diction. با او به راه is a prepositional phrase 'with him on the road' (با = 'with', او = 'him', به راه = 'towards/on the road').
segments.s655-g951: The numeral phrase uses poetic inversion: the noun (پرستنده 'devotees/worshippers') comes before its numeral 'پنجاه' = 'fifty' — Persian poetry often permits noun + number order. و is the conjunction 'and', linking the next noun phrase خادم چهل ('forty servants').
segments.s655-g952: برو here functions as a verbal adverb/old-style preverbial 'going / having gone' (poetic usage) that accompanies the main past verb برگذشتند ('they passed by / they went on'), which is plural past. شادان به دل is an adverbial phrase 'with joyful hearts' (شادان = 'joyful', به دل = 'in/at heart'). The overall clause describes them setting out and passing along, joyful in heart.
segments.s655-g1633: بپرسید is a past 3rd-person verb 'he asked.' و is the coordinating 'and.' گفتش is گفت + object clitic ـش, 'he said to him/her.' که introduces the reported command/clause. برگوی is the imperative/infinitival sense 'tell/say' used inside reported speech. راست is an adverb/adjective 'true' (i.e. 'speak the truth').
segments.s655-g1634: که begins a subordinate relative/nominal clause ('who/that'). تا here has an emphatic/extentive force (roughly 'ever'/'even' or 'that'), marking the scope of the relative; it’s not a temporal 'until' here. از گذشته is a prepositional phrase 'of/from the past.' پشیمان is an adjective 'repentant/regretful.' کراست is an archaic or corrupted form (not represented by the provided senses) functioning as a verbal/perfective element meaning roughly 'has become/has been' — i.e. 'has repented.'
segments.s655-g1635: چنین داد پاسخ is a short clause meaning 'thus gave (an) answer' — داد is the past 3rd-person 'gave' and پاسخ is 'answer.' که introduces the following relative clause. آن تیره ترگ = 'that dark shred/rag,' with آن as demonstrative, تیره 'dark/ominous,' and ترگ 'rag/shred.'
segments.s655-g1636: که is the relative 'which/that.' بر سر نهد is a verbal phrase 'puts/places upon the head' — بر 'on/upon,' سر 'head,' and نهد (from نهاد) is the 3rd-person present/past-habitual form 'places/sets.' پادشا is 'king/monarch.' روز مرگ is a noun phrase 'day of death' (i.e. the day one dies).
segments.s655-g1637: پشیمان شود is a two-word predicate 'becomes remorseful' (پشیمان adjective, شود 'become'). دل کند پرهراس is another small clause 'he makes/causes his heart to be fearful' or more smoothly 'his heart becomes fearful' — دل 'heart' is the affected noun, کند (from کردن) 'makes/does,' and پرهراس 'fearful/frightened.'
segments.s655-g1638: که here gives the reason 'because.' جانش is جان + possessive clitic 'his life/soul.' به یزدان بود ناسپاس is a predicative clause 'was ungrateful to God' — به 'to/with regard to,' یزدان 'God,' بود 'was' (past copula), ناسپاس 'ungrateful.'
segments.s655-g1639: و دیگر opens another point/answer: 'and another.' که introduces a relative clause 'who/that.' کردار دارد بسی = 'has many/much conduct/deeds' — کردار 'deed/behavior,' دارد 'has/possesses,' and بسی is an adverb meaning 'much/many' (classical usage; here modifying the quantity of deeds).
segments.s655-g1640: به نزدیک ... کسی = 'someone near to ...' — به نزدیک is a prepositional expression 'near to,' آن ناسپاسان 'those ungrateful ones' (آن demonstrative + ناسپاسان plural adjective/noun), and کسی 'someone' or 'anyone.' The whole gives 'someone who is near to those ungrateful people.'
segments.s655-g161: پس is a temporal/conjunctive adverb ('then/after') placed at the start. آگاهی (direct object: 'news/information') is the subject of آمد (past tense, 'came/reached') which follows it in typical Persian word order; به شاه بزرگ ('to the great king') is the indirect object introduced by the preposition به and comes after the verb phrase.
segments.s655-g162: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' marking the source of the news. خاقان ('khagan') is the agent/source, followed by a relative clause introduced by که ('that') with شد ('became', past tense) as the verb. نامدار is a predicate adjective 'renowned'; سترگ is another qualifying adjective attached to نامدار (poetic order places the modifiers after the verb-clause).
segments.s655-g163: ز marks source ('from'). هیتال is a proper name (the source/party named). و links هیتال and گردان; گردان functions as a noun 'troop/retinue' modifying or coordinated with the name, and آن انجمن ('that assembly') further identifies the group. Word order is poetic/elliptical: the whole noun phrase names who is involved.
segments.s655-g164: که begins a relative/explanatory clause ('that'), آمد is the past verb 'came'. ز خاقان gives the origin/agent 'from the khagan'. بریشان is بر + ایشان with the pronominal suffix indicating direction/target ('upon/against them'), and شکن is the noun 'breaker/tormentor' as the thing/person that came; together they mean 'a breaker came from the khagan against them.'
segments.s655-g165: ز here functions poetically to introduce the subject 'of/from the king Chaghani'. چغانی is an epithet or nisba attached to شاه (a name/description). که introduces a relative clause 'who', and با + بخت نو ('with new fortune') is an adverbial phrase describing the manner/state with which he came.
segments.s655-g166: بیامد is a past tense verb 'came' (here used as simple past). نشست is the verb 'sat/settled' (took his seat); Persian often strings verbs or verb phrases sequentially. از بر تخت نو literally 'from/on the new throne' uses از with بر to indicate movement onto the throne or the seat taken on the new throne (poetic order places these prepositional elements after the verb).
segments.s655-g167: پراندیشه is a prefixed adjective 'full of thought/pensive' placed before the verb; بنشست is the past-tense verb 'sat down' (the b- prefix is an older/phrasal form but functions as simple past here). شاه جهان ('king of the world') is the noun phrase serving as subject; word order is adjective + verb + subject for poetic emphasis.
segments.s655-g168: ز marks source/reason ('from/because of'). گفتار بیدار is the noun phrase 'the wakeful/alert speech' (بیدار as adjective after the noun, a common poetic order). کارآگهان is a predicate/adjectival noun meaning 'those informed' or here 'the informed/advising ones' — together the phrase means the king sat because of or in response to the alert/instructive speech.
segments.s655-g57: بتاراج here is a verbal noun meaning 'plunder' used with the light verb داد (gave) to form a compound: literally 'gave to plunder' → idiomatically 'yielded to the plunderers' or 'gave up as plunder'. آن همه خواسته is a demonstrative + quantifier + noun phrase ('all that was requested/desired'); خواسته functions as a noun (what was asked for). Word order is verbal-nominal (verbal noun + light verb) followed by the object phrase.
segments.s655-g58: هیونان واسبان is a coordinated noun phrase ('beasts and horses'); واسبان is an older/fused form meaning 'and horses/horsemen' and آراسته is a predicative adjective/participle 'adorned' that applies to the preceding nouns. There is no copula — the adjective follows the nouns and functions as the predicate ('were adorned').
segments.s655-g59: فرستاده را سر بریدند is a straightforward past-clause: object marked by را (فرستاده را) + verb بریدند ('they cut off'). The final word پست is an appositive/adjective placed after the clause to characterise the actors as 'base/despicable' (poetic word order puts the epithet after the action).
segments.s655-g60: ز marks source or 'among/from' (poetic usage) and introduces ترکان ('the Turks') as the frame. چینی سواری is a compound 'Chinese horseman(s)/cavalry' and نجست is past of جستن with negative sense here ('was not found' / 'he did not find'); in older Persian the verb can be used impersonally to mean 'there was no ... found'. The clause means roughly 'no Chinese rider was found among the Turks.'
segments.s655-g61: چوآگاهی is a fused temporal expression (چو = 'when' + آگاهی = 'news/awareness'), آمد is simple past 'came', and به plus a title locates the recipient: به خاقان چین = 'to the Khagan of China'. The clause is a temporal subordinate: 'When news came to the Khagan of China...'.
segments.s655-g62: دلش is 'his heart' (دل + enclitic possessive ش = his). گشت is past 'became/turned', and پر + noun = 'full (of) X' (پر درد = 'full of pain/sorrow'). The conjunction و links the second balanced phrase سر پر ز کین: 'head full of vengeance' where ز = 'of/from' and کین = 'vengeance/hatred'. This is a copular-like result clause: 'his heart became full of sorrow and his head full of vengeance.'
segments.s655-g63: سپه را marks the army as the object (را). ز + قجغارباشی indicates the agent/command association ('under/by the Qajghār‑bāshi' / 'assigned to Qajghār‑bāshi'); براند is past 'drove' or 'sent/led away'. The line uses compact, somewhat ambiguous prepositional linkage (ز) to indicate under whose authority the army was moved.
segments.s655-g64: به چین is the destination 'to China'. وختن is an uncertain/archaic lexical item here (textual/lexical uncertainty in the manuscript tradition); it functions adverbially or temporally in this line. نامداری is 'renown' and نماند is past negative 'did not remain' — together 'no renown remained'. The whole second half reads as a terse statement about fame disappearing in that enterprise/time.
segments.s655-g1777: سپاس is a nominal exclamation meaning 'praise' or 'thanks' and heads the clause. از introduces the beneficiary/target of the praise; here خداوند خورشید و ماه is a single noun phrase where خداوند is qualified by خورشید و ماه ('Lord of the sun and moon'). The conjunction و simply links خورشید and ماه.
segments.s655-g1778: که introduces a subordinate clause ('that' / 'who'). The clause has an omitted copula (است), so رستم ز بوزرجمهر و ز شاه reads as 'Rostam is/from Bozorgmehr and from the king'—ز is the poetic preposition 'from' indicating origin/lineage; the second ز repeats the same relation before شاه.
segments.s655-g1779: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. این کار is the noun phrase 'this matter/affair'. دلگیرت is دلگیر + enclitic -ت ('you'), idiomatically 'it makes you sad/you are displeased'. آمد is a simple past used here with the idiom 'دلگیرت آمد' meaning 'it came to sadden/it displeased you'. ببن (an archaic/poetic form of ببین) is an imperative 'look/see'.
segments.s655-g1780: ز شطرنج is an adverbial 'about/concerning chess' (ز here = 'about/of'). باید که introduces an impersonal necessity clause 'it is necessary that...'. The verb 'to speak' is omitted; رانی سخن literally 'rani (the queen) speech' means 'speak of the rani'—سخن functions as 'speech/talking of' with the action implied.
segments.s655-g1129: ز is a poetic preposition here introducing the circumstance ('from/when'); گردان (troop) is its object. چو خشنود شد is a temporal clause 'when (they) became pleased' with چو (when) + خشنود شد (became pleased). شهریار (king) appears after the clause as the main subject for the following verb. The clause order is subordinate (circumstantial) + subject.
segments.s655-g1130: بیامد is a past-tense narrative verb 'came'. به + درگاه marks motion 'into/to the presence of'; درگاه here means the royal presence/court. سالار is a title 'commander/lord' modifying the following بار (a noun); the whole phrase 'درگاه سالار بار' names the locus he approaches. The sequence is verb + prepositional complement.
segments.s655-g1131: بپرسید is a past narrative form 'he asked' (classical/poetic past). بسیار here functions adverbially as 'much' / 'many (questions)'. و connects clauses. بنواختشان is a past transitive verb with the pronominal enclitic -شان 'for them' meaning 'caused (music) to be played for them' or 'struck (instruments) for them'.
segments.s655-g1132: بهر is a preposition 'for/to/for the purpose of'. برزنی (from برزن) is a place-name/common noun 'the plain/open field' and جایگه means 'a place/space'. ساختشان is ساخت + enclitic -شان 'made for them' (he caused a place to be made for them). Word order: prepositional phrase (purpose) + location noun + verb with enclitic object.
segments.s655-g1133: وزان پس is a two-part sequence meaning 'and/after that' or 'thereupon'; both particles set temporal sequence. شهنشاه is the subject 'the emperor/king of kings'. یزدان‌پرست is an epithet 'God-worshipper' (a descriptive adjective after the title). Together they form the topical noun phrase that the next verbs govern.
segments.s655-g1134: به خاک آمد is a motion expression 'came down to the ground' with به + خاک marking direction/target and آمد 'came' in past tense. از جایگاه نشست follows with از 'from' indicating departure/source (from his seat/place) and نشست (here the verb 'sat') meaning 'sat down/settled'. The two verbs relate sequentially: he descended, then sat (away) from his place.
segments.s655-g1135: ستایش is a noun 'praise'. همی‌کرد is the classical imperfective/past-habitual form meaning 'kept doing' or 'was praising' (a common narrative device). برکردگار is a prepositional phrase literally 'upon/to the Creator' indicating the object/recipient of the praise.
segments.s655-g1136: که‌ای is the relative/introducing particle 'who/that' (here introducing direct address or a following clause). برتر از is a comparative 'higher/supreme than'; گردش روزگار is a collocation 'the course/turns of time/fate' where گردش is 'revolution/turns' and روزگار denotes 'fate' or 'the course of time'. Together they form the clause praising God's supremacy over the shifts of fate.
segments.s655-g121: A compressed classical-address sequence: همی is an emphatic/temporal particle ('then/just'); این is the proximate demonstrative 'this (one)'; the two short prepositional prefixes ب + دان and ب + دین here mark addresses ('to that', 'to these') in a tight, somewhat archaic concatenation; گفت is the speech verb ('said') and جنگ is the object/topic 'battle'. Word order is telegraphic (speaker — addressee(s) — said — topic), typical of epic diction.
segments.s655-g122: ندیدیم is the negative past first‑plural ('we did not see'); هرگز is an adverb of negation 'never'; چنین is a demonstrative/adverb 'such/so'; با is the preposition 'with'; درنگ is a noun 'delay, hesitation'. Together they form the reported utterance: 'we have never seen such a battle with (such) delay/hesitation.'
segments.s655-g123: همانا is an emphatic particle 'indeed/verily'; نه before مردم negates the predicate ('not people'); مردم is the noun 'people'; بدند is the past 3rd‑plural of بودن ('they were'); آن سپاه is the topical noun phrase 'that host/army' placed after the predicate by poetic inversion — English requires reordering for naturalness.
segments.s655-g124: نشایست is an impersonal adjectival/participial form meaning 'unfitting' or 'not proper' (here negative sense implied by the form/idiom); کردن is the light verb 'to do' used to form the verbal phrase; بدیشان 'to them'; نگاه 'a look/glance'. Together: 'it was not fitting to cast a look at them.'
segments.s655-g125: به چهره is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in/with regard to (their) appearance' (به here = 'in'); همه 'all'; دیو 'demon' used as predicate noun; بودند is past plural 'were'; و links the next predicate; دد here means 'fiend/enemy' — two predicates describing the same people.
segments.s655-g126: به دل 'in (their) hearts' (به = 'in'); دور ز = 'far from' (دور = distant; ز = from, a poetic preposition); اندیشه 'thought'; نیک و بد 'good and bad'. The phrase describes their inward state: 'in their hearts they were far from any thought of good or ill.'
segments.s655-g127: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from/because of' introducing a list of martial items; ژوپین is a gambeson (padded jacket); the sequence uses coordinating و 'and' to join نیزه (spear), گرز (mace) and تیغ (sword). This is a nominal list presented as the cause/context for what follows.
segments.s655-g128: تو گفتی is the past 2nd‑person 'you said'; the following clause is reported content: ندانند is a negative present/subjunctive form of دانستن ('they do not/know not'); راه 'the way'; گریغ is an archaic/poetic word here functioning as 'flight/retreat' (contextual interpretation). Grammatically this is reported speech: 'you said (that) they do not know the way of flight.'
segments.s655-g1553: اگر introduces a protasis (conditional) — 'if'. دادگر is a predicate noun/adjective 'just' (a just man/ruler). باشد is the classical present/subjunctive form of بودن used in conditions ('is' / 'be'), and it follows the predicate; و simply links the following adjective نیک‌نام, which describes the same subject ('well‑reputed').
segments.s655-g1554: بیابد (from یابیدن) is a third‑person present/subjunctive/indicative meaning 'attains' or 'finds' and here expresses the result clause of the condition. ز is the poetic preposition 'from / by means of', followed by the parallel nouns گفتار و کردار 'words and deeds' (source of the attainment). کام is the thing attained — 'desire / satisfaction'; the phrase بیابد ز ... کام = 'attains satisfaction from ...'.
segments.s655-g1555: بدو is the indirect object pronoun 'to him / unto him'. گفت is the past tense verb 'said' (here introducing reported speech). کاندر is a folded classical form (که + اندر) meaning 'who/which in' or 'that in', and جهان is the locative 'in the world'; مستمند is the predicate 'needy/poor'. Word order places the location ('کـاندر جهان') before the predicate.
segments.s655-g1556: کدامست is the interrogative copula 'which/which one is' (archaic contraction of کدام است). بد روز is a compound adjective meaning 'of ill‑fortune / ill‑fated' (literally 'bad day'), and ناسودمند is a negative adjective 'unprofitable, fruitless'. The copula precedes a coordinate adjectival phrase (بد روز و ناسودمند) that answers the question.
segments.s655-g1557: چنین داد پاسخ is a poetic inversion meaning 'thus gave answer' (i.e., 'he answered thus'); داد here is the verb 'gave' and پاسخ is the noun 'answer'. که introduces the content clause. درویش زشت is the noun phrase serving as the subject of the subordinate clause ('the ugly/odious dervish' — درویش here 'dervish/poor ascetic', زشت 'ugly' or 'vile').
segments.s655-g1558: The clause begun by که continues with a correlative negative idea (in the verse the pattern is نه ... نه ...): نه کام یابد نه خرم بهشت — 'attains neither desire nor the joyous paradise.' یابد (from یابیدن) is the verb 'attains/finds'; کام is 'desire/wish'; خرم modifies بهشت as 'joyful/blissful paradise'. The Persian order is object(s) before the verb (SOV) and the two negated items are linked by the correlative نَه ... نَه.
segments.s655-g1559: بپرسید and گفتا are past narrative verbs describing the same subject: بپرسید is an older/poetic past form 'he asked' (the initial ب- here is part of the classical verb form), and گفتا is the archaic past 'he said'. و simply links the two actions. که introduces an indirect question/claused content (‘that/ who …’). بدبخت is a noun meaning 'unfortunate person' (here used as the thing being asked about), کی is the interrogative 'who', and ست is the copula 'is' (an archaic short form of است) completing the embedded question.
segments.s655-g1560: که at the start of this segment introduces a relative/subordinate clause describing the 'بدبخت' just named ('who …'). همواره is an adverb 'always'. از introduces the cause/ source ('from' or 'because of') and takes درد 'pain/suffering' as its object. باید is a modal 'must/ought to', and گریست is an archaic infinitive/verb form 'to weep' or general presentive 'weep' used as the complement of باید (i.e. 'must weep'). Word order follows the classical pattern where the modal/auxiliary precedes the verb; the whole clause qualifies the noun in the previous segment.
segments.s655-g1025: This is a noun phrase: کلاهی (a hat) followed by a prepositional/relational phrase به کردار مشکین زره, where به + کردار functions idiomatically as 'in the form/manner of'. مشکین (an adjective) qualifies زره (armor/ cuirass). There is no verb here; the whole phrase describes the hat's appearance.
segments.s655-g1026: ز گوهر کشیده is an adjectival/participial phrase modifying the hat: ز (from/of) + گوهر (gem) + کشیده (past participle 'drawn/strewn'), i.e. 'drawn from gems' or 'strewn with gems'. گره برگره is a reduplicative poetic expression 'knot upon knot' (گره = knot; برگره here = 'on/over knot'), reinforcing the piled/ornamented effect.
segments.s655-g1027: گره بسته is a noun + participle ('a knot tied/bound'). از تار indicates the material or source ('from the warp/threads'). برتافته is a past participle/adjectival compound (بر + تافته) meaning 'lifted up' or 'set up' and further describes the knot; word order is noun + modifiers following it, typical of Persian poetic description.
segments.s655-g1028: به افسون here means 'by/with enchantment' (به used adverbially), followed by یک اندر دگر بافته, a compressed relative phrase 'one woven into another' (اندر = in/within; دگر = another; بافته = woven, past-participle). The whole segment says the knots are woven by magic, one within another.
segments.s655-g1029: چو begins a simile ('as if' / 'like'), followed by از غالیه ('from the costly/precious [fabric]') indicating source/material. برگل انگشتری is a compound where برگل ('flower-adorned / on-flower') modifies انگشتری ('ring'); the word order places the modifier before the head noun, producing the image 'a flowered ring taken from fine brocade.'
segments.s655-g1030: همه ('all' / 'entirely') here refers back to the ornaments/knots and functions as a quantifier. زیر is the preposition 'under/beneath'. انگشتری مشتری is a genitive/attributive phrase 'a ring of (or belonging to) مشتری'; in this poetic context مشتری is an epithet (here taken as 'Jupiter'), so the line reads 'all beneath a Jupiter-ring.' The phrase is elliptical and descriptive rather than a full clause.
segments.s655-g1031: درو is a contracted/poetic locution (= در او / thereupon) introducing what follows. شاه (the king) is the subject and نوشین‌روان is an epithet ('sweet‑souled') inserted after the noun. خیره ماند is a compound verb phrase in the past tense meaning 'remained staring/astonished' (خیره = astonished/staring, ماند = remained).
segments.s655-g1032: برو here functions as a discourse particle 'thereupon/then' (in classical usage), followed by نام یزدان ('the name of God') as the direct object and فراوان as an adverb 'many times/abundantly'. بخواند is the past tense third-person singular 'he recited/called (upon)'. Together: 'then he abundantly/ repeatedly invoked the name of God.'
segments.s655-g1521: بگویم is a first-person present/subjunctive form used here as a performative/future-like 'I will say' (classical/poetic use). کنون 'now' is a temporal adverb placed after the verb for emphasis. گفت‌ها is a nominal/collective plural 'sayings' or 'what is said' that the speaker will relate; سر-به-سر follows to indicate 'one by one' or 'in succession' (poetic order: verb + time + what + manner).
segments.s655-g1522: اگر introduces a conditional clause 'if'. یادگیری is a verbal noun 'learning' functioning as the subject of the condition. همه is a quantifier 'all/everything' modifying یادگیری. دربدر is a predicate/adjective meaning 'astray/wandering'; together the clause reads 'if all learning is astray/wandering'.
segments.s655-g1523: This is a nominal (copular) clause: خرد (wisdom) is the subject and خلعت ایزدیست is the predicate 'is a divine robe'. مرد را marks the possessor/beneficiary with the classical object/topic marker را (here 'for a man'/'of a man'). ایزدیست contains the copula -ست attached to ایزدی 'divine', giving 'is divine'.
segments.s655-g1524: ز is the poetic/prepositional 'from' leading into the following phrases. اندیشه is 'thought' (here meaning mere speculation). دورست is an adjectival predicate with the copular -ست 'is distant'. و links the two parallel predicates; دور از بدیست repeats the pattern 'is far from badness/evil', where از marks the separation and بدیست is 'is bad/evil' as predicate. Overall it describes the wise man's distance from empty thought and from evil.
segments.s655-g1525: هنرمند is 'a skilful person' or 'craftsman' as the head noun. کز is the older relative pronoun 'who/that', introducing the relative clause. خویشتن is the reflexive 'himself', and در شگفت is a prepositional phrase 'in wonder/amazement' describing his state ('who is amazed at himself').
segments.s655-g1526: بماند is a present/subjunctive form 'may/does remain' used here to state that the art/endowment continues. هنر زو = 'his art' (زو is the older form of 'از او' or the pronominal element indicating 'from him/of him'). نباید گرفت is an impersonal negative 'should not be taken' (نباید = 'ought not', گرفت in passive sense 'be taken away'). The clause means 'his art remains and should not be taken from him.'
segments.s655-g1527: همان is a demonstrative 'that/same' pointing back to the person just described. خوش‌منش is an adjectival compound 'good-natured/courteous' modifying the person. مردم here specifies 'people' or 'his folk', and خویش‌دار describes attachment or relation to one's own (in this line it means 'affectionate/attached to his own people'). The whole phrase is a descriptive noun phrase 'that courteous man, attached to his people.'
segments.s655-g1528: نباشد is the negative copular form 'is not / should not be' used here to assert a negative judgment. به چشم = 'in the eye (sight) of', followed by خردمند 'the wise' as the possessor; خوار is the predicate 'despised/low'. The phrase as a whole means 'is not despised in the eyes of the wise.'
segments.s655-g1697: چنین داد پاسخ is a compact clause meaning 'he gave such an answer.' چنين (preposed) qualifies the noun phrase 'داد پاسخ' ('gave an answer'); داد is the past verb 'gave' and پاسخ is its object. The following که begins a subordinate clause linking this answer to what it stated.
segments.s655-g1698: The relative clause introduced by که modifies the previously mentioned 'برترمنش'. باشد is the 3rd-person present/subjunctive form of 'to be' (here functioning as the copula in the relative clause). فراوان qualifies سرزنش (making it 'many/abundant reproaches'); بدو is the dative pronoun 'to him' placed before the noun. Word order is relative-clause + adverb/adjective + indirect object + noun.
segments.s655-g1699: همان نیز 'that same one also' picks out the person just mentioned; همان is a demonstrative, نیز adds 'also'. کاو here functions as an archaic relative/pronoun meaning 'who' (linking to the clause 'آز دارد'), and دارد is the present 'has/possesses.' آز is a noun 'greed'; درشت is an adjective 'coarse/large' describing the person or features.
segments.s655-g1700: پرآژنگ رخساره is a noun phrase: پرآژنگ ('adorned/with make-up') modifies رخساره ('face/complexion'). The conjunction و links the following nominal phrase بسته مشت where بسته is an adjective/participial form 'closed/clenched' modifying مشت ('fist'). The whole phrase lists physical details (adorned face and a clenched fist).
segments.s655-g1701: بپرسید is a past-tense verb 'he asked' (classical narrative past). تا introduces the indirect question here ('who is...'); جاودان دوست is the noun phrase 'eternal friend' and کیست is the interrogative + copula ('who is'). The clause functions as the content of the asking.
segments.s655-g1702: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' governing درد جدایی 'the pain of separation' (درد here connotes emotional sorrow). The relative که introduces 'who will weep'; خواهد is the future auxiliary 'will' and گریست is the verb 'to weep/weeped' used here as the future action. Together they form a relative clause modifying 'دوست' in the previous segment.
segments.s655-g1703: چنین داد پاسخ (again) 'he gave such an answer.' که introduces the content of that answer. کردار نیک is a noun phrase 'good deed/conduct' (کردار 'action/character' modified by نیک 'good'). The clause sets up what was asserted about 'good conduct.'
segments.s655-g1704: نخواهد here functions as a (negative) future/immediate modal ('will not' in classical usage) taking the following nominal infinitive-like phrase جدا بودن 'to be apart' as its complement. از introduces the source/relation 'from' and یار نیک 'a good friend' is the noun phrase governed by از. Together the construction asserts that 'good conduct will not be separated from a good friend.'
segments.s655-g1441: میان + دوتن is a prepositional phrase 'between the two (people)'. کین و جنگ are the coordinated objects 'hatred and war' and آَوَرد is a past 3rd-person singular verb meaning 'caused/brought about' (here: 'stirred up'). Word order places the prepositional phrase and objects before the verb, a common poetic order.
segments.s655-g1442: بکوشد is a classical present/jussive-form of کوشیدن with the prefix ب- meaning 'he strives/attempts'. که introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'in order that'). پیوستگی is the noun 'connectedness/union', and بشکرد is an archaic perfective/subjunctive form meaning 'to break' (here: 'to shatter/break up').
segments.s655-g1443: دگر modifies دیو: 'the other/another demon.' بی‌دانش and وناسپاس are adjectival descriptors ('ignorant' and 'and-ungrateful'); وناسپاس is a prefixed form with و- linking it to the previous adjective rather than a separate conjunction.
segments.s655-g1444: نباشد here functions as a negative jussive/optative ('may he not be' / 'let him not be' or descriptively 'is not'). خردمند and نیکی‌شناس are predicate adjectives joined by و ('wise' and 'knower/discerner of good'), so the clause says he is not (or should not be) either of those.
segments.s655-g1445: به نزدیک او is a locative phrase 'near him / in his proximity.' رای و شرم form a coordinated nominal group 'counsel and modesty,' and اندکیست (اندک است) is the fused predicate 'is little'—so the clause states that there is little counsel and little modesty near him.
segments.s655-g1446: به چشمش is an idiomatic locution 'in his eye / in his view' (چشمش here = his regard). بدو is a dative 'to him' reinforcing perspective; نیک = 'good.' هردو + یکیست is a predication 'both are one' meaning 'the two (good and bad) are the same to him.'
segments.s655-g1447: ز (poetic variant of از) is the preposition 'from/of'; دانا is the addressee 'the wise (one)'; بپرسید is the past tense 'he asked' (3rd person); پس شهریار places the subject 'then the king' after the verb in poetic word order, giving 'then the king asked the wise.'
segments.s655-g1448: که introduces an indirect question/clause ('how...'). چون here means 'how' (interrogative). دیو is the object 'a demon'; با دل is an instrumental/adverbial phrase 'with the heart' (i.e., inwardly/by heart or in one's feelings); کند کارزار = 'makes/engages in battle' (کند = 'does/makes', کارزار = 'battle').
segments.s655-g1657: چنین داد پاسخ is a fronted adverbial + verb + noun phrase: چنین (thus/so) modifies داد (gave) and پاسخ (answer) follows as the object — literally 'gave answer thus.' The particle که here begins a subordinate quotation/relative clause ('that...') and را is the definite/object marker attaching to the following noun روی (here 'countenance/face'), so که‌ای را سه روی links the quotation marker to 'three faces.' Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s655-g1658: بسازد is 3rd-person singular present (classical/poetic) used in the subordinate clause introduced by که and means 'makes/assumes.' The following خردمند is the agent ('a wise man') and با راه‌جوی is an instrumental/associative phrase ('with / toward the seeker'); the verb-before-subject order is a poetic inversion (V S ...).
segments.s655-g1659: یکی here enumerates one item ('one' / 'one of them') and is followed by آنک, a relative particle meaning 'who/that' introducing the clause. اندیشد is 3rd-person singular present/classical form 'thinks' or 'is concerned,' and از روز بد is a prepositional phrase 'about/of a bad day' (از = 'about/ concerning' here).
segments.s655-g1660: مگر functions here as 'lest'/'in case that' (introducing a feared outcome). بی‌گنه is an adjective 'innocent,' and بر تنش is the prepositional phrase 'upon his body' (بر = upon; تن + enclitic ش = 'his body'). بد رسد uses رسد (3rd-person singular, here 'befall' / 'happen to') with بد as the adverse thing — together 'that evil should befall his innocent body.'
segments.s655-g1661: بترسد is a 3rd-person singular verb 'he fears' (classical present/subjunctive form); ز (poetic form of از) marks origin/cause: 'fears on account of/from the deed.' کار فریبنده دوست is a noun phrase 'the deceitful deed(s) of a friend' (کار = deed/act; فریبنده = deceptive; دوست = friend).
segments.s655-g1662: که introduces a relative clause describing what that deceitful deed does. با here lists what is taken/affected ('with/along with'): مغز جان ('mind/brain and life/soul') followed by خواهد (3rd-person singular 'wills/asks/takes' in an older sense, here expressing what the deceit will do) and then a coordinate list و خون و پوست ('and blood and skin'). The verb precedes or accompanies the list for poetic compression.
segments.s655-g1663: سه دیگر = 'three others' (a numeral + 'other'). ز (poetic from/of) introduces the source: ز بیدادگر شهریار = 'from/of the cruel/tyrannical king.' The phrase states the origin of the additional items being enumerated.
segments.s655-g1664: که begins a relative clause describing the tyrant: بیگار بستاند is a verb phrase meaning 'he exacts/demands (forced) labour.' بیگار is the noun 'forced labour/corvée,' and بستاند is 3rd-person singular classical form 'takes/exacts' (here used in the sense 'exacts/demands'); از مرد کار ('from a man, work') uses از to mark source ('from the man') with کار specifying 'work' — together, 'who exacts forced labour/work from a man.' Note: the supplied lexical senses for بستاند did not include 'to exact/take,' so that sense is not in the provided list.
segments.s655-g65: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from/among' introducing the group; خویشان is the noun 'kinsmen/relatives' governed by ز; the two following names (ارجاسب وافراسیاب) are the persons whose kin are meant — وافراسیاب is written here as a single token starting with the conjunction و (classical orthography).
segments.s655-g66: نپرداخت is the past negative of پرداختن (here 'did not give/ spare/attend to'); it governs the following object-like phrase. یک تن is the indefinite numeral + noun 'one person' (used idiomatically to mean 'not a single person'). به + آرام و خواب marks the things not given/spared — 'to rest and to sleep' — with آرام and خواب as nouns; و simply links the two nouns.
segments.s655-g67: برفتند is the past plural verb 'they went/departed'; یکسر is an adverb meaning 'all at once/entirely' modifying the verb; به + گلزریون is the prepositional phrase giving the destination (Golzariyūn).
segments.s655-g68: همه 'all/everyone' quantifies the following coordinated nominal images. The pattern 'سر پر از خشم' and 'دل پر زخون' uses سر/dل (head/heart) followed by پر ('full (of)') and a noun giving the content; از marks 'full of X' in the first phrase (پر از خشم), while classical style permits the second phrase without an explicit از (پر زخون). The two phrases are linked by و.
segments.s655-g69: سپهدار is a title 'commander'; خاقان چین is a genitive construction 'the khagan of China' (China qualifies khagan); سنجه is an appositional personal name (here identifying the commander). بود is the past copula 'was' describing existence/presence.
segments.s655-g70: همی is an adverb meaning 'continually/still' or an emphatic 'indeed'; به آسمان بر زد is a verbal phrase where بر (a directional/preverbal particle) combines with زد ('struck/sent') to mean 'sent up/rose toward the sky'; از خاک gives the origin 'from the earth' and دود is the noun 'smoke' which is the thing sent up.
segments.s655-g71: ز marks cause or origin 'from/by'; جوش سواران is a genitive phrase 'the surge/charge of the horsemen' (جوش = surge/boiling); به چاچ اندرون is a locative phrase 'into inner Chach' with به + place and اندرون specifying 'interior/within.'
segments.s655-g72: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; خون شد literally 'blood became' (شد = 'became'); به رنگ = 'to/in the color of' introduces a simile/metaphor; آب گلزریون is a genitive 'the water of Golzariyūn' specifying the colour compared to which the blood turned.
segments.s655-g1153: به تخت آمد is a simple past motion clause: به (preposition) + تخت (destination, 'the throne') + آمد (past of آمدن 'to come'). از جایگه نماز is a prepositional phrase 'from his place of prayer' where از + جایگه ('place, position') governs نماز ('prayer') as the place he came from. Word order follows Persian SOV tendencies but with the preposition before the noun phrase.
segments.s655-g1154: ز گرگان begins another clause with the poetic preposition ز ('from') + گرگان (proper/place name, here 'Gorgan'). برفتن is an infinitival/nominal verb 'to depart' used here with گرفتند (past plural) to express onset (they 'took up' or 'began' an action). گرفتند ساز literally 'they took/ seized instruments' — گرفتند is past transitive and ساز is its object ('instruments' = they began to play or took up instruments). The clause therefore reads: 'from Gorgan they took up instruments.'
segments.s655-g1155: برآمد is a past intransitive 'arose/issued forth.' It takes the following infinitival/masdar-like خروشیدن ('roaring') as its complement: 'a roaring arose.' گاودم is an attributive noun ('cow's tail' / the tail-end) used poetically to qualify the sound (i.e. a low, bovine bellow), placed after the verbal noun to indicate the character/source of the cry.
segments.s655-g1156: ز درگاه is 'from the court' (ز + درگاه, here 'royal court' or threshold). آواز ('voice/cry') is the noun subject/instance of sound and رویینه خم is a compound adjectival phrase: رویینه ('armored') modifying خم ('curve/bend' — here a poetic reference to a bow or arched, martial object), so آواز رویینه خم = 'the armored/iron cry of the bow' or more generally 'a martial, armored cry.' The preposition z places the source as the court.
segments.s655-g1157: سپه (the army/host) is the subject. برنشست is past 'sat down/ took a seat.' و coordinates the next verb. بنه is a nominal object meaning 'a bundle/pack' (here baggage or bundles) and برنهاد ('placed down/put down') is the past transitive verb. The sequence is straightforward narrative past: 'the host sat down and laid down their bundles.'
segments.s655-g1158: This clause uses ز + یزدان نیکی دهش as a noun phrase 'a gift/of goodness from God' (ز marking source/agent in a poetic register). کرد is the past of کردن ('to do/make') used here as the main past verb meaning 'he made/caused' but idiomatically part of 'کرد یاد' = 'remembered/kept in mind.' یاد functions as the object meaning 'remembrance' or 'memory.' Together: 'he remembered (the) gift of God's goodness.'
segments.s655-g1159: This is a paratactic list introduced by the poetic preposition ز ('from/of'): ز دینار و دیبا و تاج و کمر enumerates items (dinar, brocade, crown, girdle). The repeated و are simple coordinators; the list structure means these are gifts or valuables coming from the cited source(s).
segments.s655-g1160: Another paratactic phrase continuing the list: ز گنج درم هم = 'from the treasury, dirhams as well' where هم ('also') attaches to درم. The following ز در و گهر repeats the preposition for emphasis: 'and from the gate/door (here: palace/court) gems.' The syntax repeats ز to mark separate sources and places the items (درم, گهر) after their governing nouns.
segments.s655-g1409: ده‌اند is a classical 3rd-person plural verb form meaning “give/place”; here the verb precedes its subject (اهرمَن) in inverted poetic order, so the clause reads “Ahriman gives.” هم is the adverb “also/likewise.” به + نیروی شیر uses به as an instrumental/associative preposition (“by/with”), and نیروی شیر is a genitive construction (“the strength/power of the lion”).
segments.s655-g1410: که introduces a relative/content clause (“which/that”). آرند is a present (classical) 3rd‑person plural form of آوردن ‘bring’ (here “bring/bring about”). جان و خرد is a coordinated direct object (soul and wisdom) marked by the object marker را. به + زیر conveys the idea of bringing something “under” (i.e. under control/subject to), so به here has a directional/into sense and زیر means “under/subject to.”
segments.s655-g1411: بدو is the prepositional phrase “to him/unto him.” گفت here is the past-tense verb ‘said’ (poetic narrative uses گفت after the indirect object), and کسری is the speaker’s name (Kisrā). که introduces the embedded question/relative clause. دیو چیست literally “what is a div/demon?” — چی (what) + ست (is) form the interrogative copula construction.
segments.s655-g1412: کزیشان is a fused prepositional/relative element meaning roughly “from/of them” or “which of them”; here it introduces for whom the action holds. خرد را is the definite object “wisdom,” marked by the object marker را. بباید is the modal/impersonal present/future-like form ‘one must/it is necessary,’ and گریست is the infinitive/nominal form ‘to weep/lament’ (here used as the action that must be done). The whole clause means “for/which of them must one lament wisdom.”
segments.s655-g1413: چنین داد پاسخ is a compact narrative formula: چنین (“thus/so”) + داد (past “gave”) + پاسخ (“an answer”), so “thus he gave answer.” که introduces the content of the answer. آز و نیاز are two coordinated nouns (“avarice/greed and need/longing”), the topic of the following predicate.
segments.s655-g1414: دو دیوند is a predicate identifying the preceding pair as “two diyvand(s)” (a diyvand is a madman/demonic being); در classical Persian the numeral follows the predicate noun here for poetic rhythm. با زور و گردن‌فراز uses با + noun to mean “with/by (their) strength and (their) pride/boastfulness”; گردن‌فراز is an adjective/noun meaning “proud, neck‑raising” (i.e. arrogant).
segments.s655-g1415: دگر ('other' / 'another') introduces a new class of things; the nouns خشم (anger) and رشک (envy) are linked by و and followed by the copula است 'is'. The pattern repeats with ننگ است ('shame is') and then the series closes with و کین ('and hatred'), all listing negative qualities; the repeated است is the copula asserting existence/identity.
segments.s655-g1416: چو is a comparative/illustrative particle 'as/like' (introducing examples). The following nouns نمام (a tale-bearer/slanderer), دوروی (duplicity/hypocrisy), and ناپاک دین (impure/unclean religion — adjective + دین 'religion') form a paratactic list of examples introduced by چو; و simply links the items.
segments.s655-g1313: هرآنکس که = 'whoever' (relative phrase). نپسندد is the negative present/potential of پسندیدن ('to approve/like') and here functions as a relative-clause predicate: 'whoever does not approve'. این راه ما = 'this way of ours' (این before راه, with the possessor ما following the noun in older/poetic word order). The whole segment is the protasis (if‑clause) modifying the optative main clause that follows.
segments.s655-g1314: مبادا که is an optative/negative particle meaning 'may it not be / let it not be that'. باشد is the 3rd‑person present subjunctive/jussive of بودن used here with an optative force ('be' / 'exist'), and به درگاه ما is a locative phrase 'in/at our court/presence' (به + noun, with possessor ما after the noun in poetic order). Together: 'May it not be that (he) is in our court.'
segments.s655-g1315: جهاندار is the subject 'the sovereign'. یک روز is a temporal adverbial 'one day'. بنشست is the simple past 3rd‑person singular 'sat down'. شاد is a predicative adjective following the verb, giving 'sat down joyous' (i.e. 'sat down happily'). The sequence is a standard narrative past with time‑phrase before the verb.
segments.s655-g1316: بزرگان داننده is a noun phrase 'the great/wise ones' (بزرگان 'great ones' modified by داننده 'knowing/knowledgeable'); را is the definite object marker signaling that this phrase is the direct object. بار داد is an idiomatic verbal combination 'gave a burden/charge' where بار = 'burden/responsibility' and داد = past 'gave' — together meaning 'entrusted (them) with a duty' or 'gave them the charge.'
segments.s655-g1317: سخن گفت is a verbal phrase 'he spoke' (literally 'speech he said'), a common noun+verb pairing. خندان is a participial/adverbial adjective 'smiling' modifying the action/subject. و بگشاد چهر = 'and opened/revealed his face' (بگشاد is the past/stative sense of 'to open' used here as 'his face brightened/turned open'), with چهر 'face' as its object; overall: 'he spoke smiling and his countenance opened (i.e. became cheerful).' Note the compact, poetic ordering (noun+verb, adjectives/participles interspersed).
segments.s655-g1318: بر تخت is a locative prepositional phrase 'upon the throne' (بر + تخت). بنشست is the past 3rd‑person singular 'sat down'. بوزرجمهر is the proper name of the subject; here the name follows the verb (a poetic inversion) and identifies who performed the actions just described.
segments.s655-g1319: یکی here is a numeral/adverb 'one' or 'once' modifying آفرین (an exclamation of praise). آفرین کرد is a light-verb construction: آفرین (the interjection/noun 'praise/“bravo”') + کرد (past of 'to do/make'), functioning together as 'uttered/presented a praise.' بر is the preposition 'to/toward' linking the action to کردگار (the Creator), the recipient of the acclamation.
segments.s655-g1320: This segment is a sequence of appositive epithets naming and praising the divine addressee: خداوند (God), پیروز (the Victorious — used as an epithet), و (and) پروردگار (the Lord/Nurturer). There is no new verb; these nouns stand in apposition to کردگار in the previous segment.
segments.s655-g1321: چنین گفت is an adverbial phrase + past verb: 'thus said' (چنین 'thus/so' before the verb is common in poetry). کای is the vocative particle 'O' addressing someone, and داور تازه‌روی is the noun (داور 'judge') followed by the descriptive compound تازه‌روی 'fresh-faced/newly risen' — an epithet placed after the noun.
segments.s655-g1322: که introduces a subordinate clause ('that' / 'so that' / a wish). بر تو is a prepositional phrase ('upon/against you' → here 'reach you'), and نیابد is a negative present-subjunctive/optative form of یافتن used like 'may not find/reach'. سخن زشت‌گوی is a genitive/attributive phrase: 'speech/words of the foul‑speaker' (زشت‌گوی = one who speaks ill).
segments.s655-g1323: A string of epithets: خجسته is an adjective 'blessed/auspicious' placed before the title شهنشاه (Shahanshah 'king of kings'), and پیروزگر is another epithet 'victorious/conqueror' attached to the title. The sequence is nominal apposition common in epic praise.
segments.s655-g1324: جهاندار is another honorific ('world‑ruler' / sovereign). با دانش و با گهر is a coordinated prepositional phrase 'with knowledge and with jewel(s)' (repetition of با for emphasis), where دانش is an abstract noun and گهر a concrete epithet (preciousness/treasure).
segments.s655-g1325: نبشتم is a first‑person past verb 'I wrote/inscribed' (an archaic/poetic verbal form). سخن چند follows as the object 'a few words' (سخن 'words/verse' with چند 'some/a few' after it), and بر پهلوی here means 'in/into Pahlavi' or 'in the Pahlavi manner/script' — پهلوی is likely the language/script in which the lines were written.
segments.s655-g1326: ابر دفتر is a fixed poetic compound meaning 'cloud‑patterned folio/notebook' (ابر here denotes the ornamental 'cloud' pattern used on fine paper). و connects the two objects, and کاغذ خسروی means 'royal paper' (خسروی = regal, attributive to کاغذ).
segments.s655-g1327: سپردم is the first‑person past 'I entrusted/handed over'. به گنجور is the dative phrase 'to the treasury/treasure‑house' (گنجور here = repository), and تا روزگار introduces a temporal clause 'until the time/when'.
segments.s655-g1328: برآید is an impersonal/third‑person present-subjunctive 'arise/come about' tied to تا ('until the time comes that...'). بخواند is subjunctive 'may read/should read' describing what will happen when that time comes. مگر before شهریار is 'unless/except' — here marking the possibility that the king himself might read it earlier.
segments.s655-g1225: This is a noun phrase with a following relative clause: 'همه زر و گوهر' = 'all the gold and jewels', then 'فزونی که برد' qualifies them as the 'surplus/excess that he had taken'. 'که' introduces the relative clause, and 'برد' is a simple past sense 'took/obtained' (an older, compressed style typical of epic Persian). Word order is noun → modifier (فزونی) → relative clause.
segments.s655-g1226: Main verb clause: 'سراسر به گنجور آتش سپرد' = 'he consigned/entrusted (سپرد) throughout (سراسر) to the fire's treasury (به گنجور آتش)'. 'سراسر' is an adverb meaning 'entirely/throughout' placed before the prepositional phrase 'به گنجور آتش'. 'سپرد' is past tense 'handed over/entrusted'.
segments.s655-g1227: 'پراگند بر موبدان سیم و زر' is a verbless or nominal clause typical of epic compression: 'پراگند' (scattered/broadcast) governs the prepositional phrase 'بر موبدان' = 'upon/among the mobeds' and the material 'سیم و زر' = 'silver and gold' follows. The preposition 'بر' here marks distribution toward the priests.
segments.s655-g1228: 'همه جامه بخشیدشان با گهر' continues the action: 'همه' (= all/every) modifies 'جامه' (garments) and 'بخشیدشان' is the past transitive verb 'gave them' where the clitic object '-شان' refers back to the mobeds. 'با گهر' ('with jewels') is an instrumental/qualifying phrase describing the garments' adornment.
segments.s655-g1229: 'همه موبدان زو توانگر شدند' = 'all the mobeds became wealthy from/thereby (زو)'. 'زو' is the poetic preposition 'from' (variant of از) indicating cause, and 'شدند' is the past of شدن used here as 'became'. Word order places the cause 'زو' immediately before the predicate 'توانگر شدند'.
segments.s655-g1230: 'نیایش کنان پیش آذر شدند' is a participial + locative construction: 'نیایش کنان' is a gerund/participle meaning 'praying' or 'engaged in prayer' (کنان = 'doing'), then 'پیش آذر شدند' = 'they came/stood before the fire'. 'شدند' again functions as the past-tense copula/verb meaning 'became/stood/arrived' in this idiomatic sense.
segments.s655-g1231: 'به زمزم همی‌خواندند آفرین' = 'they were chanting/praising at the zamzam/sacred water.' 'به' marks the location/target, 'زمزم' names the sacred spring or holy water, 'همی‌خواندند' is the imperfective/past-continuous style 'they were chanting/reciting', and 'آفرین' is an exclamation/term of praise ('acclamation').
segments.s655-g1232: 'بران دادگر شهریار زمین' is the object/target of the acclamation: 'آفرین بران ...' = 'praise to that ...'. 'بران' is a poetic prepositional/locative form meaning 'upon that' or 'to that one', and the three following nouns form a title: 'دادگر' (just/righteous one; here 'just ruler'), 'شهریار' (king), 'زمین' (the land/the earth).
segments.s655-g1393: چوخستو is a poetic past/participle meaning 'having risen/left' (an action already done). بیاید is a present/subjunctive form meaning 'comes' or 'goes' here; the sequence 'چوخستو بیاید' therefore reads 'having risen, he comes/goes'. به + دیگر + سرای is the destination phrase 'to another sarā (dwelling)'; note سرای is a poetic noun 'house/abode' (word-list senses given do not match this meaning).
segments.s655-g1394: هم is an adverb 'also/too' modifying the clause. ایدر (اینجا) is 'here'. پر از is the standard 'full of' construction where پر 'full' takes از 'of' plus a noun: پر از درد 'full of pain'. ماند is the past/root 'remain/was left' (third person), and به جای is a fixed locative phrase 'in place'/'at his place' (به marks relation + جای 'place').
segments.s655-g1395: کزین is an archaic/poetic compound 'کز + این' = 'from/when by this' = 'when you pass this'. بگذری is a second-person present/subjunctive or jussive 'you pass/by' (or imperative sense 'pass by'); together they mean 'when you pass by this'. سفله آن را شناس places the object 'that vile one' (آن را) before شناس, which functions as an instruction/verb 'recognize/know' (here used as an exhortation: 'know that he is base').
segments.s655-g1396: که introduces the content-clause 'that ...'. از here marks the target/recipient ('toward' or 'of') in the sense 'has no gratitude to/for X'. پاک is an adjective 'pure', یزدان is the divine 'God', ندارد is the negated present 'he does not have', and سپاس is the noun 'thanks/gratitude' (object of ندارد). The whole clause says he lacks gratitude toward the pure God.
segments.s655-g1397: دریغ آیدش is an impersonal idiom: دریغ 'pity/reluctance' + آیدش 'it comes to him' = 'he feels reluctant' / 'he would be loath'. بهرِهِ تن ز تن is a partitive/possessive phrase 'a share/portion (بهره) of the body from the body'—an idiom meaning 'even a part of his body' or 'a portion of his person'. The word order is poetic but means he begrudges giving bodily portion.
segments.s655-g1398: شود is the third-person singular of 'become'/'will be' (frequently used in classical Persian to form resultive expressions). ز آرزوها is a prepositional phrase 'because of/from his desires'. ببندد is a present/jussive form with the verbal prefix ب- meaning 'he closes' (here reflexive of speech), and دهن 'mouth' is the direct object: 'he will shut his mouth (keep silent) because of his desires.'
segments.s655-g1399: همان بهر جانش: همان 'that very' + بهر as 'benefit/boon' (here sense 'benefit') + جانش 'to his life' — 'that very benefit to his life'. که introduces a relative clause: که دانش بود 'which was knowledge/learning' (بود = 'was'). The clause apposes 'learning' as the thing that constituted his benefit.
segments.s655-g1400: نداند is the negative present 'he does not know/recognize'. نه coordinates/negates the following verb and gives 'nor' sense: 'nor'. از + دانشی is a partitive/genitive 'of/any knowledge' (دانشی = 'any learning/knowledge'), and بشنود is a present/subjunctive 'he hears' or 'he pays heed to'. Together: 'he does not know it, nor does he listen to/accept (any) learning.'
segments.s655-g1273: همه رودها (all the rivers) is a straightforward NP with همه before the plural noun رودها. همچو دریا شده is a verbless/catenative result clause where شده (the past participle / perfect of شدن) functions as 'have become' and همچو introduces a simile 'like/as'. Word order places the simile phrase (همچو دریا) directly before شده, so the whole reads 'have become like the sea.'
segments.s655-g1274: به + پالیز ... شده: here به has the sense 'into/towards' marking transformation or resultant location ('turned into / became'). پالیز گلبن is a poetic NP 'orchard/plot of rosebuds' where گلبن (rosebud) qualifies پالیز; ثریا شده is a simile/metaphor appended to describe how the rosebuds appear (like ثریا, the Pleiades). شده again marks the change/state 'became / were filled.'
segments.s655-g1275: به ایران is a dative/locative phrase placed before the object, here meaning 'to/among Iran' (i.e. to the people of Iran). زبانها is the direct object 'languages/tongues.' بیاموختند is the 3rd-person-plural past of بیاموختن; in classical usage it is transitive here, 'they taught.' The verb comes at the end, following the typical Persian SOV order.
segments.s655-g1276: روانها ('souls/minds') is the noun phrase receiving the figurative action. بدانش is a fused classical prepositional expression (بـ + دانش) meaning 'with that knowledge' or 'by means of that knowledge.' برافروختند is a metaphorical verb 'they kindled / lit up' (3rd-pl past), used figuratively for inspiring or arousing intellects; it follows the object and completes the clause.
segments.s655-g1277: ز بازارگانان 'from the merchants' uses the poetic/prepositional ز meaning 'from.' هر مرز و بوم is a coordinated phrase 'every border and country/land' where هر quantifies the compound مرز و بوم. The sequence is a noun-phrase listing origin/source (no finite verb in this fragment).
segments.s655-g1278: This segment is a series of 'from X' items, each introduced by ز (poetic 'from'). The repeated ز emphasizes each origin (ز ترک و ز چین و ز سقلاب و روم). چین here denotes the country China; سقلاب is a classical/poetic ethnonym (here treated as the Slavs / Saqalib), and روم is Byzantium/Rome. The conjunction و links the list items.
segments.s655-g1279: ستایش گرفتند uses گرفتند (3rd-pl past of گرفتن) with ستایش as its object, meaning 'they received/won praise.' بر رهنمای uses بر + noun to indicate 'on account of / as guides' or 'in the role of guides' — here it gives the reason/role for which praise was received. The verb follows the NP as usual.
segments.s655-g1280: فزایش گرفت is a somewhat elliptical clause where فزایش ('increase, prosperity') is the noun and گرفت (3rd-sg past) functions 'increase came / was obtained.' Classical Persian often uses a singular verb with an abstract noun subject. از گیا چارپای marks the source 'from plants and quadrupeds' (از = 'from'; گیا = vegetation; چارپای = four-footed animals). The sequence means their prosperity/abundance came from vegetation and beasts.
segments.s655-g1601: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed, somewhat archaic word order meaning 'gave such an answer' (چنین = thus; داد = gave; پاسخ = answer). که introduces a subordinate clause ('that...'). او را uses the object marker را after the pronoun او to mark the person affected by what follows. نیاز is a noun 'need' or 'poverty' that functions as the thing which will 'cover' or 'clothe' him in the next segment.
segments.s655-g1602: بپوشد is a subjunctive/present form of پوشیدن used here in the optative sense 'may (it) clothe/cover'; همی is a classical particle giving durative/emphatic nuance ('always/ever' or simply emphatic here). رشک با ننگ و آز is a coordinated list ('envy with shame and greed') that is syntactically the agent/associate of the covering: literarily 'may need clothe him, together with envy, shame and greed.'
segments.s655-g1603: همان here points back emphatically ('that same' / 'that very'); رشک و کینش is a coordinated noun phrase 'envy and hatred'. نباشد is the negative subjunctive/optative form 'may it not be' or 'let it not be', and نهان is the predicate/adverb 'hidden' — so the clause reads literally 'let that envy and hatred not be hidden.'
segments.s655-g1604: پسندیده is a passive/participial adjective 'pleasing, approved' (literally 'that which is liked'); او is the possessor/agent ('his' / 'to him'); باشد is the subjunctive/exhortative 'may it be'; اندر جهان is the locative 'in the world'. Together: 'may what is pleasing to him exist in the world.'
segments.s655-g1605: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'of' used with an agent ('from the patient man'); مرد شکیبا is the noun + adjective 'the patient man'; بپرسید is a past-tense 3rd-person verb 'asked' (classical narrative past); شاه is the subject/agent placed after the verb (a flexible word order common in epic Persian), giving: 'the king asked the patient man.'
segments.s655-g1606: که introduces the content of the king's question ('that/what'); از صبر دارد is literal 'what he has of patience' (از marks the partitive/source and دارد = 'he has/possesses'); به سر بر کلاه is an older, somewhat inverted phrase meaning 'to place on the head as a hat' (به سر = on the head; بر کلاه = upon/a hat) — idiomatically asking how much patience he has to wear proudly like a hat/crown.
segments.s655-g1607: چنین گفت is the standard narrative 'he said thus' or 'thus he spoke'. کان کس که is an archaic relative construction ('that person who' / 'one who'), with کان adding an old-fashioned demonstrative/relative flavor. نومید گشت is the past resultative 'became despairing/hopeless' (گشت = became).
segments.s655-g1608: دل تیره‌رایش is a compound meaning 'the darkness/gloom of his disposition/heart' (دل = heart; تیره‌رایش = gloomy/discoloured disposition). چو here functions as a temporal/simile conjunction ('when' / 'as') and خورشید گشت uses گشت 'became' — literally 'the darkness of his heart became (like) the sun.' The phrasing is poetic and condensed, so the simile/transformational sense is implied.
segments.s655-g129: همه is a universal quantifier placed before the noun phrase; چهره اژدها is a genitive/attributive noun phrase ('the face of a dragon' / 'dragon-face') where اژدها modifies چهره by juxtaposition. داشتند is the 3rd-person plural past tense of داشتن ('they had'), so the clause reads literally 'they all had a dragon-face.'
segments.s655-g130: همه again quantifies the subject (they/all). نیزه is the (collective) noun 'spear(s)'; بر is the preposition 'on/upon' and ابر 'cloud'. بگذاشتند is the past plural verb 'they placed/laid' (the prefixed b- is a common older/poetic form). Word order is subject–object–verb in this line: 'they all placed spears on the cloud.'
segments.s655-g131: This clause is verbless (a common poetic structure): همه quantifies, چنگ‌هاشان is the plural noun with the possessive enclitic -شان ('their claws'), and بسان 'like/as' introduces the comparison with پلنگ 'leopard'. The sense is 'their claws were like a leopard's.'
