segments.s655-g132: نشد here functions as a past-form verb meaning 'did not become' or 'were not' (i.e., 'were not sated'); the nominal phrase سیر دلشان ('the satiation of their hearts' = 'their hearts' satisfaction') is the patient. تو گویی is an idiomatic simile-particle meaning 'as if' (not a literal 'you'), introducing the following source phrase ز جنگ 'from/because of war'. Word order is inverted for poetic effect: 'their hearts did not become satisfied, as if from war.'
segments.s655-g133: یکی is an indefinite 'one' (a certain one). زین 'saddle' followed by ز + اسبان ('from/of the horses') marks origin/possession: 'one saddle from the horses'. نبرداشتند is past negative plural of برداشتن with the negative prefix ن- meaning 'they did not lift/remove/take.' The clause reads 'they did not take one saddle from the horses.'
segments.s655-g134: بخفتند is past plural of خفتن 'to lie down / sleep' and here indicates they 'fell asleep/lay down' (in poetic/epic context this can imply lying dead). و simply links the clauses. بر is the preposition 'on/upon', برف 'snow', and بگذاشتند 'they placed/laid' (past plural); together the clause means 'they lay down and were laid upon the snow' (or 'they fell and were laid on the snow').
segments.s655-g135: خورش بارگی را is a definite object/topic phrase marked by را; بارگی is 'foliage/leaf' and خورش here is part of that nominal expression (the exact lexical identity is somewhat specialized/poetic). همه is the quantifier 'all', خار is the predicate noun 'thorn', and بود is the past copula 'was.' The clause therefore says 'all the (said) foliage was thorns.'
segments.s655-g136: سواری 'rider' is the subject of the first small clause. بخفتی here is an archaic/past form meaning 'slept/lay down' (3rd-person past). The second coordinated clause 'دو بیدار بود' (surface has the numeral دو 'two') states 'two were awake' with بیدار 'awake' and بود 'was/were' as copula. The line contrasts the sleepers and the awake.
segments.s655-g1537: چنین داد پاسخ is a compact clause meaning “gave (this) answer thus.” چنین is an adverb meaning “thus/so” placed before the verb داد (“gave”). داد is the past verb “gave” and پاسخ is its object “answer/reply.” The conjunction که introduces the reported content (that...).
segments.s655-g1538: چنانند is a 3rd-person plural predicative form “they are such/so.” چون introduces a simile “like/as.” جفت (a pair) is compared to the preceding plural subject (بخت و هنر). با یکدیگر is a reinforcing prepositional phrase “with each other.” Word order is typical Persian: predicate (چنانند) + comparison (چون جفت) + complement (با یکدیگر).
segments.s655-g1539: چنان چون begins a comparison “just as / such as.” تن و جان is the noun pair “body and soul.” که introduces a relative/explanatory clause “which/that,” and یارند here functions as a predicative idea “are companions” (they belong together). و جفت repeats the idea “and (are) a pair,” so the clause describes the relationship of body and soul.
segments.s655-g1540: جفت here continues the description; تنومند پیدا modifies تن and means “strong/robust and apparent,” where پیدا is an adjective “visible/apparent.” The conjunction و links the second member: جان در نهفت = “the soul is hidden within,” with در (in) + نهفت (hidden) forming a locative description. The structure contrasts outward body and inward soul.
segments.s655-g1541: همان کالبد is a demonstrative noun phrase “that very body.” مرد را uses the object/topic marker را to mark the possessor/affected party (“of the man” / “a man's”), and پوششست is a nominal predicate formed with پوشش + است (here fused poetically) meaning “is a covering.” Word order places the demonstrative + noun before the possessed phrase and then the predicate.
segments.s655-g1542: اگر is the conditional “if.” بخت بیدار is a noun + adjective “fortune (is) awake/alert,” where بیدار qualifies بخت. در کوششست is a locative phrase “in/within striving/effort” plus the copula -ست (“is”), so the clause means “if fortune is awake in (one's) effort.” The copula is the classical third-person present form.
segments.s655-g1543: به کوشش is an instrumental/means phrase “by/through effort.” نیاید is a classical negative present/subjunctive form meaning “does not come/is not obtained” (here general truth). بزرگی به جای means “greatness into its place” — به جای = “into place/position” (i.e., to achieve rank). The sentence asserts that effort alone cannot bring greatness.
segments.s655-g1544: مگر here functions as “unless/except,” introducing the necessary exception. بخت نیکش = “his good/auspicious fortune” (نیکش = نیک + possessive -ش “his”). بود is the past copula “was/were” used in the conditional sense (classical usage equivalent to “be”/“were” as guide), and رهنمای is the predicate “guide.” The clause means “unless his good fortune were (is) the guide.”
segments.s655-g1593: چنین داد پاسخ is a compact classical formula meaning “thus gave an answer” (literal order: thus — gave — answer). که introduces the following noun phrase as the content of that answer. دانای پیر is a two-word descriptive noun phrase: دانای (knowledgeable/wise) modifies پیر (old/elder).
segments.s655-g1594: This clause is a relative/explanatory continuation introduced by که (here: “who/which”). با + noun (با آزمایش) indicates the means or accompaniment “by/through trial,” بود is the past copula “was,” and یادگیر is a nominal/participial description “learner” or “one who learns,” describing the preceding دانای پیر.
segments.s655-g1595: بدو گفت کسری is inverted word order typical of classical Persian: بدو (to him) + گفت (said/spoke) + کسری (Khosrow) — “Khosrow said to him.” The following که begins reported speech or a subordinate clause; رامش is a noun “tranquility/peace” and کراست here reads as a proper name or identifier attached by the clause.
segments.s655-g1596: This relative clause (که) describes the named person: دارد is the present-tense verb “has/possesses,” followed by the prepositional phrase به شادی (“in/with joy”). همی is an archaic continuous particle indicating ongoing action/state. پشت راست literally “back straight” is a compact predicate meaning ‘stands with back straight’ or ‘is upright,’ completing the description.
segments.s655-g1597: Again, چنین داد پاسخ introduces reported content. که begins the substantive clause; هر کو here is the classical expression for “whoever/anyone who” (هر = every/each, کو = who/that). ز marks source/reason “from/for” and بیم is the noun “fear,” so this yields “whoever from fear …” (i.e., whoever is free from fear or whoever is of fear).
segments.s655-g1598: بود is the past copula “was” (here used in a generalizing statement), followed by the predicate ایمن “safe/protected.” و links the next clause; باشدش is a fused form (باشد + ش) meaning roughly “it will be to him / shall be his” or “let it be his,” and it introduces the possessions زر و سیم (“gold and silver”), the things that will be his.
segments.s655-g1599: بدو گفت introduces reported speech again: بدو “to him,” گفت “said.” ما را ستایش به چیست is a classical copular question: ما را (us, accusative/topic) + ستایش (praise) + به چیست (what is by/for) — literally “What is praise for us?” or idiomatically “Of what praise are we worthy?” The short copula ست follows چی in the clause.
segments.s655-g1600: This is a follow-up question clause: به نزدیک here means “in the sight/of those near” or “to the people close by,” هرکس “each person/whoever,” پسندیده is a past participle used adjectivally “pleasing/approved,” and the interrogative کی (who) + ست (is) asks “who is pleasing?” — i.e. “Who is liked/accepted by everyone near?”
segments.s655-g41: گوی here functions as an archaic verbal/nominal preface meaning 'called' or 'they call (him)'; the phrase orders the elements as 'called (گوی) + name (غاتفر) + نام (name) + سالارشان (their commander)', where سالارشان is سالار (commander) + possessive suffix -شان 'their'.
segments.s655-g42: This is an adverbial/qualifying phrase describing reputation in battle: به (= toward/into, here 'in/at') + جنگ ('battle') + اندورن ('within, among themselves') and then the predicate/adjective نامبردارشان ('their renowned' = 'famed'); the whole means 'famed in battle among them.'
segments.s655-g43: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'; آگه شد is a stative past ('became aware', literally 'became aware') with شد as the past auxiliary; از کار = 'of the affair' (از + کار), and خاقان چین is a genitive/possessive order meaning 'the Khagan of China' (possessor after possessed).
segments.s655-g44: وزان is a linking adverb meaning 'and from that / thereupon'; هدیه شهریار زمین is a noun phrase 'a gift to the sovereign of the earth' (شهریار زمین = 'sovereign/ruler of the land'); the verb sending is left implicit in this short poetic clause and is understood from the context.
segments.s655-g45: The phrase is structured 'ز (from) + لشکر جهاندیده‌گان (army + جَهاندیده 'worldly-wise' + plural suffix گان) + را (definite object marker) + بخواند (he summoned/called)'; را marks the direct object before the verb, and بخواند here means 'he called/summoned'.
segments.s655-g46: سخن 'speech/matter' modified by the idiom سر به سر (a reduplicated expression meaning 'from head to head' → 'completely, in full'), followed by the locative phrase پیش ایشان 'in the presence of them/before them' and the verb براند (archaic 3rd-singular) which here means 'set forth/present' (literally 'drive/bring forth'); overall: 'he presented the whole matter before them.'
segments.s655-g47: چنین is the adverb 'thus/so'; گفت is the verb 'said' (here used as past 'he said') and باسرکشان is a prepositional phrase 'to/among the chiefs/headstrong ones' (با + سرکشان), with غاتفر (Ghatfar) appearing as the speaker's name. Word order places the adverb first, the verb, the indirect object phrase, and then the speaker's name.
segments.s655-g48: This subordinate clause introduced by که ('that' / 'for') expresses an ill omen: مارا ('to us' / 'for us' as the affected party) + بدآمد (an intransitive idiom 'it turned out badly' / 'an ill omen has befallen') + ز اختر به سر literally 'from the star onto the head' — a fixed idiom meaning 'an unlucky star has fallen on our heads'.
segments.s655-g1121: بگفتند is the past-tense third-person plural narrative verb ‘they said’ and introduces quoted speech. کای is an archaic/poetic vocative meaning ‘O’ placed before شاه ‘king’. ما بنده‌ایم is a present copular clause: ما (we) + بنده (servant) + the present suffix -ایم ‘we are’ (bende-îm), so ‘we are servants.’ The whole segment is a reported utterance directed to the king.
segments.s655-g1122: به فرمان تو is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘by/under your command’ (به here = ‘by’/‘under’); تو is the second-person singular pronoun. در جهان زنده‌ایم is a locative + predication: در جهان ‘in the world/this life’ + زنده‌ایم ‘we are alive / we live’ (present tense with -ایم). Together this completes the quoted statement: their being alive in the world is said to depend on the king’s command.
segments.s655-g1123: همه ‘all/everyone’ is a fronted quantifier. سرفرازیم is the adjective سرفراز ‘proud/victorious’ with the first-person plural copular ending -ایم ‘we are’. با ساز جنگ is an instrumental/accompaniment phrase: با ‘with’ + ساز جنگ ‘the instrument/tune of war’ (ساز can mean musical instrument or tune); the phrase says they are proud accompanied by the war-music or instruments.
segments.s655-g1124: به هامون places the action spatially: به here = ‘to/towards’ + هامون ‘the plain’. بدریم is a classical/poetic present/subjunctive/hortative form meaning ‘we tear/rend’ (prefix ب- + present stem, used as a volitional/future-like form). چرم پلنگ ‘the leopard’s hide’ is the object phrase (چرم = hide/leather, followed by پلنگ naming the animal) in a juxtaposed genitival relationship typical of poetic diction. The clause expresses their intended action on the plain.
segments.s655-g1125: شهنشاه (shāhan-shāh) is the subject ‘the emperor/king of kings.’ پذرفت is the simple past third-person singular ‘accepted / took.’ ز ایشان نثار is the prepositional phrase marking source/object: ز (from) + ایشان (them) + نثار (offering/gift). Word order is subject–verb–(source + object) with ز used poetically for ‘from.’
segments.s655-g1126: برستند is a past-tense third-person plural verb ‘they departed/they went away.’ پاک is an adjective ‘clean/pure’ describing their state. از بد روزگار is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘from bad fortune’ or ‘free of ill fate’ (از = from, بد روزگار = ill/favourless times/fate). The clause states the result: after offering, they left, untainted by bad fortune.
segments.s655-g1127: از ایشان ‘from them/from among them’ introduces source. فغانیش is the noun فغان ‘lament’ with the possessive suffix -یش ‘his/its’ (so ‘his/its lament’); in context it functions as a noun predicatively. بد پیشرو is a compound-like predicate: بد ‘bad’ qualifying پیشرو ‘forerunner/vanguard.’ The copula is omitted (typical of poetic Persian), so the sense is ‘their/his lament was an ill forerunner.’
segments.s655-g1128: سپاهی is a noun meaning ‘an army / soldiers.’ پسش is an adverbial/locative particle ‘after it / behind it’ (پس + ‎-ش). جنگ سازان نو is a noun phrase in which جنگ ‘war’ qualifies سازان ‘makers/creators’ (poetic compound meaning those who make war, i.e. warriors) with نو ‘new’ as a final modifier; Persian poetry often omits an explicit verb, so this verbless line states the sequence: an army followed it — new makers of war.
segments.s655-g1305: Literal word order is inverted for poetic effect: پیاده (on foot) precedes the verb بماند (remained). سوارش is 'his rider' (sوار + enclitic -ش). ز + اسب means 'from the horse' (ز as the poetic preposition 'from'). Together this yields 'his rider remained on foot from the horse' = 'his rider was left on foot (i.e. dismounted/fallen)'.
segments.s655-g1306: به + noun (پوزش) marks purpose/intent ('for/to [ask] pardon'). رود here is the 3rd-person singular classical form of رفتن 'to go' (used as a narrative/present-subjunctive form meaning 'he went/would go'). نزد + name means 'into the presence of / before' someone (نزدِ آذرگشسب = before Azar‑Gashasp).
segments.s655-g1307: عرض here functions as ‘petition / something presented’ (a formal presentation or claim) and بسترد is a classical perfect/past-form of the root ستر (to hide/veil), so عرض بسترد means roughly 'he withdrew/veiled the petition/statement'. نام دیوان اوی = 'the name/reputation of his diwan/court' where اوی is the enclitic 'his'.
segments.s655-g1308: به پای اندر آرند is literal 'they bring to/at the foot' (به = 'to/at', پای = 'foot', اندر = classical 'in/within', آرند = 3rd-person plural classical form of آوردن 'they bring'). ایوان اوی = 'his iwan' (porch/hall + enclitic his). The clause describes people bringing/setting his hall at his feet (i.e. humbling/placing it before him).
segments.s655-g1309: گناهی (a sin) with نباشد (negative of 'to be' in classical form) expresses existential negation 'there is not / shall not be'; کم و بیش = 'less and more' = 'small or great' or 'less or more'; ازین (از + این) means 'than this' or 'beyond this'. The phrase reads as a general negative: 'there is no sin, less or more, than this.'
segments.s655-g1310: ز here functions as a causal/prepositional 'for' or 'because'; پستر is an archaic/adverbial form related to پس meaning 'after/previously/thereupon' and بود is the past copula 'was'. آنک = 'that which / the one that', بد = 'bad/evil', پیش ازین = 'before this / previously'. The clause explains cause/time: 'for that which was bad had been before this.'
segments.s655-g1311: نباشد is the classical negative of 'to be' ('will not be / is not') and governs the following prepositional phrase بران ('بر' + demonstrative 'آن' contracted) meaning 'on/with that' or 'in regard to them'; شاه = 'the king'; همداستان = 'like‑minded / in agreement'. The clause states that the king will not be of the same mind with them.
segments.s655-g1312: نخواهد is the classical future/optative of خواستن 'he will not (wish/do)'; بر here is a prepositional particle 'upon/against' linking to the verb phrase (poetic word order puts it before the verb). جز از = 'except from / only from'; راستان refers to 'the upright / the righteous' (here a group). The clause means 'he will not act except for/with the righteous.'
segments.s655-g657: نباشد is a negative 3rd-person subjunctive/optative (literally 'let there not be' / 'may there not be') placed before its noun phrase in poetic word order. جدا modifies مرز ایران (ʼseparate' / 'apart'), and ز is the poetic preposition 'from' introducing چین ('China'). The whole clause reads verb + modifier + noun phrase: 'May the border of Iran not be separate from China.'
segments.s655-g658: فزاید is here an optative/present form meaning 'may it increase' (3rd-person, used like 'let/may it increase'). ز ما = 'because of/us' (preposition + pronoun). درجهان modifies آفرین ('rank/renown/praise in the world' — treated here as the sphere or degree of acclaim), and آفرین is a noun 'acclaim/praise' (not the verb 'create' nor an interjection). The clause thus expresses a wish: 'May the world's praise increase because of us.'
segments.s655-g659: پس is a connective adverb 'then/thereupon'. اندر is the locative/prepositional 'in/on/within' and precedes the past tense verb نبشتند ('they wrote/inscribed' — 3rd-person plural past). چینی حریر is a noun phrase 'Chinese silk/Chinese cloth' functioning as the object/place of inscription. Poetic order places the verb before some modifiers/objects.
segments.s655-g660: ببردند is past 3rd-person plural 'they carried / they took'. با مهر is an instrumental/attendant phrase 'with a مهر' — here مهر is best read as 'seal/stamp' (i.e. bearing a seal) rather than merely 'love'. پیش وزیر means 'before/in the presence of the vizier' (پیش = 'in front of/before', وزیر = 'vizier'). Together: 'they carried it with a seal to the vizier.'
segments.s655-g661: This is a noun phrase: 'سه مرد گرانمایه وچرب‌گوی' — 'three men' (numeral omitted in the word list) described by two adjectival modifiers. گرانمایه = 'precious/esteemed' and وچرب‌گوی (و‑ + چرب‌گوی) = 'and smooth‑tongued' / 'flattering'—a compound adjective. Adjectives follow the noun in this poetic ordering and jointly modify 'مرد'.
segments.s655-g662: گزین کرد is a light verb construction where گزین (choose) + کرد (did/made) together mean 'chose' (past, 3rd‑person). خاقان is the subject (the khagan); ز introduces the source 'from' and governs خویشان اوی 'his relatives' — اوی is a genitive pronominal 'his'. Word order here places the verb phrase before the subject and its complement, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s655-g663: برفتند is simple past 3rd-person plural 'they departed / they went away'. زان = 'from that' (ز + ان) marks origin ('from that palace'). بارگاه بلند is the place noun phrase 'the lofty/lofty palace/court' (بارگاه = royal court, بلند = high/lofty). The clause literally: 'They departed from that lofty court.'
segments.s655-g664: Two prepositional phrases indicate destination and approach: به ایران 'to/into Iran' (به = 'to/toward'), and به نزدیک شاه ارجمند 'toward/into the presence/near the noble king' where نزدیک functions adverbially/relationally ('near/in the presence of') and ارجمند is an adjective 'esteemed/noble' qualifying شاه. The doubled به is idiomatic in classical diction to mark successive directions/locations.
segments.s655-g1721: بدو is the dative/adverbial 'to him'. گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (the provided senses did not list 'said' so it is noted below). اندر چه چیزست رنج is an interrogative phrase: چه (what) + چیزست (an archaic/copular compound meaning 'is a thing' or 'there is') + رنج (suffering). Word order places the question word and copula before the noun being asked about.
segments.s655-g1722: کزو 'from which' links this clause back to 'رنج' (from that suffering). کم شود is 'becomes less' (کم = less/little, شود = becomes). مرد را marks 'man' with the object/affected marker را (here 'a man's' or 'the man's'), and آز گنج 'greed for treasure' is the thing that 'becomes less.'
segments.s655-g1723: بدو داد پاسخ = 'he gave him an answer' (بدو 'to him', داد 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). که introduces the reported speech ('that/—' often used classically to start the quotation) and ای (vocative) attaches to شهریار: 'O king.'
segments.s655-g1724: همیشه 'always' modifies the optative clause. دلت is دل + -ت (second-person possessive suffix) = 'your heart.' باد here is the optative particle meaning 'may it be' (not 'wind'). چون نوبهار = 'like the new spring.' The whole line is a blessing: 'May your heart always be like the new spring.'
segments.s655-g1725: پرستنده شاه بدخو is a noun phrase 'a worshipper/devotee of the king who is ill-tempered' (پرستنده = worshipper/devotee; شاه = of the king; بدخو = ill-tempered). ز رنج is a prepositional phrase 'from/because of suffering' that sets the cause or condition.
segments.s655-g1726: نخواهد is negative present/potential of خواستن here 'does not want / will not desire'. The following nouns تن و زندگانی و گنج ('body and life and treasure') are the things he will not desire; Persian lists items with و as 'and.' The verb governs the whole coordinated list.
segments.s655-g1727: بپرسید is past 'they asked'. و connects clauses. گفتش is 'he said to him' (گفت + enclitic ش as dative). چه دیدی شگفت = 'what wondrous thing did you see' (چه = what, دیدی = you saw, شگفت = marvelous/wondrous). The interrogative targets 'شگفت.'
segments.s655-g1728: کزان 'from/of which' refers back to that wondrous thing. برتر اندازه 'a measure higher/superior measure' — برتر modifies the notion of measure, and نتوان گرفت is a negative potential construction 'cannot be taken' (نتوان = cannot, گرفت = taken). Together: 'of which no higher measure can be taken' i.e. 'whose excellence cannot be measured above.'
segments.s655-g1545: و دیگر که introduces a new point ('and again/another thing, that...'). گیتی فسانه‌ست is a simple nominal clause: فسانه‌ست = فسانه + -ست (poetic/older form of است) ‘is a fable’. The second final noun باد is parallel to گیتی فسانه‌ست with the copula omitted (elliptical 'is' understood), so باد functions metaphorically as a predicate noun 'is (like) wind'.
segments.s655-g1546: چو here = 'like/as'. خوابی is a noun 'a sleep/dream' (here 'dream'), and the relative clause که بیننده دارد به یاد modifies it: بیننده 'the beholder/dreamer', دارد = present 'has/keeps', and به یاد 'in memory' (prepositional phrase). The whole segment reads 'like a dream that the beholder has in memory.'
segments.s655-g1547: چو ... گردد = temporal clause 'when he becomes/when he wakes' (چو = 'when', گردد = third-person singular verb 'becomes/turns'). نبیند is the negative present/gnomic form 'does not see' (ن- prefix + بیند), and به چشم is a prepositional phrase 'with (the) eye(s)' placed after the verb.
segments.s655-g1548: The repeated اگر ... اگر functions like 'whether ... or ...' rather than a real conditional. دید here is a past/plain form 'saw/has seen' which governs both alternatives (نیکویی and درد و خشم), so the sense is 'whether he had seen goodness or (had seen) pain and anger.'
segments.s655-g1549: دگر پرسشی = 'another/question more' (دگر = 'another'), برگشاد is a past/participial verbal form 'opened/was opened' meaning 'arose', and از نهفت 'from the hidden' is a prepositional phrase indicating source. Word order is typical classical Persian: modifier + noun + verbal predicate + complement.
segments.s655-g1550: بدانا = 'to the wise' (addressing the knowledgeable). ستوده is a predicate adjective 'praised' and کدامست = 'which is it?' is the interrogative copular phrase; گفت at the end marks speech ('he said'). The clause order puts the address before the question and then closes with گفت.
segments.s655-g1551: چنین داد پاسخ = 'thus gave (an) answer' (چنین 'thus', داد 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). The conjunction/relativizer که introduces the content clause: شاهی که تخت ... 'a kingship/king who (the) throne ...' — the relative clause is split, with تخت (throne) beginning the following clause.
segments.s655-g1552: تخت بیاراید = 'sets up/establishes a throne' (بیاراید = present/optative-form verb 'brings/adorns' applied to تخت from the previous segment). و زور یابد = 'and obtains/gains strength/power' (یابد 'attains/finds'), and ز بخت = 'by/from fortune' (ز = poetic 'from', بخت = 'fortune/destiny'). The verbs are third-person singular and describe qualities by which a king attains rule.
segments.s655-g649: چنان (an adverb) modifies آرزو and here means 'such' or 'so great'; آرزو is the noun 'desire/wish' and خاست is an older past tense verb meaning 'arose' (classical Persian past of خاستن). کز is the archaic/poetic preposition 'from/by' or 'that' linking this clause to the following purpose clause; فر تو (lit. 'your far/farr') means 'your royal glory' and is the object of کز.
segments.s655-g650: بباشیم is a first-person plural optative/subjunctive ('may we be' / 'let us be'); در سایه is the prepositional phrase 'in the shade/shelter'; پر تو is a possessive construction where پر means 'feather/wing' (poetic for 'wing') and تو is 'your', so the whole gives the image 'in the shelter/shade of your wing.'
segments.s655-g651: گرامی‌تر is the comparative adjective 'more dear / dearer'; از introduces the standard of comparison 'than'; خون دل is a compound 'blood of the heart' (a poetic phrase); چیز نیست is an existential negative 'there is nothing' (نیست negates the preceding noun phrase).
segments.s655-g652: هنرمند فرزند is a noun phrase 'a worthy/skillful child' (هنرمند here describes the child's excellence); با دل یکیست is a copular clause where با دل 'with the heart' means 'in accord with the heart' and یکیست ('is one') states identity or unity — 'is one with the heart.'
segments.s655-g653: یکی functions as an indefinite pronoun 'one' or 'someone'; پاک‌دامن is an adjective-noun 'chaste/innocent'; که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; آهسته‌تر is the comparative adverb 'more softly / more gently' modifying an implied verb or the following qualities.
segments.s655-g654: فزون‌تر is a comparative 'more' (literally 'increased/more abundant') and here functions to compare the following qualities; بدیدار is a noun/infinitival form 'for meeting / for seeing' (i.e., 'more apt to be met/seen' or 'readier for meeting'); و links the final comparative شایسته‌تر 'more fitting / more deserving.' The phrase lists comparative qualities of that one.
segments.s655-g655: بخواهد is a present-future/optative form meaning 'he wishes/wants' or 'if he desires'; the short preposition ز ('from') combines with the enclitic/archaic conditional من‌گر (literally 'if I' but here functioning in an older idiom meaning 'if he asks of me / if I am consulted'); پسند is the noun 'pleasure/approval' and آیدش is a verb phrase 'it comes to him' (آید + third-person enclitic ش = 'it will come to him'). Together the clause says that if he desires it from me and it pleases him, it will come to him.
segments.s655-g656: همانا is an emphatic particle 'verily/indeed'; که introduces the content clause 'that...'; این is the demonstrative 'this'; سودمند is an adjective 'useful/beneficial'; آیدش again is 'it will come to him' (predictive/resultative statement). The whole affirms that the thing just mentioned will prove beneficial to him.
segments.s655-g969: به here is a prepositional marker meaning “in/with regard to” and begins a list of qualities; Persian often repeats به before parallel nouns for emphasis. مردی is used as an abstract quality ‘manliness’ or ‘bravery’ (a nominalized adjective). پیروزی and دستگاه are coordinated by و; دستگاه in this courtly context means the courtly establishment/regalia rather than a mechanical system.
segments.s655-g970: The second recurring به continues the same parallel list. فر is the classical word for royal glory/splendour. بنیرو is a compound/adverbial meaning ‘with might/strength.’ تخت and کلاه are further items of regal insignia (throne and crown/hat) joined by و.
segments.s655-g971: Another pair of parallel prepositional phrases introduced by به: ‘with generosity and learning’ (رادی used here as the quality/virtue ‘generosity’), and then ‘with counsel (رای) and wisdom (خرد).’ Note وخرد is the older orthography where the conjunction و is attached to خرد; the whole phrase lists the means or virtues by which something is done.
segments.s655-g972: ورا is an oblique pronoun here meaning ‘in him’ / ‘in it’ (i.e. within that person or context). دین یزدان is a genitive phrase ‘the religion of God.’ همی‌پرورد is the classical present/continuous form of پروردن (‘to nurture, to foster’) with the habitual prefix همی‑; the verb takes ورا as its indirect object.
segments.s655-g973: فرستادم is first-person past ‘I sent’ (the narrator as subject). اینک = ‘now’; جهان‌بین is an adjectival compound ‘world-seeing’ used as a noun meaning ‘worldly-wise person’ or ‘worldly-wise envoy.’ خویش is the reflexive/possessive clitic attached to the noun (here: ‘my’ relative to the speaker), written in the text as خوی + ش.
segments.s655-g974: سوی is a directional preposition ‘toward’ (introducing the addressee). شاه کسری names the addressee ‘King Khosrow’ (کسری = Khosrow). به آیین خویش is a prepositional phrase of manner ‘in his/customary manner’; خویش is the reflexive possessive clitic. Grammatically خویش can refer to the nearest relevant person (here most naturally the king as the recipient) but can be ambiguous in Persian between the subject and the nearest animate noun.
segments.s655-g975: بفرموده‌ام is a classical perfect/past form ‘I have commanded/ordered’ (the prefix ب‑ marks the verbal noun/participial sense). تا introduces purpose/result here (‘so that’ / ‘in order that’). بود is the copula ‘be’ used in this purpose clause (classical style rather than a modern subjunctive marker). بنده‌وار is formed from بنده + وار and means ‘in the manner of a servant’ — i.e. humbly or servilely; it functions adverbially/adjectivally.
segments.s655-g976: چوشاید is the archaic fused form چو + شاید meaning ‘so that he may / as may be / if possible’ (expressing potential/result). پس پرده is a prepositional-like sequence ‘behind the curtain’ (پس here conveys the sense ‘behind/after’ with پرده ‘curtain/veil’), and شهریار is ‘the sovereign / king.’ Together the phrase expresses the intended outcome: that he should be placed or permitted behind the king’s curtain.
segments.s655-g1433: دگر (another) + دیو (demon) is the subject phrase. کینست is the fused copula construction کین + است ('is [kīn]') meaning 'is vengeance/hatred'; پرخشم وجوش are predicates describing that demon ('full of wrath and ardor'), where پر- is a prefix 'full of' and وجوش is a poetic noun 'boiling/zeal'.
segments.s655-g1434: ز مردم is the affected-source phrase 'upon/from the people'; بتابد is a present poetic verb (تابد ← تافت/تابیدن) 'shines/falls upon' used metaphorically; گه is an adverb 'sometimes'; خشم هوش is a compact expression where خشم (anger) acts on هوش (the mind/reason) — 'his anger sometimes overwhelms/dazes the mind'.
segments.s655-g1435: نه ... نه sets up a two-part negation: بخشایش آرد بروبر — 'he brings forgiveness directly/face-to-face' (آرد = 'brings'); بروبر is an adverb 'face-to-face/directly'; مهر is the second object 'affection/kindness/favour' and is negated in parallel by نه مهر.
segments.s655-g1436: دژآگاه (fortress-warden) + دیو (demon) is the nominal subject; the copula ی (archaic 'is') links it to the predicate پرآژنگ چهر, where پر- 'full of' + آژنگ (poetic adjective) modifies چهر 'face' — 'the fortress-warden demon is of a (dreadful/clamorous/ornamented) face' (used here as 'terrible/striking face').
segments.s655-g1437: دگر دیو نمام is the noun phrase 'another demon — a tattletale'. کو here is the archaic/poetic relative 'who' (linking to the following verb); جز دروغ 'except lies' restricts what he knows/has: 'who knows nothing except falsehoods.'
segments.s655-g1438: نداند is the negative form 'does not/knows not' (ن + دارد-from دانستن) and is followed in parallel by نراند (ن + راند) — 'nor does he drive forth/utter' — both verbs are negative and coordinate. سخن با فروغ is the object phrase 'speech with radiance' (i.e., bright/true speech), so the clause means he knows nothing and utters no radiant/truthful speech.
segments.s655-g1439: بماند is the narrative present 'remains / is left'. سخن‌چین ودوروی دیو is a coordinated noun phrase 'the tattletale and the two‑faced demon' (و linking the two epithets) serving as the subject that 'remains'.
segments.s655-g1440: بریده دل is an idiomatic compound 'with heart severed' (i.e., heedless or fearless); از بیم marks separation/source 'from fear of ...'; کیهان خدیو is a possessive/title phrase 'the sovereign (khēdiv) of the cosmos/world' — together: 'having his heart cut off from fear of the world's ruler' meaning he fears neither God nor king.
segments.s655-g1489: This is a compact, poetic clause. وزین functions as a fused prepositional phrase (و + زِ این) meaning 'from these' in classical usage; گوهران is the noun 'gems' in the plural and is the object of that preposition. آز is a noun 'greed' and دیدم is the speaker's past-tense verb 'I saw' (word order is poetic: prepositional phrase + object + verb). به رنج is an adverbial phrase 'with suffering/with pain' where به is used adverbially ('by/with') and رنج names the manner.
segments.s655-g1490: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'that/which'. همواره is an adverb 'always'; سیری is the abstract noun 'satiety/satisfaction'; نیابد is a non‑past/subjunctive form of یابیدن meaning 'does not attain/find' (negated with the initial ن-); ز is the poetic preposition 'from' and گنج is 'treasure'. Together: 'that always does not attain satiety from treasure.'
segments.s655-g1491: بدو گفت شاه is a common poetic inversion: بدو ('to him') + گفت ('said') + شاه ('the king') yields 'the king said to him'. از هنرها 'of the arts/virtues' uses از as a marker 'concerning' and هنرها is the plural 'virtues/skills'. چه به here asks 'which (one) is fitting/appropriate' — literally 'what to/for'.
segments.s655-g1492: که begins a subordinate clause 'that/so that'. گردد is a present/subjunctive form of گشتن meaning 'become' or 'come to be'. بدو again is 'to him'; مرد is 'man'; جوینده is a deverbal noun 'seeker'; مه here is literal 'moon' (used metaphorically). The clause means 'that a man becomes to him a seeker of the moon.'
segments.s655-g1493: چنین داد پاسخ is an idiomatic formula: چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' + پاسخ 'answer' = 'he gave this answer' (past). که introduces the content of the answer. هر کو ز راه is a condensed classical expression meaning 'whoever (هر کو) from the path' — here ز راه is 'from the way/path' with ز indicating source/departure.
segments.s655-g1494: نگردد is the negative present/subjunctive form of گشتن used here to mean 'does not turn/deviate'. بود is the copula (past/optative sense here, rendered as 'will be/is'). با تنی is a prepositional phrase 'with a body' (تنی = bodily / 'a body'), and بیگناه is the adjective 'innocent' or 'unharmed': 'will be with an uninjured/innocent body.'
segments.s655-g1495: بیابد is a present/subjunctive form of یافتن/یابیدن meaning 'he will attain/obtain'. ز گیتی = 'from the world' (ز = from, گیتی = the world). همه کام و نام lists the rewards: همه 'all/every', کام 'desire/wish', و نام 'and name' (i.e., renown).
segments.s655-g1496: از introduces another catalogue of gains: انجام 'completion/result', فرجام 'end/outcome', و آرام 'and calm/peace', و کام 'and fulfilment/satisfaction'. Each noun is linked by و; the repetition of کام emphasizes both wish and eventual satisfaction.
segments.s655-g665: چو at the start functions as a temporal 'when'. The clause that follows uses verb-before-subject order typical of poetry: بشنید (past) 'he heard' is placed before the subject phrase کسری; then بیاراست (past) is a transitive verb 'adorned/arranged' taking تاج as its object. In short: 'When he heard, Khosrow adorned the crown.'
segments.s655-g666: نشست is past tense 'sat' (3rd person). The subject (Khosrow) is carried over from the previous clause and is not repeated. از بر is an archaic/poetic prepositional sequence used here to indicate position 'upon/on' (the two prepositions combine); خسروی modifies تخت (an adjectival/nominative phrase 'the royal throne'), and عاج specifies the material 'ivory'. Word order places the modifiers after the verb, as is common in classical Persian poetry.
segments.s655-g667: سه + مرد is a numeral + noun phrase 'three men'. The adjectives گرانمایه و هوشمند follow the noun and are coordinated by و, a normal post-nominal adjectival order in Persian poetry: 'men who were precious/esteemed and intelligent.'
segments.s655-g668: رسیدند is past tense, 3rd-person plural 'they arrived' agreeing with 'three men'. نزدیک is an adverb/prepositional modifier 'near' placed before the place phrase تخت بلند ('the high/tall throne'), where بلند is the adjective modifying تخت.
segments.s655-g669: This noun phrase gives the amount: سه (three) modifies بدره, a poetic/archaic term whose specific meaning in this context is uncertain (a container/measure or literary term for a large amount) and therefore treated as a lexical item here; ز دینار means 'of dinars' (material/contents), and چون سی هزار is a simile/approximation 'like/as thirty thousand' with چون functioning as 'as/about' before the numeral.
segments.s655-g670: Two past verbs are coordinated: ببردند (they carried/took) followed by و کرد‌ند (and they made/did) — here کردن takes نثار as its object to mean 'made/offered a gift'. پیشش is a locative/pronominal adverb 'before him / in his presence' attached to نثار. The sequence describes bringing and offering gifts before the king.
segments.s655-g671: ز is the preposition 'from' enumerating materials. زرین and سیمین are adjectival forms meaning 'golden' and 'silvery' and are coordinated with و; دیبای چین is a compound 'Chinese brocade/silk' where چین specifies origin ('China') and follows the cloth noun dība (typical poetic order). The whole phrase lists the luxurious materials of the gifts.
segments.s655-g672: درفشان‌تر is the comparative form 'more shining/more glittering' (درفشان + comparative -تر). It takes a comparison phrase از آسمان 'than the sky' (written here as ازآسمان) and a locative complement بر زمین 'on the earth', giving the sense 'more glittering on earth than the sky.' The concatenation ازآسمان is orthographic; grammatically it is the standard comparative construction.
segments.s655-g1217: وزان = a fused poetic particle meaning 'from that' or 'and from that' pointing back to the prior scene; تخت شاه = 'the king's throne' (takh(t) + title); اندر آمد + به اسب = a verbal phrase 'came in/onto' where اندر with آمد here means 'alighted/moved from that place' and the following به اسب expresses the goal/vehicle ('onto the horse' / 'mounted the horse'). Word order is poetic but equivalent to 'the king came from the throne onto the horse.'
segments.s655-g1218: همی‌راند is a past continuous/progressive form (poetic imperfect) meaning 'he rode' or 'was riding'; تا here functions as a directional/limit marker 'to/until' introducing the destination; خان is an honorific 'lord/chief' and آذرگشسب is the personal name. Together: 'he rode up to (reaching) Khan Azar‑Gashasp.'
segments.s655-g1219: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'; از دور is an adverbial phrase 'from afar' (literally 'out of distance'); جای پرستش is a genitival noun phrase 'the place of worship'; بدید is past tense 'saw' (poetic form be‑ + دید). The clause means 'when he saw the place of worship from afar.'
segments.s655-g1220: شد is the resultive auxiliary 'became' or 'was'; از introduces the domain 'from' as in 'vanished from (his sight)'; آب‌دیده here is used descriptively of the eyes—literally 'water‑seen'—i.e. 'with eyes full of water/tearful' (poetic usage), modifying رخش 'his steed'; ناپدید means 'unseen/vanished'. The construction 'شد از X Y ناپدید' = 'Y became unseen from X (i.e. to his X).'
segments.s655-g1221: فرود آمد is a verbal compound meaning 'he alighted / dismounted'; از اسب = 'from the horse'; برسم is derived from رسیدن 'to reach/attain' and here combines with بدست 'with the hand' to mean 'reached (out) with his hand' — the poetic order places the verb/phrase before the goal which follows in the next segment.
segments.s655-g1222: به introduces the indirect object/direction 'to/toward'; زمزم is the proper name 'Zamzam' (the sacred well or its water); همی‌گفت is the past progressive 'he kept saying' or 'he was saying' (here: praying/speaking prayers); ولب را ببست literally 'and he shut his lip(s)' — لب را ببست = 'he closed his lips' (the object marker را marks 'lip(s)' as definite). The sequence indicates he prayed at the Zamzam and then sealed his lips.
segments.s655-g1223: همان پیش = 'there/at that very place, before' (همان = 'that/same', پیش = 'before/in the presence of'); آتش is 'the fire' (likely the sacred fire); ستایش گرفت is an idiomatic compound where گرفت ('took') means 'took up' or 'began' — so 'he began to praise' or 'he offered praise' in front of the fire.
segments.s655-g1224: جهان‌آفرین 'the world‑creator / Creator' is marked by را (the definite object marker) and followed by نیایش گرفت, another idiomatic use of گرفت meaning 'took to prayer' or 'began to pray' — so the whole phrase means 'he offered prayer/supplication to the Creator.' Word order places the object (جهان‌آفرین را) before the verbal phrase.
segments.s655-g809: جهانجوی is the subject (a proper name/epithet, 'the world‑seeker'). چون introduces a temporal clause here meaning 'when'; دید is the simple past 'saw'; بنواختش is past tense 'he struck' with the enclitic object ‑ش ('him/it'). The sequence Subject + temporal conjunction + past verb + verb+object enclitic is typical of classical Persian narration.
segments.s655-g810: یکی is an indefinite determiner 'a certain'/'one'; نامور qualifies جایگه and means 'renowned'; جایگه (compound noun) = 'place/position/post' (often a figurative rank); ساختش is past 'he made/built' with the enclitic ‑ش referring back to the person/object. Word order is adjective + noun + verb with the object pronoun suffixed to the verb, so 'he made him a renowned place/post.'
segments.s655-g811: ازان = 'from that/thereupon' (ablative link to the preceding event); کارخاقان is a tight compound 'the khaghan's affair/royal business' (کار + خاقان); پراندیشه is a predicative adjective 'full of thought/pensive'; گشت is the past of گشتن used as 'became/turned' (so together 'became pensive').
segments.s655-g812: به سوی is a two‑word prepositional phrase meaning 'toward/to'; شبستان خاتون = 'the lady's inner chamber/bedchamber' (خاتون governing شبستان in genitive relation, the ezāfe is implicit/classical); گذشت is the past of گذشتن 'he passed/moved', here meaning 'he went toward/entered.'
segments.s655-g813: سخنهای (literally 'the words/speeches') is the subject; نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled/sweet‑voiced' modifying سخنها; برگشاد is a past verb 'opened' or 'began/was spoken' (poetic usage where 'برگشاد' = 'were opened/uttered'). The noun phrase precedes the verb in this poetic ordering.
segments.s655-g814: ز introduces the source 'from/of' ('from the treasure'); گنج = 'treasure/wealth'; وز here is a compressed poetic form ('و ز' = 'and from') linking a second source — this compression is common in classical diction; لشکر = 'army/host'; بسی is an adverbial intensifier 'much/very' (Beyān 'greatly'); کرد یاد is a light‑verb construction where کرد ('did/made') combines with یاد ('remembrance') = 'recalled/remembered strongly.'
segments.s655-g815: بدو is the fused prepositional phrase 'to him/to her' (به + او) meaning 'to him/her'; گفت is the past 'said' (here introducing direct speech); کین is an archaic/dialectal demonstrative 'this' (not the sense 'vengeance' — classical poetry often uses کین = 'this'); شاه is 'king'; نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled' in apposition to شاه. The clause is the standard 'to‑him said: "This king ..."' construction.
segments.s655-g816: جوانست is copula+predicate 'is young' (جوان + است contracted to ست); و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; بیدار is an adjective 'awake/alert'; و again 'and'; دولت جوان is a nominal phrase 'fortune/prosperity youthful' — i.e., 'fortunate in youth' or 'youthful fortune.' The sentence lists three predicates about the king in a classical, tersely coordinated style.
segments.s655-g1265: This is a compact classical-Persian word order where the object/goal (جهانی, 'the world') and destination (به ایران, 'toward Iran') precede the verb نهادند (past, 3rd plural of نهادن, 'they set/placed/turned'). روی ('face') functions as the thing being oriented — 'set the face' = 'made (something) face toward'.
segments.s655-g1266: آسوده is an adjectival predicate 'at ease/relieved' introduced by the preposition-like particle بر here ('with/upon'); از رنج marks what they are free from. گفت وگوی is the compound گفت‌وگو ('conversation, dispute'); the line uses an old poetic grouping (split across words) meaning 'and from the talk/dispute'.
segments.s655-g1267: گلابست is an adjectival/predicative form 'rose-scented / rosy'. گویی here is the modal particle 'as if / it seems that' (an archaic/poetic use) introducing the simile; هوا را is the definite object/topic marked with را, and سرشک ('teardrop; small bead') completes the image. Note: گویی in the data is homographic with other lemmas; here it functions as a simile particle.
segments.s655-g1268: This is an inverted nominal clause: بر آسوده (literally 'upon eased') expresses the state 'were at ease/relieved', followed by از رنج 'from suffering'. مرد و پزشک ('man and physician') are the coordinated subjects/people affected. The verb 'to be' is understood (nominal sentence), a common poetic feature.
segments.s655-g1269: ببارید is the past, 3rd‑person singular (it rained/fell). برگل is a locative/covering phrase 'upon the flowers', and به هنگام نم 'at the time/hour of the dew' gives the temporal setting. Classical Persian freely orders these adverbials before/after the verb.
segments.s655-g1270: نبد is an archaic/poetic negative/existential past form meaning 'was not / did not (happen)' (here used as 'did not (be harmful)'). کشتورزی is the noun 'cultivation/agriculture'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of'; باران دژم = 'grim/sullen rain' (دژم modifies باران). Put together it says roughly that cultivation did not suffer because of the harsh rain.
segments.s655-g1271: گشت is the past of گشتن 'became/turned', so جهان گشت = 'the world became'. پرسبزه ('covered with grass/verdant') and وچارپای ('and quadrupeds/four‑footed creatures') are predicate complements describing the new state of the world.
segments.s655-g1272: This is a poetic listing: در can mean 'door/house' here (homes), و دشت 'and the plains'; گل بود indicates 'were flowers' (گل as predicate, بود as the past copula); و بام سرای links 'and roofs (بام) (were) abodes/halls (سرای)'. Note that some classical nouns (سرای = abode) appear with senses different from the provided lemma senses.
segments.s655-g1353: بدین کار چون بگذرد روزگار is a compact clause meaning 'when time passes over this deed'. بدین کار (ب + دین + کار) is a prepositional phrase 'by/for this deed' placed at the front for emphasis. چون introduces a temporal subordinate clause and governs the verb بگذرد (present-subjunctive/poetic form 'passes'); روزگار is the noun that acts as the experiential subject 'time'.
segments.s655-g1354: ازو نام نیکی بود یادگار is the main predication: ازو 'of/from him' marks source, نام نیکی is the noun phrase 'good name' (نام modified by نیکی), and بود is the past copula 'was' linking the subject to یادگار 'a memento/memorial'. The order is source → subject → copula → predicate noun.
segments.s655-g1355: ز گیتی دوچیزیست جاوید بس is a terse, poetic statement. ز گیتی 'from/of the world' is a genitive-like prepositional phrase; دوچیزیست is a contracted classical form (دو چیز است) meaning 'there are two things'; جاوید is the predicate adjective 'eternal'; بس is a final particle meaning 'enough' or 'that is sufficient' in this context.
segments.s655-g1356: دگر هرچ‌باشد نماند به کس: دگر here means 'the other/the rest'; هرچ‌باشد is an indefinite clause 'whatever it may be'; نماند is the negative present/past-stem with negative prefix ن- meaning 'does not remain' or 'remains not'; به کس is a dative/beneficiary phrase 'to/for anyone'. The clause says that anything else does not remain for anyone.
segments.s655-g1357: سخن گفتن نغز و کردار نیک is a coordinated noun phrase: سخن گفتن is a verbal-noun (the act of speaking), modified by نغز 'delightful/pithy', and joined by و to کردار نیک 'virtuous deed'. The order is noun/gerund + modifier, with the conjunction linking two enduring things.
segments.s655-g1358: نگردد کهن تا جهانست ریک: نگردد is the verb (ن- negative prefix + گردد/becomes) meaning 'does not become' or 'does not grow', so نگردد کهن = 'does not grow old'. تا introduces a temporal clause 'while/as long as'; جهانست is an archaic fused form (جهان + است) 'the world is', and ریک is an old/poetic adjective/adverb whose precise nuance is archaic here—together meaning 'while the world endures.'
segments.s655-g1359: بدین سان بود گردش روزگار is a formulaic clause 'thus/ in this manner was the turning (course) of time'. بدین سان is a fixed adverbial phrase 'in this way'; بود is the past copula 'was'; گردش روزگار is the noun phrase 'the rotation/turning of time' serving as the subject or topic of the clause.
segments.s655-g1360: خنک مرد با شرم و پرهیزگار describes the person: خنک مرد is an epithet 'a calm/pleasant man' (خنک used adjectivally), followed by a prepositional phrase با + qualities 'with modesty and piety' (شرم = modesty, پرهیزگار = pious/self‑restrained). Word order is noun phrase then characteristic phrase introduced by با.
segments.s655-g1193: This is a relative clause: 'زمینی که آباد هرگز نبود' = 'a land that was never prosperous/inhabited.' Word order places the head noun 'زمینی' ('a land') first, followed by the relative 'که' introducing the clause; 'آباد' is an adjective meaning 'prosperous/inhabited' and 'هرگز نبود' is a past negation ('was never').
segments.s655-g1194: 'بروبر ندیدند کشت و درود' is a main clause with an adverbial 'بروبر' (literary 'face to face' / 'directly') modifying the verb. 'ندیدند' is past plural 'they did not see'; the direct objects follow: 'کشت و درود' ('cultivation and [harvest/produce]'). The verb agrees with a plural subject understood from the previous narrative. Note: 'بروبر' is an emphatic literary adverb and here intensifies the negative seeing.
segments.s655-g1195: 'نگه کرد کسری برومند یافت' contains two past actions in compact epic style. 'نگه کرد' is a periphrastic past 'he looked/gazed.' The following clipped clause 'کسری برومند یافت' uses 'یافت' (past 'found/seen') with 'کسری' as the person seen and 'برومند' as his epithet ('the noble/illustrious Kisra'); the subject of 'یافت' is left implicit (the same observer from 'نگه کرد').
segments.s655-g1196: 'بهرخانه‌ای چند فرزند یافت' is an adverbial + object + verb pattern: 'بهرخانه‌ای' ('for each house' / 'in every household') sets the scope, 'چند فرزند' = 'several children' is the object, and 'یافت' is the past verb 'found.' Grammatically the verb shares its implicit subject with the previous clause (elliptical subject continuity).
segments.s655-g1197: 'خمیده سر از بار شاخ درخت' is a descriptive phrase: 'خمیده سر' is an adjective + noun ('head bent' / 'with head bowed'), and 'از بار شاخ درخت' is a causal/ablative phrase introduced by 'از' meaning 'from/because of the burden of the branch of the tree' (i.e. 'under the weight of the bough'). The modifiers follow the noun in typical Persian poetic ordering.
segments.s655-g1198: 'به فر جهاندار بیداربخت' is another adverbial phrase giving the direction/manner: 'به فر' literally 'before/under the splendour (glory),' 'جهاندار' is an epithet meaning 'world-ruler/sovereign,' and 'بیداربخت' is an adjective 'ill-fated/unfortunate' modifying that sovereign. Together they indicate that the bowed posture is in relation to the ruler's glory.
segments.s655-g1199: 'به منزل رسیدند نزدیک شاه' places the goal before the verb: 'به منزل رسیدند' = 'they arrived at the lodging,' where 'رسیدند' is past plural 'they arrived.' 'نزدیک شاه' ('near the king') is a locative phrase placed after the verb to specify proximity to the king.
segments.s655-g1200: 'فرستاده قیصر آمد به راه' is a simple clause: 'فرستاده قیصر' = 'the envoy of Caesar' (genitive relation), 'آمد' is past singular 'came,' and 'به راه' is an adverbial phrase 'on the road / along the way.' Word order is noun-phrase + verb + locative phrase.
segments.s655-g641: که introduces a subordinate (relative/explanatory) clause 'that/whose'. 'مردم به مردم' literally 'people by people' = 'people esteemed by other people' where به here functions as an agentive/ablative 'by'. بوند is an archaic/poetic past plural form of بودن ('they were'). ارجمند is the predicate adjective 'esteemed/valued'.
segments.s655-g642: اگر چند here is a concessive phrase 'if though / although'; باشد is the subjunctive/present-form of بودن 'may be / be' used here concessively ('though they be'). The adjectives بزرگ و بلند follow and qualify the (implicit) people; note poetic word-order and the idiomatic/archaic feel of 'اگر چند'.
segments.s655-g643: A noun phrase: فرستادگان (plural 'envoys'/'messengers') modified by the adjective خردمند 'wise'. من at the end is the possessive 'my' (poetic placement without an explicit ezāfe), so 'my wise envoys'.
segments.s655-g644: که begins a relative clause 'who'. بودند is past plural 'they were'. نزدیک پیوند من is a prepositional/relational phrase 'near (in) my bond' meaning 'of close kin / near relation to me' (پیوند = bond/kinship; من = my).
segments.s655-g645: چون is the temporal conjunction 'when/as'. ازان بارگه = 'from that court'. بدین بارگاه (written here with b + دین + بارگاه across tokens) means 'to this palace' — ب is the prefix 'to/into' and بارگاه is 'palace/court'; spacing is poetic. Note that دین here functions as part of the demonstrative construction 'بدین' ('to this'), not its usual lexical meaning.
segments.s655-g646: رسیدند is past plural 'they arrived/reached'. و گفتند 'and they said'. چندی ز شاه = 'a little/some words concerning the king' (چندی = some/a few; ز = about/from; شاه = king). The clause reports their arrival and that they spoke of the king.
segments.s655-g647: ز is the poetic preposition 'of/from' used to introduce items being spoken of. داد 'justice' (not the verb 'gave' here) and وخردمندی 'and wisdom' are enumerated qualities; and بخت اوی = 'his fortune' where اوی is the enclitic possessive 'his'. The nouns are listed in parallel without ezāfe, a common poetic device.
segments.s655-g648: Another enumerative phrase introduced by ز 'of': تاج (crown), سرافرازی (honor/glory) and تخت اوی (his throne). Note سرافرازی here means 'glory/honour' and اوی again marks possession 'his'. The parallelism links these items as further things spoken of.
segments.s655-g1057: پراگنده شد is a past passive/perfective phrase 'became scattered' (پراگنده + شد). The patient/subject 'ترک سیصد هزار' (Turks, three hundred thousand) follows the verb in this poetic/VSO order: verb phrase then the noun phrase identifying who was scattered.
segments.s655-g1058: به جایی نبد کوشش کارزار is an existential negative statement: به جایی 'in/at any place', نبد is an older negative past of 'بود' meaning 'was not / did not exist', and کوشش کارزار is a genitival/compound 'effort/attempt of battle' = 'attempt at fighting'. Together: 'there was no attempt at battle anywhere.'
segments.s655-g1059: کمانی نبایست کردن به زه is an impersonal/light-verb construction: کمانی (arched/of-the-bow) + کردن (light verb 'to do') names the action 'to bend/draw the bow'; نبایست is the modal past 'it was not necessary / one ought not to', and به زه ('to/onto the string') marks the target/instrument. So: 'it was not necessary to (use) the bow on the string.'
segments.s655-g1060: نه که بد از ایدر نه چینی نه مه: نه که introduces a clarification 'not that ...', که here = 'that'. بد از ایدر = 'bad/ill from here' (بد 'bad' + از 'from' + ایدر 'here'). The second half repeats the negation: 'neither Chinese nor (the) moon'—an enumerative negative (نه ... نه ...). The clause explains why the bow was unnecessary.
segments.s655-g1061: بدین سان (in this way) is an adverbial phrase; here it is split as the prefix/parts 'ب' + 'دین' + 'سان' in the markup, but together mean 'thus/in this manner'. بود is the past copula 'was', and 'فر و برز کیان' is the coordinated noun phrase 'the kings' glory (فر) and exaltedness/high rank (برز)'. The clause states that the kings' splendour and highness 'were thus'.
segments.s655-g1062: به نخچیر آهنگ شیر ژیان: به نخچیر = 'toward/for the spoil/prey' (purpose/goal), آهنگ here means 'intention/aim', and شیر ژیان is a possessive/attributive phrase 'the lion's way/ life' (i.e. lion-like manner). Together: 'with the aim of taking spoil, like a lion.' Word order puts the aim and its likeness after the noun.
segments.s655-g1063: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'whose/that'. 'نام وی و اختر' is a coordinated subject 'his name and (his) star', شاه is the predicative noun 'king', and بود is the past copula 'was'. The structure says literally 'that his name and star were (as) king.'
segments.s655-g1064: که هم تخت و هم بخت همراه بود continues the clause: هم ... هم ... is the correlative 'both ... and ...', تخت 'throne' and بخت 'fortune/fate' are the coordinated nouns, همراه بود = 'were with (him)' (همراه as 'together/attendant' + بود 'was'). The whole means 'both throne and luck accompanied him.'
segments.s655-g1457: This clause literally orders the phrase 'گذشته سخن' (the speech/words of the past) before the verb phrase 'یاد دارد' and places the subject 'خرد' after the verb — a common poetic inversion. 'یاد دارد' is present-tense 'has/keeps (in) memory'; together the clause means 'wisdom holds the memory of past words.'
segments.s655-g1458: 'به دانش' is an instrumental/adverbial phrase 'by/with knowledge', where 'به' here means 'by' or 'through'. 'روان را' is the definite object with the object marker 'را' ('the soul/spirit'), and 'همی‌پرورد' is an archaic-present form 'nurtures/fosters' (the prefix همی- marks ongoing/present action in classical Persian). The order is instrument + object + verb: 'nurtures the spirit by knowledge.'
segments.s655-g1459: The conditional particle 'وگر' = 'if'. 'خود' ('self') is the subject, and 'بود' is the past form 'were/was' (here conditional/subjunctive sense). 'آنک خوانیم خیم' is a relative clause 'that which we call khīm' (آنک = 'that/which'; خوانیم = 'we call' in classical usage). Poetic inversion places the relative phrase immediately after 'بود.'
segments.s655-g1460: The relative particle 'که' introduces a clause explaining the consequence. 'با او' = 'with him/it' (prepositional phrase), then the verb 'ندارد' ('does not have') precedes the noun 'دل' (poetic inversion again). 'از دیو بیم' is a genitival phrase 'fear of the demon' (از = 'of'/'from' here). Together: 'so that with him the heart has no fear of the demon.'
segments.s655-g1461: This clause states a consequence in the same conditional frame: 'جهان خوش بود' = 'the world would be/was pleasant' (بود here carries past/conditional sense), and 'بردل نیک‌خوی' is a locative/possessive phrase 'in the heart of the virtuous/kind‑natured.' Word order places the locative phrase after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s655-g1462: The verb 'نگردد' is negative jussive/optative ('would not turn/would not roam') and is followed by 'بگرد' which here functions as a present/stative infinitival/verb-stem meaning 'to go about/seek' (poetic usage). 'در آرزوی' is the prepositional phrase 'in/for desire' indicating the sphere of roaming. Together: 'would not go roaming about in desire.'
segments.s655-g1463: 'سخنهای باینده' is a noun phrase 'enduring (or future‑lasting) sayings/words' (باینده = 'enduring'). 'گویم' is the first‑person present verb 'I say/speak' (classical verb form), and 'کنون' is the temporal adverb 'now' placed after the verb for emphasis. The clause thus means 'I now speak enduring words.'
segments.s655-g1464: 'که' introduces an explanatory clause. 'دلرا' uses the object/definite marker 'را' on 'دل' (here functioning like the theme/experiencer), 'به شادی' = 'to/into joy' (به = 'towards/into'), and 'بود رهنمون' places the copula 'بود' (was) before the predicate noun 'رهنمون' (a guide), so literally 'the heart was (a) guide to joy' — idiomatically 'the heart was guided to joy.'
segments.s655-g1281: هرآنکس (an indefinite relative) = 'whoever, any person who'; که introduces the relative clause. آگاه بودن از + noun is the construction 'to be aware/knowledgeable of X' (آگاه از دانش = 'knowledgeable of learning'); بود is the past copula 'was' linking the predicate to the subject. Word order: relative phrase → predicate (standard Persian).
segments.s655-g1282: ز here is the poetic/partitive preposition 'from/of' used to indicate membership ('one of the speakers'); گویندگان = 'speakers' (those who speak). بر در = 'at the door/gate' (بر + در, locative phrase) and شاه is in genitive relation 'the king's (gate)'; بود repeats the past copula 'was'. The whole segment predicates membership/location for the person introduced in the previous segment.
segments.s655-g1283: A noun list joined by و: 'رد' (here appears as a nominal/title whose exact sense is not matched by the provided senses), موبد = Zoroastrian priest, و linking, بخردان (plural) = 'the wise ones' and ارجمند postpositively modifies them as 'esteemed/valuable.' The sequence names the people being described in the next segment.
segments.s655-g1284: بداندیش is an adjective/noun 'malicious, ill‑wishing'; ترسان here is predicative 'frightened/afraid' (they are terrified). ز بیم گزند is a prepositional phrase 'from/because of fear (بیم) of harm (گزند)', giving the cause of their fear. Overall: these people were ill‑disposed and afraid because they feared harm.
segments.s655-g1285: چوخورشید is the fused expression چو + خورشید = 'like the sun' (or 'as the sun'); گیتی = 'the world' (direct object); بیاراستی is a past tense verb 'adorned' (classical Persian past form with -ی ending that can mark 2nd or 3rd person). The phrase means 'you/one adorned the world like the sun.'
segments.s655-g1286: خروشی = 'a clamor/cry/commotion' (subject or arising event); ز درگاه = 'from the court/royal presence' (ز = 'from'); برخاستی is past 'arose/rose up' (classical past form). Together: 'a cry arose from the court.'
segments.s655-g1287: که‌ای here is an archaic/exclamatory address equivalent to 'O' (vocative) calling the listeners; زیردستان = 'subordinates, subjects'; شاه جهان is a title phrase 'king of the world' (شاه modified by جهان). The whole is a vocative: 'O subjects of the king of the world'.
segments.s655-g1288: مدارید is used as a jussive/imperative admonition (here 'do not ...' is implied by the context); یک تن = 'one person' (object), بد = 'badly/ill', اندر نهان = 'in secret/privately'. The clause commands the subjects not to treat someone ill in private (i.e., do not secretly mistreat a person).
segments.s655-g1073: This segment is a simple enumerative series of place-names joined by the conjunction و. In classical/poetic Persian the conjunction is often attached to the following word (e.g. وخوارزم, وآموی). There is no verb here; the list will be completed by the predicate in the next segment.
segments.s655-g1074: This is the main predicate clause: بسی یاد دارمی = 'I hold much in memory / I remember greatly.' بسی is an adverbial intensifier ('much/very'), یاد is the noun 'memory/remembrance', and دارمی is the present-tense first-person form 'I have/hold' in an older dialectal/poetic spelling. با introduces the manner 'with' and درد و غم are coordinated nouns 'pain and sorrow' that qualify the speaker's remembrance.
segments.s655-g1075: ز at the start is the poetic preposition 'from/ because of' marking cause. The phrase lists two causes: بیداد 'injustice/oppression' and (وز) رنج 'suffering, hardship' associated with افراسیاب (Afrasiab). The middle token وز is a fused classical form linking to the following noun and functions like 'and' + preposition here in the poetry, joining بیداد and رنج.
segments.s655-g1076: کسی را is the topical/affected phrase 'anyone' marked by the object/topic marker را. نبد is an archaic negative copular/impersonal verb meaning 'there was not / did not have' (classical variant of نبود). جای آرام وخواب is a compound nominal phrase 'a place of rest and sleep' (جای = place, آرام = repose/quiet, وخواب = 'and sleep'); together they state what did not exist for anyone.
segments.s655-g1077: چوکیخسرو آمد begins a temporal clause: چو = 'when', کیخسرو (Kay Khosrow) is the subject, آمد is the simple past 'arrived'. برستیم از اوی is a poetic/descriptive phrase: برستیم is a past form meaning 'we were freed/escaped/removed' and از اوی means 'from him' (اوی = him). The clause is therefore 'When Kay Khosrow came, we were freed from him.'
segments.s655-g1078: جهانی here stands for 'the world / the people' and is the subject of برآسود, a past passive/intransitive verb 'was at rest / ceased to be troubled'. از governs گفت وگوی: 'from/of talk/conversation' (گفت = speech, وگوی in the poetic form relates to گفت‌وگو). The whole means 'the world was at peace from talk' (i.e. ceased its disturbances of argument).
segments.s655-g1079: This temporal clause begins with ازان پس 'after that' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'then/after'), followed by چو = 'when' introducing the subordinate clause. ارجاسب شد زورمند is a simple past causative-like sequence: ارجاسب (the proper name) شد = 'became' and زورمند = 'powerful' (predicate adjective). The construction means 'after that, when Arjasp became powerful...'.
segments.s655-g1080: This is the result clause: شد (became) introduces the change of state of این مرزها 'these borders/frontiers'. پر ز is a classical poetic pattern meaning 'full of' (پر = full (of), ز = 'of/from'), followed by the coordinated nouns درد و گزند 'pain and harm/injury'. So: 'these borderlands became full of pain and injury.'
segments.s655-g953: چوپردخته شد = a poetic compound where چو (when) + پرداخته (past participle, 'fashioned/finished') is followed by شد (past auxiliary) to yield 'when it was fashioned/finished'. زان is an adverb/pronoun meaning 'thereupon/from that', linking result to the next clause. دبیر follows the verb bi-amad ('came') as a postverbal subject — a stylistic inversion common in poetry (verb before subject).
segments.s655-g954: بیاورد is a past/historic form meaning 'brought' (common in classical/Persian epic). It governs a coordinated list of direct objects: مشک و گلاب و حریر — 'musk, and rosewater, and silk.' The conjunction و links the nouns in the usual way; وحریر (with initial v‑ as liaison) is simply 'and silk'.
segments.s655-g955: یکی is an indefinite subject 'someone/a certain one'. نامه is the object 'a letter'. بنوشت is an archaic/historic past form 'wrote' (the expected verb is نوشتن; the given lemma entry seems mistaken), and ار (if) here introduces a description/condition 'if/when (it was) ...' with تنگ‌وار ('narrow in form') qualifying the letter — the phrase is elliptical: 'one wrote a letter, (which was) narrow in form.' Word order places the descriptive phrase after the verb.
segments.s655-g956: پر before a noun compound means 'full (of)'; here پر آرایش و بوی و رنگ و نگار lists qualities of the letter: آرایش 'adornment', بوی 'scent', رنگ 'color', نگار 'picture/illustration' — linked by و. The whole phrase functions as a adjectival/qualifying phrase: 'full of adornment, scent, color and illustration.'
segments.s655-g957: نخستین (first) is placed before the verb ستود (past 'praised') to mark that the action happened first. آفریننده را is the definite object ('the Creator') marked by را; here را indicates the definite/primary object of the praise.
segments.s655-g958: A series of epithets (جهاندار و بیدار و بیننده) further specifies the object of praise; each noun is linked by و and the final را repeats the definite marker for emphasis/clarity. The sequence functions appositively: 'the Creator — the world‑ruler (sovereign), the awake, the beholder.'
segments.s655-g959: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause ('for/that'). هرچیز is the generic subject 'whatever/anything'. کو here is an archaic relative/particle linked to سازد (classical usage; it connects the relative idea 'that which...'); سازد is the present/habitual classical form 'makes/creates'. اندر is the preposition 'in', and بوش is an archaic form meaning 'its scent' (classical equivalent of بویش). The clause says 'that whatever He makes, in it is His scent.'
segments.s655-g960: بران سو ('بران' = 'upon/therefrom', 'سو' = 'side/toward') forms a prepositional/locative phrase 'thereupon/from that side'. بود is the copular verb 'was' (past). بندگان را روش is a noun phrase 'the servants' conduct/way' with را marking the definite object; combined with بود the sense is 'from that came/was the way of the servants' (i.e. the servants' behaviour followed from that fact). Note the classical word order where such noun phrases can follow the copula.
segments.s655-g1041: ازین شهرها چون برفت is a temporal clause placed before the subject: ازین شهرها (from these cities) + چون (classical 'when') + برفت (archaic past of رفتن, 'went forth/left'). آن سپاه is the subject 'that army' following the clause. Note the older past form برفت with a prefix ب‑, common in epic diction.
segments.s655-g1042: همی marks a repeated/continuous past action (progressive/emphatic). The verb فرستاد (dispatched) follows it; شاه (the king) comes after the verb in poetic inversion (فرستاد شاه = the king sent). مرزبانان is the direct object (the border‑governors/guards) being sent.
segments.s655-g1043: جهان شد پر از داد نوشین‌روان = 'the world became full of the justice of the sweet‑souled.' شد is simple past 'became'; پر از + noun means 'full of ...'; داد نوشین‌روان is a genitive/attributive sequence where نوشین‌روان qualifies داد ('the just acts/justice of the sweet‑souled').
segments.s655-g1044: بخفتند is the past plural 'they slept/lay down.' بردشت here should be read as the locative phrase بر دشت 'on the plain' (written together in the manuscript), so the clause means 'they slept on the plain.' پیر و جوان 'old and young' follows the locative, naming who slept. Note poetic word‑joining and inversion.
segments.s655-g1045: یکایک 'one by one' (an adverbial distributive) + همی‌خواندند (past iterative/progressive 'they were chanting/kept chanting') + آفرین (an acclamation/interjection 'bravo' or 'praise'). Word order places the adverb first for emphasis and the interjection as the object of their chanting.
segments.s655-g1046: ز هر جای = 'from every place/quarter' (ز = from, هر جای = every place). برشهریار زمین is a compressed poetic phrase: بر (to/toward) + شهریار (the king) + زمین (the earth) yielding 'to the king of the earth' or 'to the sovereign on earth' — the ezāfe/genitive relationship is implicit in epic style.
segments.s655-g1047: همه (everyone) + دست برداشته (a participial phrase 'having lifted their hands' or 'raising hands') + به آسمان (toward the sky/heaven). The participle describes the crowd's gesture; the agent 'they' is understood from the context.
segments.s655-g1048: که‌ای here introduces an exclamation/vocative (roughly 'O' in older poetic usage: که + ای), addressing کردگار ('the Creator'). مکان و زمان are the two nouns in a genitive‑like apposition 'of place and time' — together yielding the vocative 'O Maker/Creator of place and time.'
segments.s655-g1113: چنان رای دیدند is a fixed-looking verbal phrase: چنان (= "such/thus") modifies رای (counsel/decision) and دیدند is the past-tense verb (they saw/decided). The phrase means “they took such counsel/so decided.” یک سر سپاه is the content of that decision: سر سپاه is a compound meaning “a chief/commander of the army”; یک marks it as one such commander.
segments.s655-g1114: که introduces a subordinate clause giving the decision’s content (“that …”). آیند is 3rd-person plural present (here used in a jussive/optative or future sense: “should come/come”), agreeing with the delegates who will come. با هدیه is an instrumental/with-phrase meaning “bearing a gift/tribute,” and نزدیک شاه places the action spatially (“near/to the king”).
segments.s655-g1115: چو is the temporal conjunction “when.” نزدیک + proper name (نوشین‌روان) forms a prepositional phrase meaning “near Nushin‑rūvān.” آمدند is simple past 3rd-person plural “they came” describing their arrival.
segments.s655-g1116: همه is the plural subject “all/everyone.” یک دل و یک‌زبان is an idiomatic pairing meaning “of one heart and one tongue” (i.e., unanimous, of one mind and one voice). آمدند repeats the past verb “they came,” here emphasizing that they approached together and in unanimity.
segments.s655-g1117: چنان گشت is an impersonal past construction: چنان “so/thus” + گشت (became/was turned) = “it became so.” ز انبوه درگاه شاه is an ablative/agentive phrase “from the multitude/throng of the king’s court,” giving the cause or source of that state.
segments.s655-g1118: که introduces the result/consequence clause: بستند is past 3rd-person plural “they closed/blocked.” The phrases برمور و بر پشه راه repeat the preposition بر (“against/on”) for parallelism — literally “on/against ants and on/against flies the way” — idiomatically meaning the passage was so blocked that even ants and flies had no way through.
segments.s655-g1119: همه is the plural subject “all.” برنهادند is a past-tense verb constructed with the prefix بر- plus نهادند (placed/put); in this idiom (سر بر زمین نهادن) it means “to prostrate/lay one’s head on the ground.” سر is the direct object “head” and برزمین is the adverbial phrase “on the ground.”
segments.s655-g1120: همه again is “all.” شاه را shows the king as the indirect object with the accusative marker را. خواندند is past 3rd‑person plural of خواندن; here it functions as “they called out/uttered” rather than a literal summons. آفرین is the exclamation/praise “Afarin” ("bravo/praise") that they address to the king.
segments.s655-g1369: بدو گفت is the common poetic order for 'he said to him' (literally 'to him said'); بدو is a dative prepositional form ('to him'). گفت is the reporting verb in simple past (here used narratively) placed after the indirect object for emphasis; فرخ is the speaker's name (here in subject position after the verb, a frequent poetic inversion). کدامست is a fused interrogative ('which is it?') that begins the direct question; مرد is the noun 'man' (the head of the interrogative phrase).
segments.s655-g1370: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that'); دارد is the present-tense verb 'has' (present simple, literary), so the clause reads 'who has...'. دلی شاد is a determiner+noun+adjective cluster ('a heart joyful' / 'a joyful heart') with the adjective after the noun. بی‌باد سرد is a compact poetic qualification: two adjectives linked to the heart, literally 'without wind, (and) cold' — here functioning as adjectival phrases qualifying دلی (i.e. 'not shaken by wind or cold' / 'untroubled by wind or cold').
segments.s655-g1371: چنین گفت is a fixed phrase 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus/so'); گفت again is the reporting verb (poetic past narration). کانکو is the speaker's name following the verb (inversion for style). بود is the past copula 'was' linking to the predicate بیگناه, so the clause means 'he was innocent.'
segments.s655-g1372: This clause presents an action affecting او (him). نبردست here functions as an adjectival/epithetic form (literary/compound) and in context is best read as a description attached to the combat/war sense ('battle‑hardened' / 'of fighting'), though its precise nuance is poetic. آهرمن is the agent 'Ahriman' (the devil); او را marks the patient with the accusative/topic marker را (showing 'him' as object); ز راه is a prepositional phrase 'from the path' (ز = 'from', راه = 'path/way') indicating direction or cause ('turned him from the way'). Word order places the agent before the object-marked pronoun, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s655-g1373: بپرسیدش is an imperative ('ask him') with the clitic -ش marking 'him'. از governs the object of questioning here and means 'about/concerning.' کژی is an abstract noun 'crookedness/wrongness' and functions as the first object of the question; و links to the next noun phrase; راه دیو is 'the way/path of the demon' with the possessive relation implied (noun + noun in apposition). The verb is implied by the initial imperative and governs both coordinated objects.
segments.s655-g1374: ز here means 'concerning' or 'from' in a poetic sense introducing the following topical phrase. راه is again 'path' and جهاندار is an epithet 'world‑ruler/sovereign' modifying or titling the following noun; کیهان here means 'world' (the universe) rather than the physical cosmos as a concept, and خدیو is a title 'khedive' (a ruler). The whole phrase names a high ruler whose 'path' is under question.
segments.s655-g1375: بدو گفت again is 'he said to him' (dative + past verb). فرمان یزدان is a genitival phrase 'the command of Yazdan/God' (noun + proper name/genitive). بهیست is an archaic/literary predicate meaning 'it is fitting' or 'it is right' (impersonal copular construction common in classical Persian), so together this segment states 'He said to him, the command of God is right.'
segments.s655-g1376: که introduces a content clause/explanation ('that'); اندر is the literary preposition 'in/within'; دوگیتی literally 'the two worlds' (classical term for the two realms, e.g. this world and the next) is the locative domain. ازو is a prepositional pronoun 'by/through him/it' referring back to فرمان یزدان (i.e. 'by it'); فرهیست is a literary predicate meaning 'is honored/illustrious' (here passive/state sense). The clause reads 'that in the two worlds, by it he is honored.'
segments.s655-g1329: بدیدم is the 1st-person past perfective 'I saw'. که introduces the subordinate clause ('that'). این is the demonstrative 'this' modifying گنبد 'dome'. دیرساز is a postpositive adjective/compound meaning 'long-built' / 'slowly made' describing the dome.
segments.s655-g1330: نخواهد + infinitive (گشادن) expresses negative future/irrealis here: 'will not ...'. همی is the classical continuous/emphatic particle (here 'still/indeed' / aspectual marker). لب گشادن is an idiom 'to open the lip' = 'to speak/reveal'; گشادن is the infinitive 'to open/reveal'. به راز means 'to/about the secret' (به marking the object of revelation).
segments.s655-g1331: اگر introduces a conditional clause 'if'. مرد here carries the epic meaning 'a man / a valiant man' (heroic sense). برخیزد is the present-subjunctive/optative 'should rise / rises' used in conditional protasis. از تخت بزم is a prepositional phrase 'from the throne (تخت) of the banquet/feast (بزم)'.
segments.s655-g1332: نهد is the present/subjunctive form of نهاد here meaning 'he places / will place'. بر کف خویش is literal 'upon his own palm/hand' — بر + کف (palm) + خویش (possessive formed from خوی + enclitic ش). جان را is the definite object marked by را ('his life' as the object). برزم is an archaic adverbial/locative meaning approximately 'in battle / on the field' (an old-form adverbial used with placing one's life).
segments.s655-g1333: زمین را: زمین is the direct object 'the land/country' and را marks it as definite. بپردازد is a verbal form (present/future) meaning here 'deliver / free / cast away' the land from (از) the enemies; از دشمنان marks the source/agent from which the land is freed. The verb is transitive and takes زمین را as its object.
