segments.s655-g1334: شود is the impersonal/future 'it will become'. ایمن is the predicate adjective 'safe, secure'. از رنج آهرمنان is a prepositional phrase 'from the suffering/torment of the Ahrimans' (آهرمنان = Ahriman/devils) that specifies what the land will be safe from.
segments.s655-g1335: شود = 'he will become'. پادشا is 'king/sovereign' (epic form). بر جهان = 'over the world'. سر‌به‌سر in classical usage often means 'throughout' / 'entire', modifying the scope 'over the whole world' (here functioning adverbially after جهان).
segments.s655-g1336: بیابد is the present/future form of یابیدن 'he will find / finds'. سخنها is the plural object 'the speeches / words / discourse'. همه 'all' emphasizes totality. دربدر is an adjective/adverb 'wandering/astray/scattered' describing the state of those speeches (i.e., discourse will be in disorder or scattered). The verb-front order (verb before object) is a common stylistic choice in poetry.
segments.s655-g1753: دروغ (subject) begins the clause: a noun phrase 'a lie'. آنک is an archaic/poetic relative pronoun 'that/which' introducing a descriptive clause about the lie. The adjectives follow the relative pronoun in the order 'بی‌رنگ و زشتست و خوار' — note زشتست is an adjectival predicate formed with the bound element -ست (old/poetic copula) meaning 'is ugly', and خوار is a final adjective meaning 'despised/worthless'. The conjunction و simply links the coordinate adjectives.
segments.s655-g1754: This short clause uses the correlative construction 'چه ... و چه ...' = 'whether ... or ...'. Each 'چه' introduces one of the two alternatives and is followed by the preposition بر 'against/on' and the noun. Word order is correlative: 'چه بر X و چه بر Y' = 'whether toward X or toward Y'.
segments.s655-g1755: This is a topical/locative phrase: 'به گیتی ز نیکی چه چیزست گفت' — literally 'in the world, from goodness, what thing is there said'. به گیتی 'in the world' (prepositional locative), ز نیکی 'of/from goodness' is a genitive/partitive link, چه چیزست is the interrogative 'what thing is there' (چیزست = 'there is a thing' in this poetic form), and گفت here functions as 'was said' / 'he said' or 'a saying' (a nominalized sense) introducing the reported question.
segments.s655-g1756: که is the relativizer/quotative linking the reported content ('that…'). هم آشکارست و هم در نهفت is a balanced clause using هم ... و هم ... to mean 'both ... and ...': آشکارست ('is manifest', again using the older -ست copula) and در نهفت ('hidden within' — در + نهفت, where نهفت is an adjective/participle 'hidden, concealed'). Word order places the predicate after the subjectless connector (typical of Persian poetic style).
segments.s655-g1757: کزو = 'from which / from whom' (ک + زو; poetic relative/adverb of source). مرد داننده is a noun phrase 'a knowing man' (مرد + داننده 'knower/wise one'). جوشن کند is a verb phrase: جوشن 'cuirass/armor' as object and کند (present/past-poetic 3rd-person auxiliary) 'makes/does' — so 'from which a knowledgeable man makes armor'. Word order is source → agent → object → verb (poetic inversion).
segments.s655-g1758: روان را is the definite object ('the soul' marked by را). بدان is the compound prepositional phrase 'with that / by that' (ب + دان in classical style = 'by/with that'), followed by چیز 'thing' used here as a resumptive/demonstrative nominal ('that thing'), روشن کند = 'makes clear / enlightens' with کند as the verb. Overall: 'and with that thing he makes the soul clear/enlightened.'
segments.s655-g1759: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed poetic formula: چنین = 'thus/so', داد here is the past 'gave' (داد پاسخ = 'gave answer'), and پاسخ = 'an answer'. که introduces the content clause (quotative/relative). کوشان is a participle/adjective 'those striving' (agent/subject of the content clause). The following ب + دین is an old/poetic prepositional/infinitival cluster meaning 'for this / in this cause' (در سخن = 'in this matter'); contextually it marks the domain of striving.
segments.s655-g1760: به گیتی is a locative phrase 'in the world'. نیابد is the present/subjunctive/poetic negative form of 'find' (نیابد = 'does not find' / 'finds not' depending on context); جز از = 'except from'; آفرین here functions as a short form referring to the Creator/that which creates (poetic usage). The clause literally: 'finds in the world nothing except from the Creator.' Note the inverted/elliptical, highly condensed poetic word order.
segments.s655-g1241: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate time clause. کسری is the proper name (Kisra/Khosrow). بیامد is past simple, 3rd-person singular 'came'. برتخت is the fused prepositional phrase 'بر + تخت' meaning 'upon/on the throne'. خوی + ش forms the possessive/enclitic construction (خویش) 'his/one's own' — here the two tokens are split in the line for metrical reasons, with ش the enclitic 'his'.
segments.s655-g1242: This is a nominal/nominalized continuation describing what accompanied the king: a coordinated NP 'گرازان و انباز' (two plural nouns) followed by the prepositional phrase 'با بخت خویش' = 'with his own fortune'. با is the preposition 'with' and خوی + ش again yields the possessive idea 'his (own)'. انباز is an archaic/uncertain word treated here as a substantive; the whole phrase is a verbless accompaniment to the main clause introduced by چو…بیامد.
segments.s655-g1243: جهان is the subject 'the world.' چون here means 'like/as' introducing a simile 'like Paradise' (بهشتی). شد is the past auxiliary 'became' and آراسته is a past‑participle/adjective 'adorned' that predicates the new state of the world.
segments.s655-g1244: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of' (here marking cause or source). داد means 'justice' and خوبی 'goodness'; the repetition of ز before each item is emphatic. پر از (پر + از) is the idiom 'full of' and خواسته is a noun 'that which is desired'/'wishes' — so literally 'from justice and from goodness (it was) full of desired things.'
segments.s655-g1245: نشستند is a 3rd-person plural past verb 'they sat/ceased/settled' and here functions idiomatically with ز + verbal noun to mean 'they refrained/ceased from.' شاهان is the subject 'the kings.' ز + آویختن (a verbal noun) marks the activity they gave up: 'from hanging about/loitering' (آویختن here = 'to hang about/loiter').
segments.s655-g1246: به هر جای is a locative phrase 'in/at every place' (به + هر + جای). The coordinated phrase بیداد و خون ریختن uses the verbal noun/infinitive ریختن 'to spill/ shed' governing both nouns: 'to do injustice and to shed blood.' The whole segment completes what the kings stopped doing.
segments.s655-g1247: Again جهان is the subject. پر شد از is the verbal phrase 'became full of' (پر = 'full (of)', شد = 'became', از = 'of/from'). فره ایزدی is the compound 'divine glory' — فره 'glory/divine charisma' qualified by ایزدی 'divine'.
segments.s655-g1248: ببستند is a transitive past 3rd‑person plural 'they bound/tied/closed (up)'. گفتی here is a parenthetical/poetic particle functioning like 'so to speak' or 'one might say' (not the literal 'conversation'); it is archaic/idiomatic. دو دست 'two hands' is the direct object (hands of evil), followed by از بدی 'of evil' specifying whose hands were bound.
segments.s655-g89: This is a simple nominal clause with a locative subject followed by a predicative adjective phrase: 'بخارا' (Bukhara) is the place-name subject, 'پر از' is the fixed expression 'full of' (پر + از), followed by the list of things filling it 'گرد و کوپال' (dust and maces). 'بود' is the past copula omitted from this segment but understood to link the predicate to the subject.
segments.s655-g90: A relative clause introduced by 'که' (which/that). 'لشکرگه' ('army-camp') is the locative noun, followed by the possessor 'شاه هیتال' (King Hital). 'بود' is the past copula 'was', so the whole clause specifies that the place just mentioned served as Hital's camp.
segments.s655-g91: 'بشد' here is the narrative/past sense 'it happened / he came' (an epic past marker). It takes the proper name 'غاتفر' (Gatafar) as its subject. 'با سپاهی چو کوه' is an instrumental/qualifying phrase: 'with an army like a mountain' — 'چو' is the simile particle 'like'.
segments.s655-g92: 'ز هیتال' ('from Hital') gives origin; 'گرد آوردیده' is a compound verbal idea: گرد + آوردیده = 'gathered/collected' (perfect participial notion 'having brought together'), and 'گروه' is the noun 'group/host' that this participle modifies. Together: 'a group gathered from Hital.'
segments.s655-g93: 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when'. 'تنگ' ('narrow') and 'اندرآمد' together express 'became narrow' (poetic past-state description). 'ز هر سو سپاه' (literally 'from every side the host') places the approaching troops adverbially after the clause — word order in Persian often places the agent/subject at the end for emphasis.
segments.s655-g94: 'ز تنگی' ('because of the narrowness') gives the reason. 'ببستند' is a past transitive verb 'they closed / they blocked'. The phrase 'بر باد راه' is idiomatic here: 'راه' is 'the road/way' and 'بر باد' (literally 'to the wind') signals that the road was rendered unusable/no escape remained — together: 'they closed off the road, leaving no way out.'
segments.s655-g95: 'درخشیدن' is an infinitival/verb-noun form 'the shining / to shine' used in a paratactic poetic clause; it governs the genitive 'تیغهای سران' ('the swords of the chiefs/heads'). The construction produces a terse image: 'the chiefs' swords shone.'
segments.s655-g96: Another paratactic clause: 'گراییدن' (an older verb meaning 'to incline, to turn, to swing') is parallel to 'درخشیدن' and takes 'گرزهای گران' ('heavy maces') as its subject/object — 'the heavy maces were swung/turned.' The lack of an explicit auxiliary is typical of epic terseness.
segments.s655-g937: بفرمود is a past, honorific/periphrastic verb meaning 'he commanded/said' placed before the title for poetic inversion; خاقان is the title 'khaghan' (the ruler) and پیروزبخت is an epithet attached after it meaning 'fortunate' or 'victorious', so the whole segment reads 'the fortunate Khaghan commanded'.
segments.s655-g938: که introduces a subordinate content clause ('that/so that'); بنهند is a 3rd‑person plural subjunctive/imperative form of نهادن 'to place/set' used after a command to mean 'that they place'; برکوهه is a prepositional phrase 'on the mountain' (بر + کوه with an archaic -ه ending); پیل تخت is the noun phrase 'elephant throne' (پیل 'elephant' modifying تخت 'throne'), functioning as the object of بنهند.
segments.s655-g939: برو is an imperative 'go' used as an instruction (here functioning like 'do this' or 'make'); بافته is a participial/adjectival form 'woven' modifying شوشه 'tassel', so 'بافته شوشه' = 'a tassel woven'; سیم و زر list the materials 'silver and gold' with و as the coordinating conjunction.
segments.s655-g940: به here marks direction/placement 'into/within' (classical Persian often uses به + noun to indicate where something is put); شوشه repeats 'tassel' as the target; درون explicitly means 'inside/within'; چند گونه is a quantifying phrase 'several kinds' (چند = several, گونه = kind/type) and گهر is the direct object 'jewel(s)', so together 'put several kinds of jewels within the tassels.'
segments.s655-g941: درفشی درفشان is a doubled epithet: درفشی (banner-related noun) followed by درفشان 'shining/glittering', forming 'a glittering banner'; به + دیبای چین specifies material/appearance 'in/with brocade of folds' (دیبا 'brocade', چین 'fold(s)', an adjectival noun), so the whole phrase describes a shining banner made of folded brocade.
segments.s655-g942: که is the relative 'which/that'; پیدا نبودی is a negative past construction where پیدا 'visible/seen' plus نبودی (archaic past 'was not') yields 'which was not seen'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of', and دیبا زمین 'the brocade of the earth' (دیبا 'brocade', زمین 'earth/world') forms the comparative scope — 'the like of which was not seen on the earth.'
segments.s655-g943: به here introduces the accompaniment/measure phrase 'with/amounting to' (here 'with a hundred of his men'); مردش is 'his men' (مرد + possessive -ش); از جای 'from its place' (از = from, جای = place); برداشتند is the past plural 'they lifted/took up/removed' and is the main verb of the clause.
segments.s655-g944: ز هامون uses ز 'from' plus هامون 'the plain' (poetic landscape) to mark origin; به گردون 'to the sky/heavens' uses به for direction; برافراشتند is past plural 'they raised/hoisted up' and completes the action begun in the previous clause — 'they raised (it) from the plain to the heavens.'
segments.s655-g777: برایشان (برای + شان) is the indirect object ‘for them/their’ (literally ‘for them’). یکی خلعت is the direct object phrase ‘one robe of honour’ (یکی = one, خلعت = robe of honour). افگند is the simple past (poetic/archaic) of افکندن ‘to throw/lay/bestow’ and is the main verb; شاه at the end is the subject ‘the king’ (poetic word order: object + verb + subject instead of more neutral SOV).
segments.s655-g778: کزان is a connective/relative-like particle ‘from which/whereof’ linking this clause to the previous action. ماند is past of ماندن ‘remained/was left’ (here: ‘resulted, was left’). اندر + شگفتی سپاه is a prepositional phrase ‘in/among the amazement of the host’ (اندر = in, شگفتی = astonishment/marvel, سپاه = the army). Together: ‘which left the army in astonishment.’
segments.s655-g779: گزین کرد is a compound past: گزین (to choose/select) + کرد (did) = ‘chose/selected’. کسری is the subject ‘Khosrow (Kisra)’. خردمند و راد are the qualities (adjectives) of the person chosen: ‘wise and generous’ (the object ‘a man’ is implied in Persian). Word order is subject + verb + object/adjectival phrase (poetic flexibility).
segments.s655-g780: کجا here functions as a relative/locative element ‘whose/where’ in classical usage introducing the identification of name. نام او بود is straightforward copular past ‘his name was…’ (او = he/his, بود = was). مهران ستاد is the proper name/title being predicated; ستاد here appears as part of that epithet/title rather than as an independent finite verb.
segments.s655-g781: ز ایرانیان نامور is a prepositional/attributive phrase ‘from the Iranians, renowned’ (ز = from/of, ایرانیان = Iranians, نامور = famous/renowned). صد سوار follows as the quantified noun phrase ‘a hundred horsemen/riders’. The phrase lists origin, fame, number and type in typical poetic order.
segments.s655-g782: سخنگوی و شایسته و نامدار are three coordinate epithets describing those horsemen: سخنگوی (literally ‘speaker’/‘orator’, here ‘eloquent’), شایسته ‘worthy/fit’, نامدار ‘renowned/illustrious’. The repeated و simply strings the attributes together.
segments.s655-g783: چنین گفت is ‘thus said’ (چنین = thus, گفت = said — poetic past). کسری is the subject ‘Khosrow’ and به مهران ستاد marks the indirect object/recipient ‘to Mehrān Setād’. Word order places the verb phrase before the subject as is common in epic poetry.
segments.s655-g784: که introduces reported content ‘that’ (here a blessing/wish). رو شاد is an idiomatic wish ‘may your face be joyful’ (رو = face/countenance, شاد = happy). و پیروز adds ‘and victorious’ (پیروز = victorious). با مهر و داد is a prepositional phrase ‘with affection/kindness and justice’ (با = with, مهر = love/kindness, داد = justice). The whole clause is an optative/blessing formula.
segments.s655-g369: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of' (classical form of از). دینار here means 'money' in general. پوشش is a noun 'covering/protection' and نیاید is a negative present/poetic form meaning 'does not come' or 'does not avail'. نه خورد is a terse coordinate phrase: نه negates خورد ('to eat' here taken literally as 'be eaten' or metaphorically 'to be of use/consumed'); the order places the negated verb after the noun phrase for poetic effect.
segments.s655-g370: نه آغاز یک عبارت متصل است ('nor')؛ گستردنی is an adjectival/participle form meaning 'that can be spread' or 'spreadable/extendable'. روز ننگ و نبرد is a prepositional/time phrase 'on the day of shame and of battle' (روز = 'day'). The whole segment says money cannot be 'spread' (i.e., distributed or make a show) when shame and fighting occur.
segments.s655-g371: بدو is the dative-style classical pronoun 'to him/for him'. ایمنی is a noun 'safety/security'. باید is the impersonal/modal 'it is necessary / one must'; here it governs a small nominal list. و connects the following item; خوردنی is used as a noun/adjective meaning 'food' or 'something to eat'. The segment lists essentials ('for him: safety and food').
segments.s655-g372: همان is a demonstrative 'that very / the same'. پوشش repeats 'covering/protection' as the same thing needed. و connects to نغز گستردنی, where نغز is an evaluative adjective 'delightful/fine' and گستردنی is again the participial/adjectival 'spreadable / to be spread' (i.e., a graceful or well-distributed provision). The phrase names the preferred qualities of the 'covering' or provision.
segments.s655-g373: هرآنکس is a classical indefinite 'whoever'. که introduces the relative/subordinate clause 'who'. از بد uses از in the causal/concern sense 'because of / through (fear of)'; بد here means 'badness/misfortune'. هراسان is a predicate/adjective 'frightened', and شود is the classical third-person present/future of شدن 'become'. Together: 'whoever becomes frightened because of misfortune...'.
segments.s655-g374: درم (dirham) is the coin; خوار is an adjective 'despised/cheap' that here predicates how the dirham is regarded. گیرد is the classical present/simple verb 'takes'/'seizes' (literally 'he takes [it]'); syntactically the phrase means 'he treats the dirham as low/cheap' or 'he makes the dirham despicable'. تن آسان شود is a separate clause: تن 'the body/person' + آسان 'easy/at ease' + شود 'becomes' — 'his body becomes at ease' (i.e. he seeks bodily comfort).
segments.s655-g375: ز is the poetic 'from/of'. لشکر is 'army/host' (genitive source). سخنگوی is a compound noun 'spokesman/speaker', and ده is the numeral 'ten' placed after the noun in classical word order. برگزید is the past/simple verb 'he chose/selected'. The clause means 'he selected ten spokesmen from the army'.
segments.s655-g376: که introduces a relative clause 'who'. دانند is present/subjunctive-like 'they know' or 'are capable of'; here it describes the chosen men. گفتار دانا is 'the speech/words of the wise' (گفتار = 'speech', دانا = 'wise'). شنید is the verb 'to hear' (classical form); together the clause says these men 'know how to hear/receive the words of the wise' — i.e., they are fit listeners/interpreters.
segments.s655-g1001: دلی شاد با هدیه و با نثار is a single circumstantial phrase: دلی شاد (lit. 'a heart joyful') uses دل (here spelled دلی) + adjective شاد to mean 'with a joyful/gleeful heart'. The two prepositional phrases با هدیه و با نثار ('with gift(s) and with offering(s)') follow and specify how that joy is expressed. Word order places the nominal phrase before the prepositional complements; this is typical in older/poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g1002: همه مهربان و همه دوستار is a copied/adverbial predicate describing the crowd: همه (everyone/all) + adjective مهربان ('kind') and then repeated همه with noun/adj دوستار ('devotee, well-wisher'). The repetition of همه is emphatic ('all were kind and all were affectionate/devoted'). The construction omits the copula (بود) as is normal in Persian.
segments.s655-g1003: ببستند آذین به شهر و به راه: ببستند is a past plural (3rd person) form of بستن (here with an extra initial ب- typical in classical/poetic usage) meaning 'they fastened/tied/strung up'. آذین ('decoration') is the direct object and the two به-phrases (to/for the city and to/for the road) mark the locations where decorations were set up.
segments.s655-g1004: درم ریختند از بر تخت شاه: درم ('dirham/coins') is placed before the verb ریختند (past plural of ریختن, 'they poured/they cast'), showing the object-before-verb order common in Persian. از بر تخت شاه is a classical prepositional phrase meaning 'from before/upon the king's throne' (از بر is an older phrasing meaning 'from in front of / from the place of').
segments.s655-g1005: به آموی و راه بیابان مرو lists destinations/routes: به + place/route. Here the phrase names two directions/ways: 'to Amu' and 'the road of the Merv desert.' In this line آموی functions as a place-name (not the kinship sense in the provided list) and بیابان مرو = 'the desert of Merv.'
segments.s655-g1006: زمین بود یک سر چو پر تذرو is a verbless/predicative clause with copula بود ('was'). زمین ('the land/ground') is subject; یک سر here is an idiomatic adverbial ('all over/entirely, on every side'); چو = 'as/like' introduces a simile; پر تذرو means 'full of tazru' (tذرو 'scattering'/'sprinkling'). So the literal structure is 'the land was entirely, as if full of scattering.' This is an archaic/poetic phrasing meaning the ground was everywhere scattered/sprinkled (with whatever was being cast).
segments.s655-g1007: چنین تا به بسطام وگرگان رسید: چنین ('thus/so') is an adverb linking back to the preceding action; تا + place = 'as far as' (here 'as far as Bistam and Gorgan'); رسید is the past 3rd-singular of رسیدن 'arrived/reached'. The clause reports the procession's advance to those towns.
segments.s655-g1008: تو گفتی زمین آسمان را ندید consists of تو گفتی ('you said' — گفتی here is 2nd-person past of گفتن, introducing reported speech) followed directly by the reported clause زمین آسمان را ندید. In the reported clause زمین (subject) آسمان را (object marked by را) ندید (past negative 'did not see') — S‑O‑V order is preserved. The result is literal reported speech: 'you said the earth did not see the sky.'
segments.s655-g25: طرایف is a plural noun 'curiosities/oddities' placed at the start as topic. که introduces a relative clause ('that/which'); باشد here is the classical present/subjunctive form of 'to be' linking the relative clause to its location; به چین اندرون (preposition + place + noun) gives the locative 'in/at China’s inner (court)'. Word order is Topic + relative clause + copula + locative phrase.
segments.s655-g26: بیاراست here is a perfect/past/adjectival sense 'was adorned' or 'arrayed'; از هر دری is a prepositional phrase 'from/out of every ...' (here meaning 'from every land/region'); برهیون is a proper name and the agent here (Barhiyun) who is presented as the one making the adornment.
segments.s655-g27: ز introduces a source or material 'from/of' (poetic preposition); دینار چینی is a genitive noun phrase 'Chinese dinars'; the second ز before بهر has a causal/for-purpose sense 'for' and بهر نثار means 'for the sake of (the) offering/dedication'.
segments.s655-g28: به گنجور is a prepositional phrase 'to/into the treasury'; فرمود is the past/polite verb 'he said/commanded' taking the following clause; تا introduces the measure 'up to/as many as' here followed by سی هزار 'thirty thousand'.
segments.s655-g29: بیاورد is a past tense verbal clause 'he brought (them)'; و coordinates the next action; با هدیه‌ها is an instrumental phrase 'with gifts'; یار کرد is a light verb construction 'made (someone) a friend/ally' — literally 'did companion', meaning 'won over/made allies'.
segments.s655-g30: دگر را uses را as the definite object marker: 'the other (one)'; همه بار is an idiomatic expression 'completely/entirely laden' (همه = wholly, بار = load); دینار کرد is 'made (into) dinars' or more naturally 'filled with dinars' — کرد is the light/past verb completing the predicate.
segments.s655-g31: سخنگوی مردی بجست is ordered as Verb-final: 'he sought a spokesman/man' (بجست is past 'sought'); از مهان is a source phrase 'from among the guests/visitors' indicating where the spokesman should come from.
segments.s655-g32: خردمند is an appositive noun 'wise (man)'; و connects the following participial phrase; گردیده گرد جهان is a compound: گردیده is a verbal/adjectival participle 'turned/been about' and گرد جهان 'around the world' — together 'one who has gone/traveled around the world.'
segments.s655-g409: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' (archaic/poetic). آگاهی 'information, news' is the subject/thing that arrives; آمد is simple past 3rd-person 'came/reached'. به is the preposition 'to/towards' introducing the place(s) reached, and ماچین و چین are place names (Ma-chin and China) coordinated by و.
segments.s655-g410: بگوینده is an agent-noun 'the one who speaks / the speaker'. برخواندیم is a compound past verb (بر + خواندیم) meaning 'we called out / we proclaimed / we recited' (1st-person plural past). آفرین here is an interjection 'Bravo!' / an acclamation, the thing cried aloud after برخواندیم.
segments.s655-g411: ز is the poetic preposition 'from / because of' introducing the cause. پیروزی شاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's victory' (noun followed by its possessor). و links the coordinated noun مردانگی 'manliness / manhood'.
segments.s655-g412: This is a straightforward coordination of abstract nouns: خردمندی 'wisdom/cleverness', و 'and', شرم 'shame/modesty' (here as a positive quality), و 'and', فرزانگی 'sagacity/learned wisdom' — a series of attributes listed in parallel.
segments.s655-g413: همه is 'all / everything' (the subject). دوستی functions as a predicate noun 'friendship / goodwill'. بودی is an archaic past copula 'was' (third-person). اندرنهان is a compound adjective/adverb 'hidden within / kept secret', modifying the state described.
segments.s655-g414: که introduces the subordinate clause expressing purpose/result ('so that' / 'that'). جوییم is the present/subjunctive-like form 'we seek / we would seek' from جستن (the sense list provided does not include the verbal reading). باشهریار is 'to be king' (become sovereign) and جهان is 'the world' — together giving 'that we seek to be sovereign of the world.'
segments.s655-g415: چو is 'when' (temporal). آن نامه 'that letter' is the direct object of بشنید, where بشنید is past simple 'he heard'. و links the second object 'گفتار اوی' — گفتار 'speech / words' and اوی is the possessive 'his', so 'his speech.'
segments.s655-g416: A coordinated list of qualities/possessions: بزرگی 'greatness / grandeur', و 'and', مردی 'manhood / bravery', و 'and', بازار اوی 'his bazaar / his commerce' (بازار here is literal 'market' or metaphorically 'trade/affairs'); اوی again is the possessive 'his', attaching to بازار.
segments.s655-g177: یکی آگهی یافتم ناپسند is SOV classical word order: 'یکی آگهی' = 'a certain piece of news', 'یافتم' is the past 1st-person verb 'I obtained/found', and 'ناپسند' is a postpositive adjective qualifying that news ('unpleasant'). The adjective follows the verb phrase in this poetic register, so the whole line reads 'I found/received a certain news (which was) unpleasant.'
segments.s655-g178: سخنهای ناخوب و ناسودمند is a noun with coordinated adjectives: 'سخنهای' (pl. 'words/speeches') followed by two attributive adjectives 'ناخوب' and 'ناسودمند' joined by 'و'. In classical style the adjectives follow the noun; together they specify the content of the unpleasant news ('bad and unprofitable words').
segments.s655-g179: ز هیتال وز ترک وخاقان چین is a classical enumerative phrase that repeats the preposition 'ز' (from). Here the form 'وز' is a fused poetic form (و + ز) meaning 'and from' before the next item. 'هیتال' is a proper name; 'خاقان چین' reads as 'the khaghan of China' (possessor/owner relation expressed without an explicit ezāfe in this older style).
segments.s655-g180: وزان مرزبانان توران زمین continues the enumeration: 'وزان' (= و + ز + ان) functions as 'and from them/and from that', linking to the next group. 'مرزبانان' is the plural 'marzbans' (border-guard/governors). 'توران زمین' is a genitival phrase 'the land of Turan' (land follows the regional name in this poetic order).
segments.s655-g181: بی‌اندازه لشکر شدند انجمن places the adverb 'بی‌اندازه' ('immeasurably/excessively') before the noun phrase. The verb 'شدند' (they became) follows; classical inversion places 'انجمن' (assembly) after the verb so that the sense is 'the assembly became an immeasurable army' or 'the gathering swelled into an immense host.' The plural verb 'شدند' reflects the collective/multitude sense.
segments.s655-g182: ز چاچ وز چین وز ترک و ختن is an enumerative string introduced by repeated prepositions 'ز' and the fused form 'وز' (و + ز) meaning 'and from', listing origins of the assembled host. The items are place/people names (چاچ = Chach; چین = China; ترک = Turks; ختن = Khotan/Khitans) rather than their dictionary senses as verbs.
segments.s655-g183: یکی هفته هیتال با ترک و چین is an adverbial temporal phrase 'یکی هفته' = 'for one week' followed by the subject cluster 'هیتال با ترک و چین' = 'Hital with the Turks and China' (with + companions). In classical Persian 'یکی هفته' functions adverbially to mark duration.
segments.s655-g184: ز اسبان نبرداشتند ایچ زین: 'ز اسبان' = 'from horses' (i.e., 'on horseback'), 'نبرداشتند' is the negated past plural verb 'they did not take/raise' (here 'did not mount' or 'did not put themselves on'), and 'ایچ زین' is an emphatic negative phrase 'not at all (into) the saddle' — classical placement puts the emphatic negative 'ایچ' before 'زین' ('saddle'). Overall: 'they did not mount the saddle at all.'
segments.s655-g105: یکی هفته is an adverbial time phrase ‘one week’ (here 'for a week'); آن لشکر جنگجوی is a noun phrase 'that warrior-host' where آن is a demonstrative before the head noun لشکر and جنگجوی is a postpositive adjective ('warlike, warrior‑like'). Word order is the typical older Persian NounPhrase with modifiers following or preceding as shown.
segments.s655-g106: This is a verbal complex meaning 'had been coming/advancing': بروی here functions as part of a motion expression (from the root رو/رفت 'go'), اندر is a locative/into particle, آورده بودند is the past perfect/perfect construction ('had brought' or here 'had made/put'), and the final روی is the noun 'face/direction' used idiomatically with آوردن ('to turn/bring one's face toward'). Together they express 'had turned their faces toward / had advanced'. بود(ند) is the auxiliary marking the past-perfect aspect.
segments.s655-g107: به هر جای is a locative phrase 'at/in every place' (به here marks location/toward), برتوده‌ای is an adverbial/descr. 'in heaps' (the suffix -ای makes it adverbial), and کشته بود literally 'there were slain' or 'the slain lay'—کشته is noun/adjective 'the killed/slain' and بود provides the past-state ('there were'). The word order places the locative first and the predicate (killed + copula) later, common in poetry.
segments.s655-g108: ز خون starts the cause/agentive phrase 'from/because of blood'; خاک و سنک 'earth and stone' are the affected surfaces; ارغوان گشته بود is a perfective result clause 'had become crimson' (گشته, perfect participle of شدن, + بود as auxiliary). The sequence means 'the earth and the stones had turned crimson with blood.'
segments.s655-g109: ز بس here means 'from the abundance/so many' (بس indicating multitude), followed by a series of weapons linked by و: نیزه (spear), گرز (mace), کوپال (a named heavy club/weapon), تیغ (sword). This is a simple coordinating list introduced by ز بس 'from so many...'.
segments.s655-g110: توگفتی literally 'you said' is used here idiomatically/impersonally (a common poetic device meaning 'one might say' or 'so it was said'); همی is the old continuous/emphatic particle (here reinforcing the ongoing appearance), سنگ is 'stones', بارد is used as a verbal idea 'to fall/rain' (poetic usage: 'stones rained'); ز is the preposition 'from', and میغ 'blade' names the source. Grammatically this reads like an impersonal observation: 'it was said / it seemed that stones rained from the blades.' Note: بارد as 'rain/fall' is a poetic verbal usage, not the common adjectival sense 'cold'.
segments.s655-g111: نهان شد is 'became hidden' (نهان 'hidden' + شد 'became'); بگرد is a verbal form from گردیدن 'to turn' used here to indicate turning/revolving (a poetic present/imperative form used narratively), and اندرون آفتاب 'inside the sun' or 'within the sun's circle' is the locative phrase. Together they describe the sun's turning and vanishing (hidden) — a compact poetic clause where verbs and locatives follow the descriptive adjective.
segments.s655-g112: پر از خاک شد is a result clause 'became full of dust' (پر 'full (of)' + از + خاک + شد 'became'); چشم پران عقاب is a genitive/appositive phrase 'the eyes of the winged eagle' or 'the eagle with winged (feathered) eyes'—پران is the adjective 'winged/feathered' modifying عقاب. The whole segment means 'the eagle's eyes were filled with dust.'
segments.s655-g1161: This is a noun list introduced by the preposition ز (’from’), with items joined by و. پوشیده is an adjective (past participle) following the implied noun رویان to mean “veiled/covered (faces/ones)”; Persian often places adjectives after the noun they modify or in apposition in a catalogue. The coordination (و … و) simply strings together possessions or gifts.
segments.s655-g1162: دگر here is an attributive adjective meaning “another/other” modifying the following noun phrase مهد پیروزه (a cradle/seat described by the adjective پیروزه). The adjective پیروزه (postpositive) names the material or colour (turquoise); تخت عاج is a coordinated noun phrase: تخت (throne) followed by عاج (ivory) as its descriptor.
segments.s655-g1163: نشستند is a 3rd-person plural past tense verb ‘they sat’ (verb first as is normal in narrative Persian). بر زین is a locative prepositional phrase ‘on/upon saddles’. The following noun پرستندگان can be read as either the possessor of زین (‘the worshippers’ saddles’) or as an appositive/subject depending on context; Persian allows such flexible ordering.
segments.s655-g1164: This phrase strings modifiers and a noun: بت (idol) + آرای (from آرایی, ‘adornment/ornaments’) functions as a compound/epithet (’idol-adorned’/’idol-decked’), then وهرگونه‌ای (‘and of every kind’) links to بندگان (servants/people). The sequence shows postpositive modifiers and coordination typical of poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g1165: فرستاد is the simple past 3rd-person singular ‘he sent’. یکسر is an adverb modifying the verb (here ‘at once/straightaway’). سوی طیسفون is a directional phrase ‘toward Ctesiphon’ (سوی + place). The normal narrative order places the verb before its adverbial and directional complements.
segments.s655-g1166: شبستان چینی is a noun plus postpositive descriptor: ‘a Chinese bedchamber/inner hall’ with چینی functioning adjectivally. به پیش اندرون is a purpose/destination phrase: به + پیش (’before/in front of’) + اندرون (’inner quarters’), meaning ‘for the front of the inner apartments’ or ‘into the inner palace’ — the preposition به marks direction/purpose.
segments.s655-g1167: Two prepositional phrases joined by و begin the line: به فرخنده فال (‘on an auspicious omen’) and به‌روز آسمان (‘on a day of heaven’ / ‘on a bright/fortunate day’). Each begins with the preposition به marking circumstance or time; این تركيبها are poetic locutions indicating favourable timing.
segments.s655-g1168: برفتند is the past 3rd-person plural ‘they went/proceeded’. گرد is an adverb ‘around/about’ and combines with the verb (گرد برفتند = ‘went about’). اندر + ـش is the locative with the enclitic pronoun ش (‘in it/his’), and خادمان (servants/attendants) is the subject; Persian allows the subject to follow the verb and locative, so the clause reads ‘his servants went about within it.’
segments.s655-g153: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. هیتالیان is a proper/clan name in the oblique (genitive) following ز. کودک و مرد وزن is a coordinated list: کودک 'child' and مرد 'man'; وزن here is a poetic orthographic contraction of و + زن ('and woman'), i.e. 'and women' (common in older/poetic spelling). The whole group gives the people who act.
segments.s655-g154: برین = بر این ('on/over this') where ین is the demonstrative 'این' attached to بر. یک سخن = 'one word/matter/utterance' (سخن as 'speech/matter'). برشدند is past, 3rd-person plural (بر + شدند) 'they rose/appeared/rose up'; انجمن 'assembly' names what came into being. Word order is slightly inverted (verb with plural marking then the noun), a poetic pattern: the clause means 'an assembly rose up regarding this one matter.'
segments.s655-g155: چغانی is a nisba/epithet or proper name. گوی here is the noun 'speaker/sayer' (from گفتن) rather than 'sphere'. بود is the past copula 'was', linking the predicate to the following name; فرخ‌نژاد is a family name or epithet serving as the predicative identifier.
segments.s655-g156: جهانجوی is a compound noun 'world-seeker / ambitious one' describing him. پر + noun is an adjectival phrase meaning 'full (of)'; پر دانش = 'full of knowledge'. The sequence و بخش و داد lists qualities: بخش (here 'generosity, gift/grace') and داد ('justice'). The adjectives/nouns are coordinated to enumerate his virtues.
segments.s655-g157: خردمند is an adjective 'wise'. و links clauses. نامش is 'name' + the possessive clitic -ش ('his'), written separately in the tokenization. فغانیش here functions as a proper name ('Faghaniš') in the copular clause 'نامش ... بود'. بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s655-g158: که is the relative pronoun 'who/that', introducing a descriptive clause. با + noun = 'with' (instrument/possession); the repetition با ... و با ... is emphatic ('with treasure and with an army'). خوی + ش in the token list composes خویش 'his own/kin' (خوی 'kin/character' + -ش possessive). بود closes the clause as the past copula. Overall: 'who had treasure and his own army.'
segments.s655-g159: بزرگان is the plural noun 'the great ones / elders'. هیتال is the tribal/place name governing the following phrase. وخاقان is 'and khaghan' (the conjunction joined to the title); چین here denotes the country 'China' qualifying the khagan (خاقان چین = 'the Chinese khagan'). The phrase lists the dignitaries involved.
segments.s655-g160: به شاهی is a prepositional phrase 'to kingship / to the throne' (به + شاهی). خواندند is the past transitive 'they called/summoned' (here 'they proclaimed/appointed'); آفرین is an acclamation 'bravo/praise' that follows the verb. برو appears in the line as part of the verbal phrase (classical/poetic collocation) meaning roughly 'to (the) kingship' / 'to proclaim to kingship' — its exact literal sense is 'go', but here it is a fossilized element in the phrase 'به شاهی برو خواندند' meaning 'they acclaimed/installed as king.'
segments.s655-g1737: فلک (subject) appears first in poetic order and here means 'fate/the heavens' providing guidance; رهنمونش is رهنمون + ـش (possessive 'his/for him') meaning 'guidance' or 'guide for him'. به سختی is an adverbial phrase 'with severity/harshly' (به + سختی) and بود is the past copula 'was', so the clause literally says 'The heavens' guidance for him was severe.'
segments.s655-g1738: همه is the quantifier 'all/everything' as subject; بهر او is a poetic prepositional phrase 'for him' (بهر = for); شوربختی is the predicate noun 'misfortune', and بود is the past copula 'was'. Word order is topic–phrase then predicate, typical of Persian poetry: 'All, for him, was misfortune.'
segments.s655-g1739: گران‌تر اِست a comparative phrase 'heavier/more weighty' (گران + تر). چه دانی is an interrogative 'what do you know?' or 'do you know anything?', with دانی (2nd singular present 'you know') in an archaic/poetic form. بدو 'to him' precedes the verb, and گفت شاه places the verb 'said' before the subject 'the king' (inverted order typical of poetry): 'Do you know anything heavier? the king said to him.'
segments.s655-g1740: چنین is a discourse adverb 'thus/so'; داد is the past verb 'gave' and پاسخ is its object 'answer/reply' (چنین داد پاسخ = 'thus gave answer'). که introduces reported speech or a content clause 'that', and سنگ گناه is a noun phrase 'the stone of sin' (noun + noun in construct), with the copula omitted as is common in poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g1741: بپرسید is a past reporting verb 'he asked'. کز is the poetic relative 'which/of which', forming the question phrase 'کز برتری کارها' = 'which of superiority among deeds' (i.e. 'which is superior among deeds'). برتری is the abstract noun 'superiority' and کارها 'deeds' is its domain.
segments.s655-g1742: This continues the question: ز (poetic 'from/of') گفتارها 'from words' هم 'also' ز کردارها 'and from deeds/actions'. The repeated ز is the poetic genitive/prepositional marker 'from', so the clause reads 'from words as well as from deeds,' completing 'which is superior...'.
segments.s655-g1743: کدامست is the interrogative 'which is it?' or 'which one is it?' and is followed by two prepositional phrases joined by و: با ننگ و با سرزنش 'with shame and with reproach'. The repetition of با is emphatic/poetic, listing the two manners under consideration.
segments.s655-g1744: که introduces a relative/content clause. باشد is an archaic/subjunctive/present-form copula meaning roughly 'may be' or simply 'is' within the clause; ورا is the prepositional 'to him', هر کسی 'anyone/whomever', and بدکنش is a substantive/verb phrase 'does ill' or 'is an ill‑doer' (here meaning 'one who treats him badly'). The clause therefore says 'that to him anyone does ill,' i.e. 'whoever ill‑treats him.'
segments.s655-g1617: چنین گفت is a formulaic opening meaning "thus he said." کان کو is an archaic relative phrase (classical کآن کو) meaning "he who/that which," followed by خردمند نیست "is not wise;" word order is literary: verb (گفت) precedes the quoted relative clause.
segments.s655-g1618: توانگر کش is a compound in which کش functions as an agentive/relational element attached to توانگر to denote "the (one) wealthy/wealthy one." از marks source/possession here ("of/ by"), بخت فرزند نیست is a predication "is not a child/offspring of fortune" (i.e. not produced by luck); نیست negates the clause in present/literary sense.
segments.s655-g1619: بپرسید is the past (literary) form "he asked." شاه is the subject following the verb (common in poetry). از + دل مستمند means "about/of the afflicted/needy heart," with از marking the topic or source.
segments.s655-g1620: نشسته is a participial/adjectival form "seated/sitting" describing the heart; به گرم اندرون is a prepositional phrase where به functions as "in/to" (poetic use) + گرم اندرون "warm inner chamber"; بی‌گزند is an adjective "unharmed/safe," finishing the descriptive phrase.
segments.s655-g1621: بدو گفت = "he said to him" (بدو = to him). با دانشی پارسا is an instrumental/identifying phrase literally "with knowledge, a pious one," here functioning as an appositive: "a pious man of learning said to him." The adjective/nominal order is literary.
segments.s655-g1622: که introduces the content clause ('that' / 'so that'); گردد is a subjunctive/present-tense literary form meaning "becomes" or "will become." The sequence برو ابلهی پادشا is an inverted/poetic ordering meaning essentially "(becomes) a foolish king." (برو here is archaic/poetic in use and the whole phrase places predicate and noun in a compact, irregular order.)
segments.s655-g1623: بپرسید again is the past literary "he asked." نومیدتر is the comparative adjective "more hopeless/more despairing," followed by کس کدام ("which person?/who?") — a rhetorical question asking to identify the person.
segments.s655-g1624: که introduces the relative clause "who/that"; دارد is present "has/possesses" (3rd person); توانایی و نیک‌نام are the two things possessed: "ability (توانایی) and good repute (نیک‌نام)." The clause specifies which person is meant.
segments.s655-g81: This is a series of sources introduced by the poetic preposition ز (z) 'from' at the start; nouns are joined by و 'and'. وز here is the fused form و + ز 'and from' (common in classical verse) so the sequence lists origins (mountain, plain, sand, and a sound-word). Word order is coordinate noun phrases joined by و; شخ is an onomatopoeic word describing the sound or action of sand/dust.
segments.s655-g82: بجوشید is a past literary verb meaning 'boiled/surged' (verb before subject, a common poetic inversion). لشکر is the subject 'the army'. چو introduces a simile 'like/as', followed by the two simile terms مور و ملخ. The whole clause likens the massing troops to swarms.
segments.s655-g83: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بگذشت is a past narrative verb 'passed' with خاقان as its subject. برود is a classical narrative/past-form 'went' (verb before the following name); برک in this context is a proper name (a person accompanying the narrative) rather than the common noun meanings listed.
segments.s655-g84: توگفتی is the 2nd-person past 'you said' (narrative). همی functions as an adverb meaning 'continually/kept on' in classical usage. تیغ is the object 'sword(s)'; بارد here is not the adjective 'cold' but a verbal form (from باریدن) meaning 'may...rain' (optative/subjunctive) with فلک 'the sky' as its subject — the clause expresses a wish 'may the sky rain swords.' The supplied senses for بارد do not match this verbal use, so that sense is indicated separately.
segments.s655-g85: سپاه ('the host/army') is the topic; انجمن کرد is a compound verb 'made an assembly/gathered' (کرد as the light verb). بر introduces the purpose or occasion here with the nouns مای و مرغ: classical بر + noun can indicate 'for/with reference to' (i.e. for wine and birds/feasting). مای is the poetic word for 'wine'.
segments.s655-g86: سیه گشت is a past construction 'became dark' (گشت = 'became'); خورشید is the subject placed after the verb by poetic inversion. چون introduces a simile 'like/as', and پر چرغ is a genitive-like descriptive phrase 'the feather (or plume) of a lamp' — the sun is compared to that dim/sooty image. پر here means 'feather/plume' and چرغ is 'lamp'.
segments.s655-g87: ز بس is an emphatic classical expression meaning 'from so many' (an intensifier); here it introduces the abundant instruments of war. نیزه is 'spears' and وتیغهای is the fused form of و + تیغهای 'and swords' (تیغهای is the plural). بنفش describes the swords as 'purple' (a color adjective following the noun phrase).
segments.s655-g88: درفشیدن here is a verbal form referring to the action of displaying or raising banners (the exact classical verbal nuance is specialized in epic contexts). گونه گونه is a reduplicated distributive/adverb meaning 'of various kinds' or 'one kind after another'. درفش is the noun 'banner/standard' — the phrase means that banners of many kinds were shown. Word order places the verb nominal/participial form before its noun complement in poetic style.
segments.s655-g1345: ز روی ریا is a prepositional phrase: ز (from/of) + روی (face/appearance) + ریا (hypocrisy/ostentation) = 'by/for the sake of show'. هرچ (whatever) is a free relative modifying گرد آورد, where گرد آورد is a compound verb (گرد + آورد) in past/finished sense 'gathered' (third-person singular). Word order is typical Classical Persian: relative + verb after the modifying phrase.
segments.s655-g1346: ز صد سال بودنش is a temporal/measure phrase: ز (poetic 'from' / 'even with') + صد سال بودنش 'his being a hundred years' (بودن as verbal noun/infinitive 'to be' with possessive clitic ش). برنگذرد is a present/subjunctive-form verb (نَـــ + گزَرد root) used here with future/gnomic force 'does not / will not pass' — i.e. 'will not endure'.
segments.s655-g1347: شود is the simple present/subjunctive of شدن 'become' (here used twice to link coordinated predicates). خاک functions as the predicate noun 'dust/ashes' (خاک شود = 'become dust'). وبی‌بر (bi-bar) is an adjectival/nominal phrase 'without profit' modifying رنج اوی 'his toil/suffering'; the second شود attaches to that phrase to mean 'his toil becomes unprofitable/fruitless.' The pronoun اوی ('his') attaches to رنج.
segments.s655-g1348: به دشمن بماند is a short clause: به (to/for) + دشمن (enemy) + بماند (remain, third-person present/subjunctive/optative), meaning 'will remain to/with the enemy.' همه گنج اوی ('all his treasure') is the logical subject/object phrase; word order places the locative/verb before the possessed noun phrase, with اوی as the possessive clitic 'his.'
segments.s655-g1349: نه ... نه is the correlative negative 'neither ... nor.' نه فرزند ماند = 'no child/descendant remained' with ماند (remain) as the finite verb; the following نه تخت و کلاه repeats the negative particle and lists a coordinated noun phrase (تخت 'throne' and کلاه 'cap/crown'). The verb ماند is understood to govern the subsequent noun phrases by ellipsis.
segments.s655-g1350: The pattern continues: نه ایوان شاهی (no royal iwan/hall) uses ایوان ('iwan/porch, palace-portion') modified by شاهی ('royal, of kingship'). The final coordinated phrase نه گنج و سپاه ('nor treasure and army') completes the list; the negative نه scopes over each coordinated noun phrase while the verb ماند from the first clause is still understood.
segments.s655-g1351: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when.' بشنید is the past tense 'he heard' and آن is the demonstrative object 'that' (referring back to the preceding news). جستن here is a verbal noun/infinitive 'to seek / to strive' used after the main verb to indicate attempted action ('he sought/attempted'). و باد اوی pairs the conjunction و with باد used as an optative particle 'may' (not 'wind' here) plus the clitic اوی 'his/him' — a laconic optative wish (elliptical) aiming ill or misfortune at him.
segments.s655-g1352: ز گیتی is a prepositional phrase 'from/throughout the world.' نگیرد is present/subjunctive with negative prefix ن- on گیر 'take/seize' meaning 'will not take / will not keep.' کسی‌یاد اوی is the object phrase: کسی‌یاد (anyone's remembrance / 'anyone remember') plus the possessive clitic اوی ('of him'); word order places verb before its object, so the clause states 'no one in the world will take/hold his memory' i.e. 'no one will remember him.'
segments.s655-g985: که is the subordinating/complementizer 'that' introducing the clause. دادی is simple past 2nd-person singular 'you gave'. کسی از مهان جهان functions as the indirect object phrase 'someone/from the guests of the world' (کسی = 'someone'; از + noun = 'from/of'), placed after the verb by poetic word order.
segments.s655-g986: فرستاده را is the definite direct object ('the messenger') marked by را. آشکار here means 'openly' (an adverb) and is paired with the following ونهان, which is a poetic concatenation meaning 'and hidden/secret' (i.e. 'openly and secretly'). The object marker ties فرستاده به دادی earlier.
segments.s655-g987: همان نیز is an adverbial cluster meaning 'that same/likewise' or 'also'; it modifies the clause. یارانش is a noun with the possessive suffix -ش ('his companions'). را marks them as the definite object. هدیه داد is the verbal phrase 'gave a gift' with داد as past tense 'gave'.
segments.s655-g988: ز دینار و ز مشکشان is a poetic list meaning 'with/ in the form of dinars and (with) their musk'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from/with'. مشکشان contains the pronoun suffix -شان referring back to یاران (their musk). کرد شاد is 'made (them) happy' where کرد is the causative/do verb and شاد 'happy'; the object (them) is contextually the previously marked یارانش.
segments.s655-g989: همی‌رفت is the imperfective/past-continuous literary form 'he was going' (همی- marks continuous or habitual past). با دختر is a prepositional phrase 'with the daughter'. وخواسته is the conjunction و plus the participle/ adjective خواسته 'chosen/desired' functioning as a modifier that connects to the following noun phrase.
segments.s655-g990: سواران و پیلان آراسته is a coordinated noun phrase 'horsemen and elephants, adorned'; آراسته is a postpositive adjective 'adorned/ornamented' modifying both nouns, a common poetic word order (adjective follows the nouns).
segments.s655-g991: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'. تا introduces the extent 'as far as/until'. لب رود جیحون is a genitive noun phrase 'the brink/bank of the Jeyhun river' (لب = 'brink/bank'; رود جیحون = 'river Jeyhun'). کشید is the simple past of کشیدن 'he drew/extended/reached' used here in a poetic sense 'he went/reached' the river bank.
segments.s655-g992: به مژگان marks the target 'onto (his) eyelashes' (به = 'to/onto'). همی is the continuous marker (emphatic present/past progressive). از دلش خون کشید literally 'he drew blood from his heart', where از introduces the source, دلش is 'his heart' (دل + -ش), خون 'blood', and کشید is again the past 'drew' (a poetic image of grief causing bleeding to the eyelashes). Word order places the target phrase first for emphasis.
segments.s655-g601: نباید که is an impersonal negative modal meaning 'it should not be that / one should not'; it introduces a subordinate clause. پیروز گشته is a perfect participial phrase 'having become victorious' (پیروز = 'victorious', گشته = 'become/turned'), and به جنگ is a locative/goal phrase 'in/into battle' modifying how one became victorious. The sequence places the participial phrase before the main result clause introduced next.
segments.s655-g602: همه نامها is the subject phrase 'all the names / everyone’s reputations' (نامها here = reputations/fame). بازگردد is a 3rd-person singular present-subjunctive/formal-jussive verb 'return / should return' used after نباید که to state the undesired result. به ننگ is a prepositional phrase 'to shame / into disgrace' giving the destination/state of the return.
segments.s655-g603: ز هرگونه موبدان is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from/of all kinds of mōbeds (priests)'. خواستند is a past-tense 3rd-person-plural verb 'they requested/they asked/wished' and governs the preceding noun phrase as its object: 'they asked of every sort of priest.'
segments.s655-g604: چپ و راست is an idiom 'left and right' meaning 'on all sides / here and there / variously'. گفتند is the past 3pl 'they said' (reporting speech), and و آراستند is a coordinated past verb 'and they arranged / made ready / adorned'—together indicating they spoke in many directions and put things in order.
segments.s655-g605: چنین گفت خاقان is a fronted report 'thus said the khaghan' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said', خاقان = 'the khaghan/ruler'). که introduces the content clause; اینست راه is a verbless nominal clause 'this is the way' where اینست = 'this is' and راه = 'way/method.'
segments.s655-g606: که here is the complementizer 'that'. مردم is the direct object 'people' and فرستیم is 1st-person-plural subjunctive/jussive 'we send / let us send' expressing intended action. نزدیک شاه is a locative phrase 'near the king' specifying where the people are to be sent.
segments.s655-g607: به اندیشه در کار is an adverbial phrase 'with thought / by thinking regarding the matter' (به + اندیشه = 'with thought', در کار = 'in the affair/with respect to the matter'). پیشی کنیم is a 1st-person-plural jussive/subjunctive 'let us take precedence / let us be first' (پیشی = precedence, کنیم = 'let us do').
segments.s655-g608: بسازیم با شاه is a coordinated jussive clause 'let us make / establish (something) with the king' (بسازیم = 'let us build/make', با شاه = 'with the king'). وخویشی کنیم continues in parallel 'and let us make kinship' (وخویشی = 'kinship/relationship', کنیم = 'let us do'), i.e. 'establish familial or allied ties.'
segments.s655-g1585: بدو گفت کسری is an instance of classical Persian word order where the indirect object/oblique (بدو = 'to him') precedes the verb 'گفت' (said) and then the subject 'کسری' (Kisra); together it means 'Kisra said to him'. The following 'که آباد شهر' begins a subordinate/interrogative clause introduced by 'که' (that/which) describing 'شهر' (city) with the adjective 'آباد' (prosperous/inhabited).
segments.s655-g1586: کدامست is the interrogative 'which is it?'. The coordinating و links the two questions. ما is the subject 'we'; ز here is the poetic form of از ('from') often contracted before pronouns (زو = از + او) so 'ما زو' = 'what do we have from it'; چه is the interrogative 'what'; داریم is present indicative 'we have'; بهر is used as a noun meaning 'benefit/advantage' (not merely the preposition). The whole phrase means 'and what do we have from it as a benefit?'.
segments.s655-g1587: چنین داد پاسخ = 'thus gave an answer/ thus replied': چنین (thus) modifies the verb phrase. داد here is the verb 'gave' and پاسخ is the direct object 'answer/reply'; که introduces the content of the reply ('that/which'). آبادجای is a compound noun 'prosperous place/land' functioning as the topic of that content clause.
segments.s655-g1588: ز داد جهاندار is a genitive/agentive phrase 'by/from the justice of the sovereign' where ز = 'by/from' and داد = 'justice' (noun). باشد is the classical present-subjunctive/copular form often used to state existence or being ('is' / 'may be'); here it links the predicate to آبادجای. به پای is a prepositional phrase literally 'at the foot/base of' indicating dependence ('rests upon').
segments.s655-g1589: بپرسید کسری is past narrative 'Kisra asked' (in narrative style the verb can precede the subject). که introduces the dependent clause describing 'which (one)'. بیدارتر is a comparative adjective 'more awake/alert' modifying the omitted noun that follows (picked up in the next segment).
segments.s655-g1590: پسندیده‌تر مرد و هشیارتر contains two comparative adjectives. پسندیده‌تر is 'more liked/esteemed' (comparative of پسندیده) directly modifying مرد ('man'). The conjunction و links another comparative adjective هشیارتر ('more alert/prudent') which, by ellipsis, also modifies 'مرد' without repeating the noun.
segments.s655-g1591: به گیتی is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in the world' (به + گیتی). کدامست is the interrogative 'which is it?'. با من بگوی is a request/imperative structure meaning 'tell me' where با + pronoun (با من) functions as the indirect object 'to me' and بگوی is the subjunctive/imperative form of گفتن used to mean 'say/tell'.
segments.s655-g1592: که introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that/which'. بفزاید is the subjunctive/optative of 'فزودن' meaning 'may increase' or 'increases'. از is the preposition 'from/of'; دانشش is 'his knowledge' with the enclitic possessive -ش attached to دانش; آبروی is the noun 'reputation/renown' and is the thing said to increase. Grammatically this clause expresses the intended/resulting increase of reputation deriving from his learning.
segments.s655-g1297: کسی کو کند در زن کس نگاه is an older/classical word order: کسی کو 'anyone who' (کسی = anyone; کو here functions as a relative particle = 'who'), کند is the finite verb 'does/casts', and در زن کس نگاه is the prepositional object 'a look at another's woman' (در + زن = 'at a woman', کس after زن gives the sense 'another's' / 'someone else's'). Together: 'anyone who casts a look at another's wife'.
segments.s655-g1298: چوخصمش بیاید به درگاه شاه: چو (archaic) = 'when'; چشمش 'his eye' is fused here into the token; بیاید is the present/subjunctive/optative third‑person singular of آمدن 'to come' (= 'comes' / 'should come'); به درگاه شاه = 'before / into the presence of the king' (درگاه = royal court/presence). The clause means 'when his eye comes into the king's presence'.
segments.s655-g1299: نبیند مگر چاه ودار بلند: نبیند is the negative form of دیدن 'to see' and here carries the sense 'will see nothing' / 'shall not see', مگر = 'except'/'but', and چاه و دار بلند = 'the pit and the tall gallows' (ودار written together = و + دار, دار here = gallows). So: 'he will see nothing except pit and high gallows.'
segments.s655-g1300: که با دار تیرست و با چاه بند: که introduces an explanatory clause ('for'/'because'); classical contraction -ست on تیر (تیرست) is really تیر + است 'is/there is', so با دار تیرست means 'with the gallows there is (an) arrow(s)' (i.e. arrows used at the gallows/for punishment); و با چاه بند = 'and with the pit (there are) fetters' (بند = fetter). The clause explains the dangers: arrows at the gallows, fetters in the pit.
segments.s655-g1301: وگر اسب یابند جایی یله: وگر = 'if' (conditional); اسب یابند = 'they find/obtain a horse' or 'they find a horse' (یابند is subjunctive/indicative 3pl of یابیدن 'to find'); جایی یله = 'a place that is loose/secure enough' (جایی = somewhere/a place; یله = 'loose/unrestrained' meaning 'a spot where one can let the horse loose'). The whole conditional: 'if they find a loose place for the horse...'
segments.s655-g1302: که دهقان بدر بر کند زان گله: که introduces the result/relative clause ('where/so that'); دهقان = 'the peasant'; بدر here is the noun 'badr' = 'full moon' (classical usage often signals 'by night' or 'at full moon'); بر کند = بر + کند 'does/puts upon' but here functions as 'will take/drive off' (classical word order places بر before the verb); زان گله = 'that flock'. So: 'whereupon the peasant, at full moon/by night, will carry off that herd.'
segments.s655-g1303: بریزند خونش بران کشتمند: بریزند (3pl) 'they pour/spill', خونش = 'his blood' (خون + possessive -ش), بران = 'upon that'/'on him', کشتمند = 'the murderer' (compound noun). The clause: 'They will spill his blood upon that murderer.'
segments.s655-g1304: برد گوشت آنکس که یابد گزند: برد here functions as 'takes/carries off' (object-before-verb order is classical: 'گوشت برد' = 'takes the flesh'); گوشت = 'flesh/meat'; آنکس 'that person' (= 'the one'); که یابد گزند = relative clause 'who receives/suffers harm' (یابد = 'finds/receives', گزند = 'harm'). The whole: 'the murderer carries off the flesh of the one who is harmed.'
segments.s655-g481: خروش آمد is a simple past clause with the verb آمد ('came/rose') following the noun — ‘a roar rose/was heard’. و simply links the next noun phrase. ناله کرنای is a possessive/nominal phrase where ناله (‘wail/cry’) is qualified by کرنای (‘of the horn/trumpet’) — poetic word order places the lamenting noun before its modifier.
segments.s655-g482: هم here means ‘also/too’ and introduces an additional sound. از پشت پیلان is a prepositional phrase ‘from behind the elephants’ (از = from, پشت = behind, پیلان = elephants). جرنگ درای is a verbless descriptive phrase: جرنگ = ‘clanging/din’ and درای functions adjectivally/attributively (‘possessing/bringing’) so the sense is roughly ‘a clanging was heard’ coming from behind the elephants.
segments.s655-g483: تبیره زنان is a compound: the instrument-name (تبیره) followed by زنان, here a participial/agentive form meaning ‘those striking/striking (it)’ (i.e. the players). پیش بردند is past tense ‘brought forward/advanced’ (بردند = they carried/took forward) and سنج is the object (here a musical instrument, ‘cymbals/metal percussion’ in context).
segments.s655-g484: زمین آمد literally ‘the earth came’ — a poetic way to say the ground heaved/shook (آمد past). از سم اسبان is an ablative phrase ‘from the hooves of the horses’ (سم = hoof, اسبان = horses). به رنج = ‘with/into suffering/strain’ — an adverbial phrase describing the manner (the pounding caused the earth to heave painfully).
segments.s655-g485: شهنشاه is the subject ‘the emperor’. با introduces accompaniment ‘with’ and is followed by a coordination of companions/items: خود (‘himself’, emphatic), و گبر (‘and the gabr’ — here a companion/figure; the noun گبر can mean a Zoroastrian or an individual called ‘gabr’), و سنان (‘and the spear’ or the named lance/sinan). The list after با is simply coordinated by و.
segments.s655-g486: چپ و راست (‘left and right’) gives the directions. گردان و پیچان are active participles/adjectival verbal forms meaning ‘turning’ and ‘twisting’ and they modify/act on the following noun عنان (‘the reins’). Together they function as the verbal action: ‘turning and twisting the reins left and right.’ Word order places the participles before the object for emphasis (poetic style).
segments.s655-g487: فرستادگان is the subject ‘the envoys/messengers’. خواندند is past tense ‘they sang/voiced/uttered’ (here used for intoning an acclamation). آفرین is the exclamation they vocalize — an acclamation meaning ‘bravo/hallelujah/praise’.
segments.s655-g488: یکایک is an adverb ‘one by one’. نهادند is the past of نهادن ‘to place/set’ (they placed). سر بر زمین is the direct object phrase ‘head upon the ground’ (سر = head, بر = on/upon, زمین = ground) and describes a prostration/bowing action.
segments.s655-g977: خرد is the noun ‘wisdom’ placed before the verb as the (grammatical) object. گیرد is 3rd person singular present used optatively/jussive here (‘may he/one take’ or 'let one acquire'). از فر و فرهنگ اوی is a prepositional phrase 'from his far and farhang/culture' (فر = royal glory; فرهنگ = culture). اوی is the archaic possessive 'his/her', attached after the noun.
segments.s655-g978: بیاموزد is a 3rd person singular present with an optative/jussive nuance here meaning 'may he/one learn' (not 'teach'). آیین و آهنگ اوی are the objects 'his rites/customs and his melodies' (آیین = custom/ceremony; آهنگ = melody). The conjunction و links the two objects; اوی again marks possession 'his'.
segments.s655-g979: که here introduces the wish/clause ('that' / linking particle). بخت و خرد form a compound subject 'fortune and wisdom'. رهنمون is a predicative noun 'guide' that takes the possessor تو after it ('your guide' — رهنمون تو). باد is the optative verb 'may it be' (classical/poetic use of باد to express 'may').
segments.s655-g980: بزرگی و دانش is another compound (greatness and knowledge). ستون is the predicative noun 'pillar' (i.e. support) and تو follows it to indicate 'your pillar' (word order: predicate + possessor). باد again is the optative 'may it be'.
segments.s655-g981: نهادند is the past tense 3rd-person plural 'they placed/set' (here the verb, not the noun senses). مهر is the object 'affection/kindness'. از بر مشک چین is a locative/ablative phrase 'on/upon the musk-folds' (از بر marking location or source; مشک چین = 'musk-folds' or 'musk‑scented folds').
segments.s655-g982: فرستاده را is the definite object 'the envoy' (را marks the object). داد is the past 'gave'. و connects the two coordinated verbs. کرد آفرین is a light-verb construction where کرد 'made/did' combines with آفرین (an acclamation) to mean 'uttered/expressed praise' or 'cried “afarin!”'.
segments.s655-g983: یکی خلعت = 'one (a single) robe of honour' (یکی as an indefinite numeral). از بهر means 'for the sake/purpose of' (here 'for Mehrān'). مهران is a proper name. ستاد is a past verbal form here meaning 'he set/placed/presented' (classical past of ستادن/ستودن depending on nuance), so the clause says he gave/assigned one robe to Mehrān.
segments.s655-g984: بیاراست is a past/actional form 'he adorned/arrayed (him)'. کان functions here as an archaic linking/relative particle ('such that' / 'that'), introducing the following clause. کس ندارد به یاد literally 'no one has (it) in memory' — i.e. 'no one remembers its like'; کس = anyone/person, ندارد = 'does not have', به یاد = 'in memory'.
segments.s655-g113: بهشتم سوی غاتفر گشت گرد is a compact clause: بهشتم (به + ـم) functions as a directional/possessive phrase ('to/for my heaven' or 'my heaven' in context), followed by سوی 'toward' and the proper name غاتفر as the goal. گشت is the past of گشتن used here as 'turned/was turned' (become/turn), and گرد is an adverb meaning 'around/about' (indicating the turning motion). Word order is goal after the direction marker, then the verb and adverb, typical of classical Persian compression.
segments.s655-g114: سیه شد جهان چوشب لاژورد literally: 'black became the world, (as) when night (falls) lapis.' سیه شد uses the adjective سیه + شد (become) to say 'became dark.' جهان is the subject 'the world.' چوشب is a temporal phrase 'when night fell' (archaic compound), and لاژورد is a simile/complement ('like lapis‑lazuli/azure'); overall: 'the world became dark like lapis when night fell.'
segments.s655-g115: شکست اندر آمد به هیتالیان: شکست is a noun 'defeat' functioning as the subject/affected event; اندر آمد is a verbal phrase 'came in/occured' (a poetic way to say 'befell'), and به هیتالیان marks the target/recipient ('upon the Hitalians'). The verb-phrase order (noun + آمد) is common in Persian poetic narration.
segments.s655-g116: شکستی که بستنش تا سالیان is a relative clause describing that defeat. شکستی is the indefinite/remarking form 'a defeat'; که introduces the relative clause 'which/that'; بستنش is بستن + ـش (the verb 'to bind/close' with a third‑person object suffix) here meaning 'its binding/that bound them' (i.e. the effect that held/struck them); تا سالیان means 'for/up to years' — so 'a defeat that held them for years.'
segments.s655-g117: ندیدند وهرکس کزیشان بماند: ندیدند is the past negative 'they did not see' (classical negative form). وهرکس 'and whoever' begins the dependent clause; کزیشان is کز + ایشان meaning 'of/from them' ('whoever from among them'); بماند is the present/subjunctive/optative third‑person 'remains' or 'would remain' used here to introduce the hypothetical/anyone‑who construction.
segments.s655-g118: به دل در همی نام یزدان بخواند: به دل 'in/to the heart' (literally 'to the heart'); در همی is an adjectival/phrasing meaning 'in confusion / distressed' (همی here functions as a poetic particle often intensifying or forming fixed compounds), نام یزدان 'the name of God' is the object, and بخواند is the third‑person singular present/subjunctive form of خواندن used here to mean 'would call/utter/cry (the name)'. Together: 'would call God's name in his troubled heart.'
segments.s655-g119: پراگنده بر هر سویی خسته بود: پراگنده 'scattered/widespread' describes the state, بر هر سویی 'on every side/toward every direction' gives the spatial frame, خسته بود is 'was weary/tired' (بود in its past sense). The clause describes a widespread, exhausted condition all around.
segments.s655-g120: همه مرز پر کشته و بسته بود: همه 'all/the whole' modifies مرز 'border/frontier'; پر + کشته is the idiomatic 'full of slain' (پر here 'full (of)'), و بسته بود adds 'and was closed' (بسته 'closed/sealed' with بود as past auxiliary). The clause states that the frontier was filled with corpses and was shut/secured.
segments.s655-g1505: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین = 'thus', داد = past 'gave', پاسخ = 'answer'); که introduces a subordinate clause. دانش بهست is a nominal clause with بهست (classical/poetic form meaning 'suffices'/'is enough') as the predicate: 'that knowledge suffices.'
segments.s655-g1506: خردمند خود is 'the wise man himself' (خردمند = 'wise', خود = emphatic 'himself'). برجهان is used as a nominal/epithet meaning 'a tower/eminence over the world' and برمهست is the copular/predicative element (poetic) 'is a (tower/fortress)' — together: 'the wise man himself is a tower above the world.' Word order places the predicate after the subject in elevated poetic style.
segments.s655-g1507: که introduces a reported clause: 'that...'. دانا here means 'the wise/learned.' بلندی normally means 'height' or 'high rank' (the lemma list here does not show that sense), and نیازد (classical third-person present/past form of نیازیدن) means 'needs' or 'requires' — in context negative sense is implied from the following: 'the wise do not need high rank for (the sake of) treasure' (به گنج = 'for/with respect to treasure').
segments.s655-g1508: تن خویش را is an object phrase 'his own body' (here written as خوی + ش = خویش). را is the definite/object marker. دور دارد is a compound meaning 'keeps (it) far/away' (دارد 'has/keeps'). ز رنج = 'from suffering/hardship' — literal: 'he keeps his body away from suffering.'
segments.s655-g1509: The preposition ز ('about/from') marks the object 'نیروی خصمش' = 'the force/power of his enemy.' بپرسید is the past/formal verb 'asked' (third person), and شاه 'the king' is the subject placed after the verb in classical word order: 'The king asked about his foe's force.'
segments.s655-g1510: که begins the reported question/clause 'that/how...'. چون here functions as 'how' (classical register). جست is a past form of 'to leap/spring' (used figuratively 'when you spring into action'). خواهی is a modal/future-like element 'will you' or 'you will', and همی is an emphatic/continuative particle; دستگاه here means 'operation/arrangement/force.' Together: 'how, when you spring into action, will you conduct/arrange the operation?'
segments.s655-g1511: چنین داد پاسخ 'he gave such an answer' (as above). که introduces the content: 'کردار بد' = 'bad conduct/deed' and بود is the copula/past 'was' that follows: 'that the conduct was bad.' This segment sets up the judgment about behavior.
segments.s655-g1512: بود is the past copula 'was.' The noun خصم 'enemy' follows (classical order), and روشن‌روان و خرد are two adjectives/epithets modifying it: روشن‌روان 'bright‑souled/clear‑minded' and خرد 'wise' (here adjectival). The clause reads 'the foe was bright‑souled and wise.'
segments.s655-g1033: Word order is inverted for emphasis: سزاوار او جای (a place suitable for him) comes before the verb. شاه is the agent in final position; بگزید is the simple past ('chose'). او is the beneficiary ('for him') attached to سزاوار ('suitable for him').
segments.s655-g1034: بیاراستند is past 3rd plural of بیاراستن ('they adorned/arranged'). از پی + noun here means 'one after another/over the course of', so از پی ماه گاه means 'month after month' (ماه = month, گاه = time/occasion). The clause modifies the previous action (they continued to decorate it over succeeding months).
segments.s655-g1035: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'; it introduces a temporal clause. آگاهی ('news/information') is the grammatical subject and آمد is the simple past 'came'. به marks the indirect object 'to' (here 'to the Khagan'), followed by the title خاقان چین ('the Khagan of China').
segments.s655-g1036: ز is the poetic/prepositional form of از ('from'). The clause lists the sources of the news: ز ایران ('from Iran') و ز شاه ایران‌زمین ('and from the king of the Iranian realm'). The repetition of ز is a classical stylistic feature marking each source separately.
segments.s655-g1037: وزان is a compound preposition/conjunction 'and from that / from that joy' linking to what follows. شادمانی = 'joy/rejoicing'. به فرزند اوی places the beneficiary/target: به + فرزند = 'for/to the son', and اوی is the older possessive/pronominal form 'his' (here 'his son' or 'to his son').
segments.s655-g1038: شدن is the copular/auxiliary 'to become' used here as a finite predicate 'became'. شاد وخرم is a coordinate pair 'happy and merry' — note the surface token is fused (وخرم) but functionally it is و + خرم ('and merry'). به پیوند اوی 'at/with his union' uses به + پیوند ('bond, joining') + اوی ('his'), giving the reason/state: he became joyful at that joining.
segments.s655-g1039: بپردخت is a past 3rd-singular verbal form (پرداختن) meaning here 'dealt with' or 'subdued'; it governs the following coordinated place‑names. The list سغد وسمرقند وچاج is a series of direct objects connected by و, so the verb applies to all three regions.
segments.s655-g1040: به is the preposition 'to/for' introducing the indirect object; قجغار names the place (Kashgar). باشی here stands between the place and the verb as a title/epithet attached to the place (literally ‘the bashi of Kashgar’). فرستاد is the past 3rd‑singular transitive verb 'sent/dispatched' and تاج is its direct object 'crown' placed after the verb, yielding the order 'to X (the bashi of Kashgar) he sent a crown.'
segments.s655-g1385: A nominal relative clause: هران چیز 'those things' (هران = each/those + چیز = thing) is followed by the relative/archaic particle کان and the predicate بهره تن بود 'was the body's portion'. بهره تن is a genitive phrase ('bargain/share of the body'), and بود is the past copula placed at the end as usual in Persian.
segments.s655-g1386: روانش is 'his soul' (روان + possessive suffix -ش). پس از مرگ is a prepositional/time phrase 'after death' (پس از = after), and روشن بود is the adjectival predicate 'was bright/clear' with the past copula بود at the end.
segments.s655-g1387: ازین هر دو 'of these two' (از = 'of/from' + این written as ین here) + هر دو = 'both/each of the two'. چیزی ندارد دریغ is a negated existential-possession phrase literally 'he does not have anything of regret' i.e. 'he has no regret'. The negative ند- دارد construction places the verb before the noun دریغ as the affected noun.
segments.s655-g1388: The clause begins with که (a relative/conjunction 'that'), then بهر 'for' introduces reasons. نیامست‌گر is an archaic fused conditional: نیامست + گر (from نیامد گر) meaning roughly 'if/that he had not come' or 'he did not come' with conditional flavor; the subject is omitted. The final بهر تیغ 'for the sword' gives the second motive. The line is elliptical and lists two motives (gain and sword) which he did not come for.
segments.s655-g1389: کسی 'someone' + کو (classical relative particle here = 'who/that') + بود 'was' introduces a relative clause. بر خرد پادشا is a circumstantial phrase 'king with/by wisdom' (بر used to mean 'in/regarding' or 'by' here, followed by خرد 'wisdom' and پادشا 'king'). Word order places the copula after the nominal phrase.
segments.s655-g1390: روان را uses the direct-object/topic marker را attached to روان 'soul'. ندارد is the present/past-stem verb 'does not have' (here idiomatically 'does not allow/does not put'), and به راه هوا is a prepositional phrase 'onto the path of هوا' where هوا means 'desire/passion'. The whole means he does not let his soul be led along the way of desire.
segments.s655-g1391: سخن نشنو is an imperative negative 'do not listen to speech/words' (نشنو = 'do not hear/listen'). ازمرد 'of/from a man' (از = 'of/from' attached to مرد), and افزون منش is a noun phrase 'of greater/lofty origin/standing' with افزون ('more/greater') modifying منش (here 'origin/lineage' or 'standing').
segments.s655-g1392: که introduces a relative clause 'who/for' giving the reason. با + جان روشن is instrumental/'with a bright soul' (جان = soul/life, روشن = bright), بود is the past copula 'was', and بدکنش is a descriptive compound meaning 'bad-in-conduct' or 'ill‑behaved/evil-doer' functioning as the predicate describing that man.
segments.s655-g1529: This conditional clause lists four abstract nouns connected by و (and). اگر at the start is the protasis marker 'if'. The nouns are in their bare (indefinite) forms and simply juxtaposed: بخشش (generosity/donation), دانش (learning/knowledge), رسم (custom), داد (justice). The final و before داد is the usual coordinating conjunction linking the series.
segments.s655-g1530: خردمند is the agent noun 'a wise person'. گرد آورد is a light verbal compound meaning 'gather/bring together' (گرد + آوردن); here گرد is the particle/element that combines with آورد to make 'gather'. با + نژاد marks what the virtues are combined 'with' — نژاد = lineage/pedigree. The clause completes the protasis started by اگر: 'if (these things) a wise man gathers with his lineage.'
segments.s655-g1531: This is a nominal list (بزرگی 'greatness', افزونی 'increase/abundance', راستی 'uprightness/truthfulness') coordinated by و. The sequence names the positive qualities that will be discussed in the following clause.
segments.s655-g1532: همی‌گیرد is an older/formal present/imperfective form (همی- prefix + گیرد) meaning 'takes/assumes' or here better 'is taken away / is diminished' in context; it governs an ablative از marking source or cause. از خوی بدکاستی literally 'from a bad/baseness-disposition' — i.e. 'because of a base/deficient nature'. Together: '... are (or become) affected/diminished by a base disposition.'
segments.s655-g1533: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that/thereupon' (ازان + پس). بپرسید is the classical past/preterite third-person form meaning 'he asked' (notice the older -ید ending). کسری is the proper name 'Kisra' (Khosrow). ازوی 'from him' indicates the person Khosrow addressed or questioned.
segments.s655-g1534: This is the content of Khosrow's question. که‌ای is the classical interrogative/relative combination meaning 'who are you?'
