segments.s655-g1627: بپرسید is a second‑person (or narrative past) form of پرسیدن; in this context it functions as an instruction or narrative imperative ‘ask.’ ازو is the enclitic prepositional phrase ‘of/from him,’ and شاه نوشین‌روان is the title + epithet ‘the king, Sweet‑soul.’ The noun phrase follows the verb as is common in Persian.
segments.s655-g1628: که‌ای here introduces a relative clause ‘who is’ (که = that/who; the -ای functions as an archaic copular/relativizing element). مرد is the noun ‘man/person,’ and دانا و روشن‌روان are two coordinating adjectives ‘wise and bright‑souled.’ The clause describes the king named in the previous segment.
segments.s655-g1629: که دانی functions as a parenthetical/hortatory ‘for you know that…’ دانی is 2nd‑person present of دانستن ‘you know’ (used rhetorically). بی‌نام is a predicative adjective ‘nameless’ and آرایشست is a nominal predicate with the archaic copula -ست (‘is an adornment’). The clause asserts that lack of a name can be regarded as an adornment.
segments.s655-g1630: که once more introduces a subordinate clause. او is the 3rd‑person pronoun ‘he,’ and از در is a fixed prepositional phrase ‘from/of the door/gate/house (figurative).’ مهر و بخشایشست uses مهر ‘love/affection’ coordinated with بخشایش (forgiveness/mercy) and the archaic copula -ست on بخشایش to predicate the relation: he is of the gate/house of love and forgiveness.
segments.s655-g1631: بدو is the dative adverb ‘to him,’ placing the speech target before the reporting verb. گفت is the past/ narrative ‘said,’ and مرد is the speaker ‘a man.’ فراوان گناه is a noun phrase ‘(of) many sins’ (فراوان modifies گناه). The typical Persian ordering places the indirect object (بدو) and the verb before the subject (مرد) in poetic/narrative style.
segments.s655-g1632: This is the speaker’s self‑description: گنهکار is an adjective/noun ‘sinner/sinful,’ درویش is the social/ascetic identity ‘dervish/poor ascetic,’ and بی‌دستگاه is an adjectival phrase meaning ‘without means/support’ or ‘unassisted.’ Adjectives are listed after the noun or as predicates in this concise poetic clause.
segments.s655-g17: یکی چند بنشست با رای‌زن is an idiomatic clause meaning ‘a few sat down with the advisers.’ یکی چند is an elliptical quantitative phrase (‘a certain number / a few’), بنشست is the past singular of بنشستن (‘to sit down’), and با رای‌زن is a prepositional phrase ‘with the counsels/advisers’ (با + noun). Word order is Subject (implicit ‘some people’) + verb + accompaniment.
segments.s655-g18: همه نامداران شدند انجمن: همه (everyone/all) is the subject, نامداران (the famed/illustrious ones) describes who ‘all’ refers to, شدند is the past plural of شدن used here as ‘became / formed,’ and انجمن is the result noun ‘assembly/meeting.’ The structure is ‘All the famous men formed an assembly.’
segments.s655-g19: بدان دوستی را همی جای جست: here the prepositional-pronominal combination ب + دان functions as classical بدَان (‘to/for that’). دوستی را is a definite direct object marked by را (‘that friendship’), همی is an archaic continuous/affirmative particle (stative/iterative nuance), and جای جست literally ‘sought a place’ = ‘sought to establish/settle.’ The verb جست is past and takes the preceding phrase as its object/goal.
segments.s655-g20: همان از رد و موبدان رای جست: همان (‘that/same’) picks out the foregoing matter; از introduces the sources/people consulted; موبدان are the Zoroastrian priests (mōbeds). رای جست is a verbal phrase ‘sought opinion/counsel’ (رای = opinion/planning, جست = sought). The whole means ‘for that he sought the opinion of ... and the mobeds.’
segments.s655-g21: یکی هدیه آراست پس بی‌شمار: یکی (‘one / a certain person’) is the agent, هدیه آراست is a past verbal phrase meaning ‘adorned/prepared a gift’ (آراست used transitive: ‘set out / presented’), پس بی‌شمار is an elliptical continuation ‘afterwards, countless [others].’ The clause is compact and classical in style, implying many followed that example.
segments.s655-g22: همه یاد کرد از در شهریار: همه (‘all/everyone’) is subject, یاد کرد is a compound verb ‘made mention / commemorated / paid homage’ (یاد here used in a verbal sense with کردن), and از در شهریار = ‘at the gate/court of the king’ (در used as ‘door/court’). The clause says that all commemorated or presented themselves at the king’s court.
segments.s655-g23: ز اسبان چینی و دیبای چین is a list of items introduced by the poetic preposition ز (short form of از = ‘from’). اسبان چینی = ‘horses of China’ (چینی as adjectival origin), و links the next item, دیبای چین = ‘brocade/silk of China.’ The two genitive/attributive modifiers indicate origin or source.
segments.s655-g24: ز تخت وز تاج وز تیغ و نگین continues the enumerative list. Each ز (here merged with و as وز in the manuscript) functions like ‘and from’ (poetic repetition of the preposition). تخت = throne, تاج = crown, تیغ = sword, and نگین = gem; و between the last two is the ordinary conjunction ‘and.’ The clause is a string of accretive gifts introduced by repeated ’from.’
segments.s655-g569: به بالای سروست is an adjectival/locative phrase: به بالای + سروست locates or compares him with the cypress (literally “at/above the cypress”). The clause then continues elliptically with و هم زور پیل, where و هم = “and also,” and زور پیل is a noun phrase “the strength/might of an elephant” (the verb “has/is” is implied by the poetic style).
segments.s655-g570: به بخشندگی همچو دریای نیل is an adverbial simile: به بخشندگی marks manner/quality (“in generosity”), همچو introduces the comparison (“like/as”), and دریای نیل is the construct “the sea of the Nile” (i.e. the Nile as a vast sea) completing the simile.
segments.s655-g571: چو is the temporal conjunction “when.” برگاه is the subject “royal court/platform,” باشد is the copular verb in a poetic/present-subjunctive form “is,” and سپهر وفاست is the predicate nominal (“a sky/firmament of fidelity”) — word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect (verb before predicate).
segments.s655-g572: به آورد گه هم is an adverbial cluster: به آورد = “brings” (he/it brings), گه = “occasion/occasionally/sometimes,” and هم = “also/even”; نهنگ بلاست is a predicative phrase where نهنگ = “whale” and بلاست (poetic copula form) asserts that it is a calamity — i.e. “it sometimes brings forth even a whale (a great) calamity.”
segments.s655-g573: اگر begins a conditional “if.” تیز گردد = تیز + گردد is “becomes/turns swift” (گردد = become, in poetic/subjunctive-present form). بغرد appears as the noun/subject after the verb (poetic word order) — here understood as the thing/person that becomes swift — and چو ابر is a simile “like a cloud.”
segments.s655-g574: از آواز او marks the cause/agency “by/from his voice.” رام گردد is passive/subject-raising: رام = “tame,” گردد = “becomes/is made,” and هژبر at the end is the patient/subject (“Hezbar” as a personal name or epithet) — together: “Hezbar becomes tamed by his voice.”
segments.s655-g575: وگر = “but if”/“and if.” می‌گسارد is the present/progressive poetic form of a verb meaning “scatters” or “disperses/rousts” (the mi‑ prefix marks present habitual), and به آواز نرم gives the means/manner “with a soft/gentle voice.”
segments.s655-g576: همی is a habitual/emphatic particle meaning “always/still.” دل ستاند is verb-object order where دل = “heart” (object) and ستاند (from ستاندن) = “seizes/wins/steals” (here “wins hearts”). به گفتار گرم marks the means “by/with fervent/warm speech.”
segments.s655-g385: برفتند is the past tense, 3rd person plural verb 'they went/they departed'. The phrase follows a classical word order where the verb appears and is followed by the actor/quantifier هر ده ('all ten') and then the directional phrase برشهریار, where بر is the classical preposition indicating motion toward the king.
segments.s655-g386: This segment lists the things they carried or offered. ابا here functions as a (poetic/archaic) marker equivalent to 'with' introducing the list; the nouns follow in coordination: نامه (a letter), هدیه (gifts), and نثار (offerings). The repeated و simply joins the items; با before نثار is the standard preposition 'with'.
segments.s655-g387: چون here is a temporal/concessive conjunction (classical use: 'when'/'since') followed by دید (past tense 'he saw'). بنواختشان is a past-tense verb (with the object suffix -شان 'them') from نواختن with the b- prefix (classical preformative), meaning 'he caused (music) to sound' or 'struck up (music) for them'.
segments.s655-g388: ز is the poetic/clipped form of از ('from'/'about') introducing source or topic; خاقان is the title 'Khaghan'. بپرسید is past 3rd singular 'he asked/enquired'. The conjunction و links to بنشاختشان, a classical verb with the object suffix -شان meaning 'he recognised them' (here used as a single action following the question).
segments.s655-g389: نهادند is the past 3rd plural of نهادن 'they placed/put down'. The direct object سر ('their heads') comes next, followed by the locative/prepositional phrases پیش او ('in his presence/before him') and بر زمین ('upon the ground'), so the sequence is verb → object → location (classical ordering).
segments.s655-g390: بدادند is past 3rd plural 'they gave' (بدادن). پیغام is the head noun 'message' followed by the genitive phrase خاقان چین — 'the Khaghan of China' — where چین ('China') specifies which Khaghan. The whole phrase therefore means 'they gave the message of the Chinese Khaghan.'
segments.s655-g391: به چینی is the classical way to mark language—'in Chinese' (به + language). یکی is the numeral/indefinite 'one' or 'a certain' modifying نامه‌ای (an indefinite letter). برحریر is a compound prepositional phrase 'بر + حریر' meaning 'on silk' (i.e., written on silk). The ordering is manner/language → quantifier + noun → material.
segments.s655-g392: فرستاده here is a participle/adjective meaning 'sent' (or can be read as 'messenger' in other contexts); it qualifies the letter. بنهاد is a classical past form (root نهادن) meaning 'he placed/presented' (here: 'they put/presented'), followed by پیش دبیر 'before the scribe/secretary' where پیش means 'in the presence of/ before'. The structure is participle + placing-verb + destination.
segments.s655-g1233: و زانجا بیامد سوی طیسفون is a simple narrative clause: و (conjunction 'and') + زانجا ('from there') + بیامد (past tense 'came/advanced') + سوی (preposition 'toward') + طیسفون (place name 'Ctesiphon'). Word order is the expected Persian sequence: conjunction — adverbial — verb — direction — place.
segments.s655-g1234: زمین شد ز لشکر که بیستون is a compressed, poetic clause: زمین شد ('the earth became' or 'the ground was made') + ز لشکر ('from/by the host/army') + که بیستون (a simile: 'like Bisotun'). Note the predicate شد follows the noun زمین (typical Persian S–V). The preposition ز here marks cause or accompaniment ('by/with/from'), and که introduces the comparison.
segments.s655-g1235: ز بس خواسته کان پراگنده شد: ز بس ('because of so much / from so much') + خواسته کان (literally 'what was taken/desired from the mines' — کان here 'mine/ore') + پراگنده شد ('was scattered/laid bare'). In poetic diction the noun phrase 'خواسته کان' compresses 'that which was taken from the mines'; پراگنده شد is a stative result.
segments.s655-g1236: ز زر و درم کشور آگنده شد: ز (marker 'by/from') + زر و درم ('gold and dirhams/coins') + کشور آگنده شد ('the realm became full'). آگنده شد is a compound stative ('became filled'), and the preposition ز lists the material cause or source.
segments.s655-g1237: وزان شهر سوی مداین کشید: وزان (و + زان, 'and from that') + شهر ('city') + سوی مداین ('toward Madā'in') + کشید (past of کشیدن here 'drew/marched/sent forth'). In epic style کشید is used for movement/advance. The agent is implicit (the commander); the phrase means 'from that city he marched toward Madā'in.'
segments.s655-g1238: که آنجا بدی گنجها را کلید: که ('where/that') + آنجا ('there') + بدی (an archaic past-form here meaning 'gave/placed' — verb) + گنجها را (direct object 'the treasures', marked by را) + کلید ('the key'). Poetic word-order places the verb before the object and the complement; the sense is 'there he put/gave the key of the treasures.' Note: بدی is an archaic verbal form not listed in the provided nominal senses.
segments.s655-g1239: گلستان چین با چهل اوستاد is a noun phrase: گلستان چین ('the Golestan of China' — i.e., a Chinese garden or an ornamental retinue) + با چهل اوستاد ('with forty masters/experts'). با introduces accompaniment or the composition of the retinue; اوستاد is plural 'masters/teachers/experts.'
segments.s655-g1240: همی‌راند در پیش مهران ستاد contains two verbs in sequence: همی‌راند (imperfect/progressive 'was driving/leading') + در پیش مهران ('in front of Mehran') + ستاد (past 'he set up/established'). The line uses parataxis: he led (the retinue) and established a headquarters/camp before Mehran; ستاد here means 'set up' (established).
segments.s655-g329: 'وزان' is a fused poetic prepositional phrase (و + زَ آن) meaning 'and/from that' or 'by that', marking cause. 'بخت' is a noun 'fortune' and governs the following coordinated nouns 'پیروزی و دستگاه' ('victory and the court'); 'و' simply coordinates them. 'دستگاه' here means the royal establishment/court rather than a technical system.
segments.s655-g330: This is a locative-range phrase plus a participial adjective and the head noun: 'ز دریا به دریا' = 'from sea to sea' (ز = 'from', به = 'to/towards'), 'کشیده' is the past participle of کشیدن used adjectivally ('drawn/stretched'), and 'سپاه' is the noun 'army' governed by that participle ('an army drawn from sea to sea').
segments.s655-g331: بپیچید is the simple past of پیچیدن with the prefix بِـ, here 'turned' or 'changed direction', and precedes the subject (خاقان) in verb–subject order common in classical Persian. چو introduces a temporal clause 'when', and 'آگاه شد' is 'became aware' with شد functioning as the intransitive perfective/copular verb and آگاه as predicate adjective.
segments.s655-g332: 'به رزم اندرون' is a directional/prepositional phrase 'towards/into the battle's interior' (به = 'towards/into', رزم = 'battle', اندرون = 'inside'). The clause's core is 'راه کوتاه شد' = 'the road/way became short' (راه = subject, کوتاه = predicate adjective, شد = 'became'), i.e. the approach to the fighting was shortened or grew short.
segments.s655-g333: 'به اندیشه بنشست' literally 'he sat into thought' — بنشست (past of نشستن) used idiomatically for 'sat down to ponder/entered into deliberation' with به + noun marking the mental state. 'با رای‌زن' means 'with counselors' (با = 'with', رای‌زن = one who gives counsel); the phrase indicates he consulted with advisers.
segments.s655-g334: 'بزرگان لشکر' is the subject ('the great/leading men of the army'), 'شدند' is the past plural of شد used as a copula/auxiliary meaning 'they became' or 'they were', and 'انجمن' is the predicate noun 'an assembly/meeting' — idiomatically 'the army's leaders convened' or 'gathered in council.'
segments.s655-g335: 'سپهدار خاقان' is a genitival phrase 'the khagan's spahdar/commander' (title + possessor). 'به دستور گفت' means 'said by order' or 'declared by command' where به functions adverbially ('by') and دستور is 'order/command'; گفت is the past-tense verb 'he said' (here introducing reported speech).
segments.s655-g336: که introduces the content clause ('that'); 'این آگهی' = 'this news/information'; 'خوار' is an adjective meaning 'despised/worthless' applied to the news (here a descriptive qualifier), and the verbal phrase 'نتوان نهفت' is a classical negative ability construction: نتوان (could not / cannot) + bare past-stem نهفت (to hide) = 'could not be hidden' / 'cannot be concealed.'
segments.s655-g1769: به پاسخ is an adverbial phrase meaning "in answer / in reply." نگه‌داشتن is the infinitive "to keep / to keep watch," functioning as the content of the reply; گفت is the past-tense verb "said" and خشم is its subject (Anger). The word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: adverbial + quoted infinitive + verb + subject.
segments.s655-g1770: که introduces a subordinate clause meaning "that / for (the fact that)." از + بیگناهان (= ازِ بیگناهان) marks possession/association "of the innocent." بخوابند is a 3rd-person plural verbal form (subjunctive/jussive sense here: "may sleep / fall asleep"). چشم is the noun "eye(s)" and functions as the grammatical subject whose action is expressed by بخوابند; the phrase order is compacted for verse.
segments.s655-g1771: دگر means "the other / on the other hand." آنک is a classical relative pronoun "the one who / that which." بیدار داری is a verb phrase: بیدار (awake) is a complement and داری is the present-tense verb "you hold/keep" (here: "you keep [someone] awake"). روان is the direct object "the soul / spirit." Word order places the complement and verb before the object for poetic compactness.
segments.s655-g1772: بکوشی is a hortative/imperative-subjunctive form of کوشیدن meaning "strive / make an effort." The pronoun تو is emphatic and follows the verb ("you, strive"). در کارها is a prepositional phrase "in (your) affairs / matters." تا توان is a limit phrase "as far as (your) power/ability," where تا means "up to / as far as" and توان is the noun "ability/power."
segments.s655-g1773: فروهشته is a past-participial/adjectival form meaning "scattered / cast down." کین برگرفته is a participial phrase: برگرفته (taken/seized) governs کین (hatred/vengeance) and together modify امید ("hope seized by hatred"). The three words form a compact noun phrase describing the state of hope.
segments.s655-g1774: بتابد is a present-tense poetic form of تابیدن "to shine/beam," here "(may) shine / shines." روان is the subject "the soul / spirit." ز + و (written as separate items here) stands for از او "from it/him" (z = from; و represents old orthography of او), so روان زو = "the spirit from it." به کردار شید means "in the conduct/guise of a shid (love‑mad/lover)," where به indicates manner and کردار is "deed/behavior."
segments.s655-g1775: ز کار بزه is a prepositional noun phrase "from the act of sin / from sinful deed(s)" (ز = from). چند is an interrogative "how much / how many," and یابی is the present 2nd-person form "you find." مزه is the object meaning "taste / pleasure." The clause asks rhetorically how much pleasure one gains from sinful acts.
segments.s655-g1776: بیفگن is an older/poetic verb meaning "cast away / throw off." مزه is the object "the taste / pleasure." دور باش is an imperative construction: دور (far) + باش (be! imperative) = "be far (away)." از بزه is the prepositional phrase "from sin/fault." Together the segment commands: "throw away the pleasure; be far from sin."
segments.s655-g361: چو (written here fused as چوبشنید) + شنید is a temporal clause: 'when he heard'. خاقان is the subject in the nominative (the Khagan). ز + موبد is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from / on the part of the mobed' and سخن is the direct object 'speech/words'. Word order is classical: temporal clause 'when he heard' + subject + source + object.
segments.s655-g362: یکی رای شایسته افگند بن: یکی modifies رای as 'one / a single' counsel; رای here means 'plan / counsel'. شایسته is an adjective 'fitting / appropriate'. افگند is the past tense 'he cast / put forth / laid down' (classical poetic verb). بن here functions as 'foundation / basis' (i.e. 'laid as a basis'), so the whole phrase means 'he put forward a single fitting counsel as a basis.'
segments.s655-g363: چنین گفت is 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said' — classical past). با introduces the indirect object 'to' or 'with'; کاردان راه‌جوی is a compound nominal phrase: کاردان 'skilled/expert' qualifying راه‌جوی 'seeker / traveler' (i.e. 'an experienced/seeking guide' or 'a skilful traveler'). The sequence means 'he said thus to the experienced traveler.'
segments.s655-g364: که here introduces the indirect question/explanation 'that / asking what...'. این را places 'this' as the definite object (را marks it). چه بیند is an interrogative clause 'what will (he) see/think' — بیند is classical present/subjunctive/present-future 3rd person of دیدن. خردمند روی is a descriptive phrase 'the wise-faced' i.e. 'the wise man' (خردمند = wise; روی literally 'face' used adjectivally to mean 'one of such a face' = the wise). The clause thus reads 'what will the wise man see (regarding) this?'
segments.s655-g365: دوکارست is a compact predicate 'it is twofold / there are two matters' (دوکار + است). پیش اندرون is a locative phrase 'before / in the presence of the inner (court)' (پیش = before/in front of; اندرون = inner quarters). ناگزیر is a predicate adjective 'inevitable / unavoidable'. The phrase means 'there are two unavoidable things before the inner court.'
segments.s655-g366: که introduces an explanation or result clause 'that / namely'. خامش is 'silence / to be silent'; نشاید is a classical impersonal/formal negative 'it is not fitting / should not be' (present). بدن خیره خیر is a verbless, tersely ordered clause: بدن literally 'body' used idiomatically for a person; خیره 'staring / astonished'; خیر 'good' — together meaning 'it is no good to stand there stunned.' The clause as a whole says 'silence is not fitting, and there is no good in standing there amazed.'
segments.s655-g367: که begins the explanatory clause 'for / that'. آن را 'that (thing)' marked as definite object/topic. به پایان is an adverbial phrase 'to an end / in the end'. جز از رنج نیست is a negative existential: جز = 'except', از رنج = 'from/of suffering', نیست = 'is not / there is not' — together 'there is nothing (for it) except suffering' or 'in the end there is nothing but hardship.'
segments.s655-g368: به از بر پراگندن گنج نیست is a proverbial clause whose word order is compressed: به از (better than) + بر پراگندن 'to scatter / distribute' (پراگندن = to scatter/spread) + گنج 'treasure' + نیست 'is not / there is not'. Literally: 'There is no treasure better than scattering (it),' i.e. 'no treasure is better than giving / distributing wealth.'
segments.s655-g1377: دربرتری is a fused prepositional/nominal phrase (در + برتری) meaning 'for/with regard to superiority' placed before the noun it modifies. راه آهرمنست is a nominal clause where آهرمنست contains the archaic/fixed copula -ست (≈ است), literally 'is Ahriman' — together: 'the way of seeking superiority is Ahriman's.'
segments.s655-g1378: که here introduces a causal/explanatory clause ('for/because'). مرد پرستنده را is the object marked by را (the person hostile to whom follows), and دشمنست uses the same -ست copula to mean 'is an enemy' (predicate follows the marked object in classical order).
segments.s655-g1379: The clause begins with the predicate خنک ('refreshing/pleasant') followed by درجهان ('in/for the world' — here thought of as a circumstantial or evaluative phrase) and then the subject مرد پیمان منش ('a man of covenant/disposition'), where پیمان‑منش is a compound meaning 'one whose nature is fidelity/keeping his word.'
segments.s655-g1380: که here is a relative/introducing particle 'who/whose.' پاکی وشرمست is a nominal predicate saying 'purity and modesty are ...' with شرمست containing the copular -ست; پیرامنش is a locative/relational compound meaning 'around him' (پیرام + نش with the possessive -ش understood).
segments.s655-g1381: چو (here attached to جانش as چوجانش) functions as 'when' (temporal clause). جانش ('his soul') is subject, تنش را ('his body' with the object marker را) is the object, and نگهبان بود is the predicate 'was guardian' — subject + object + predicate order with بود as past copula.
segments.s655-g1382: همه زندگانیش is a quantified noun phrase 'all his life' (همه quantifies زندگانیش), and آسان بود is the predicate 'was easy' with بود marking past/state. Word order is noun phrase then predicate.
segments.s655-g1383: بماند (from ماندن with the prefix ب-) here functions as 'remained/was left' with the dative phrase بدو ('to/with him'). رادی و راستی are coordinated nouns 'generosity and uprightness' that are the thing that remained with him.
segments.s655-g1384: نکوبد is a negative-form verb (ن‑ + کوبد) meaning 'did not strike/befall' (classical literary usage), followed by the coordinated objects درکژی و کاستی 'crookedness and lack/defect.' The negative prefix attaches to the finite verb to deny that these evils affected him.
segments.s655-g545: This clause gives the source/content: ز (a poetic preposition 'from' or 'concerning') + پیغام 'message' with the generalizer هرچش 'whatever' placed after it; به دل بود is the copular clause 'was in the heart' (بود = past third‑person of 'to be'). The final نیز 'also' links this clause to the next clause (parallel 'also' structure). Word order is topical: source + content + locative/copula.
segments.s655-g546: This clause states the action: به گفتار 'in words' (به + گفتار, here 'in/by speech'), بر نامه 'upon/to the letter' (بر + نامه), followed by the past verb بفزود 'he added'. The final نیز repeats the 'also' linking the two parallel acts (what was in his heart was also put, in words, upon the letter).
segments.s655-g547: بخوبی is an adverbial phrase 'in good/ proper manner' modifying the verb برفتند (past, third person plural 'they departed/left'). ز ایوان شاه is a prepositional phrase 'from the king's iwan' (ز + noun + possessor follows the noun in Persian).
segments.s655-g548: ستایش‌کنان is an adverbial participle ('praising', formed with -کنان) describing the manner of action; برگرفتند is past 3pl 'they took up' or 'they set out upon' and راه is the object 'the road/way'. Persian allows the participial adverb before the verb to indicate simultaneous action.
segments.s655-g549: رسیدند is past 3pl 'they arrived'. پس here is temporal 'then/afterwards'. پیش خاقان چین is the prepositional phrase 'in the presence of the Khagan of China' (پیش = 'before/in the presence of'; خاقان چین — 'Khagan of China', with چین specifying the khagan's domain).
segments.s655-g550: سراسر زبانها is a nominative/topical phrase 'throughout the tongues' (i.e. 'from every tongue'); پر از is the idiom 'full of' and آفرین is the noun 'praise/acclamation'. The whole predicate means 'every tongue was full of praise.'
segments.s655-g551: جهاندیده is an epithet 'world‑wise' or 'experienced' modifying خاقان 'Khagan'. بپردخت is past 3rd person singular (classical form of پرداخت/پرداخت) meaning 'he bestowed/paid/assigned' and جای is the direct object 'a place/seat' — thus 'the world‑wise Khagan allotted a place.'
segments.s655-g552: بیامد is past 'came'; برتخت is a fused prepositional phrase 'on the throne' (بر + تخت); او before the noun is the third‑person pronoun 'his' (poetic placement of possessive pronoun before the noun), and رهنمای is 'guide/adviser'. Together: 'his guide came to the throne.'
segments.s655-g921: وزان پس is a compound temporal connective meaning “and/from that, then/after that.” It introduces the subsequent clause. یکی گنج آراسته is a noun phrase: یکی (=a certain/one) modifies گنج (treasure) which is described by the adjective/past-participle آراسته (adorned), with the adjective following the noun in the classical poetic order.
segments.s655-g922: بدو is the clitic/prepositional phrase ‘to him/for him’ marking the beneficiary. در + ز + هر گونه‌ای together mean ‘from/among every kind’ (در ‘in/among’, ز ‘from’, هر گونه‌ای ‘every kind’). خواسته is a past participle/nominal form ‘requested/desired’ that completes the verb idea: ‘he requested (خواسته) [one treasure] for him from every kind.’
segments.s655-g923: This segment is a simple enumerative list introduced by repeated ز (=‘from/of’), listing types of wealth: ز دینار (‘of dinars’), ز گوهر (‘of gems’), طوق (‘collar/necklace’), تاج (‘crown’). The repeated ز is a poetic device to mark each item in the list.
segments.s655-g924: همان here functions as a demonstrative/appositive ‘namely/the same,’ introducing a specification of the foregoing list. مهر پیروزه is a compound noun phrase ‘a (turquoise) seal/medallion’ (مهر used in the sense of a stamped/ornamental piece) and تخت عاج is ‘an ivory throne,’ with the adjective-like nouns following the head nouns in the classical order.
segments.s655-g925: یکی دیگر = ‘another/one other.’ ازعود هندی literally reads ‘of/from (az) oud (agarwood/oud) Indian,’ i.e. an item made from or associated with Indian oud; هندی is the adjective ‘Indian.’ به زر is a prepositional phrase indicating the material/embellishment ‘with gold’ (here the particle به is used in the sense ‘by/with’).
segments.s655-g926: برو بافته is a compound verbal/adjectival phrase meaning ‘woven/embroidered (بافته) with brocade or gold-work’ — برو here is part of the compound describing the manner/material of weaving (an archaic/poetic collocation). چند گونه گهر follows as an adjectival quantifier ‘several/kindly kinds of gems’ (چند ‘several’ modifying گونه ‘kinds’, with گهر ‘gems’ as the head).
segments.s655-g927: ابا here is a poetic spelling equivalent to با (‘with’) and introduces accompaniment — ‘with each one…’. هر یکی = ‘each one,’ and افسری شاهوار is a noun phrase ‘a diadem/royal helmet (افسری) of regal/lofty character (شاهوار).’ The sequence thus means ‘with each [treasure] a regal diadem.’
segments.s655-g928: This is a numeric and purpose phrase: صد اسب و صد استر = ‘a hundred horses and a hundred pack-animals/mares’ (classical Persian places the numeral before the noun). به زین و به بار is a pair of prepositional phrases ‘for the saddle and for the load,’ i.e. some are saddled (برای سواری) and some are for carrying burdens (برای بار).
segments.s655-g417: فرستاده راجایگه ساختند is a compact poetic clause: فرستاده is the direct object 'the messenger' (sense: messenger). راجایگه here functions as را + جایگه 'a place/seat for (him)', so the compound means 'made a place for the messenger'. ساختند is past tense, 3rd person plural 'they made/constructed'. The object precedes the verb in the normal Persian order, and را signals the definite object.
segments.s655-g418: ستودند بسیار و بنواختند contains two coordinated actions. ستودند is past 3pl 'they praised' and بسیار (postverbal adverb) intensifies it 'very/greatly'. و links the next verb; بنواختند is past 3pl 'they played/struck' (instruments). The verbs are simple past, third-person plural, in a paratactic sequence typical of epic narrative.
segments.s655-g419: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. خوان و می is a coordinated noun phrase 'the feast/table and the wine' (خوان = banquet, می = wine). آراستی is a past/ephemeral form meaning 'was arranged/was set' (poetic past), so the clause reads 'when the feast and wine were set'. میگسار follows as the nominative 'the cupbearer' (the agent who acts next).
segments.s655-g420: This clause is poetically inverted: فرستاده را (the messenger + object marker) precedes the verb راخواستی, which is an archaic/past 3rd‑person form meaning 'asked for/wanted (him)'. شهریار at the end is the subject 'the king' (delayed for emphasis). So the literal order is 'the king asked for the messenger.'
segments.s655-g421: ببودند is an epic/archaic variant of بودند 'they were' (past, 3rd person plural). یک ماه is a duration adverbial 'one month'. نزدیک شاه is a locative phrase 'near the king' (نزدیک = near). The clause reads 'they were (stayed) one month near the king.'
segments.s655-g422: This gives the specific locations with repeated locative marker به 'to/at': به ایوان بزم 'at the banqueting iwan/hall' و به نخچیرگاه 'and at the hunting-ground'. The repeated به marks the two separate places where they remained.
segments.s655-g423: یکی here is the indefinite 'a certain' / 'one' (poetic) introducing an event. بارگه 'court/pavilion' is the thing built, and ساخت is past 'built/made' (3rd person). روزی functions as a temporal adverb 'one day' (poetic usage). به دشت is the locative 'in the plain.' The order places the indefinite and object before the verb, a common narrative pattern.
segments.s655-g424: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from/through' indicating cause; گردسواران 'the cavalry/horsemen' are the causal agent. هوا تیره گشت is a short clause 'the air became dark' (هوا = the air/sky, تیره = dark, گشت = became). Together: 'the air grew dark from the horsemen.'
segments.s655-g1009: زآیین (z-āyīn) is a poetic fused prepositional phrase meaning 'by/according to the custom/rite' and comes first as an adverbial phrase. که introduces a relative clause modifying 'آیین' ('the custom that...'). بستند is a past-tense, 3rd-person-plural verb ('they closed/blocked'); بر + noun (بر شهر و دشت) is the usual postposition phrase 'on/upon the city and plain' showing the object/target of بستند.
segments.s655-g1010: براهی is a fused prepositional phrase 'بر + راه' (here 'on/along the road'); که again introduces a relative clause ('which/that'). لشکر is the subject of the relative clause and همی‌برگذشت is a poetic imperfective/past progressive 'kept passing' (همی‑ marks continuous aspect) — together 'on the road which the army was passing along.'
segments.s655-g1011: وز ایران is an adverbial phrase meaning 'from Iran' (وز is an archaic/poetic variant of 'از'; here the provided senses did not include 'from', so -1 is returned). همه کودک و مرد و زن is a noun list with همه ('all/every') fronting the enumeration 'child, man and woman' — a typical Persian coordination where و connects items.
segments.s655-g1012: به راه = 'onto the road/toward the road' (به as directional preposition). بت چین here reads as two nouns in apposition: بت 'idol' + چین 'Chin/China', i.e. 'toward Chin (the land associated with idols)' — they 'شدند' (past plural of شدن) meaning 'became/assembled' and انجمن is the result noun 'an assembly/meeting.' The verb شدند is used intransitively to indicate they gathered or took that course.
segments.s655-g1013: ز بالا بر ایشان is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'from above upon them' (ز = 'from', بالا = 'above', بر = 'upon', ایشان = 'them'). گهر ریختند is a verb phrase: گهر 'jewels/gems' is the direct object and ریختند is past‑tense plural 'they poured/shed' (classical past of ریختن). The order (adverbial → object → verb) is normal in Persian poetry.
segments.s655-g1014: به پی here means 'along/at the footsteps/track of' (پی as 'foot/track'); زعفران و درم are the items scattered ('saffron and dirhams/coins'). بیختند is a problematic form in the lemma list (the provided senses do not match a clear verbal sense), but in context it functions as a past-tense verb meaning 'they scattered/strewed' — sensical reading therefore uses the given nouns as objects and the verb as the action performed.
segments.s655-g1015: برآمیخته is a prefixed participle/adjective meaning 'mingled/combined' and modifies the following noun phrase طشتهای (the plural 'basins/bowls'). خلوق in the manuscript likely intends 'خلائق' (the people/creatures), but the provided senses do not include that meaning, so a direct sense mapping is not available; syntactically this whole noun phrase is an ablative/agentive condition describing what was mixed.
segments.s655-g1016: جهان پرشد از expresses a result: جهان ('the world') + پرشد (past tense 'became full/was filled') + از ('with/from'). ناله کوس و بوق is the agent/content of filling: ناله 'lament/cry' followed by the instruments کوس (war‑drum) and بوق (horn/trumpet). The ordering 'جهان پرشد از ناله ...' is a common Persian construction meaning 'the world was filled with ...'.
segments.s655-g1289: This is a relative clause starting with the indefinite relativizer هرآنکس 'whoever'. که introduces the clause. از کار is a prepositional phrase 'from/of work' and دیده‌ست is the perfect (دیده است written in the poetic contracted form) meaning 'has seen/experienced'; رنج is the object 'suffering/hardship'. Word order places the perfect before its object (typical in poetry: دیده‌ست رنج = 'has experienced hardship').
segments.s655-g1290: نیابد is a negative present/subjunctive-form used impersonally/third-person to mean 'does not/shall not find' (classical/poetic usage). به اندازه is a prepositional phrase 'to/according to the measure/extent (of)...' and links to the following noun phrase رنج گنج 'suffering — treasure' giving the comparison 'a treasure equal to the suffering'.
segments.s655-g1291: بگویند is a narrative/indirect-report verb in a plural form used in epic diction ('they say' / 'it is said'); یکسر is an adverb 'altogether/at once'; به سالار بار is a prepositional phrase 'to the lord/master of the بار' — here سالار is the title 'chief/master' and بار refers to the charge/responsibility (i.e. the treasury or its burden). Together this segment frames reported speech addressed to the treasurer.
segments.s655-g1292: کز begins a content clause 'that/from whom' (relative/conjunction), آنکس 'that person' is the head of the following relative idea, and کند is the present/subjunctive 3rd‑person of کردن used here as 'should do' or 'does' in reported instruction. مزد او خواستار is a noun phrase 'one who asks for his wage' (مزد = wage/pay, او = his, خواستار = petitioner/one who requests). The whole clause specifies whom the treasurer is to deal with.
segments.s655-g1293: وگر is the conditional 'if' (or 'but if'); فام here stands for 'reputation/trace' (poetic use), and خواهی is the 2nd‑person present/future 'you want/seek'. بیاید is a present/subjunctive/optative 'it comes/it will come' (poetic form), and ز راه is the source phrase 'from/along the road'. The sequence expresses a conditional: if you seek fame, it will come along the road.
segments.s655-g1294: درم is the small coin 'dirham' (here a metonym for a small payment); خواهد is the verbal root 'will/wants' (here 'will demand' or 'will require'); از مرد is 'from a man/person' and بی‌دستگاه is an adjective 'uncontrived/unassisted/simple'. Together: 'he will require a dirham from the simple man.'
segments.s655-g1295: نباید is an impersonal modal 'one should not/it ought not' (here giving a general rule); که introduces the subordinate clause 'that he should find' and یابد is the present/subjunctive 'find/obtain'. تهیدست is an adjective/noun 'needy/poor person', and رنج is the object 'suffering/hardship'. The construction says what the poor ought not to expect to obtain.
segments.s655-g1296: This is a relative/explanatory clause introduced by که. گنجور is 'treasury/treasure-house' (an agentive/locative noun), فامش = 'its fame/trace' (فام + possessive ـش), and بتوزد is an archaic/poetic 3rd‑person present-form verb (the prefixed ب- is common in epic diction) meaning roughly 'sends out' or 'casts forth'. ز گنج 'from the treasure' gives the source. The clause states that the treasury-house sends forth its reputation from the treasure itself.
segments.s655-g1065: This is a compact past-time clause: وزان پس (poetic compound) functions as 'from that time / after that'; بزرگان is the subject 'the great ones / nobles'; شدند is the past plural of شدن ('they became') used here to predicate a change of state; انجمن is a predicate noun 'an assembly/meeting' — together شدند انجمن = 'assembled / convened (lit. became an assembly)'.
segments.s655-g1066: از ... تا sets a spatial range 'from ... to ...'; آموی and شهر چاچ و ختن are the endpoints. تا is used as 'as far as / up to'; شهر qualifies چاچ as 'the city of Chach'; the final و links the two place-names. Note these are toponyms in context (classical Persian place names).
segments.s655-g1067: بگفتند is the simple past 'they said' introducing a reported clause; کاین is the fused subordinating 'که + این' meaning 'that these'; شهرهای is the plural noun 'cities' and فراخ is the adjective 'spacious' placed after the noun, a normal classical/poetic adjective order.
segments.s655-g1068: پر از is a fixed phrase 'full of' (پر followed immediately by the preposition از), followed by a coordinated list of contents: باغ, میدان, ایوان, کاخ. The coordinating و links the items; the whole phrase is the complement of 'said (that) these cities were full of...'.
segments.s655-g1069: This is another از ... تا range: the poetic preposition ز (variant of از) begins 'from', تا gives the end point 'to/as far as'. The sequence lists place-names (چاچ, برک, سمرقند, سغد) joined by و. Note: برک here is used as a place-name in context (not the common noun senses).
segments.s655-g1070: بسی here functions as an adverbial intensifier 'much / many' (classical usage), followed by the past copula بود 'was/lay' and the predicate adjectives ویران 'ruined' and آرام جغد 'quiet / still like an owl'. Grammatically, the adjectives follow the copula; آرام modifies جغد (a poetic simile: the silence of the owl).
segments.s655-g1071: A simple coordinate list of place-names joined by و: چغانی, وسومان, وختلان, و بلخ. Each item is a toponym (districts or cities); the leading و on some items is simply the conjunction repeated in sequence (classical listing).
segments.s655-g1072: شده is the past participle/perfect of شدن used here with روز to mean 'the day(s) had become'; بر marks the adverse effect 'upon/for' هر کسی ('each/every person'); تار و تلخ are two coordinated adjectives 'dark/gloomy and bitter' describing those days. Word order places the verb form (شده) before the subject-like noun روز in typical classical phrasing.
segments.s655-g217: بریشان is the contracted classical form بر ایشان ('upon/for them') serving a dative/benefactive sense. سزد is an impersonal present/past-modal 'it befits/they deserve'. هرچ آید ز بد is a relative clause ('whatever comes from bad'), with آید (present/habitual/historical present of آمدن) meaning 'happens' and ز introducing the source 'from/through'. The whole segment states that ‘for them it is fitting/they deserve whatever bad happens’.
segments.s655-g218: هم 'also' links this clause to the previous one. از شاه گفتار نیکو is a prepositional-phrase (از = 'from') with گفتار نیکو 'good speech' as its object; the clause-final سزد repeats the impersonal 'it befits'. The order (source + noun + adjective + predicate) is typical of poetic/classical Persian where the copular/comment follows the phrase it comments on.
segments.s655-g219: ازیشان is بر/از + ایشان ('from/among them'), placed frontally. اگر نیستی is a conditional 'if there is no / if there be no' (نیستی here denotes nonexistence/lack). کین و درد are coordinated nouns 'hatred/vengeance and grief/affliction'. Together the segment reads 'if among them there is no hatred and grief'.
segments.s655-g220: جز 'except' introduces the limiting phrase جز از خون آن شاه آزادمرد — 'except from the blood of that noble king'. از is the source-marker 'from', خون is literal 'blood' (here meaning the bloodshed of the king), آن شاه آزادمرد is the noun phrase 'that noble/free man king' specifying whose blood.
segments.s655-g221: بکشتند is a past-tense transitive verb 'they killed' (suffix shows 3rd-plural). پیروز را uses را as the definite direct-object marker; here پیروز is the object (a proper name). ناگهان is an adverb 'suddenly' placed after the object, a common poetic order with the verb up front.
segments.s655-g222: چنان is a demonstrative/qualifier 'such/so', modifying شهریاری which in this context names the ruler/sovereign. چراغ جهان is a nominative-like apposition 'lamp/light of the world' (a lofty epithet). The phrase is a poetic apposition describing the person they killed.
segments.s655-g223: مبادا is an optative/negative wish 'may it not be / lest'. It governs the subjunctive-clause که باشند یک روز شاد, with باشند (subjunctive/present) 'they be' used impersonally here to mean 'there be'. یک روز شاد = 'one happy/joyful day'. The clause as a whole is a wish forbidding the occurrence of such a day.
segments.s655-g224: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/when'. هرگز نخیزد is negative-subjunctive 'may never arise/begin' (نخیزد from خاستن/خیزیدن used here as 'arise' or 'come into being'). ز بیداد داد places داد ('justice') as arising 'from' (ز) بیداد ('injustice/oppression'), i.e. 'that justice should never rise from oppression.'
segments.s655-g761: رسید is a past tense verb 'arrived/reached' placed at the start (poetic inversion). این فرستاده is the noun phrase 'this messenger/envoy' following the verb; به introduces the destination or recipient. Here the prepositional phrase به آفرین names the place or person reached (آفرین functions as a proper name or vocative in context).
segments.s655-g762: This is a nominal apposition/epithet describing the addressee: ابا is attached to the preceding name/title (here treated as part of the ruler's name/title), followed by an adjectival phrase گرم گفتار 'warm/ardent of speech' modifying the title خاقان چین 'Khagan of China' (خاقان governs the genitive چین 'of China'). Word order is name/title → modifier → title, typical of epic diction.
segments.s655-g763: شنیدم is first-person past 'I heard.' ز is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'concerning' and links the source phrase that follows; پیوستگی here means 'continuity' or 'succession,' and هرچ گفت is a noun phrase 'whatever was said' (گفت used nominally ≈ 'speech/what was said'). The whole segment thus frames the source of the narrator's knowledge.
segments.s655-g764: ز پاکان 'by/from the pure (people)' names the speakers; که introduces a relative/content clause 'that/which': او is the third‑person pronoun 'he,' دارد is a present/habitual 'has/keeps,' اندر is the preposition 'in/within,' and نهفت is an adjective/participial form 'hidden/concealed.' Grammatically the clause says that he keeps (or has) those matters hidden inside himself.
segments.s655-g765: مرا شاد شد دل shows a typical Persian inversion: دل 'the heart' is the grammatical subject, شاد شد is the past 'became glad,' and مرا (literally 'me' or 'my') marks possession/affected person ('my heart'). زپیوند تو is a prepositional phrase 'because of/from your bond/union' giving the cause of the joy.
segments.s655-g766: بویژه is an adverb 'especially.' ز is the preposition 'because of/from,' پوشیده 'hidden/secret' modifies فرزند 'son/descendant,' and تو is the possessive 'your.' The noun phrase asserts that it is especially the (your) hidden son that causes the special joy.
segments.s655-g767: فرستادم is the first‑person past 'I sent.' اینک is the adverb 'now/behold' and frames the action as current; یکی هوشمند is the object 'a/someone wise' (یکی used as 'a certain' or 'one'). Word order places the verb first (poetic) and then the noun phrase object.
segments.s655-g768: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that': دارد is 'has/possesses,' خرد is the abstract noun 'wisdom' or 'judgment,' جان او را is the object 'his life' with را marking the definite object, and ببند is a verb 'bind/secure/fasten' (here best read as the action the wise man is to undertake or is capable of undertaking). Syntactically this clause qualifies the sent man by his capacity to 'bind' or secure that life.
segments.s655-g993: همی‌بود is a past/habitual modal/iterative construction meaning 'was (continually)/was about to' (here: 'was on the point of'). تا introduces the clause that follows ('that' / 'so that' / 'until'); رود in this line is used as an archaic/verbal sense ('that he go') rather than the noun 'river' and functions as the subordinate verb; بگذاشتند is the past plural of گذاشتن with the causative/letting sense 'they let/they set free'. Grammatically this whole piece reads as a past habitual or immediate-past-periphrasis: 'they were about to let (him) go.'
segments.s655-g994: ز خشکی is a simple prepositional phrase 'from the dry (place/ground)' (ز = 'from'); بران is an adverbial/locative particle 'thereon/toward that' linking back to the previous reference; روی here means 'face' (literal or metonymic for 'countenance'); برداشتند is past plural 'they lifted/took up' and governs the object 'روی' (they lifted their faces toward that).
segments.s655-g995: ز جیحون is a locative/source phrase 'from Jeyhun' (the river); دلی is a noun used here as 'a heart' (possessive/qualifying), followed by the adjective phrase پر زخون 'full of blood' (پر = 'full (of)') which modifies دلی. بازگشت is the simple past third-person singular 'he returned.' The word order is typical Persian: source/place → noun phrase → predicate verb.
segments.s655-g996: ز فرزند 'from (his) son' is another source phrase; با درد is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with pain' (با = 'with'); انباز is an archaic/uncertain adjective or state-marker (here left untranslated as a specialized term), and گشت is a past intransitive 'became/turned' (he became/was made). Together the clause says he came back from his son in pain and became/was made anbāz — an old poetical state word that requires contextual glossing.
segments.s655-g997: جو آگاهی آمد is a noun phrase + verb 'news/information came'; جو here is used poetically as a small-quantity or introductory particle and is not the literal 'barley' (so its lexical sense is archaic/figurative in context); آگاهی 'information/news' is the noun and آمد is the past 'came'. ز مهران ستاد places the action as coming 'from Mehrān' and has ستاد as a past verb meaning 'he set up/established' (i.e. Mehrān had established/campaigned or set up a camp), so the whole clause conveys 'news came that Mehrān had set up.'
segments.s655-g998: همی هر کس is an emphatic distributive 'each and every person' (همی intensifies the ongoing/actional sense); آن مژده را is the definite object 'that good news' (را marks the direct object); هدیه داد is a compound verb phrase 'gave as a gift' (هدیه = 'gift/tribute', داد = past 'gave'). Word order is standard: subject phrase → object marked by را → verb.
segments.s655-g999: یکایک is an adverb meaning 'one by one' or 'each and every'; همی‌خواندند is the past/habitual verb 'they were calling out/reciting' (here used to indicate repeated exclamations); آفرین is an interjection/acclamation 'bravo!' or 'praise' and serves as what they called aloud. Together: 'one by one they shouted “Afārīn!”'.
segments.s655-g1000: ابرشاه ایران وسالار چین is a sequence of titles: ابرشاه 'supreme/great king', ایران 'of Iran', و سالار 'and commander/lord', چین 'of China'. The two title+place pairs are coordinated: 'the mighty king of Iran and the lord/commander of China.' There is no finite verb here because this is a nominal apposition naming the addressee of the acclamation.
segments.s655-g593: This clause is past-tense SOV. چوخاقان is a poetic fusion equivalent to چو خاقان (‘when the khaghan’) and functions as a temporal/subject phrase. آن سخنها is the object phrase 'those words' (demonstrative + plural noun). شنید is the simple past of شنیدن (‘to hear’) placed at the end as the finite verb.
segments.s655-g594: بپژمرد is an intransitive past-form meaning 'withered/turned pale' (ب‑ is a common past prefix in older Persian). وشد is the conjunction و plus شد (‘and became’), linking the two results. چون introduces a simile 'like', گل is the literal 'flower', and شنبلید names a specific plant (fenugreek) used as a poetic simile—together: 'and became like the fenugreek flower.'
segments.s655-g595: دلش ('his heart') is the possessed subject (دل + enclitic possessive ش). زان سخنها is an ablative phrase 'from/by those words' (زان = از آن). بدو here is a dative/benefactive 'to him' or emphasizes the effect on him; نیم شد literally 'became half'—an idiomatic expression meaning 'his heart was broken/halved' or 'his heart was overcome.' Word order: Subject – ablative – indirect object – predicate.
segments.s655-g596: وز here is the poetic fusion of و + ز (i.e. 'and from/by'), introducing the next clause. اندیشه مغزش is a noun phrase where اندیشه ('thought') qualifies مغزش ('his brain/mind'). پر (followed by از + بیم) functions as an adjective 'full (of)'; از بیم literally 'with/of fear'. شد is the past copula 'became'—'and from thought his brain became full of fear.'
segments.s655-g597: پراندیشه is an adjective used as a substantive/epithet 'the pensive/overthinking one'. بنشست is the simple past 'sat down'. با + رای‌زن is a prepositional phrase 'with a counselor' (companionship/in consultation). The sequence reads subject (or epithet) + verb + adverbial accompaniment.
segments.s655-g598: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'. گفت here is the past verb 'he said/spoke' (the lexical list given treats گفت as a noun in some senses, but in context it is the verb). با نامدار انجمن is a prepositional phrase meaning 'before/with the renowned assembly' (با used to indicate presence/among). The clause: 'thus he spoke to the renowned assembly.'
segments.s655-g599: که‌ای بخردان is a vocative address: که‌ای (equivalent to 'ای') + بخردان (plural vocative 'wise ones') — 'O wise men'. روی این کارچیست is an interrogative phrase: روی (here used figuratively as 'aspect/meaning/aim') + این کار ('this deed') + چیست (copula interrogative 'what is'). Together: 'O wise men, what is the point/meaning of this action?'
segments.s655-g600: پراندیشه وخسته is a coordinated description 'pensive and weary' (پراندیشه, then و‑ + خسته). ز آزار is a prepositional phrase 'from/through torment' modifying the adjectives. کی ست is the interrogative copula 'who is it?' — so the full question asks which person is this pensive, tormented, and weary one.
segments.s655-g241: تو is the second‑person pronoun (subject). داناتری is the comparative form of دانا ('more knowing' / 'wiser'); Persian places the adjective/comparative after the subject here. از introduces the standard of comparison ('than' in English) followed by بزرگ انجمن 'the great assembly' (بزرگ modifying انجمن).
segments.s655-g242: نبایدت is an impersonal/necessity construction meaning 'it should not be for you' / 'you need not' (the clitic -ت marks the dative/ethical relation to 'تو'). فرزانه here is a predicate noun/adjective 'a sage, wise one'. و links the two predicates; رای is 'counsel/plan' and زن is here not the noun 'woman' but part of a verbal phrase meaning 'to give' or 'to deliver' counsel in this older style; the whole clause says it is not necessary (or fitting) for you to act the sage or to give counsel.
segments.s655-g243: تو را uses the object/topic marker را in a dative/ethical sense ('to you' / 'for you'), and زیبد (a present/impersonal form of زیبیدن/زیبا بودن) means 'it befits' or 'is fitting'. اندر جهان 'in the world' is the locative phrase. تاج وتخت is a coordinated noun phrase 'crown and throne' (تاج و تخت).
segments.s655-g244: که introduces a clause of explanation ('for' / 'in that'), با is the preposition 'with'. فر is the Zoroastrian/royal concept of 'farr' (royal glory). The following و coordinates an archaic term برزی (here treated as a substantive paired with فر in a poetic list). The second با repeats 'with' and joins رای ('counsel') and بخت ('fortune' / 'fate'). The whole segment lists the qualities that accompany the fitting kingship.
segments.s655-g245: اگر introduces a conditional ('if'). شاه is the subject 'the king'. سوی is a directional postposition meaning 'toward' or 'to'. خراسان is the place name 'Khorasan'. شود is the present/subjunctive form of شدن used here as the verb of the protasis ('if the king goes/becomes toward Khorasan').
segments.s655-g246: ازین is the fused form of از این ('from this' / 'of this'); پادشاهی is 'kingdom' / 'kingship' and is the theme affected in this clause. هراسان is an adjectival/predicative form meaning 'alarmed' or 'frightened'. شود is the copular/auxiliary 'becomes' so the whole clause says 'this kingship becomes frightened/throws the realm into alarm.'
segments.s655-g247: هرآن گه که is a temporal conjunction meaning 'whenever' or 'at any time that'. بینند is the third‑person plural present subjunctive/indicative 'they see' (classical style). بی‑شاه is a compound adjective 'without a king' (بی- prefix = 'without') modifying بوم 'land' / 'homeland', so بی‌شاه بوم = 'the land being kingless'.
segments.s655-g248: زمان تا زمان is an idiomatic repetition meaning 'from time to time' / 'occasionally'. لشکر is the subject 'an army' and آید is the present tense 'comes'. ز is the poetic form of از meaning 'from', and روم is 'Rome' / Byzantium. The clause therefore states that, whenever the realm is kingless, an army comes from Rome from time to time.
segments.s655-g1729: چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus' placed before the verb to modify it. گفت is the past 3rd-person verb 'he said' (classical narrative), and با + person (با شاه بوزرجمهر) marks the addressee ('to/with the king Bozorgmehr'); the word order is slightly inverted in poetic/narrative style but means 'thus he said to King Bozorgmehr.'
segments.s655-g1730: که introduces a subordinate clause ('that...'). یک سر is an idiomatic adverbial phrase meaning 'wholly' or 'entirely' (literally 'one head'); شگفتست is fused poetic form of شگفت است ('is wondrous') serving as the predicate; کار سپهر is a genitive construction 'the work/affair of the sky/heavens.'
segments.s655-g1731: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain' or numeral 'one' introducing the noun; مرد is the object/participant 'a man'; بینیم is the classical poetic 1st-person-plural verb 'we saw' (used in narrative); با دستگاه is a prepositional phrase 'with pomp/with an establishment/retinue' describing his appearance.
segments.s655-g1732: کلاهش is the possessed noun 'his hat' (کلاه + -ش). رسیده is a participial/adjectival/past form 'reached' or 'extended' describing the hat. بابر سیاه is a modifier phrase describing the hat; سیاه is the adjective 'black' following the noun as is normal in Persian. (Note: the lexical item بابر here appears in the manuscript as a single token describing the hat's form/style; grammatically it functions as a noun or noun-like modifier followed by the adjective سیاه.)
segments.s655-g1733: که introduces a relative clause describing the previously mentioned man. او is the subject 'he.' دست چپ را is the marked direct object phrase 'the left hand' (را marks definiteness/object). نداند is an archaic/politened present-subjunctive/negative form meaning 'does not/was not able to know' (here 'could not distinguish'). ز راست uses the poetic preposition ز 'from' to mean 'from the right,' so the whole line means 'who could not know the left hand from the right.'
segments.s655-g1734: ز بخشش فزونی uses ز 'from' plus بخشش 'giving/grant' and فزونی 'excess' to express 'surplus in giving.' نداند repeats the negative 'did not/was not able to know.' نه کاست puts نه 'nor' before کاست (here meaning 'diminution/shortage') to say 'nor shortage.' The construction says he could not tell surplus from shortage in giving.
segments.s655-g1735: یکی here is quantitative 'one' (a single); گردش is 'revolution/circuit' and وظیفه syntactically takes آسمان بلند as its genitive modifier: 'the circuit of the high/lofty sky.' The adjective بلند 'high/lofty' follows the noun as usual in Persian.
segments.s655-g1736: ستاره is the noun 'a star' (subject). بگوید is the 3rd-person present/subjunctive/future-form 'will say' or 'tells' in poetic narration (here functioning as future/authoritative speech). که introduces an indirect question: چونست is contracted poetic form of چون است 'how it is,' and چند is 'how many' — together 'that how it is and how many (there are).'
segments.s655-g721: چنین (thus) is an adverb modifying the whole reply; داد پاسخ is a poetic inversion of usual گفت or پاسخ داد (literally 'gave an answer'); که introduces the reported content ('that ...'); خاقان چین is the title + place ('the Khaghan of China'), with چین = China as a specifier of خاقان.
segments.s655-g722: بزرگست is the fused poetic form of بزرگ است (‘is great’); و links the following attributes; با دانش is an instrumental/qualifying phrase ('with learning', i.e. 'learned'); و آفرین here qualifies the ruler as deserving praise ('praiseworthy' or 'worthy of praise').
segments.s655-g723: به marks the object/goal ('toward/with'); فرزند is the person he seeks alliance with; پیوند جوید is a verbal phrase meaning 'seeks a bond/union' (جویدن = 'to seek' here); همی is the archaic continuous/present particle indicating ongoing action ('keeps on').
segments.s655-g724: رخ دوستی ('the face of friendship') is a genitive/attributive phrase (رخ = face; دوستی = friendship); را is the definite object marker applied to that phrase; بشوید is here from the root شد/شو meaning 'become/assume' (poetic usage: 'assumes the friendly countenance'); همی again marks ongoing action.
segments.s655-g725: هرآنکس 'whoever' introduces the conditional/relative clause; که is the relativizer 'who/that'; دارد is present third-person 'has'; روانش is روان plus the 3rd-person possessive -ش ('his soul'); خرد follows as the predicate/noun complement meaning 'wisdom' (his soul 'has wisdom').
segments.s655-g726: به چشم خرد is an adverbial phrase 'with/by the eye of wisdom' (به with means 'by/with' + 'چشم خرد' = 'the wise eye'); کارها is the object 'affairs/deeds'; بنگرد is the present/subjunctive of نگر ('to look at / to consider') placed at the end as the finite verb, so the clause means 'he regards matters with the eye of wisdom.'
segments.s655-g727: بسازیم is first-person plural present/subjunctive used with future/hortative force ('we will make/arrange'); و coordinates the next clause; این رای فرخ is the object phrase 'this fortunate/auspicious counsel'; نهیم is the first-person plural of نهاد/نه ('to place/set') meaning 'we will set/place.'
segments.s655-g728: سخن هرچ ('whatever speech/words') is the topic; گفته‌ست is a perfect/periphrastic form meaning 'has been said' or 'that has been said' (گفته + است contracted); پاسخ دهیم is a hortative/future 'we will give an answer' (دهیم = first-person plural subjunctive used here for future action).
segments.s655-g577: This short clause names the tree and its position. خجسته is an epithet ('blessed/auspicious') placed before the noun سرو ('cypress'); شست is a past-form verb here meaning 'stood/was placed' (an archaic/poetic past) and links the subject to its location. بر + گاه و تخت uses بر ('on/upon') + the coordinate phrase گاه و تخت (‘court and throne’) to indicate location.
segments.s655-g578: This phrase specifies one particular bough of that tree. یکی is an indefinite numeral/adjective 'one/a certain' modifying the following nouns; بارور ('fruitful/fertile') and زیبا ('beautiful') are descriptive adjectives that qualify شاخ ('bough, branch') and/or درخت ('tree'). Word order is adjective(s) + noun sequence typical of poetic Persian where several modifiers and related nouns follow each other.
segments.s655-g579: همه شهر ایران is a fronted noun phrase 'all the cities/towns of Iran.' سپاه follows as an appositive 'a host/army' describing the multitude represented by those towns. The final ویند is an archaic suffix or epithet attached here (annotated as ‑vind) and functions as a poetic tag attached to سپاه, so the whole reads like 'the host‑vind' (an old descriptive ending).
segments.s655-g580: This short noun phrase identifies those people: پرستندگان ('worshippers/devotees') is the head noun and کلاه ویند is a compound where کلاه ('cap/hat') is qualified by the same archaic tag ویند (‑vind); together it describes the kind of followers (literally 'worshippers of the kâlah‑vind' or 'devotees wearing the vind‑cap').
segments.s655-g581: چو سازد is the conditional/temporal 'when he makes/sets up' (چو = 'when' + سازد = 'he makes/sets'). به دشت و اندرون بارگاه gives the location: به دشت 'into/at the plain' and اندرون بارگاه 'the inner part of the palace/royal court' (اندرون = 'within', بارگاه = 'royal court'), so the whole segment means 'when he sets up the inner court on/in the plain'.
segments.s655-g582: نگنجد همی is a negative present sense, 'does not fit / cannot be contained' (نگنجد from گنجیدن 'to be contained'); همی is a frequent classical particle here supporting the present/continuous or emphatic reading. درجهان should be read as 'in rank / within the world's order' (در + درجه notionally), آن سپاه 'that host' is the subject of نگنجد, so the clause says that host cannot be accommodated within any worldly rank/order.
segments.s655-g583: همه گرزداران is a plural noun phrase 'all the mace‑bearers.' با introduces the accompanying qualities 'with' and ties them to the noun; زیب و وفر are two coordinated nouns/abstracts meaning 'beauty' and 'splendour/glory' describing the appearance or regalia of those mace‑bearers.
segments.s655-g584: همه پیشکاران is 'all the attendants/servants.' به + زرین کمر is a classical construction meaning 'with/attired in' or 'having' a 'golden girdle' (زرین 'golden', کمر 'girdle/belt'). The structure parallels the previous half-line: all attendants endowed with golden belts.
segments.s655-g1097: به هنگام is a prepositional phrase meaning "at the time of" (به + هنگام). پیروز is a noun/adjective 'the victor' used as the subject; چون is a simile marker 'like/as'; خوشنواز is an adjective 'sweet-sounding, melodious' describing the victor. Word order is typical Persian: time phrase first (به هنگام پیروز), then the simile (چون خوشنواز) that links to the following verb clause.
segments.s655-g1098: جهان is the direct object 'the world' and کرد is the main past-tense verb 'made/did' (3rd sg.). پر is the preposed adjective 'full (of)' that governs the following nouns: درد 'pain/affliction', گرم 'burning/fervent', and گداز 'ardor/anguish'; the three nouns are coordinated by و. The sequence 'پر X و Y و Z' = 'full of X, Y and Z.'
segments.s655-g1099: مبادا is a negative optative/negative jussive meaning 'may it not be / lest'. فغانیش is a noun with the possessive suffix -ش (his/its lamentation). فرزند اوی is a noun phrase 'his son/child' where اوی is the archaic third‑person enclitic/pronominal element ('his'); the clause is elliptic (no overt verb) and reads as a wish: 'may his lament not [be for] his son...'.
segments.s655-g1100: This is a coordinated list introduced by the poetic particle مه appearing before each noun as an honorific/intensifier (an archaic/poetic usage). خویشان 'relatives', تخت 'throne' are the listed nouns; ومه shows the conjunction + particle (و + مه) and introduces اورند (a proper name/place, here 'Orand' or 'Arvand'); اوی again is the archaic third‑person enclitic 'his'. Grammatically the whole phrase continues the object of the preceding wish: 'may his lament not be for X, Y, and Z.'
segments.s655-g1101: جهاندار is an honorific noun 'sovereign' and کسری is the proper name 'Kisra/Khosrow' in apposition. کنون is an adverb 'now' placed before the verb clause to mark time. مرز ما is a noun + genitive 'our border' functioning as the object of the upcoming verb. Word order places the title and name together before the temporal adverb and object.
segments.s655-g1102: بپذرفت is the past tense (3rd sg.) 'he accepted / received' (here Khosrow accepted the border). The conjunction و links the next clause. پرمایه شد is a light-verb construction where پرمایه 'rich/abundant' is predicated and شد 'became' is the copular verb ('became rich'). ارز ما 'our wealth/treasure' is the noun phrase whose state is described by 'پرمایه شد' — 'our wealth became abundant.'
segments.s655-g1103: بماناد is an optative/jussive form 'may it remain' (wishing/benedictive). تا + جاودان expresses duration 'until/so that it be eternal' — here effectively 'forever.' این is the demonstrative 'this' (the thing wished to remain). بر اوی is a prepositional phrase 'upon him' with اوی as the archaic third‑person pronoun/enclitic. The clause is a conventional wish: 'May this remain eternal upon him.'
segments.s655-g1104: جهان سر به سر is an idiomatic expression: جهان 'the world' + سر به سر ('completely, throughout') = 'the whole world.' چون introduces a simile/comparison; here it is repeated for emphasis: چون تن و چون سر اوی = 'like his body and like his head.' و is the coordinating conjunction. Grammatically the clause specifies the scope of the wish: 'may the whole world, wholly, be like his body and like his head (i.e. belong to/honor him).' اوی again is the archaic possessive/enclitic 'his.'
segments.s655-g393: دبیر آن زمان نامه خواندن گرفت is structured with the subject phrase دبیر آن زمان ('the scribe/secretary at that time') followed by the verbal-noun phrase نامه خواندن ('the reading of the letter') which functions as the object. گرفت is a past-tense verb (3rd sing.) meaning 'took' or 'took up' and here has the idiomatic sense 'began/undertook to read.'
segments.s655-g394: همه انجمن ماند اندر شگفت is a nominal subject (همه انجمن = 'the whole assembly') followed by the past verb ماند ('remained'). اندر شگفت is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in wonder/astonishment.' Note انجمن is a collective noun, which is why the verb appears as ماند (singular form).
segments.s655-g395: سر نامه بود از نخست آفرین: سر نامه ('the head/heading of the letter') is a fixed collocation meaning the opening line; بود is the past copula 'was.' از introduces the source 'from,' and نخست آفرین is a compound epithet ('First‑Creator') — a poetic name for God where آفرین functions as 'Creator.'
segments.s655-g396: ز دادار بر شهریار زمین: ز (poetic form of از) marks origin 'from the Creator' (دادار). بر is here a dative/prepositional usage meaning 'to/toward' and links to شهریار زمین ('the king/ruler of the land'), i.e. 'to the king of the earth.' The phrase gives the addressee of the letter.
segments.s655-g397: دگر سر فرازی و گنج و سپاه begins with دگر ('another/further') introducing a list: سر فرازی ('pride, high station' — literally 'raising of the head', an idiomatic nominal phrase), then گنج ('treasure') and سپاه ('army'). Word order is enumerative, joined by و.
segments.s655-g398: سلیح وبزرگی نمودن به شاه: سلیح ('arms/weapons') and بزرگی ('greatness') are the objects shown; نمودن here functions as a past tense verb meaning 'they showed/displayed' (in epic/poetic usage the full ending may be elided), and به شاه marks the indirect object 'to the king.'
segments.s655-g399: سه دیگر سخن آنک فغفور چین: 'سه دیگر سخن' = 'three other words/phrases' (numeral + modifier + noun). آنک is a classical conjunction introducing reported content ('that'), and فغفور چین is the title 'the faghfur (emperor) of China' — a formal designation of that sovereign.
segments.s655-g400: مراخواند اندر جهان‌آفرین: مراخواند is a past 3rd‑person verb 'he summoned/called me' with the object pronoun مرا preceding the verb (classical word order). اندر here means 'into/in the presence of' and جهان‌آفرین is a poetic epithet 'World‑Creator' (i.e. God).
segments.s655-g473: This is a nominal list describing what belonged to (or accompanied) the referent. همان خود is emphatic (‘the very/own’), و links the items, and کوپال اوی is a possessed noun phrase where اوی is the archaic possessive clitic ‘his’. Word order is head-first: determiner/emphatic + possessed + conjunction + another possessed item.
segments.s655-g474: نبرداشتی is a negative past/perfective predication here (rendered impersonally as ‘had nothing in battle’); جز introduces the exception (‘except’). بر و یال اوی is a coordinated phrase where اوی (= ‘his’) attaches to the pair; یال means ‘mane’. The unusual item بر here functions as a noun meaning a projecting part (tusk/forepart) rather than the usual preposition.
segments.s655-g475: کمانکش نبودی is a predicative clause in the past tense (نبودی = ‘you were not’); the predicate is the noun کمانکش (‘archer’). به لشکر چنوی is a prepositional phrase marking the milieu (‘in/among the host’) with چنوی (an older form of چنان/چنین) functioning as a comparative/adverb ‘like that’. Word order: predicate followed by locative phrase.
segments.s655-g476: This continues the negation with نه introducing the second negative member: ‘nor…’. ازنامداران is a genitival/partitive phrase (‘of/among the renowned’), چنان is a demonstrative/adverb ‘such’, and جنگجوی is the noun ‘warrior’. The whole phrase means you were not such a fighter even among the celebrated.
segments.s655-g477: به آوردگه رفت is a motion clause: به + place (آوردگه ‘place of arrival/arena’) + رفت (‘went’). چون پیل مست is a simile placed after the verb phrase (‘like a drunken elephant’); مست is a predicate adjective modifying پیل. The simile follows classical Persian order: verb + چون + noun + adjective.
segments.s655-g478: This is an instrumental/qualifying noun phrase: یکی گرزه گاو پیکر به دست = ‘one mace (گرزه), of ox‑body (گاو پیکر), in (his) hand’. یکی is an indefinite numeral ‘one/a certain’, گرزه is the head noun, گاو پیکر is a compound attributive (literally ‘ox‑bodied’) and به دست marks the instrument/possession ‘in (the) hand’. Perso‑classical ordering places the size/shape modifier after the head.
segments.s655-g479: This is a locative and accompanying phrase: به زیر اندرون = ‘to/at the lower/inner place’ (به + زیر + اندرون), با ره گامزن = ‘with the marching way/with the marching ranks’ where ره (way/line) + گامزن (those who step/march) forms a genitival phrase. The sequence places location first and the accompaniment second, as typical in poetic diction.
segments.s655-g480: ز بالای او is a prepositional phrase ‘from above him’ (ز = from, بالای = above), followed by the verb phrase خیره شد انجمن where انجمن (assembly) is the subject and خیره شد is a compound predicate ‘became fixed/stared’ (خیره ‘astonished/staring’ + شد ‘became’). Word order here is slightly inverted (locative phrase + predicate + subject), a frequent poetic arrangement.
segments.s655-g145: که ... باشد introduces a purpose/subjunctive clause: 'that he may be ...'. او شاد باشد is a third‑person subjunctive/hortative (classical narrative uses باشد for 'may he be' / 'let him be') meaning 'that he be happy'. بنوشین‌روان is an adjectival/epithet (literally 'soul‑delighting' or 'soul‑refreshing') placed after the clause as a modifier referring to an implied cup/drink (a poetic epithet for the draught).
segments.s655-g146: بدو is the dative 'to him / for him'. دولت پیر گردَد جوان is a purposive/blessing clause: دولت 'fortune/prosperity' + پیر 'old' + گردد 'becomes' (subjunctive/optative) + جوان 'young' — 'may his old fortune become young again.' Word order places the indirect object (بدو) before the noun phrase.
segments.s655-g147: بگوید here is the narrative present meaning 'he says / he told' (classical epic uses present for reported action). It takes the dative بدو 'to him' and the direct object کار خاقان چین 'the affairs/deeds of the khagan of China' (خاقان چین = 'the Chinese khagan'). Word order: verb + indirect object + object.
segments.s655-g148: جهانی here is adverbial 'throughout the world' (literally 'worldly/universal'); برو is an imperative/hortative 'go forth' (or narrative present 'let them go'); بر کنند is a verbal construction ('they do/make upon' → 'they cause/bring about'); and آفرین is 'acclamation/praise' (an object/result). Together the clause expresses 'let the world go forth and acclaim (them)'.
segments.s655-g149: که introduces an explanatory/relative clause ('for' / 'in that'); با is the preposition 'with'. فر is the Persian technical term for royal divine glory. و برزست (برزست = 'was exalted' / 'highness') and و بخش (بخش = 'gift, bounty') enumerate the qualities; و خرد 'and wisdom' completes the list — a series of nouns joined by و and governed by با.
segments.s655-g150: همی is a habitual/continuative particle 'ever/always'. راستی را (راستی + object marker را) marks 'truth' as a definite object. خرد پرورد is the verb phrase 'nurtured / fostered wisdom' (پرورد here is past/historical 'nurtured'). The line compresses two objects/qualities — 'fostered truth and nurtured wisdom' — typical of epic parallelism.
segments.s655-g151: نهادست is a perfective/passive‑style verb 'was placed / was set' (or 'is placed'); بر is the preposition 'on/upon'. قیصران is the plural 'Caesars / emperors'. باژ و ساو appear as names/identifiers following قیصران (proper names in apposition). The clause means something (e.g. that dignity/authority) was laid upon those emperors.
segments.s655-g152: ندارند is a negative present 'they do not have' used impersonally here ('no one has...'). با او is the prepositional phrase 'with him'. کسی in negative contexts functions as 'anyone / no one' (here 'no one'). زور و تاو are the pair of qualities 'strength (زور) and radiance/glow (تاو)'. The whole clause: 'with him no one possesses strength and radiance.'
segments.s655-g833: چوآن (= چو + آن) begins a temporal clause ‘when that…’; the order is typical of classical Persian: temporal introducer + object + verb + subject. نامه is the direct object ‘that letter’; برخواند is a past-tense verb meaning ‘read aloud / caused to be read’; خاقان چین (khagan + China) is the subject placed after the verb and means ‘the khagan of China’.
segments.s655-g834: ز here means ‘because of / at’ and links to پیمان as its object: ‘he laughed because of the treaty’. بخندید is simple past ‘he laughed’ (third-person). وز is an archaic/fused particle (ו + ز) used to connect the next action; in this line the verb ‘choose’ appears without an explicit auxiliary, a concise poetic usage where گزین (the root ‘choose’) serves as the following action and به marks the target/direction. Thus the segment reads roughly ‘he laughed at the treaty and thereupon chose…’.
segments.s655-g835: کلید شبستان is a noun phrase ‘the key of the bedchamber’ (possessive relation expressed by adjacency). بدو داد is the verb phrase ‘gave to him’ (داد = past ‘gave’; بدو = ‘to him’). و connects this clause to what follows. گفت here introduces reported speech (‘and said’); note that the sense-list provided for گفت did not include the simple past ‘said’, so the mapping for that word is left unspecified (see senses).
segments.s655-g836: برو is an imperative ‘Go!’. تا introduces a purpose clause here and should be read as ‘so that / in order that’. کرا is an archaic interrogative pronoun ‘who’ (object of seeing). بینی is the present/subjunctive 2nd-singular form ‘you may see’ used in purpose clauses (in Persian the present-subjunctive form is used for such jussive/purpose senses). اندر نهفت is a prepositional phrase ‘hidden inside’ where نهفت is a past participial/adjectival form meaning ‘hidden/concealed’. Together: ‘Go, so that you may see who is hidden inside.’
segments.s655-g837: پرستار با او بیامد is a straightforward clause: پرستار ‘the attendant/attendant(s)’ (here agent) + با او ‘with him’ + بیامد past ‘came’. The numeral 'چهار' following the verb is an abrupt classical placement meaning ‘four (of them) came’; the line uses compact, slightly irregular classical ordering.
segments.s655-g838: که introduces a subordinate clause giving the reason or circumstance (‘for / because’). خاقان is the subject ‘the khagan’; بدیشان is a dative phrase ‘to them / for them’. بدی is a substantive here meaning ‘harm/ill’ and استوار is an adjective ‘established/firm’ modifying that harm — i.e. ‘the khagan had fixed/appointed a firm harm upon them.’ (This clause is terse and archaic in tone; the grammar is subject + indirect object + noun predicate modified by استوار.)
segments.s655-g839: چو (here fused in the token چومهران) introduces a temporal clause ‘when…’. مهران ستاد is a personal name (Mehrān Setād) or name + epithet; آن سخنها is ‘those words’ (آن = that/those; سخنها = words); شنید is the simple past ‘he heard’. The order is typical: temporal marker + name + object + verb.
segments.s655-g840: بیاورد is a past-tense verb ‘he brought’ (classical perfect/past form). با + استواران is a prepositional phrase ‘with the steadfast ones’ (استواران = plural ‘the steadfast / stalwart men’, here functioning like guards). کلید is the direct object ‘the key’. Word order: verb + prepositional phrase + object is an acceptable classical arrangement emphasizing the action.
segments.s655-g585: This is a short list introduced by the preposition 'ز' (z) meaning 'from'. Word order is preposition + noun(s). 'پیل' (pīl) is the noun 'elephant' (direct object of 'ز'). The particle 'وز' here is an archaic/poetic connector appearing between items in a list (not one of the provided literal senses), and 'بالا' follows as a noun/adjective meaning the raised seat/howdah ('high' seat). Then 'و از' begins the next item 'از تخت عاج' ('from the ivory throne'); 'تخت عاج' is a genitive-like noun phrase 'throne (تخت) of ivory (عاج)'.
