segments.s655-g586: Another coordinated list begun by 'ز' ('from'). 'اورنگ' is used as 'throne' (a lofty seat). Again 'وز' functions as a poetic connective (not well represented by the literal senses given). 'یاره' here means attendants/followers. The repeated 'و' are simple coordinating conjunctions 'and', and 'طوق' and 'تاج' are physical regalia ('collar/necklace' and 'crown').
segments.s655-g587: 'کس' here functions negatively = 'no one' (classical usage). The phrase 'آیین او' is a possessive noun phrase 'his pomp/ceremonial display' (آیین = regalia/pomp). 'رانداند' is an archaic past (third-person plural) form of راندن; Ferdowsi sometimes uses it in extended/poetic senses — grammatically it is a past-tense verb form. 'شمار' is the noun 'number/count' and in this construction is linked to the verb phrase (literally 'drove off the count' but idiomatically 'could not be counted' or 'whose number could not be matched').
segments.s655-g588: 'به گیتی' is a locative phrase 'in the world' (classical 'به' used here as a locative/prepositional marker). 'جز' means 'except', and the following 'از' introduces the exception ('except from' → 'except'). 'دادگر' is an epithet 'the just' (here adjectival noun), and 'شهریار' is 'king'. The whole segment supplies the restriction: nobody in the world except the just king.
segments.s655-g589: This conditional begins with 'اگر' = 'if'. 'دشمنش' is 'his enemy' (possessive clitic -ش). The phrase 'کوه آهن' is a compound 'a mountain of iron' (کوه = mountain, آهن = iron), and 'شود' is the present/subjunctive/potential form of شدن 'to become'/'to be' used here to state the hypothetical ('were/should become').
segments.s655-g590: The preposition 'بر' here means 'upon/in the face of' and takes the noun 'خشم' ('anger'). 'او' is the possessive 'his'. 'چشم' is 'eye' (subject of the following predicate), 'سوزن' is literally 'needle' used metaphorically, and 'شود' (again) is 'becomes'. The clause structure is: conditional clause → then (under his anger) the eye becomes a needle (i.e., a sharp/painful thing).
segments.s655-g591: 'هرآنکس که' is a general relative/clause meaning 'whoever'/'anyone who'. 'سیر' here is an adjective/participial idea 'sated' or 'satiated', so 'سیر آید' literally 'satisfaction comes' → 'becomes sated'. 'از روزگار' is a prepositional phrase 'by/from fate/time' (روزگار used here in the sense 'fate/fortune'). The clause sets the condition/background for the following result clause.
segments.s655-g592: This is the result clause: 'شود' = 'becomes' (present/future-like or potential), 'تیز' here means 'swift' (i.e., rapid in action), 'وبا' is 'the plague/scourge' as subject, 'او' = 'him' (the person affected), 'کند' is the present/past auxiliary meaning 'does/makes', and 'کارزار' is 'battle/struggle'. Grammatically: 'the plague becomes swift and makes battle with him' — a compact way to say misfortune quickly attacks the one who grows sated with life.
segments.s655-g873: This is a pair of prepositional/simile phrases giving the attributes of the ruler: به + noun marks the target/measure ('to/in respect of'); بالای سرو = 'the height/top of the cypress' and به رخ چون بهار = 'to a face like spring' (رخ 'face' with a simile introduced by چون). These phrases function adverbially or as descriptive modifiers of the following clause.
segments.s655-g874: بداند here is a literary past/epic usage of دانستن meaning 'he considered' or 'he deemed'. It takes the infinitival/verbal-noun clause پرستیدن شهریار as its object: 'to worship the king'. The infinitive (پرستیدن) + noun (شهریار) functions as 'worshipping the king' which is what he judged appropriate.
segments.s655-g875: همی here is an emphatic/continuous adverb ('still/continually' or 'keeps on'). کودکی نارسیده is a noun phrase 'an unripe/immature child'. به جای means 'for the place/post' (جای in the sense of rank/office). Together this gives the repeated action or situation that someone selects an unready child for a position.
segments.s655-g876: برو برگزینی is a colloquial/poetic sequence where برگزینی is 2nd-person present (you choose) or an exhortation 'go, choose' (the verb برگزین with -ی). نه‌ای is a negative copular form here ('you are not' / 'not being') and پاکرای is an adjective 'pure-minded, of pure intent'. The clause accuses the addressee of lacking pure intent while choosing the unripe candidate.
segments.s655-g877: Word order is verb-object-subject (literary inversion): چنین پاسخ = 'such an answer' (object/phrase), آورد = past tense 'brought/gave' (here 'answered'), مهران = the personal name as subject; ستاد is attached after the name (here functioning as a verbal epithet or part of the formulaic line). Overall: 'Mehran gave such an answer.'
segments.s655-g878: که introduces the content clause ('that...'). خاقان is the subject 'the khagan'. اگر + verb بپیچد forms a conditional/subjunctive ('if he should turn/turns'). ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'away from', and داد means 'justice' — so the clause means 'if the khagan turns his head away from justice.'
segments.s655-g879: بداند again 'he thought/deemed' introducing a reported clause with که ('that'). شاه جهان = 'the king of the world' (subject phrase). کدخدای here functions as a predicative noun ('village chief') that the shah might apply — the clause sets up what he presumed the king would call him.
segments.s655-g880: بخواند is a 3rd-person (subjunctive/hortatory) form of خواندن meaning 'would call/summon' (here 'would call [someone something]'). مرا (split in the word list as م + را) is the object 'me' (را is the definite object marker). نیز = 'also'. ناپاک رای is a noun phrase 'impure counsel/opinion' (ناپاک modifies رای). The sequence says 'he would call me [a village headman] and also declare my counsel impure.'
segments.s655-g865: من is the 1st-person subject 'I'. این را is a topicalized, definite object ('this' + object marker را) and که introduces a relative clause modifying این. بی‌تاج و آرایشست is a poetic concatenation of adjective + copula (آرایش + است → آرایشست), literally 'uncrowned and (is) unadorned.' Word order is subject — object(+relative clause) — (verb later).
segments.s655-g866: گزیدم is the simple past 1st-person 'I chose.' که here introduces a causal/explanatory clause ('because/that'), این refers back to the foregoing 'this', اندر is the archaic preposition 'in/within', and افزایشست is the predicate with the copular -ست meaning 'it is increase' or 'it effects increase.' The whole clause gives the reason for choosing.
segments.s655-g867: به رنج is an adverbial phrase 'with toil/through hardship' (به used adverbially). از پی is an idiom 'in pursuit of/after' (پی = pursuit), and به گزین آمدم uses the infinitival stem گزین with آمدم ('I came to choose' / 'I came for choosing'); آمدم is past 1st-person. The sequence expresses motive and manner: 'I came after/pursuing, with hardship, to make a choice.'
segments.s655-g868: نه negates the following clause. از بهر is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning 'for the sake/sake of,' دیبای چین is an object phrase 'brocade of (or from) China' (دیبا = brocade/silk, چین here functions as 'Chinese' / 'China'), and آمدم is again 'I came.' Together: 'I did not come for the sake of brocaded/Chinese silk.'
segments.s655-g869: بدو is the prepositional/allative 'to him' (to+him) and گفت is the past verb 'said' (here introducing reported/direct speech). خاتون is the speaker 'the lady.' که + ای begins the vocative/introducing particle plus vocative 'ای' producing the address 'O old man' (که here helps introduce the quoted words). مرد پیر is the direct address 'old man.'
segments.s655-g870: نگویی is a negative present/subjunctive/hortative form (ن‑ + گویی) used as a polite interrogative/hortative 'will you not / do you not say' (2nd person). همی is an archaic adverbial particle giving emphasis/continuity ('indeed/still' in poetry). یک سخن is the object 'one word' and دلپذیر is the adjective 'pleasant' modifying سخن.
segments.s655-g871: تو is the 2nd-person subject 'you.' آن را که introduces a relative clause 'that which/one who' (آن + object marker را, followed by relative که). با + فر و زیبست و رای lists qualities 'with glory, and (is) beauty, and counsel' — زیبست is a poetic concatenation (زیب + است) functioning as 'is beautiful' inside the descriptor, and رای denotes 'counsel/opinion/wise counsel.' The whole segment begins the relative description of the person.
segments.s655-g872: دل فروز is a compound-like phrase 'heart-illuminating / heart-kindling' (دل = heart, فروز as participial/attributive element 'illuminating' or 'glorious'). گشته is the perfect/passive participle 'has become' (here part of a verbal cluster), رسیده is 'arrived/attained,' به is the preposition 'to' and جای is 'place/station.' Together they say that he has become heart-illuminating and reached his proper station.
segments.s655-g1137: Subject 'تو' (you) + past verb 'دادی' (you gave) with the object pronoun split across the tokens 'م' + 'را' (poetic orthography for 'مرا' = me + object marker). The list of direct objects 'فر و فرهنگ و رای' follows the object pronoun; Persian permits this ordering in elevated/poetic style.
segments.s655-g1138: 'تو' is again the (implied) addressee; 'باشی' is the 2nd‑person singular subjunctive/optative used here as an imperative/request 'be'. 'بهر نیکی‌ای' is a purpose/benefit phrase ('for the sake of goodness' with the poetic/indefinite -ای on نیکی), and 'رهنمای' is the predicative noun 'guide' (you, be guide for goodness).
segments.s655-g1139: 'هر آنکس که' is a free relative 'whoever/anyone who'; 'یابد' is a 3rd‑person verb meaning 'finds/obtains' (here: 'gains/learns'); 'ز من آگهی' is a prepositional phrase 'information/news from me' where the poetic preposition 'ز' = 'from' precedes 'من'.
segments.s655-g1140: 'ازین پس' is a fixed temporal phrase 'from now on / after this' (here written as two tokens 'از' + 'ین' + 'پس'); 'نجوید' is a negative jussive/present form 'should not seek' (3rd pl form used generically); 'کلاه مهی' is the object 'moon‑like cap/crown' with the adjective 'مهی' following the noun in poetic order.
segments.s655-g1141: The phrase 'همه کهتری را' ('all the lesser ones' with object marker را) is fronted for emphasis; 'بسازند' is a 3rd‑person plural verb meaning 'they make/set/appoint' and 'کار' is the resulting activity/task — together, 'they put all the lesser ones to work' (poetic inversion of object and predicate).
segments.s655-g1142: The negated verb 'ندارد' (literally 'does not have') precedes the indefinite subject 'کسی' producing the meaning 'no one has ...'; 'زهره کارزار' is a compressed genitival compound (zahr/fortune of battle) functioning as the object of ندارد — a poetic way to say 'no one has the (proper) fortune/aptitude for battle.'
segments.s655-g1143: 'به کوه اندرون' is a locative phrase ('into / in the inner part of the mountain'); the coordinated subjects 'مرغ و ماهی' ('bird and fish') are then located by 'بر آب' ('on the water') — poetic juxtaposition places the locative phrase before the nouns for emphasis.
segments.s655-g1144: 'چو' introduces a temporal clause 'when'; 'من خفته باشم' is 'when I am asleep' with 'باشم' as the 1st‑person subjunctive used after the temporal particle; 'نجویند' is 3rd‑person plural present negative 'they do not seek' and 'خواب' is the object 'sleep.' The verb follows the subject phrase in typical Persian clause order, here in an elevated/poetic register.
segments.s655-g449: همه دشت functions as a locative/quantifying phrase meaning 'throughout the plain' (همه here = 'whole, entire'). ژوپین‌زن و نیزه‌دار is a coordinated noun phrase ('zhupin‑clad women and spearmen') that serves as the nominal subject/topic of the line; Persian coordination is simple conjunction without an ezāfe. Word order places the quantifier/locative first, then the coordinated nouns.
segments.s655-g450: به یک‌سو ... به یک‌سو is an adverbial repetition meaning 'on one side ... on the other side'; به introduces the directional/adverbial phrase ('towards/on'), یک‌سو = 'one side'. پیاده and سوار are short nominal/adjectival words indicating mode of fighting ('on foot/infantry' and 'riders/horsemen'); the phrase is elliptical (no verb), listing dispositions.
segments.s655-g451: فرستاده‌گان را uses را as a definite/topic marker attached to the plural noun 'envoys/messengers' (the clause is nominal and verbless in the verse). ز هر کشوری is a prepositional phrase introduced by the poetic form ز (= از) meaning 'from', with هر کشوری 'from every country/land'.
segments.s655-g452: This continues the origin-list: ز 'from' governs both coordinated nouns. هر is distributive ('each/every'), and نامداری و هر مهتری are coordinated items meaning 'every notable' and 'every chief/elder' — the preposition z is understood to apply to both.
segments.s655-g453: شگفت آمد is a verbal phrase 'wonder/marvel came' (شگفت functioning as a noun 'wonder' and آمد, past tense 'came'). از introduces the source: 'from his لشکر (host/army) and ساز (instruments/music)'; اوی is a pronominal suffix ('his') referring back to the commander. The clause is compact and poetic.
segments.s655-g454: همان is a demonstrative/adverbial 'that same' modifying the coordinated nouns چهره و نام وآواز ('face/appearance, name, and آواز'); اوی is again the pronominal suffix 'his', attached after the coordination to mark possession of the whole list. In Persian poetry a single pronoun frequently follows a string of coordinated nouns.
segments.s655-g455: فرستادگان یک به دیگر is a reciprocal expression 'the envoys one to another' (یک used for 'one' + به دیگر 'to another'). به راز is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in secret/privately'; here به forms the adverbial construction with راز ('secret').
segments.s655-g456: بگفتند is past third‑person plural 'they said'. What follows is reported speech: کین here functions as an archaic demonstrative/particle introducing the statement ('this/that'); this use is not the lexical sense 'vengeance' listed in the provided options (so it is flagged below). شاه گردن‌فراز is a nominal phrase 'the proud king' (شاه + adjectival compound گردن‌فراز = 'proud/lofty').
segments.s655-g897: همی‌کرد is the archaic/habitual past auxiliary meaning 'kept doing' or 'would do' and here gives a continuous past sense; موبد is the subject (mōbed, Zoroastrian priest). به + اختر is a prepositional complement 'at/to the star(s)' and نگاه is the object 'gaze' placed after the prepositional phrase (poetic word order: auxiliary + subject + prepositional phrase + object).
segments.s655-g898: This nominal phrase lists the concerns the priest was watching the heavens for. زکردار is an epithet meaning 'of good/virtuous conduct' modifying خاقان ('khaghan'); و simply links the two nouns; پیوند شاه is a compound 'the king's bond/union' (پیوند used as 'bond, tie'). Note there is no ezāfe particle written; poetic noun phrases often omit or compress connectors.
segments.s655-g899: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' modifying the speech verb; گفت here functions as the past-tense verb 'said' (the supplied senses did not include 'said' so no exact sense index is available). فرجام is personified as the speaker (i.e. 'Fate'/'Outcome') and کای شهریار is a vocative: کای = 'O' addressing شهریار 'king'.
segments.s655-g900: دلت را is 'your heart' formed from دل + 2nd-person enclitic ت, plus را as the definite/object marker. ببد is an archaic form related to 'become/ be' (here jussive/optative sense 'let (it) be'); هیچ 'at all' negates intensity; رنجه is a verbal/adjectival noun meaning 'troubled / distressed', and مدار is an imperative/negative particle here (classical usage) signaling 'do not' — this last sense is not among the provided lexical senses, so no exact index is given for مدار.
segments.s655-g901: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause 'for/that'; این کار = 'this deed/affair' (کار here = 'deed/affair'); جز بر بهی نگذرد is a negative-except construction: جز = 'except', بر بهی = 'upon/for the good' (بهی = 'good/fortunate'), and نگذرد (from گذشتن) is a present/future-oriented verb 'will not pass/come to pass' — together 'this will occur for none except the good.'
segments.s655-g902: This terse clause states the failure of hostile counsel. ببد is the past/inasmuch-as copular/auxiliary 'was/it became' (here roughly 'it was that'); رای means 'plan/counsel' and is being negated by نسپرد, a past negative of سپردن 'to entrust/commit' (i.e. 'did not entrust/put into effect'). دشمن جهان is a genitive phrase 'enemy of the world' modifying رای: 'the counsel of the world's enemy did not take effect.'
segments.s655-g903: چنینست is the fixed copular/adverbial phrase 'it is thus' or 'such is'; راز is the nominative/complement 'secret' and سپهر بلند 'the high/heavenly sky' is the genitival/attributive noun phrase 'of the lofty heavens' (بلند 'lofty/high' postpositively qualifying سپهر).
segments.s655-g904: همان here is a pointing/demonstrative 'that very' or 'the same'; گردش اختر is 'the turning/circuit of the star' (گردش = rotation/circuit, اختر = star) and سودمند is a postpositive adjective 'beneficial/profitable' qualifying either اختر or the whole phrase (i.e. 'the beneficent star' or 'the star's beneficent course').
segments.s655-g1105: که opens a subordinate/result clause ('so that' / 'that'); از وی is an ablative/agent phrase 'through/from him' (از + وی). زمین is the grammatical subject 'the land'; داد is a direct object meaning 'justice'; بیند is a 3rd-person singular present of دیدن ('sees'); کنون is a final adverb 'now' modifying the verb.
segments.s655-g1106: نبینیم is a negated 1st-person-plural present/subjunctive form of دیدن ('we do not / may not see'); رنج is the noun 'suffering' as its object; ونه is the coordinating negative 'and not / nor' linking the next clause; ریزیم is 1st-person-plural of ریختن ('we spill/pour'), here used with خون 'blood' as its object.
segments.s655-g1107: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that / afterwards' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'after/then'); the following ز marks origin or source in a poetic register; هیتال، وترک، وختن are presented as names or agents listed after ز (the three items form the source/participants).
segments.s655-g1108: به is the preposition 'to/into' introducing the destination; گلزریون is a proper name/group; برشدند is a past plural verb 'they rose/assembled/advanced' (بر-شدند); انجمن is the noun 'assembly/meeting' as the result or location of that action.
segments.s655-g1109: به هر سو is a locative phrase 'to every side / everywhere'; که introduces a relative/subordinate clause ('where / that'); بد modifies موبدی as 'bad/ill' and موبدی is the title 'móbed' (Zoroastrian priest); کاردان follows as an adjective/noun 'skilled, expert', forming part of a catalogue of people.
segments.s655-g1110: This segment lists qualities or types joined by و: ردی (an epithet here, literally 'worthless' in the lexicon), پاک ('pure'), وهشیار ('and alert/wise' — وهشیار = و + هشیار), و connects items, and بسیاردان is a compound adjective 'very learned'; the phrase functions as further descriptions of the people present.
segments.s655-g1111: ز پیران is an ablative/partitive phrase 'from / among the elders'; هرآنکس که ('whoever that') introduces a free relative; بد modifies رای‌زن, so بد رای‌زن = 'one who gives bad counsel' or 'bad counsellor', the predicate of the relative clause.
segments.s655-g1112: بروبر is an adverb/adverbial phrase 'face-to-face / directly'; ز ترکان marks the other party 'with/from the Turks'; شدند is the past plural verb 'they became / they were' (here functioning as 'an assembly was formed' intransitively); انجمن is the nominative/complement 'assembly/meeting' that came into being.
segments.s655-g1089: جهان is the subject 'the world'. سوی داد is a prepositional phrase 'toward justice' (سوی = toward + داد = 'justice'). آمد is the simple past 'came/turned' and governs the clause; the following و ایمنی ('and safety') is coordinated as a second nominal result/predicate (poetic ellipsis of a copula or result: 'the world came toward justice and (toward) safety'). Word order is the older Persian N + PP + V, with a coordinated noun after the conjunction.
segments.s655-g1090: ز بد is an ablative/cause phrase 'from/ because of evil' (ز used poetically for 'from'). بسته شد is a passive/resultative compound: بسته (bound/closed) + شد (became/was) = 'was bound'. دست آهرمنی literally 'the hand of Ahriman' — here دست functions idiomatically as 'power/control' of Ahriman. Word order places the cause before the passive verb as is common in classical style.
segments.s655-g1091: چوخاقان functions here as a fused temporal/nominal phrase 'چو خاقان' = 'when (he was) khaghan' or 'as khaghan' (an archaic/poetic circumstantial). جهان بستد is a past transitive clause 'he closed/shut the world' (بستد = closed). از یزدگرد marks the agent/source 'by Yezdgerd' (classical use of از to indicate 'by').
segments.s655-g1092: ببد is an archaic past copula/auxiliary 'it was' / 'he was'. تیزدستی is a nominal phrase 'nimble‑handedness' or 'dexterity'. برآورد is a verb meaning 'brought forth/shown; accomplished' (here 'displayed'). گرد is used adverbially ('around/about' or 'everywhere') indicating the extent of the action: 'he displayed dexterity everywhere.'
segments.s655-g1093: بیامد is the simple past 'came/arrived'. جهاندار is a title 'sovereign/king' placed before the proper name. بهرام گور is the personal name with epithet: بهرام (name) + گور (here the epithet 'Gur' meaning 'onager'), a fixed poetic name form.
segments.s655-g1094: ازو is the adverbial/pronominal source 'from him / because of him'. گشت is the past of گشتن 'became/turned' and here introduces a nominal predicate (گشت خاقان = 'became khaghan'). پر + از introduces the content of fullness: پر از درد و شور = 'full of pain and passion' (پر = 'full (of)', از = 'of', درد and شور are the nouns). Note: the clause is compact/poetic and 'ازو' marks the source or cause that made the khaghan filled with pain and passion.
segments.s655-g1095: شد is the past 'became/was'. از داد او is an ablative/cause phrase 'because of his justice' (از marks cause; داد = 'justice'; او = 'his'). شهرها چون بهشت is the subject + simile 'the cities (became) like paradise' (چون = 'like/as', بهشت = 'paradise'). The verb appears before the causal phrase as is common in elevated diction.
segments.s655-g1096: پراگنده شد is a passive/resultative turn 'were scattered/dispelled' (پراگنده = 'scattered/widespread' used here in the sense 'dispersed'). کار ناخوب و زشت is the affected domain 'bad and ugly deeds' (کار = 'deed/affair', ناخوب = 'bad', زشت = 'vile'). Together: 'bad and vile deeds were scattered/dispelled.'
segments.s655-g841: درحجره (compound) = “in/at the chamber” (در + حجره). بگشاد is an older past form with the prosthetic b- (ب‑گشاد) meaning ‘opened’ (past, 3rd sing. implicit subject). و اندر شدند links with a second action: اندر = “into / within” and شدند is the past 3rd‑pl. of شدن used intransitively here (‘they became / went’) so together و اندر شدند = “and they went inside.” Word order is noun/place → verb → conjunction + prepositional phrase + verb.
segments.s655-g842: پرستندگان is the plural noun ‘devotees / worshippers’ (here best read as a group of admirers/attendants). داستانها is the plural ‘tales / stories’ (object). زدند is the past 3rd‑pl. of زدن; in poetic/narrative contexts زدن often means ‘to perform/recite/utter’ as well as ‘to strike’, so here it means ‘they began to tell/perform the tales.’
segments.s655-g843: که introduces a subordinate/relative clause. آن را که اکنون تو بینی = “that which you now see” (آن + enclitic/object marker را + relative راکه/که). اکنون تو بینی is a present clause ‘you now see’. بداد is an archaic past/formative verb (here best read as a past verbal turn with prosthetic b‑, related to دادن/دادن‑type forms) and functions as the verbal core of the clause; the clause continues into the next segment which gives who has not seen it.
segments.s655-g844: A paratactic list of agents who have not seen the thing previously mentioned: ستاره ندیدست و خورشید و باد = ‘the star has not seen it, nor the sun, nor the wind.’ ندیدست is a negative perfect/past form (‘has not been seen / did not see’) and the nouns are enumerated with و between them; the object is the phrase from the previous segment.
segments.s655-g845: شبستان is the noun ‘chamber/inner room’ (subject). بهشتی is an adjective ‘heavenly’ modifying it. شد is the past 3rd‑sing. of شدن ‘became’ and introduces the resulting state, and آراسته is a predicative past participle/adjective ‘adorned’ describing the chamber after شدن. The order is noun + modifier → copular/become verb → predicate adjective.
segments.s655-g846: پر از = the idiom ‘full of’ (پر + preposition از). The following nouns list what fills the place: ماه و خورشید = ‘moon and sun’ (poetic imagery). پرخواسته is a compound adjective ‘much‑desired / much sought after’ qualifying the contents implicitly (‘full of moon and sun and much‑desired [things]’).
segments.s655-g847: پری چهره is a compound noun/adjective phrase ‘fairy‑faced’ (a very beautiful person). بر گاه = ‘upon the seat/throne’ (بر = on; گاه here used as place/seat in poetic register). بنشست is past 3rd‑sing. ‘sat down / took her seat.’ پنج appears here as the numeral that the next segment specifies (i.e. there were five in all), so the clause ends with the seating of the fairy‑faced one and the numeral picks up the following description.
segments.s655-g848: همه = ‘all / everyone’ (referring to the five). برسران تاج is a compact phrase meaning ‘crowns upon their heads’ (برسران expresses location ‘on the heads of’ + تاج ‘crown’). و در زیر گنج = ‘and beneath (them) [was] treasure’; در زیر is the locative compound ‘under/beneath’, here indicating that treasure lay below. The copular verb ‘was’ is implied in poetic telegraphic style.
segments.s655-g697: همه (quantifier) precedes the noun دشمنان (plural) to mean 'all/enemies collectively.' پیش تو is a prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of you'; the copula بنده‌اند is third-person plural (-اند) meaning 'they are servants' or 'they are subject to you.' Word order is typical Persian: quantifier + noun + prepositional phrase, then predicate.
segments.s655-g698: وگر is a conditional particle 'if/and if' introducing a concessive clause. کهتری here describes 'lesser/of lower rank' (an adjectival/nominal idea). راسرافگنده‌اند is a poetic compound verb in the third-person plural (‑اند) meaning 'they are extravagant' or 'they show wasteful abundance'; the verb follows the subject phrase as usual.
segments.s655-g699: همه بیم places the quantifier 'all/everyone' with the noun 'fear' to express 'everyone feared.' زان introduces the source ('from that') and لشکر چاج names the army (چاج here is a proper name). بود is the past copula 'was,' so the clause literally reads 'there was fear from that army (of) Chāj.'
segments.s655-g700: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of.' خاقان is the subject ('the khagan'); که is a relative/causal clause marker 'who/for that,' introducing the reason. با ... و با ... بود is a serial prepositional phrase 'was with treasure and with the crown' (با = 'with'); بود is the copula in past tense indicating possession/attribute.
segments.s655-g701: به فر is an instrumental/causal phrase 'by/with the farr (royal/divine glory)'; in classical Persian the preposition به can indicate 'by means of.' شهنشاه شد is 'the Shahanshah became' (شد = become); نیک‌خواه is a predicative adjective/noun 'well-wisher' or 'benevolent' describing the resulting state. The verb شد places the predicate after the subject, a common classical order.
segments.s655-g702: همی is an archaic/classical verbal prefix/adverb meaning 'continually/always' and marks habitual action here. راه جوید is the verb phrase 'seeks/seeks a way' (جوید = present/habitual in classical style). به نزدیک شاه is a directional phrase 'toward/near the king' (به + adjective نَزدیک + noun شاه), showing the aim of the seeking.
segments.s655-g703: هرآنکس که دارد is a standard relative clause 'whoever has/possesses.' ز introduces origin/association 'from among' (ز گردان = 'from the ranks/company'); گردان خرد is a genitive grouping 'the troop/ranks of wisdom' or figuratively 'the host of the wise' (گردان used metaphorically as 'ranks' or 'company'). دارد is present indicative 'has.'
segments.s655-g704: تن آسانی و راستی lists the things that are produced/maintained: تن 'the body' + آسانی 'ease/comfort' and راستی 'truth/uprightness.' پرورد is the verb 'nurtures/fosters' (classical present form); verbs commonly appear at or near the end of the clause in Ferdowsi's verse.
segments.s655-g1641: بپرسید و گفت is a sequence of past-tense actions (‘he asked and said’); بپرسید is a past form ‘asked’ and is followed by و (and) linking گفت, but گفت here functions as the verb ‘said’ introducing direct speech. ای is the vocative particle ‘O!’ placed before the address, and خرد یافته is a compound adjective: خرد (wisdom) + یافته (a past participle ‘found/acquired’), literally ‘wisdom-found’ = ‘wise, learned.’
segments.s655-g1642: هنرها is the plural noun ‘arts/skills.’ یک اندر دگر بافته is a poetic phrase: یک ‘one’ + اندر دگر ‘into another/one within another’ + بافته (past participle of بافتن) ‘woven.’ Together it describes arts mutually interwoven or ‘arts woven into one another,’ with بافته functioning adjectivally/predicatively to characterize هنرها.
segments.s655-g1643: چه دانی is an interrogative phrase (‘how/what do you know’), where چه has an exclamatory/interrogative flavor and دانی is the present/past-root form ‘you know/know’ (classical word order places the verb after the question word). کزو = از + که contracted into a single pronominal/relational form ‘from whom/from which,’ تن here means ‘person/man,’ بود is the past copula ‘was,’ and سودمند is the predicate adjective ‘useful, beneficial.’ The clause asks rhetorically how one could know from whom benefit came.
segments.s655-g1644: همان is a demonstrative ‘that same/that very one’ referring back to the person in question. بر دل هر کسی is a prepositional phrase ‘upon the heart of every person’ (بر + دل + هر کسی), and ارجمند is an adjectival predicate ‘esteemed/precious.’ The copula (بود) is omitted here, so the phrase reads ‘that very one is dear to every heart.’
segments.s655-g1645: چنین داد پاسخ = ‘thus he gave an answer’ (چنین ‘thus/so’, داد ‘gave’ used as the verb, پاسخ ‘answer’). The following که introduces the content clause (reported speech or the proposition being given). ناتندرست is an adjective meaning ‘unwell/unsound’ and functions as the predicate of that content clause.
segments.s655-g1646: The second که here is causal (‘for/because’): it introduces the reason. دل را uses را as the definite/direct-object marker applied to دل (‘the heart’), جز از = ‘except/from nothing but,’ شادمانی = ‘joy,’ and نجست is the past form of جستن here meaning ‘sought’ (so the clause means ‘for the heart sought nothing but joy’). In context this gives the reason the speaker calls him ‘unsound.’
segments.s655-g1647: چو is the temporal conjunction ‘when.’ از درد = ‘from/of pain.’ روزی here is a poetic adverb meaning ‘for a while/one day’ (classical usage), and بسستی is an archaic/poetic past-form meaning ‘you were at rest/relieved/ceased’ (derived from بسیدن); بود is the past copula ‘was.’ Together the clause gives a temporal setting: ‘When for a time you ceased from pain…’. Note: روزی and بسستی are poetically used forms and differ from modern colloquial forms.
segments.s655-g1648: همه آرزو is the subject phrase ‘all/whoever’s desire’ or simply ‘every wish.’ تندرستی is the predicate noun ‘health/well‑being,’ and بود is the copula ‘was.’ The line states that once pain was relieved, all one’s wishes were directed toward health.
segments.s655-g209: چه is the interrogative 'what'. بینید is 2nd-person plural present (you see / do you see) used here to address the audience; word order is verb before adverbial phrases as is common in classical verse. یکسر and کنون are adverbs ('all at once' / 'now') and اندرین ('among these' / 'in this') localizes the question to the present situation.
segments.s655-g210: چه again introduces a second rhetorical question 'what'. سازیم is 1st-person plural present/subjunctive ('shall we do' / 'can we do') used deliberatively ('what shall we do'). با is the preposition 'with/about', followed by ترک ('Turk' / the Turks) and وخاقان چین 'and the Khaghan (ruler) of China' (وخاقان is the title, چین is the place).
segments.s655-g211: بزرگان is the plural noun 'the great/elders'. داننده is a participle 'knowing' used attributively (postpositive adjective) modifying بزرگان. برخاستند is a past, 3rd-person-plural verb 'they rose / stood up'.
segments.s655-g212: همه 'all/everyone' quantifies the subject; پاسخش is 'their/his answer' (پاسخ with the 3rd-singular possessive suffix -ش). را is the definite-object marker placed after the possessed noun, and بیاراستند is the past 3rd-plural of آراستن 'to arrange/compose/prepare', here 'they fashioned/composed (the reply)'.
segments.s655-g213: گرفتند is past 3rd-plural 'they took up / began' (idiomatically 'they burst into ...'). یک سر is an adverbial phrase 'all at once' / 'together'. برو آفرین is an exclamatory phrase; آفرین is the acclamation 'bravo' or 'well done' and here functions as the shouted praise (برو is part of the idiomatic exclamation).
segments.s655-g214: که‌ای introduces the words they cried: که here works as a quotative/relativizing particle and ای is the vocative 'O' attached to شاه 'king'. نیک 'good/auspicious' and اختر 'star' are epithets (اختر is metaphorical 'star' = fortunate/illustrious), و is the conjunction 'and', پاکدین is a compound epithet 'pure-in-faith / pious'.
segments.s655-g215: همه 'all' quantifies مرز هیتال 'the border/frontier of Hital' (مرز + proper name in apposition/genitive). آهرمنند is a predicative adjective/description meaning 'are like Ahriman' / 'are demonic'; the -ند ending marks 3rd-person plural.
segments.s655-g216: دورویند is an adjectival verb 'they are two-faced/duplicitous' (3rd-plural). واین = و + این 'and this'; the clause 'این مرز را دشمنند' uses مرز را with را as a topical/definite marker and دشمنند as the predicate 'they are enemies (of it)'. The implied subject in this second clause is the same group mentioned before.
segments.s655-g1361: مکن is the negative imperative 'do not' placed before the vocative شهریارا ('O king') — شهریارا is the vocative form addressing the king. گنه is the object 'sin'; تا توان ('as far as you can') is an adverbial phrase where تا has the sense 'as far as' and توان is ability (auxiliary sense), so the whole chunk reads 'Do not, O king, sin as far as you are able.'
segments.s655-g1362: بویژه = 'especially' (introductory adverb). کزو is the relative/prepositional 'from whom / in whom' introducing a subordinate description; شرم دارد روان is a poetic, slightly inverted order meaning 'the soul has shame/modesty' (normally روان شرم دارد). دارد is the present/regular 'has'. Together: 'especially in the case of one whose soul possesses modesty.'
segments.s655-g1363: The two nouns بی‌آزاری ('harmlessness/inoffensiveness') و سودمندی ('and usefulness') are given and followed by the imperative گزین ('choose' — an older/poetic imperative of گزیدن/گزینیدن). The word order (nouns then verb) is a common poetic command formation: 'Choose harmlessness and usefulness.'
segments.s655-g1364: که introduces an explanatory or causal clause ('for'/'that'), اینست is the contracted poetic form of این است ('this is'). فرهنگ here means the teaching/proper conduct (sense 'culture/learning'), followed by the coordinate آیین و دین ('ritual/rite and religion') specifying the domain; the clause reads 'for this is the teaching/propriety of ritual and religion.'
segments.s655-g1365: ز من = 'from me' (ز is the poetic preposition 'from'); یادگارست is the fused predicate یادگار است ('is a keepsake/memento') contracted in poetry; چندی سخن = 'a short/some speech' (چندی used as 'a little/some'). Altogether: 'A short saying is a keepsake from me.'
segments.s655-g1366: گمانم is the speaker's 'I think' / 'my belief' (گمان + enclitic ـم) introducing the following clause. که = 'that'; هرگز نگردد کهن uses poetic/old-fashioned verb form نگردد (negated stem ن‑ + گردد) meaning 'will not become' or 'does not become' (subjunctive/optative sense used for future/result), and کهن = 'old/ancient'. So: 'I think that it will never become old.'
segments.s655-g1367: چو = 'when' introducing a temporal clause. بگشاد is an archaic/past form meaning 'opened' or 'unfolded' (here likely 'began to speak' by metonymy), used poetically; روشن‌دل is a compound adjective 'clear‑hearted/bright‑minded' preceding شهریار ('the king'), so the phrase is 'when the clear‑hearted king opened (i.e. began).'
segments.s655-g1368: فروان modifies سخن کرد: فروان ('abundantly') + سخن ('speech') + کرد (past of 'to do' used with سخن to mean 'spoke'). زو ('from/of him' — z + o) + خواستار ('desirous') form a postposed adjectival/relational phrase 'desirous of him' referring to the addressee; overall: 'he spoke copiously, desirous of him.'
segments.s655-g257: کسی را نبد آرزوی is an archaic negative existential statement. نبد (old form of نبود) is past negative 'was not / there was not', and کسی را (with را marking the person phrase) functions like 'anyone/no one' as the scope of the negation. گرد رزم here links the desire to the activity — 'desire for (going into/about) battle.' Word order places the verb (نبد) before its arguments, typical of older Persian.
segments.s655-g258: به بزم و بناز اندرون کرده خوی is a result clause describing a turned disposition. به + noun expresses direction/tendency ('toward feasting and pride'), اندرون qualifies the locus as 'within/indoors', کرده is the past participle of کردن used here as a perfective ('had made/turned'), and خوی is the direct object 'disposition/habit'. The sequence is typical: prepositional phrase (به بزم و بناز) + locative (اندرون) + compound predicate (کرده خوی).
segments.s655-g259: بدانست is a past-tense verb 'knew/learned'. In classical Persian the verb may come before its subject: here شاه جهان ('king of the world') is the subject. کدخدای functions as the source/agent from whom the knowledge was obtained (object or source noun), so the clause reads 'the world-king learned from the kadhkhoda'.
segments.s655-g260: که introduces a content clause ('that/what'). اندر دل = 'in the heart(s)' (locative), بخردان is the plural/relational form meaning 'the wise' (wise men), چی + ست (چیست) = 'what is' (interrogative/copula), and رای = 'opinion/counsel'. Together the clause means 'what counsel was in the hearts of the wise.'
segments.s655-g261: چنین داد پاسخ = 'he gave such an answer / he answered thus': داد is the past tense 'gave', with پاسخ as its object. The following که introduces the quoted content. یزدان سپاس is an inversion typical in poetry meaning 'praise be to God' (سپاس = praise/thanks placed after the divine name).
segments.s655-g262: کزو = 'from whom' (relative/ablative referring back to یزدان), دارم is present tense 'I have' (here a present/factual verb), اندر دو گیتی = 'in the two worlds' (a poetic expression for both worlds/life and afterlife), and هراس = 'fear'. The clause literally says 'from whom I have fear' — i.e. 'whom I fear in both worlds.'
segments.s655-g263: که introduces a report clause ('that'). ایشان = 'they'. نجستند here is jussive/past with negative prefix ن- meaning 'they did not seek' or more idiomatically 'they did nothing but'; جست(ن) can mean 'seek' or 'strive', so نجستند جز ... = 'they sought nothing except ...'. جز = 'except', خواب = 'sleep', وخورد = 'and ate' (و+خورد; خورد is past 'ate'). The segment thus means 'they did (nothing) but sleep and eat.'
segments.s655-g264: فراموش کردند is the compound past 'they forgot' (فراموش + کردند). گرد here is an older prepositional/adverbial marker meaning 'concerning/about' or 'around', and نبرد = 'battle/struggle'. The phrase means 'they forgot about the battle' (i.e. they neglected fighting).
segments.s655-g1465: همیشه (an adverb) prefaces the noun phrase to mark habituality: “always/ever”. خردمند و امیدوار is a coordinated adjective/noun phrase (خردمند “wise” and امیدوار “hopeful”), which in poetry can function as adjectives or as substantives (“the wise and the hopeful”). Word order is typical Persian: adverb + noun/adjective phrase.
segments.s655-g1466: نبیند is the third person singular present (classical/poetic) of دیدن with the negative prefix ن- here meaning “does not see” or “never sees.” جز ... از is the limited-exceptive construction: جز (except) + از (from/of) + شادی روزگار (the joy(s) of the times). روزگار here designates the state/“times” rather than a single calendar day.
segments.s655-g1467: نیندیشد is the third person singular present (classical poetic form) of اندیشیدن with the negative prefix ن-: “he does not think/does not consider.” از کار بد یک زمان groups the preposition از + object ‘کار بد’ (“bad deed”) and a temporal phrase یک زمان (“for one moment/one instant”); the whole segment says he does not give a moment’s thought to an evil deed.
segments.s655-g1468: ره راست is a noun phrase meaning “the straight/right way” (ره = way; راست = straight/right). گیرد / نگیرد are present-tense third-person forms of گرفتن arranged in contrast (he may take / he may not take) — a poetic coordination that gives an alternation or uncertainty; کمان is the object ‘bow’ placed after the verbs for emphasis. The line therefore ties the idea of path (ره راست) and the act of taking/upholding (گرفتن) with the bow (کمان) in a compact, poetic way.
segments.s655-g1469: دگر here functions as a discourse adverb “furthermore/else/again.” هر که خشنود باشد به گنج is a relative clause: هر که = “whoever,” خشنود باشد is a present/subjunctive-like form of بودن (“is/ be”) used in poetry to mean “is content,” and به گنج marks the domain/instrument “with treasure.” The clause sets up ‘whoever is satisfied with wealth.’
segments.s655-g1470: نیازد is the present of نیازیدن, here read as ‘has no need’ / ‘does not need’ in the poetic sense (context makes it negative/privative: one who is content does not require more). نیارد in the manuscript/poetic usage is a paired verb form that follows to complete the clause; taken together with the following object تنش را به رنج it yields ‘(he) does not need, (he) does not bring/subject his body to hardship.’ تنش را is the definite object (‘his body’) marked by را, and به رنج specifies the state ‘to hardship.’ (Note: نیارد’s given lexical senses do not match its poetic use here; the reading is idiomatic and the sense mapping is therefore left unspecified below.)
segments.s655-g1471: کسی کو is a poetic/archaic relative construction: کسی = ‘one/someone’ and کو here functions like a relative/oblique marker “for whom/whose” (classical usage different from the modern interrogative کو ‘where’). به گنج و درم is a prepositional phrase ‘with/to treasure and dirham (coin)’ and ننگرد is the third person present negative/privative form from نگریستن/نگریدن meaning ‘does not look at / does not regard.’ The segment says ‘one who does not regard treasure and coin.’
segments.s655-g1472: همه روز او is the time-focused subject phrase ‘all his days’ (همه modifies روز; او = he). بر خوشی is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘in/over happiness’ (بر + noun used poetically for ‘in the state of’). بگذرد is the third-person present/subjunctive poetic verb ‘passes/spends’; the whole segment means ‘all his days pass in happiness.’
segments.s655-g185: به فرجام هیتال is a prepositional/time phrase: به + فرجام ('to/at the end of') with هیتال specifying whose/which end. برگشته شد is an intransitive/predicative construction in the past (برگشته + شد as auxiliary), meaning 'returned' or 'were returned' — here an impersonal/past reportive: 'they returned'.
segments.s655-g186: دو بهره = 'two shares/parts' (two groups/divisions). مگر here functions adversatively ('however/but'); خسته و کشته شد uses two past participles/adjectives (خسته 'weary', کشته 'slain') followed by شد as the past auxiliary, giving 'were exhausted and were slain.'
segments.s655-g187: بدان is an archaic/contracted locution (به + آن ≈ 'by/with that'); نامداری is the noun 'renown/fame' governed by it. که introduces a relative clause specifying that renown ('which Hital had'); هیتال بود is the simple past copula 'Hital was/possessed (it).' Together: 'by that renown which Hital had.'
segments.s655-g188: جهانی here denotes 'the world' (subject); پر از = 'full of' (پر followed by از + noun). گرز و کوپال are the items filling it ('maces and cudgels'), and بود is the past copula 'was.' The whole means 'the world was full of maces and cudgels.'
segments.s655-g189: شگفتست is a predicative adjective 'it is astonishing/wondrous.' کآمد is a contracted sequence meaning 'that came' (که آمد), بریشان = بر + ایشان 'upon them', and شکست is the noun 'defeat' — 'it is astonishing that defeat came upon them.'
segments.s655-g190: سپهبد is the subject/title 'spahbed' (the general). مباد is an optative/negative particle 'may it not be' (a wish that something not happen). ایچ intensifies the negation ('not at all' / 'God forbid'), and با رای پست is an instrumental/qualifying phrase 'with/base counsel' — literally 'with base counsel.' Altogether: a wish that the spahbed not be guided by base counsel.
segments.s655-g191: اگر introduces a counterfactual condition 'if.' غاتفر is a proper name (the person in question). داشتی is the past-tense form used for the hypothetical ('had' / counterfactual possession). نام و رای = 'name (reputation) and counsel' — two qualities whose possession is being supposed.
segments.s655-g192: نبردی here is the verbal idea 'would not have carried/taken' (نب- + بردی), forming the counterfactual result clause. سپهر ('the sky/heavens') functions as the agent (a poetic personification), آن سپه را is the definite object 'that army' (آن + سپه + را), and ز جای = 'from its place.' The clause says the heavens would not have removed that army from its place.
segments.s655-g1577: چنین (thus) is a sentence adverb placing what follows as a reported utterance; گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (third person) and کان کس که is an archaic demonstrative + noun + relative clause meaning 'that one who...'. کوشاترست is a comparative adjectival predicate 'is more/most diligent' describing that person. The word order is typical classical Persian: adverb + verb + demonstrative phrase + relative clause describing the subject.
segments.s655-g1578: دو گوشش = 'his two ears' (دو + گوش + enclitic possessive ش). بدانش is the fused prepositional phrase 'به + آن + دانش' meaning 'to/for that knowledge' (i.e. 'to that knowledge'). نیوشاترست is a comparative form from نیوشیدن 'to listen/hear' with -تر- and the copular suffix -ست, meaning 'was more attentive/eager to listen'. The segment gives a predicate about the previously mentioned person.
segments.s655-g1579: بپرسید here is a past third‑person plural form 'they asked' (classical narrative). ازو = 'from him' (object pronoun), and شهریار جهان is an appositive noun phrase 'the king/ruler of the world' identifying the person addressed. The sequence is 'they asked of him — the world‑king'.
segments.s655-g1580: از + آگاهی = 'about / concerning knowledge' (از used to mean 'concerning' here). نیک و بد are the coordinated adjectives 'good and bad' modifying آگاهی. در نهان = 'in secret' or 'in hidden matters' (در + نهان). Together the phrase specifies the topic of the question: hidden knowledge of good and evil.
segments.s655-g1581: چنین داد پاسخ = 'thus gave an answer' (چنین 'thus', داد past of دادن 'gave', پاسخ 'answer' as object). که introduces the content clause ('that'). از آگهی here functions as 'from/with information' — a prepositional phrase indicating possession or source ('with abundant information').
segments.s655-g1582: فراوان بود = 'was abundant' (فراوان modifying آگهی; بود is the past copula). کژ is a predicate adjective 'crooked/wrong/perverse' linked to the clause by بود. مغزش تهی = 'his brain empty' is a nominal predicate (the verb 'was' is understood from بود earlier); تهی 'empty' describes مغز with the enclitic ش marking 'his'.
segments.s655-g1583: مگر introduces an exception or contrast ('except/only that'). آنک is an archaic relative/demonstrative 'that which/that' and گفتند is past plural 'they said'. خاکست جای is a verbless reported clause 'the place is ashes' (خاکست 'ashes', جای 'place'); classical Persian often omits the copula 'is' in such clauses.
segments.s655-g1584: ندانم = 'I do not know' (negative present). چه گویم = 'what I should say' where چه is interrogative 'what' and گویم is the present/subjunctive 'I say / I should say' used in indirect expression. ز دیگر سرای = 'of/about the other abode/realm' (ز = 'about/from', دیگر 'other', سرای 'abode/house/world'), completing the speaker's admission of ignorance about the other world.
segments.s655-g1705: چه ماند = an interrogative/nominal phrase 'what remained' with چه ('what') followed by the past verb ماند ('remained/was left'). بدو گفت puts the dative pronoun بدو ('to him') before گفت ('said'), which introduces the quoted content. جاوید چیز is the noun phrase 'an eternal thing' with adjective جاوید preceding the noun چیز as is usual in Persian poetry.
segments.s655-g1706: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause ('that'). آن چیز repeats the demonstrative + noun 'that thing.' کمی is an adverb 'a little/slightly.' نگیرد is the negative present/subjunctive form of گرفتن used here intransitively to mean 'is not diminished/does not take (away)' — the verb appears at the end as in conventional Persian clause order. به marks the agent/cause ('by/with') and نیز is an emphatic adverb 'also/even' (here: 'not even a little').
segments.s655-g1707: چنین داد پاسخ is a common poetic sequence 'thus gave (a) reply' with چنین ('thus/so') + داد ('gave') + پاسخ ('answer'). که introduces the content of that answer. انباز مرد is a noun phrase 'the (ambiguous/archaic) man' — انباز is an uncertain/archaic descriptor here followed by مرد ('man').
segments.s655-g1708: A triadic negative description: نه ... نه ... نه ... with three verbs. نه کاهد = 'does not decrease/become less' (کاهد here functions as a present/stative verb 'diminish'). نه سوزد = 'does not burn/ache' (سوزد in present sense 'suffers/burns'). نه ترسد ز درد = 'nor fears because of pain' with ز ('from/because of') + درد ('pain') marking the cause. The negatives precede each verb, a standard poetic enumeration.
segments.s655-g1709: چنین گفت = 'thus said.' کین جان دانا بود is a clause 'that soul was wise' where کین is an archaic/ambiguous item (not the common modern demonstrative), جان 'soul' is the subject and دانا 'wise' is the predicate adjective followed by بود (past 'was'). The sequence is standard subject–predicate with the copula at the end.
segments.s655-g1710: که introduces a relative clause 'which/that.' بر آرزوها توانا بود = 'was powerful over (his) desires' with بر + plur. آرزوها ('over/despite desires') and adjective توانا 'capable/powerful'; بود is the past copula. Word order is preposition + object + predicative adjective + copula.
segments.s655-g1711: بدو گفت = 'he said to him' with the dative pronoun بدو before گفت. شاه‌ای is a vocative 'O king' (شاه with the vocative -ای). خداوند مهر is an honorific/title sequence 'lord/sovereign of kindness' where خداوند functions as 'lord/sovereign' and مهر means 'kindness/affection' addressing the king respectfully.
segments.s655-g1712: چه باشد = a rhetorical interrogative 'what would be / what can be' with چه ('what') + باشد (subjunctive/potential 'be'). به پهنا = 'in breadth/extent' using به + پهنا; فزون از سپهر = 'greater than the sky' where فزون ('more/greater') takes از to mark the standard of comparison and سپهر 'the heavens/sky.' Overall this is a comparative rhetorical question.
segments.s655-g1401: بپرسید کسری is a V–S order typical of classical Persian: بپرسید is a past-tense/perfective verb 'asked' and کسری is the subject 'Kisra' following it. The following که آغازِ subordinate clause: که از کهتران introduces the source/party (از = 'from, of') and frames the subordinate question.
segments.s655-g1402: کرا is an archaic interrogative pronoun meaning 'who' (object form). باشد here is a 3rd-person-form used in indirect/question clauses (subjunctive/episodic 'is/may be') linking the question to the predicate. اندیشه مهتران is a noun phrase 'the counsel/thought of the chiefs' with اندیشه ('intention, counsel') governed by the following noun مهتران ('the great/elders'), even though the Ezāfe is not written.
segments.s655-g1403: چنین گفت is 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus/so'); classical word order places the adverb/phrase before the verb. کان کس که here is an archaic relativizing phrase meaning 'the one who' or 'that man who' (کان + کس + که), with داناترست ('more learned') predicated of that person.
segments.s655-g1404: بهر آرزو is a prepositional phrase 'with regard to / for desire' (بهر = 'for, regarding', آرزو = 'desire'). بر before تواناترست is the preposition linking the comparison; تواناترست is a comparative/superlative-form adjective 'was more capable/powerful'. The whole clause states a comparative relation: with respect to desire he surpassed the powerful.
segments.s655-g1405: کدامست دانا is an interrogative/relative opening 'which/what wise (one)'; classical Persian often uses کدامست + noun to ask or rhetorically introduce a person. بدوشاه is a prepositional phrase 'to the king / before the shah' (به + شاه fused), and گفت is the verb 'said' (here placed after its indirect object phrase).
segments.s655-g1406: که introduces the subordinate content of what was said. دانش بود uses بود as the past/ascriptive copula 'was'. مرد را places 'the man' as a marked object/topic (را marks the definite object). درنهفت is a past/passive lexical verb 'was hidden' (literally 'was hidden/lay hidden'), so the clause reads 'that knowledge was hidden in/for the man' (i.e. knowledge lay hidden in the man).
segments.s655-g1407: چنین گفت again 'thus said'. کان کو is an archaic/poetic relativizer meaning 'he who / the one who' ( کان + کو ), and به فرمان دیو is an adverbial phrase 'at/by the demon's command' (به = 'by/at', فرمان = 'command', دیو = 'demon').
segments.s655-g1408: نپردازد is a negative present/subjunctive form meaning 'does not/ would not depart/stray' (ن‑ negation + پردازد). از راه کیهان is a prepositional phrase 'from the path of the cosmos/world' (از = 'from', راه = 'path/way', کیهان = 'cosmos/universe'). خدیو at the end is a vocative or address 'O Khedive' (ruler).
segments.s655-g617: ردان را is a topical/direct-object phrase (را marks the group being affected). پسند آمد is a fixed past idiom ‘to be pleasing’ (پسند + آمد ‘came to please’). این رای‌شاه is the logical subject placed after the verb for poetic emphasis: ‘this royal counsel’ (i.e. the king’s advice) was pleasing to the ردان.
segments.s655-g618: به آواز is an adverbial phrase meaning ‘with a voice / aloud’ (به here adverbial). گفتند is the past plural ‘they said’. کاین است راه is the reported clause introduced by the demonstrative कاین (that/this): ‘that this is the way’ — راه here = ‘way/method’.
segments.s655-g619: ز لشکر = ‘from the army’ (ز is the poetic preposition ‘from’). سه پرمایه را is the definite object ‘three substantial/important ones’ (را marks them). برگزید is the simple past ‘he selected/chose’ (subject is contextually the king).
segments.s655-g620: که introduces a relative/subordinate clause describing those chosen: ‘who…’. گویند و دانند are verbs used attributively here (‘who speak and who know’ — i.e. eloquent and wise). پاسخ شنید is the main verb of the clause ‘he heard (their) answer’; شنید is past ‘heard’ and پاسخ (‘answer’) is its object.
segments.s655-g621: درگنج is a locative compound ‘in the treasury’ (در+گنج). دینار is the direct object ‘dinar(s)/coins’. بگشاد here functions as a past action ‘he opened/released’ (the treasure/coins). و گفت links to the reported speech that follows: ‘and said…’.
segments.s655-g622: و گفت که introduces reported speech (that/‘…’). گوهر چرا باید اندر نهفت is a rhetorical question inside that speech: گوهر ‘a gem’, چرا ‘why’, باید modal ‘should/ought’, اندر ‘in/within’, نهفت ‘be hidden’ (here functioning as a passive/participial idea ‘be hidden’).
segments.s655-g623: اگر is the conditional ‘if’. نام را ‘the name/reputation’ is marked with را (object/topic). راباید is the fused form (را + باید) functioning as modal ‘ought/must’/‘if one seeks…’ in this idiomatic listing. و ننگ را links the contrasting noun ‘and disgrace/shame’ also marked by را — the whole phrase lists one set of values.
segments.s655-g624: وگر is the alternative conditional ‘but if / or if’. بخشش و رزم و آهنگ را is a coordinated list (‘generosity / battle / song’) with a single final را marking the whole series as the (alternative) object/topic of preference; آهنگ here = ‘melody/song’ (the entertainment/arts sense).
segments.s655-g169: به ایوان بیاراست جای نشست is best read as a locative phrase + descriptor + noun + verb: به ایوان 'to/into the iwan', بیاراست is a past/adjectival form 'well‑adorned' modifying ایوان, جای 'a place/seat', and نشست is the 3rd‑person past ('he sat' or 'took his seat'). Word order (prepositional phrase + modifier + noun + verb) is typical in poetry: the description of the place comes before the action of sitting.
segments.s655-g170: برفتند گردان خسروپرست is a short clause with the past plural verb first (a common poetic order): برفتند 'they departed/went away' (3rd person plural), گردان 'the troop/retinue' is the agent/topic that follows, and خسروپرست is a compound attributive noun/adjective 'Khosrow‑worshipping' modifying گردان.
segments.s655-g171: This is a sequence of priestly names/titles. ابا here is a proper name (not the verbal sense given in the mini‑lexicon), موبد 'mōbed' is the priestly title, موبدان (mobadan) functions as the title 'mobadan' (chief of priests / plural/title), and اردشیر is a personal name. The cluster should be read as names and titles in apposition.
segments.s655-g172: چوشاپور is a proper name (Chushapur). وچون here works as 'and (also)/and likewise' (a poetic connector: و + چون), introducing the next named person; یزدگرد is the name Yazdegerd and دبیر is his office/title 'secretary' or 'scribe' in apposition to that name.
segments.s655-g173: همان بخردان نماینده راه is a nominal group: همان 'those same/that very', بخردان (plural) 'the wise ones', نماینده 'representatives/agents', and راه 'way/road' (here roughly 'of the realm' or 'of the routes/regions' — a poetic phrase identifying their remit). The nouns are in apposition: 'the same wise representatives (of the realm/ways)'.
segments.s655-g174: نشستند یک سر بر تخت شاه: نشستند is the past plural verb 'they sat/settled', followed by the adverbial/distributive phrase یک سر 'one apiece/one each' and the locative بر تخت شاه 'upon the king's throne' (بر = on; تخت شاه = the king's throne). The order (verb then phrase) is typical poetic word order; the whole means they took their places at the king's throne.
segments.s655-g175: چنین گفت کسری که‌ای بخردان is built as 'چنین گفت کسری' = 'thus Khosrow said', followed by the formulaic address که ای بخردان = 'that—O wise ones' (که + ای here introduces a vocative/quotation: 'that, O wise men'). گفت is the past 'said' (the provided lexicon entry did not list the verbal sense), and کسری is the speaker's name (Kisra/Khosrow).
segments.s655-g176: جهان گشته و کار دیده ردان is a descriptive clause addressing the addressees: جهان گشته 'having gone/been about the world' (گشته is a perfect participle), و 'and', کار دیده 'having seen affairs' (دیده = seen, participle), and then ردان is a final adjective/noun whose precise sense is unclear in the supplied list — the clause as a whole praises their world‑traveled, experienced status ('you who have traveled the world and seen its affairs').
segments.s655-g489: This clause describes motion toward a place: به + محل marks direction ('to/into the iwan'). شد is used here in a narrative past sense meaning 'came/arrived' (an older/poetic use). از دشت شاه جهان is a postposed source/location phrase ('from the plain of the king of the world'), with شاهِ جهان as a genitive/title modifying دشت.
segments.s655-g490: یکایک is an adverbial ('one by one', 'each in turn') modifying the verb برفتند, the 3rd-person plural past of رفتن ('they went / they departed / they proceeded'). با introduces accompaniment ('with'), and اومهان means 'the guests/visitors' who accompany them.
segments.s655-g491: بفرمود is an honorific past ('he commanded/said'); تا introduces a subordinate purpose/result clause here ('that/so that'). پیش او شد دبیر = 'a scribe came before him' (پیش + او = 'in his presence/ before him', شد marks the event/existence), with دبیر as the noun (the scribe) functioning as the subject/predicate noun of that subordinate clause.
segments.s655-g492: This is a nominal apposition giving the identity/title of the scribe: موبد موبدان is an honorific chain ('mōbed, (chief of) the mōbedān'), followed by the proper name اردشیر. ابا here appears as part of the name/title in the manuscript and does not carry the common lexical sense 'to refuse' in this position.
segments.s655-g493: به قرطاس is a locative/directional phrase here ('on/upon the paper'); برنامه خسروی is a noun + adjective ('the royal/program of the king') — Persian places adjectival modifiers after the noun, so برنامه (plan/register) is modified by خسروی (royal).
segments.s655-g494: نویسنده is 'the scribe' (here the acting copyist). بنوشت is the archaic/poetic past form meaning 'wrote' (here treated as a single verbal form, not the clan element listed). بر پهلوی indicates the medium/language ('in Pahlavi' — بر + پهلوی = 'in/on Pahlavi').
segments.s655-g495: قلم is the subject 'the pen'. چون here functions as a comparative/illustrative 'as/like' (essentially introducing the object 'the two faces'); دو رخ را is the definite object marked by را ('the two faces'), and به عنبر بشست is the verb phrase 'washed/washed with amber' — به + noun (here in the sense 'by/with') indicates the instrument/material (amber/ambergris). بشست is the past-tense verb 'he washed'.
segments.s655-g496: سرنامه ('title' or 'prefatory heading') is the object; کرد is the simple past 'made/did' ('he made as a title'). آفرین is the word chosen as the heading (here 'Afreen' — an acclamation/praise), and از نخست ('from the first/at the beginning') specifies that this heading is placed from the very start.
segments.s655-g857: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when' fused to the name مهران (چو + مهران = 'when Mehrān'). ستاد is a past action meaning 'stood up / rose' (poetic past). اندرو is a fused locution meaning 'at him / upon him' (in/at + him) and is the object of the following verb. بنگرید is a past/perfective gaze verb 'looked' (classical form). The clause order is: temporal conjunction + subject + locative/object phrase + verb.
segments.s655-g858: یکی را uses یکی 'a certain person' as the direct object with the object marker را. بدیدار is an archaic/fused expression (به دیدار) meaning 'to meet / to see' here functioning as the verbal phrase 'saw / encountered'. چون in this relative/comparative use introduces the clause 'whom/as whom' (classical relative sense 'that/whom'). او is the third‑person pronoun 'him' and ندید is the past negative 'did not see' (so 'whom he had not seen').
segments.s655-g859: بدانست is a past cognitive verb 'he realized / perceived.' بینادل is a compacted/poetic form (rare) here best taken as 'in (his) heart / within' — an adverbial expression describing where he understood it. رای is 'counsel / plan' and راد is an adjective 'noble / generous' modifying رای; together 'رای راد' = 'a noble plan/counsel.'
segments.s655-g860: که is the complementizer 'that' introducing the content of the realization. دورند is the present/past descriptive 'are distant / remain distant' (here descriptive of state). خاقان و خاتون is the coordinated subject 'the khâqân and the khâtun.' ز is the poetic preposition 'from,' and داد is the noun 'justice' (so ز داد = 'from justice' = 'far from justice').
segments.s655-g861: به is the prepositional marker here indicating instrument/means ('with' — literally 'to/with the turban'). دستار و دستان is a coordinated instrument phrase 'turban and (his) hands' (ودستان = 'and hands' or simply 'and hands' fused). همی is the classical continuous/emphatic particle signaling ongoing action ('continually' or an aoristic marker). چشم اوی is the possessed noun phrase 'his eye' (اوی = his). This whole phrase is an instrumental/accusative phrase that will be completed by the verb in the next segment.
segments.s655-g862: بپوشید is the past transitive verb 'he covered / concealed' (here: covered his eye). وزان is a conjunctive/temporal 'and from that / and because of that.' تازه شد is the compound 'became fresh / was renewed' (تازه = new/fresh; شد = became). خشم اوی is the possessed noun phrase 'his anger.' Together: 'he covered (his eye), and from that his anger became renewed.'
segments.s655-g863: پرستنده را uses پرستنده ('attendant / devotee') with the object marker را to indicate the addressee of گفت. گفت here is the speech verb 'he said' (classical placement: indirect object + گفت). نزدیک شاه is a locative modifier 'near the king' describing the attendant's position (or the place of speaking).
segments.s655-g864: فراوان is an adverb/adjective 'many / abundant' and pairs with بود 'there was / were' (بود = past of 'to be'). The following coordinated list یاره و تاج و گاه gives the things being said to exist: یاره 'attendants/companions', تاج 'crowns', and گاه here 'occasions / pomp / occasions' (a nominal element in a catalogue). The order is predicate (فراوان) + copula + list of items.
segments.s655-g1017: همه یال اسبان = literally “the whole manes of the horses”; همه modifies یال and appears before the noun (classical order). پر از is a predicate phrase meaning “filled with,” so مشک و می are the contents governed by از here. وامی is a poetic orthography for و می (and wine). The whole phrase functions as the subject/predicate describing the horses' manes.
segments.s655-g1018: شکر با درم ریخته = “sugar together with dirhams (coins) scattered/was scattered.” با links شکر and درم (= “along with”). ریخته is a past/predicative participle meaning “strewn/scattered” (not literal pouring here). زیر پی is a fixed locative phrase “under the feet/hoofs” (زیر = under, پی = foot/track/hoof).
segments.s655-g1019: ز بس = a poetic short form of از بس, meaning “from so much” / “because of so many.” ناله followed by a list of instruments (نای و چنگ و رباب) names the source of the lament; the instruments are in apposition to ناله (the lament of the ney, harp, and rebab).
segments.s655-g1020: نبد is an archaic/poetic negative verb meaning “there was not / did not remain.” The word order is inverted for poetical effect: نبد (predicate) + بر زمین جای (locative: “a place on the earth”) + آرام وخواب (objects: “rest and sleep”). وخواب is و fused to خواب (= “and sleep”).
segments.s655-g1021: چوآمد = چو + آمد, a fused form meaning “when (he/she/it) came.” بت here is a poetic epithet meaning “beauty” (not literal idol). اندر شبستان شاه is a classical-word-order prepositional phrase: اندر = in/into, شبستان = bedchamber, شاه = the king (genitival phrase: the king's bedchamber).
segments.s655-g1022: به مهد اندرون = “toward/at the inner cradle” (به marks direction; اندرون qualifies مهد as inner). کرد نگاه is the verb phrase “made/turned a glance”; کسری (Kisra/Khosrow) is the agent. The order here is verb + subject (کرد کسری نگاه) — a poetic inversion: literally “he turned a glance, Khosrow.”
segments.s655-g1023: یکی = “a certain / one.” سرو is the standard poetic metaphor ‘cypress’ for a tall, elegant person. دین here functions as an epithet attached to سرو (classical poetic usage: “savior/ornament/of the faith”); this is a conventional poetic tag rather than the literal ‘religion.’ از introduces the source/comparison clause that follows (از برش گرد ماه = “from her cheek the round moon” = ‘whose face is like the round moon’). برش (literally “section/profile”) is used metonymically for the cheek/side of the face; گرد ماه = “roundness of the moon” (moonlike face).
segments.s655-g1024: نهاده is the past participle “placed/put.” به + مهد اندرون indicates the location/direction “on/at the inner mounded place (head/cradle)” — مهد here = “head/cradle” metaphorically for the crown of the head, اندرون qualifying it as inner. بر ز عنبر کلاه = “a hat of amber” with ز (poetic از) marking material/source (of amber) and کلاه as the object; the whole clause says she had set an amber hat upon her (moonlike) head.
segments.s655-g497: بران is an adverb meaning “thereupon / then” placed before the verb phrase; دادگر is an adjective or epithet “just” placed before the proper name کوسپهر (an anthroponym) to form the noun phrase “the just Kospehr.” آفرید is a past-tense perfective verb “created / made” that follows the subject phrase.
segments.s655-g498: This is a nominal list (بلندی و تندی و مهر) followed by the repeated verb آفرید; the conjunction و simply links the coordinate nouns. بلندی here functions as a noun ‘loftiness / height’ (an abstract quality), while تندی adds the sense of swiftness/fierceness; مهر is another abstract noun ‘affection / favour.’ The final آفرید repeats the action ‘created’ and applies to the whole list.
segments.s655-g499: همه functions as a universal quantifier “all / everyone” modifying بنده‌گانیم. بنده‌گانیم is the 1st‑person plural copulative form ‘we are servants’ (the -(ی)م ending marks 1st‑person plural). و links the clauses; او is the third‑person pronoun “he,” and پادشاست is a nominal predicate ‘is king/sovereign’ (pādašāh + -st).
segments.s655-g500: This clause places the abstract subject خرد “wisdom” before a prepositional phrase برتوانایی او “according to / by his ability,” and ends with گواست, an archaic/copular verb meaning ‘is witness / testifies’ (it functions as ‘attests’ here). Word order is Subject — (prepositional phrase) — Verb, with برتوانایی qualifying the scope of the attestation as ‘by his power/ability.’
segments.s655-g501: نفس is a noun here meaning ‘soul / person’ (i.e., any individual). جز is the particle ‘except,’ and به فرمان combines the preposition به “by/through” with فرمان “command,” marking the limiting condition. اونشمرد is a past‑tense verb form ‘he counted / reckoned’ (here: ‘he counted no one except by his command’); the verb follows the exception phrase, giving the clause its negative/exclusive sense.
segments.s655-g502: This short clause uses پی مور (an emphatic or idiomatic phrasing referring to even an ant or a tiny creature) followed by a negative verb (نسپرد, literally ‘did not entrust / did not hand over’). بی او is ‘without him,’ and زمین is the object ‘the earth.’ The overall construction says he would not entrust the earth even to an ant apart from him; note that پی is idiomatic here and the exact lexical senses in the supplied list do not match this usage.
segments.s655-g503: ازو is the prepositional form ‘from him / of him’ (object of the request). خواستم is 1st‑person singular past ‘I asked / I begged’ (here used with ازو to mean ‘I asked him’). تا introduces purpose ‘so that / in order that,’ and آفرین is an object meaning ‘praise / commendation’ (an acclamation to be sent). The sequence expresses a request with purpose: ‘I asked him so that he might send praise.’
segments.s655-g504: رساند is an infinitive/past stem meaning ‘to send / to convey’ (here the intended action of what was asked). ز + ما is the source phrase ‘from us,’ سوی marks the direction ‘to/toward,’ and خاقان چین is the recipient title ‘the Khaghan of China.’ Word order is verb (action) followed by a compact prepositional phrase indicating origin and destination.
segments.s655-g881: من is the first-person subject 'I'. این را is the object phrase: این 'this' with the definite object marker را immediately after it, marking what is approved. پسندم is the verb 'I approve/like' with the enclitic -م showing 1st-person singular present; که introduces the subordinate clause ('that...'). بی‌تخت is an adjective phrase 'without throne/dethroned' (بی- = without) modifying the following noun عاج ('ivory'), which begins the subordinate clause's object list.
segments.s655-g882: ندارد is third-person singular 'does not have' (present/habitual—classical narration often uses present). ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. بن 'root/foundation' here names the source ('from the stock/root of ...'), followed by یاره (a name/tribal/person reference) and then a coordinated list وطوق وتاج 'and necklace and crown' introduced by the prefixed و- (poetic orthography attaches و to the following word). The verb ندارد governs the whole prepositional phrase 'ز بن یاره وطوق وتاج' as the things he lacks.
segments.s655-g883: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. مهتران is the plural 'elders/chiefs' (the decision-makers) and is the subject of the verb. این points to 'this' (the proposal just stated). نبینند is negative subjunctive/ present plural 'they do not see/accept' (in this context 'do not approve' / 'do not accept'); رای is 'counsel/opinion/plan' and is the object of نبینند: 'if the elders do not accept this counsel.'
segments.s655-g884: چو here = 'when'/'if' (classical usage can be temporal or conditional). فرمان is 'the command/decree' and بود is the copula 'is/was' (here meaning 'if/when the command stands'). باز گردم is the verb phrase 'I will return/turn back': باز (again) + گردم (1st-person singular form of گشتن/گردیدن 'turn/return'); the personal ending -م marks 'I'. به جای is the prepositional phrase 'to my place/station.'
segments.s655-g885: نگه کرد is a compound verb 'made a look / looked / fixed his gaze' (نگه 'look' + کرد 'did/made'). In poetic word order the verb precedes the subject: خاقان (the khaghan) is the agent. به گفتار اوی is a prepositional phrase 'at/to his speech/words' where به marks the target and اوی is the possessive 'his' (archaic form) modifying گفتار.
segments.s655-g886: شگفت آمدش is an impersonal/experiential construction meaning 'it appeared wondrous to him' (آمدش = 'came to him', with the clitic -ش = 'to him'). رای وکردار اوی are the items that struck him: رای 'counsel/plan' and وکردار 'and (his) deed/behavior' with اوی 'his' again; together they form the subject/experiential cause of شگفت آمدش ('his counsel and conduct amazed him').
segments.s655-g887: بدانست is 'he knew/was aware.' کان is an archaic particle introducing the following nominal phrase (comparable to 'that' or 'that which'); together they introduce the content of his knowledge. پیر پاکیزه مغز is a nominal phrase: پیر 'old man' (here a person of age/experience), پاکیزه 'pure/clean' modifying مغز 'mind/brain' — idiomatically 'a pure-minded old man' or 'an old man of clean/pure intellect.'
segments.s655-g888: بزرگست is a copular predicate 'is great' (بزرگ + -ست, archaic copula). The coordinating و links the following predicate شایسته کار نغز: شایسته 'worthy/fit for' governs the noun phrase کار نغز 'fine/excellent work/deeds' (نغز = 'pithy/well-done/excellent' in classical diction). Together: 'he is great and fit for fine deeds.'
segments.s655-g689: This is a prepositional/causal phrase headed by the poetic preposition ز (here = 'because of' / 'from'). بس is an intensifier meaning 'so much' or 'abundant'; it sits before the noun خوبى ('goodness, kindness'). The coordinated nouns پوزش و آفرین follow with و linking them, forming a list of the qualities/actions causing the result in the next clause.
segments.s655-g690: که introduces a subordinate clause ('that' / 'so that'). پیدا is predicative here meaning 'became apparent' / 'was revealed'. بد is the substantive/adjectival content 'evil / ill' (i.e. the bad element). از + گفت marks source 'from the words/speech', and خاقان چین is a genitival noun phrase 'the khaghan of China' (China as the domain). Word order is typical classical Persian: predicate (پیدا) + complement (بد) + source phrase (از گفت ...) .
segments.s655-g691: A noun phrase: همه ('all') + سرفرازان (plural noun 'the proud/ honorable ones') followed by the adjective پرهیزکار ('pious/abstinent'). In classical Persian adjectives commonly follow the noun; here the whole phrase means 'all the noble/pious people'.
segments.s655-g692: ستایش گرفتن is an idiomatic verb phrase meaning 'to give/offer praise' or literally 'to take praise' in past plural گرفتند ('they took/offered'). برشهریار is a prepositional/oblique phrase 'toward the king / to the king' (بر used here as 'to/in the presence of').
segments.s655-g693: که introduces reported praise/exclamation. یزدان سپاس is an exclamatory formula 'God be thanked' or 'thanks to God' (یزدان = God, سپاس = thanks). و links the next phrase. بدویم پناه is elliptical and uses the verb بدویم (here functioning like a hortative/subjunctive 'may [to] come/rush'), so the phrase means roughly 'and may refuge come to him' or 'may refuge hasten to him' (پناه = refuge/protection). This is a poetic, somewhat condensed construction.
segments.s655-g694: The second که clause gives a reason/explanation: ننشست is the past negative of نشستن ('did not sit'). یک شاه literally 'one king' is used distributively/indefinitely here as 'any king' or 'no king'; بر پیشگاه = 'before the presence / in the royal presence'. Together: 'for no king sat before the (royal) presence.'
segments.s655-g695: به پیروزی ('with/into victory') sets the circumstance or result. فرو + اورند here represent a split compound verb (frozen poetic orthography of فروآوردند / فروآورد) meaning 'they brought down' / 'they humbled' or 'caused to come down' — the verb is past plural. In other words, 'they brought (him) with victory and brought (him) low' (fرو as the verbal prefix 'down-'). Note that the manuscript splits the prefix and verb across words; parse them together as one verbal idea.
segments.s655-g696: A prepositional/qualitative list: بخوبی ('with goodness' or 'in goodness'), ونرمی ('and gentleness'), و پیوند شاه ('and the king's bond/union' or 'royal ties'). بخوبی uses the prefix بـ- meaning 'with/ in', and the coordinated nouns describe qualities or attributes associated with the king. The exact relation (whether these modify the bringing mentioned before or describe the king) is left elliptic by the poet.
segments.s655-g905: کزین is a fused prepositional phrase (ک + زین) meaning 'from this'; دخت خاقان is a genitive 'daughter of the Khaghan' (noun + possessor). وز here is an archaic poetic contraction of و + ز (i.e. 'and from'), joining the two sources. پشت شاه is a genitival/relational phrase 'the shah's پشت' (literally 'back' but here 'lineage/backing/support'), completing the 'from X and from Y' frame.
segments.s655-g906: بیاید is a third-person present/subjunctive/optative form used to mean 'will come' or 'may come' (future emergence). The following nominal group 'یکی شاه زیبای گاه' is the logical subject: یکی ('a certain' / 'one'), شاه ('king'), زیبای گاه = زیبای + ی (ezāfe) + گاه, 'beautiful/of the time' — an adjectival phrase 'handsome of his age'. Note the verb precedes the subject for poetic inversion.
segments.s655-g907: برو is an imperative/optative 'go' used to introduce the action; شهریاران is the plural noun 'kings' (subject/agent). کنند is third-person plural present/subjunctive 'they may do/let them do' and governs the following object/utterance; آفرین here is an interjection/noun 'acclamation, praise' (what they will utter). The construction effectively commands/permits the kings to utter praise.
