segments.s655-g908: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the same' referring back to the person mentioned earlier. پرهنر is an adjective 'full of art/skillful' (پر- prefix meaning 'full of'); سرفرازان is a plural noun 'the proud/victorious ones'. چین is a place-name 'China' attached appositionally/attributively, so the whole phrase means 'those same skilled, proud men of China.' Word order: demonstrative + adjective + plural noun + place-name.
segments.s655-g909: چو here means 'when' (introducing a temporal clause). بشنید is simple past 'he heard' (3rd sg). خاقان names the subject/agent (the Khaghan); دلش is 'his heart' where ش is the enclitic possessive 'his'. گشت خوش is a verbal predicate 'became glad' (گشت = became; خوش = glad). Note the inverted/poetic order (verb before subject is acceptable in classical Persian).
segments.s655-g910: بخندید is the simple past 'she laughed' (3rd sg). خاتون is the noun 'the lady/consort' acting as subject (placed after the verb for poetic style). خورشیدفش is an epithet/name 'Khurshidfash' (lit. 'sun‑shining' or 'radiant') that identifies the lady; it follows خاتون as a title/epithet.
segments.s655-g911: چو = 'when' (temporal). از چاره دلها is a prepositional phrase 'from/regarding the remedy/solution of the hearts' (دلها = hearts, plural). بپرداختند is past plural 'they attended to / dealt with' (here 'they took care of' or 'settled'), so the clause means 'when they took care of the hearts' remedy' — i.e. when they had satisfied/arranged what their hearts required.
segments.s655-g912: فرستاده را is the definite object 'the messenger' marked by را. پیش functions adverbially 'in the presence of / before (them)'. بنشاختند is past plural 'they set/placed (to sit)' — here 'they had the messenger set down before them' (causative/putting into place). The whole means 'they placed the messenger before them.'
segments.s655-g801: چو بشنید is the fused archaic/poetic temporal clause 'when (he) heard' (چو = when + شنید = heard). مهران is the temporal clause's subject ('when Mehrān heard'). ستاد این ز شاه is a small reported clause following it: ستاد (past 'praised') with این ('this') as its object and ز شاه ('from/by the king') marking the agent/source; word order is inverted and compressed in poetry (verb then object then agent).
segments.s655-g802: بسی here functions as an adverbial intensifier 'very/much' (classical/poetic 'many/much'). آفرین is an exclamation/noun 'acclamation' and کرد is the light verb 'did/made', together meaning 'uttered acclamations'. بر + [تاج و گاه] is a prepositional phrase 'upon the crown and (the) throne' (گاه used poetically as 'seat/throne').
segments.s655-g803: برفت here is a poetic past verb meaning 'he departed / left' (verb-before-constituent order is common in poetry). از بر is the idiom 'from before' and گاه گیتی‌فروز is a compound noun 'the world‑illuminating seat' (i.e. the throne); together the phrase means 'he departed from before the world‑illuminating throne.'
segments.s655-g804: به marks direction 'to/toward'. فرخنده modifies فال: 'فرخنده فال' = 'auspicious omen.' و simply links the next phrase. بخرداد is the old/prepositional form 'on Khordad' (i.e. on the day named Khordad) and روز is 'day' (so 'on the Khordad day'). The whole segment indicates his destination/time: toward an auspicious divination on Khordad day.
segments.s655-g805: به خاقان چین marks the indirect object/recipient 'to the Khaghan of China' (به = to/for). آگهی شد is a passive construction 'it was made known / was announced' (آگهی = information/notice; شد acts as auxiliary/passive marker). که introduces the reported content: 'that...'.
segments.s655-g806: فرستاده here functions as the verbal/participial notion 'sent' (i.e. 'had sent'), followed by the things/persons sent. مهران is the personal name. ستاد in this position is part of the list following مهران (in the manuscript it appears as a name or item in the list) and so is treated as an item, and سپاه means 'army/host' — the phrase lists what the king sent. (Note: ستاد elsewhere can be a verb; here the poetic/listing context makes it function as a nominal element.)
segments.s655-g807: چوآمد is the fused temporal clause 'چو آمد' = 'when he came' (چو = when + آمد = came). به نزدیک indicates motion toward proximity 'to near / close to' and خاقان چین names the addressee/location 'the Khaghan of China.' Word order is compact and characteristic of epic verse.
segments.s655-g808: زمین را ببوسید: زمین ('the earth') plus the definite object marker را and the past verb ببوسید 'kissed' — 'he kissed the earth.' و کرد آفرین: the conjunction و links the clause and کرد with آفرین forms the light-verb construction 'made an acclamation' (i.e. 'cried “bravo”'). Note the verb 'کرد' follows the noun 'آفرین' here (poetic ordering).
segments.s655-g1081: 'از ایران چو گشتاسب آمد به جنگ' is a temporal/adverbial clause: 'از ایران' = 'from Iran' (prepositional phrase) + 'چو' (archaic poetic 'when') introducing the clause. 'گشتاسب آمد' is simple past 'Goshtasp came', and 'به جنگ' is the directional complement 'to/into the battle.' Word order is typical of poetry: temporal introducer (چو) before the subject and past verb.
segments.s655-g1082: 'ندید ایچ ارجاسب جای درنگ' is inverted (verb-first) poetic order: 'ندید' (past negative 'did not see') + 'ایچ' (intensifier 'nothing/at all') + 'ارجاسب' (subject) + 'جای درنگ' (object: 'place/room for delay'). Literally: 'saw nothing, Arjasp, a place for delay.' Thus the clause means 'Arjasp saw no place to linger.'
segments.s655-g1083: 'برآسود گیتی ز کردار اوی' uses a perfective/state verb first: 'برآسود' = 'was at rest/relieved.' 'گیتی' (the world) is the grammatical subject following the verb (poetic V–S order). 'ز کردار اوی' = 'from/because of his deeds' where 'ز' = 'from' and 'اوی' is the possessive 'his.'
segments.s655-g1084: 'که هرگز مبادا فلک یاراوی' is a subordinate optative/wish clause introduced by 'که' = 'that/so that.' 'هرگز' = 'never' and 'مبادا' is a negative optative ('may it not be' / 'let it not happen'). 'فلک' ('heaven' / 'fate') is the would-be subject and 'یاراوی' means 'his ally/helper' (یار + اوی). Together: 'may heaven never be his ally/friend.' Note the archaic optative 'مبادا.'
segments.s655-g1085: 'ازان پس چونرسی سپهدار شد' combines temporal/connective particles and a subordinate time clause: 'ازان پس' = 'after that/thereupon.' 'چونرسی' is the fused poetic form of 'چون رسی' = 'when/as soon as he arrived.' 'سپهدار شد' is past 'became spahdār (commander).' The clause thus means 'thereupon, when he arrived, he became commander.'
segments.s655-g1086: 'همه شهرها پر ز تیمار شد' has subject 'همه شهرها' = 'all the cities.' 'پر ز' = 'full of' (poetic construction: adjective 'پر' + preposition 'ز' = 'full from/of'), 'تیمار' = 'care/anxiety,' and 'شد' = 'became.' Literal: 'all the cities became full of care/worry.'
segments.s655-g1087: 'چوشاپور ارمزد بگرفت جای' uses 'چو' = 'like/as' (here 'like Shapur'). 'ارمزد' names the deity Ahura Mazda and functions here as an instrumental/agentive invocation ('by/with Ahura Mazda' or 'through Ahura Mazda'). 'بگرفت' is past 'took/seized' and 'جای' = 'place/office/position' is the direct object. Poetic order places the simile and divine name before the verb.
segments.s655-g1088: 'ندانست نرسی سرش را ز پای' begins with 'ندانست' = 'he did not know/he was unaware.' The following verb 'نرسی' is used impersonally/privatively in the sense 'would happen/befall' (third-person sense), so the clause means 'he did not know that (such) misfortune would befall.' 'سرش را ز پای' literally 'his head from his feet' is an idiomatic violent image = 'his head be severed/be taken from his feet' (i.e., beheaded); 'را' marks the definite object. The subordinate 'که' is omitted (typical in poetry).
segments.s655-g97: 'توگفتی' is past second-person singular 'you said'. 'که' introduces reported content ('that'). 'آهن' is the noun 'iron' used poetically for metal/weapon. 'زبان' is used figuratively as 'tongue/speech' (what something 'speaks'). 'داردی' functions here as 'has' (classical/poetic form with -ی); the clause order is subject (آهن) + object/attribute (زبان) + predicate (داردی), typical of Persian SOV syntax in older poetry.
segments.s655-g98: This clause continues the reported speech. 'هوا' is the subject 'the air/wind'. 'گرز را' is the definite object marked by 'را' (here 'the mace/club'). 'ترجمان' is a noun 'interpreter/translator' used poetically (the thing that 'interprets' or manifests the mace). 'داردی' again appears in the same archaic/poetic form meaning 'has' (here: '[the air] is the mace's interpreter' — the predicate links to گرز را). Word order: subject (هوا) + object (گرز را) + predicate (ترجمان داردی).
segments.s655-g99: 'یکی' here is indefinite 'a certain / a' (classical narrative 'one/there came a'). 'باد' is the subject 'wind'. 'برخاست' is past 'rose, arose'. The conjunction 'و' links the next noun phrase. 'گردی سیاه' is a noun phrase 'a black dust/cloud' (گردی = dust, سیاه = black). The clause order is typical: (indefinite) + subject + verb, then conjunction + noun phrase.
segments.s655-g100: 'بشد' is the classical/poetic past/resultive 'it became / it happened' (here 'appeared/was made'). 'روشنایی' is the noun 'light/brightness'. 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'from'. 'خورشید و ماه' are the coordinated sources 'sun and moon'. The clause is verb-first here ('بشد روشنایی...') which is common in older Persian poetry for effect: 'light came from the sun and moon.'
segments.s655-g101: This clause names the peoples who come together. 'کشانی' functions here as an ethnonym (Kushans) though the lemma is ambiguous; 'وسغدی' = 'and Sogdians' (the conjunction 'و' attached). 'شدند' is the past plural 'they became / they were' (here passive/causative sense: 'were brought/assembled'). 'انجمن' is the noun 'assembly/meeting' that they enter or are made into. Word order: subject(s) + verb + complement (classical narrative style).
segments.s655-g102: 'پر از آب' is the adjectival phrase 'full of water' (i.e., filled with water/tears). 'رو' here means 'faces' (lit. 'face(s)') and follows to specify what is full/affected. 'کودک و مرد' are coordinated nouns 'children and men'. The final token 'وزن' in the provided segmentation appears to be a mis-tokenization (should be 'و زن' = 'and women'); given that, the intended meaning is 'children, men, and women'. The phrase as a whole describes the assembly being full of water streaming on the faces of the people.
segments.s655-g103: 'که' is the subordinating particle 'that/for', introducing a clause. 'تا' here has the sense 'until / as long as'. 'چون' means 'as / such as / so' (showing manner). 'بود' is the past copula 'was'. 'کارآن' = 'the work/affair of that (thing)' with 'آن' pointing to 'that battlefield'. 'رزمگاه' is 'battlefield'. Together the phrase means 'that, until/while the affair of that battlefield was as it was' — a temporal/conditional clause setting the circumstance.
segments.s655-g104: 'کرا' is an archaic interrogative/relative 'who/whom'. 'بردهد' is an older/periphrastic form meaning 'gives/causes to give' or 'sets in motion' (poetic usage). 'گردش' is 'turning/circuit/rotation' (here motion or course). 'هور' is poetic 'splendor/light' (or the sun's radiance) and 'و ماه' is 'and the moon'. Grammatically this is a verbless/elliptical question/clause in structure—'who would cause/set the rotation of sun and moon'—using archaic verb form and nominal complements.
segments.s655-g9: A noun phrase headed by سپهدار (the commander). The preposition با governs a list of accompanying items (لشکر, گنج, تاج) joined by و; با here means 'with' and attaches to the whole series. Word order is typical: head noun followed by accompaniment introduced by با.
segments.s655-g10: Contains place-name material. بگلزریون is a proper name (likely a toponym or tribal name). بودزان here is written as a unit with the name (treated as part of the locative phrase rather than a simple past auxiliary); روی functions as a local preposition 'upon/on' and introduces چاج (another place-name or locality). The whole segment gives a locative specification ('at/among B... on the face/surface of Chaj'). Note: these are archaic place-names, typical of epic diction.
segments.s655-g11: سخنهای کسری is a genitive construction: 'the words/sayings of Kisra' (سخنهای is the plural construct). به گرد جهان is a prepositional phrase meaning 'around the world' (به + گرد 'around' + جهان 'world'). گرد here is a spatial adverb 'around/about.'
segments.s655-g12: پراگنده is a predicative adjective 'scattered/widespread' and شد is the past auxiliary 'became' (literal 'became scattered'). درمیان مهان is an adjunct 'among the guests/visitors' (درمیان = among, مهان = guests), specifying where they were scattered.
segments.s655-g13: به introduces a set of qualities or manner ('by/in/through'). The three coordinated nouns (مردی، دانایی، فرهی) are abstract qualities: مردی = manliness/valor, دانایی = wisdom, فرهی = nobility/cultured bearing. The conjunction و simply links the items in the list.
segments.s655-g14: Two coordinated nouns giving results or possessions: بزرگی 'greatness/grandeur' and آیین شاهنشهی 'the ritual/ceremony of kingship' (شاهنشهی qualifies آیین). There is no explicit verb here; the construction lists attributes or things possessed by the subject previously mentioned.
segments.s655-g15: A nominal + temporal phrase: خردمند خاقان = 'the wise khaghan' (adjective before noun). بدان روزگار is a fused prepositional/adverbial expression 'in that time/age' (بدان = به + آن 'to/at that'; the text shows the elements split but they function together as the temporal marker). روزگار is the temporal noun 'time/age.'
segments.s655-g16: An action clause: همی here gives an aspectual flavor (continuous or persistent past) and precedes the object دوستی 'friendship.' جست is the past tense (perfective) of 'seek' here meaning 'sought.' با + شهریار is the accompaniment/target 'with the king.' Together: 'he sought friendship with the king.'
segments.s655-g713: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; بیگانه modifies ایوان (adjective before noun: 'foreign/strange iwan'). بپرداختند is a past tense 3rd-person plural verb; in classical usage it can indicate movement or an action taken by the group (here rendered 'rose/left' from the iwan). The clause as a whole gives the source/starting place of the action.
segments.s655-g714: فرستاده is the definite direct object; را is the Persian object marker. پیش functions as an adverb/prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of.' بنشاختند is a past tense 3rd-person plural verb meaning 'set/placed/made sit' here (they caused the envoy to sit before [the court]). The word order is Subject (implicit) + object + locative phrase + verb.
segments.s655-g715: شهنشاه is the subject 'the Shah' (compound royal title). بسیار is an adverb modifying the verb (here 'greatly' or 'with much'), and بنواختشان is a past 3rd-person verb with the enclitic object -شان 'them' attached; in classical Persian نواختن can mean to 'play (music)' or more generally to 'entertain/honour' — grammatically it's verb+object, indicating the Shah's action toward them.
segments.s655-g716: به نزدیکی تخت is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to/at the nearness of the throne' (به introduces the locative relation; نزدیکی is 'nearness' and تخت 'throne'). بنشاختشان is again a past verb with the 3rd-person plural object enclitic -شان; here it functions to indicate the Shah 'placed/caused them to sit' close to the throne. Word order places the locative phrase before the verb.
segments.s655-g717: پیام is the direct object 'message'; جهاندار is a genitival modifier ('of the sovereign' or 'the sovereign's') placed after the noun as is common in Persian poetic style. بگزاردند is a past 3rd-person plural verb meaning 'caused/placed/auspiced' — here best rendered as 'they conveyed/presented' the message (the verb takes the preceding noun phrase as its object).
segments.s655-g718: براسب is a prepositional/adverbial compound 'on horseback' (بـ +اسب fused in poetry); سخن is here an instrumental/means 'with words' and پای ('foot') names the limb used. بفشاردند is a past 3rd-person plural verb 'they pressed' or 'they stamped/struck' — together the clause says they urged on (their mounts) with speech and by pressing with the foot. The two phrases give two simultaneous means of urging the horse.
segments.s655-g719: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; بشنید is a past 3rd-person verb 'he heard.' شاه is the subject 'the king.' آن سخنهای is the demonstrative + plural noun 'those words/speeches' and گرم here is an adjective meaning 'warm/ardent' (i.e., earnest or fervent). The clause sets the temporal condition for what follows.
segments.s655-g720: ز introduces the source 'from' (here 'from the ...'); گردان چینی is a noun phrase 'the Chinese troop/host' (گردان = troop; چینی = Chinese). به + آواز نرم is an adverbial construction 'in a soft/gentle voice' (به functions adverbially here, آواز = voice, نرم = soft/gentle). Grammatically this whole segment gives the source of the speech and the manner in which it was delivered.
segments.s655-g1481: چنین داد پاسخ is a poetic word order for چنان پاسخ داد / چنین پاسخ داد meaning 'he gave such an answer' — چنين is a demonstrative/adverb 'thus', داد is the past tense verb 'gave', and پاسخ is the direct object 'answer'. The particle که introduces the reported clause ('that...'). راه خرد is a noun phrase in which خرد (wisdom) modifies راه (way/path); Persian ezāfe is omitted in poetic diction.
segments.s655-g1482: ز هر دانشی bi-gamān بگذرد: ز (poetic genitive/ablative 'from, out of') + هر دانشی 'every kind of knowledge' (دانشی is an indefinite/collective form) + بی‌گمان 'undoubtedly' (adverb). بگذرد is 3rd person singular present/subjunctive of گذشتن used here to state a general truth ('passes/exceeds'); word order places the adverb before the verb.
segments.s655-g1483: همان is a demonstrative 'that same' pointing back; خوی نیکو is a noun + adjective 'good custom/habit' (در نوشتار کلاسیک خوی often = habit/custom). نیکوکه here is a compact poetic form equivalent to 'نیکو (که)' — an adjectival/relative shaping the following clause, and مردم بدوی means 'the people of old/original people' where بدوی is a postpositive adjective.
segments.s655-g1484: بماند is 3rd person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن ('may it remain' / 'it remains') often used optatively in classical poetry. همه‌ساله is an adverbial 'yearly/every year'. با آب روی is an instrumental/qualitative phrase 'with the water of the face' (آبِ روی) — a poetic image meaning a fresh/clear countenance; note the ezāfe is omitted, a common poetic contraction.
segments.s655-g1485: وزین here is poetic and can be read as an adjective 'dignified/weighty' modifying گوهران ('gems'), or as the fused phrase و زین ('and from these'); in either reading it qualifies the following mention of a particular gem. گوهران (plural) followed by گوهر (singular) is a poetic switch from the group to a single exemplar (a gem) and استوار is a postpositive adjective 'steadfast, firm'.
segments.s655-g1486: تن خشندی is an adjectival noun phrase 'a body (person) of delight/one pleased' where خشندی (delight, pleasure) qualifies تن (body/person). دیدم is the past tense 'I saw'. از روزگار is an ablative/agentive phrase 'from/of fate/time' indicating source/cause; here روزگار carries the sense 'fate/the course of time'. Word order places the object phrase before the verb (common SOV pattern in Persian poetry).
segments.s655-g1487: وزیشان is a fused poetic form (و ز ایشان) meaning 'and/from them' (the literal lexeme list also allows readings like 'weight/burden', but here it functions as the fused prepositional phrase). امیدست is a fixed predicate 'there is hope'. آهسته‌تر is a comparative adverb 'more gently/more slowly' expressing the hoped-for manner; the clause overall is optative/expectational ('there is hope that they will be...').
segments.s655-g1488: برآسوده is a participial/adjectival form meaning 'at ease/relieved', and از رنج specifies the thing they are relieved from ('from suffering'). The conjunction و links the coordinated comparative adjective شایسته‌تر ('more worthy/more deserving'), all predicated of the same implied subject marked earlier by وز ایشان.
segments.s655-g225: چنینست is a fused form of "چنین است" (copula) meaning 'thus/so'. It precedes an epithet phrase in inverted, poetic word order: بادافره (an adjectival epithet) modifies دادگر (the noun), so the whole chunk reads 'thus is the wind‑borne/fleeting just one' (i.e. 'thus is the just ruler, like a gust').
segments.s655-g226: همان points back to a previously mentioned person ('the same' or 'that very one'). بدکنش را is a definite/affected-marked phrase (را marks the person affected) meaning 'the evildoer'; بد آید به سر is an idiomatic intransitive clause meaning 'evil/misfortune comes upon the head' (آید = 'befalls/happens'); به سر literally 'to the head' = 'befall him'. The present tense آید states a general/traditional fact.
segments.s655-g227: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from/about/because of' and attaches to خاقان ('the Khagan'). اگر introduces a conditional ('if'). The verb راند (past of راندن) follows the subject شاه in inverted poetic order; سخن ('speech/words') is the object/what is being produced — so the clause reads roughly 'if, on account of the Khagan, the king utters words...'.
segments.s655-g228: که is a relative/connector 'that/which' linking to the preceding 'speak/words'. دارد is the present indicative 'he has/bears'. به دل is a locative expression meaning 'in the heart' (به here functions like 'in' poetically). کین و درد کهن lists inner states: کین 'hatred/vengeance' and درد 'sorrow/affliction', qualified by کهن 'ancient/long‑standing'.
segments.s655-g229: سزدگر is an evaluative word here meaning 'fitting/appropriate' (poetic adjective/participial sense). ز خویشان افراسیاب is a genitive/partitive phrase 'from/of Afrasiab's relatives/kin' specifying the group to which the evaluation applies. Word order is compressed as is common in poetry.
segments.s655-g230: بدآموز is an adjective 'corrupting/ill‑instructive' describing a person; دارد is the present 'has' (3rd sg.). دو دیده پرآب is a noun phrase 'two eyes full of water' where پرآب ('full‑of‑water') is a compound adjective meaning 'tearful' or 'water‑filled'. The whole clause states that the corrupting person possesses two tearful eyes.
segments.s655-g231: دگر here functions as a transitional 'again/on the other hand'. آنک is a relative/pronominal 'that/who' introducing 'the victorious one' (پیروز). شد is past 'became', and دل گرفت is a compound where گرفت (passive/affected sense) means 'the heart was seized/affected' — i.e. 'he was stricken in heart'. The order is poetic but reads 'when that victorious one became, his heart was seized'.
segments.s655-g232: اگر introduces the condition 'if'. ز + و here functions jointly as the classical contraction 'زو' = 'از او' ('from him' / 'of him' = 'him' as object), so 'اگر زو بترسی' = 'if you fear him'. بترسی is the present-subjunctive/2nd-sg or general present 'to fear'. نباشد شگفت is a fixed phrase 'there is no wonder' (literally 'wonder does not exist'), with نباشد negating existence of wonder and شگفت 'wonder/marvel'.
segments.s655-g441: به چینی here is a prepositional phrase meaning “in Chinese / in the Chinese manner” (به + language). نمود is the poetic past tense of کردن/نمودن meaning “made/displayed/formed.” آنک is an archaic conjunction/pronoun “that/which” introducing the following predicative phrase; شاهی is an adjective/noun meaning “royal / kingship.” کراست is an older poetic form written as one word (likely کرا + است or a proper/name-like noun) functioning as the predicate (i.e. ‘was/formed a kras’).
segments.s655-g442: ز ... تا is a standard poetic span marker “from ... to ...” (ز = from, تا = as far as/to). خورشید و پشت ماهی supply the endpoints (“from the sun to the back of the moon”); پشت = “back/behind.” کراست repeats the same noun/predicate as above, completing the idea of extent reaching between those extremes.
segments.s655-g443: هوا پر شد is a verb phrase “the air became full” (هوا subject, پر = full, شد = became). از introduces the cause/contents (“with / from”), جوش here means a boiling/surge or tumult (a dust‑cloud or agitated motion), and گرد after جوش functions as ‘around/about’ (showing the surge surrounds the riders). سوار (riders) is the noun that the surrounding motion refers to.
segments.s655-g444: زمین پرشد parallels the previous clause: “the earth/ground became full.” از again marks what fills it: آلت کار is a compound meaning “implements/tools” (آلت = instrument, کار = work/affair), and زار is an adjective “woeful/wretched,” qualifying those implements (i.e. grim/terrible implements, here weapons).
segments.s655-g445: به دشت اندر آورد is a verb phrase “brought into the plain” (به = into/towards, دشت = plain, اندر = in/into, آورد = brought). گه ساختند literally “they made/constructed a place/occasion” (گه = a place/occasion; ساختند = they built/made), i.e. they set up a position or prepared the ground.
segments.s655-g446: سواران جنگی is a noun phrase “warlike horsemen / fighting riders.” همی‌تاختند is the imperfective past form (همی‑ + تاختن) meaning “they charged / were rushing (forward),” with همی giving the continuous/iterative flavor.
segments.s655-g447: به here introduces a list of instruments/weapons (in the sense “with” or “equipped with”). The sequence names weapons: کوپال (a named weapon or proper noun for a weapon), تیغ (sword), بتیر (by/with arrow — the form emphasizes the arrow as instrument), and کمان (bow). The conjunction و simply links the items.
segments.s655-g448: بگشتند is a past plural verb “they turned/returned.” گردنکشان is a substantive epithet meaning “the neck‑cutters / rebels / proud ones” (an epithet for insurgents). زمان here (with an implied یک) means “a moment / at once” — indicating it happened in a single instant.
segments.s655-g433: 'بدیبا' is an adverb placed before the predicate meaning 'marvellously/beautifully'. 'بیاراسته' functions as a past‑participle/adjectival predicate meaning 'set up/ornamented' (a poetic/state form); the phrase ends with the locative 'پشت پیل' ('the back of the elephant'), where Persian word order here places the modifier/predicate before the noun phrase it describes.
segments.s655-g434: 'بدو' is the dative/locative particle 'to it/onto it' (i.e. onto the elephant's back). 'تخت پیروزه' is a noun phrase 'turquoise throne' (تخت = throne, پیروزه = turquoise), and 'هم‌رنگ نیل' is an adjectival phrase 'same‑coloured as indigo' (هم‌رنگ + [implicit ezāfe] + نیل); in poetic prose the ezāfe is often omitted and modifiers cluster after the head.
segments.s655-g435: This is two parallel noun + adjective constructions: 'زمین پرخروش' = 'the earth full of tumult' (پرخروش is an adjective meaning 'raging/turbulent' placed after the noun), and 'وهوا پر ز جوش' = 'the air full of boiling/surge' where 'پر' = 'full (of)' and the prepositional particle 'ز' introduces the source/contents ('of') followed by 'جوش' = 'surge/boiling'.
segments.s655-g436: 'همی' is a classical adverbial particle giving a sense of immediacy or ongoing action ('then/at once/indeed'); 'کر شد' is a verbal phrase meaning 'was/made/became' in this context (a classical/perhaps contracted form used in older Persian), and 'مردم تیزگوش' is the subject plus predicate adjective: 'the people (became) sharp‑eared' = 'became attentive/all ears'. 'شد' is the auxiliary 'became'.
segments.s655-g437: 'فرستاده' is a noun 'messenger' (or 'one who is sent'); 'بردع' is an ablative/locative adjunct meaning 'with a cloak' (cloak as attribute, here without an explicit preposition — a common poetic compression); 'وهند و روم' lists the destinations 'to Hind and Rome' (the coordinating 'و' links the two place names).
segments.s655-g438: This is a double genitive/agent phrase introduced by the preposition 'ز' ('from/of'): 'ز هر شهریاری' = 'from every ruler/sovereign' and the following 'ز آباد بوم' = 'from every settled/inhabited land' (آباد + بوم = 'prosperous/inhabited homeland'). The repeated 'ز' is a poetic coordinate linking the two sources.
segments.s655-g439: 'ز' is the preposition 'from' and governs 'دشت' ('the plain'); 'سواران' = 'horsemen/riders' and 'نیزه گزار' is a compound meaning 'spear‑bearers/spearmen' (here in apposition/coordinate with سواران). The sequence omits conjunctions and uses concise poetic coordination.
segments.s655-g440: 'برفتند' is the past plural 'they went/rode off'. 'یک سر' (here as an adverbial phrase, comparable to 'یکسر') means 'all at once/straightaway' (literally 'one head' used idiomatically), and 'سوی شهریار' is the prepositional phrase 'toward the king' (سوی = toward, شهریار = king).
segments.s655-g1425: This is a nominal subclause introduced by نیاز (an evaluative/lamenting noun) plus the connective آنک 'that'. او را is the definite object (او marked by راست) referring forward to the person being described; ز اندوه و درد is a prepositional phrase ('from/because of sorrow and pain') giving the cause. The clause supplies the object that the following verb (بینند) will take.
segments.s655-g1426: همی is a continuous/poetic adverbial particle here ('continually/indeed') placed before the predicate. کور is a predicative adjective ('blind') and بینند is the 3rd-person plural present/epic-form of دیدن ('they see'); its object (او را) was given in the previous segment, so together it means 'they see him as blind'. و links this clause to the following nominal phrase; رخساره زرد is a verbless descriptive phrase 'his countenance (was) pale', with رخساره = complexion/face and زرد = pale.
segments.s655-g1427: کزین ('from this') opens a resultative/temporal clause. بگذری is a poetic/impersonal present-subjunctive form of گذشتن used to mark that something 'passed/ensued'. خسروا here is treated as an outcome noun/verb-form meaning 'loss/they perished' and is followed by the appositive دیو رشک 'the demon Envy' (دیو = demon, رشک = envy), so the phrase describes the coming-about of loss in the form of the demon of envy.
segments.s655-g1428: یکی introduces an indefinite single person ('a certain' / 'one'), دردمندی functions as the nominal 'an afflicted person' (or 'one who suffers'), بود is the past copula 'was', and بی‌پزشک is an adjectival/compound prepositional phrase 'without a physician' describing that person. The whole segment states that one afflicted person was without medical care.
segments.s655-g1429: اگر introduces a conditional clause. در زمانه here means 'in the age' or idiomatically 'in the world/fortune'; کسی is an indefinite pronoun 'anyone', and بی‌گزند is an adjectival predicate 'unharmed/without harm'. The clause sets the condition for the following result clause (which supplies the verb).
segments.s655-g1430: به تندی is an adverbial phrase 'quickly/soon' (به + abstract noun تندی). شود is the present/subjunctive of شدن used as 'becomes' (resultative), and جان او is the possessed noun 'his life' (جان = life, او = his). دردمند is the predicate adjective 'afflicted/in pain'. The verb precedes its subject here, a common word order in classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g1431: دگر modifies ننگ to mean 'another' or 'a further' shame; ننگ دیوی is a genitive phrase 'the disgrace/shame of the demon' (the genitive relation is encoded poetically with word order). The particle ی followed by بود is an archaic/expletive sequence with بود functioning as the past copula 'was'. با ستیز is an instrumental/causal phrase 'with/through strife' or 'in fighting', giving the means/context for the shame.
segments.s655-g1432: همیشه 'always' sets the habitual/time frame. ببد (poetic past/become) together with the participle کرده forms a literary periphrasis indicating a past resultant state ('had become' / 'had made'), where کرده is the verbal participle 'made/done'. چنگال تیز is the noun + adjective 'sharp claws' (چنگال = claw, تیز = sharp), the thing produced by the continual fighting.
segments.s655-g681: بفرمود is a past‑tense, honorific third‑person verb meaning ‘he commanded/said’ and introduces the people who are to act. The following nouns تاموبد و رای‌زن are the direct objects/recipients of that command: the high priest (تاموبد) and the counselor (رای‌زن), joined by the coordinating conjunction و.
segments.s655-g682: برفتند is a past plural verb ‘they went/they departed’. با + NP here indicates the company or the target/assembly they join (literary use where ‘with’ implies going to/joining a group). نامدار انجمن is a noun phrase ‘the renowned assembly’ serving as the destination/venue.
segments.s655-g683: چنین گفت is a formulaic clause ‘he said thus/so’ with گفت as the past third‑person verb introducing reported speech. کان here begins the content of that report (classical connective = ‘that’), and نامه برحریر is the noun phrase ‘the letter on silk’ (برحریر = ‘on silk’ functioning as an adjectival/locational phrase modifying نامه).
segments.s655-g684: بیارند and بنهند are jussive/subjunctive plural verbs (classical/poetic forms) expressing what should be done — ‘let them bring’ and ‘let them place’ — coordinated by و. پیش دبیر is a prepositional phrase ‘before/in the presence of the scribe’, where پیش marks 'in front of / before' and دبیر = 'scribe/secretary'.
segments.s655-g685: همه نامداران is the subject phrase ‘all the renowned (men)’. نشستند is the past‑plural verb ‘they sat’, and گرد functions as a post‑verbal adverbial meaning ‘around/about’ (i.e., they sat around [someone]). The typical S‑V‑AdverbOrder is used: subject + verb + گرد.
segments.s655-g686: خرامان is an adverb/participial adverb ‘in a stately/proud gait’. The construction بر + شاه + شد + یزدگرد is an older literary order meaning the named person moved toward or came before the king: شد (past third singular) here combines with بر to indicate approach/turning toward, with یزدگرد as the subject placed after the verb for rhetorical effect.
segments.s655-g687: چو is a subordinating conjunction here meaning ‘when’. آن نامه‌بر is ‘that letter‑carrier/envoy’, and شاه ایران is the indirect object/goal ‘to the king of Iran’. بخواند is a narrative past/performative verb ‘he read (aloud)’; in classical usage the prefix بـ may appear in forms that correspond to past narrative or perfective reading.
segments.s655-g688: همه انجمن is the subject ‘the whole assembly’. در شگفتی is a prepositional phrase ‘in astonishment/wonder’ and بماند is a past‑narrative third‑person singular ‘remained/was left’ describing their continued state after the reading.
segments.s655-g137: The verb نداریم (negative present 'we do not have / cannot bear') is fronted for emphasis, followed by the subject ما. تاب is a noun meaning 'endurance/strength' here. خاقان چین is a genitival phrase 'the Khaghan of China' (چین = China) and is the thing whose pressure/endurance is being denied.
segments.s655-g138: The modal باید ('must') governs the idea of گذر کردن ('to pass'). In classical/poetic word order the infinitive is expressed with the stem گِذر + کرد, here inverted as 'گذر کرد باید' meaning 'must pass'; the subject (the Khaghan of China) is carried over from the previous clause. به ایران‌زمین = 'into/to the land of Iran' with به meaning 'towards/into'.
segments.s655-g139: گر (here with vocative ای) introduces a conditional/address: 'If — O base one —'. که introduces the subordinate clause. فرمان برد is used idiomatically as 'to obey an order' (فرمان = order; برد here functions as part of the verbal phrase 'فرمان بردن' = 'obey'). غاتفر is a proper name (subject of the subordinate clause).
segments.s655-g140: ببندد is a third‑person singular present/optative form (prefix بـ + root بند + ending ـد) functioning here as a future/intent sense 'he will bind / fasten'. به فرمان کسری = 'by the command of Khosrow', where به means 'by' (instrumental/agency). کمر here means 'girdle / belt' (the thing to be fastened).
segments.s655-g141: سپارد is a transitive verb 'he entrusts/has entrusted' placed before its indirect object بدو ('to him'). شهر هیتال را is the definite direct object marked by را ('the city of Hītāl'). The verb‑object order and the postpositive را are typical of classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s655-g142: فرامش (a noun 'forgetfulness' / 'forgetting') combines with کند (the light verb 'to do/make') to mean 'causes to forget' or 'makes [someone] forget'. گرز و کوپال را is the direct object of that action (marked by را): 'the mace and Kūpāl'. The verb کند is present/optative and here describes an action resulting from the prior entrustment.
segments.s655-g143: وگرنه = 'otherwise'. خود here emphasizes the subject ('we ourselves'). از + تخمه = 'from the stock/seed/lineage of', so از تخمه خوشنواز indicates origin or lineage; خوشنواز here functions as a name/epithet (the ancestor or eponymous figure of the line).
segments.s655-g144: گزینیم is first‑person plural present/subjunctive 'we will choose / we shall select' (classical poetic form). جنگاوری is a noun meaning 'a warrior / martial man' and سرفراز is a post‑positive adjective meaning 'proud / victorious / honored' describing that warrior.
segments.s655-g1049: تو opens the line as a vocative/imperative address (‘thou’). این داد is a noun phrase: این (this) + داد (here ‘justice’ or ‘grant’). بر شاه کسری is a prepositional phrase marking the target (بر + NP = ‘upon/against/for King Khosrow’). بدار is an imperative verb form directed to تو; in classical poetic usage it functions as ‘hold/keep/grant’ and takes این داد as its object.
segments.s655-g1050: بگردان is an imperative verb ‘turn’/‘turn away’ (causative sense). ز جانش is ز + possessive suffix ـش, literally ‘from his life’ (agent/source phrase). بد روزگار is a noun phrase where بد (bad/evil) modifies روزگار (fate/time), so the whole clause asks to ‘turn away bad fate from his life’. Word order puts the verb first (imperative) and its complements after it.
segments.s655-g1051: که introduces an explanatory/relative clause (‘for/that’). از governs the following coordinated nouns: فر و اورند او — فر (‘farr’, royal glory) and اورند (a poetic term of splendour) which is postposed with the pronoun او (‘his’), so both nouns belong to ‘his’. در جهان is a locative complement ‘in the world’. The sequence is typical of classical Persian: preposition + nouns + possessor + locative.
segments.s655-g1052: بدی (misfortune/evil) is the theme; دور گشت is a verbal phrase where دور (distant/removed) precedes گشت (past of گشتن ‘became/was turned’), so ‘was driven far away’ or ‘became distant’. آشکار و نهان (‘manifest and hidden’) are adverbial/qualifying terms placed after the verb to specify that the departure of evil was both open and secret.
segments.s655-g1053: به نخجیر is an adverbial/locative phrase ‘on a hunt’ (به + نخجیر). چون او here functions as a temporal/concessive conjunction + pronoun (‘when he’ / ‘as he’ — classical usage), and به گرگان رسید is the verbal clause with رسید (past ‘arrived/reached’) following the place complement; overall: ‘when he went hunting and reached Gorgan’.
segments.s655-g1054: گشاده is an adjective ‘open/relaxed’ placed before کسی (here used with the negated verb to mean ‘no one’). روی خاقان is a genitive phrase ‘the khaghan’s face’ (روی = face, followed by the possessor خاقان). ندید is the past negative ‘did not see’, so the whole segment means that nobody saw the khaghan’s face in an open/cheerful way.
segments.s655-g1055: بشد is a past/resultative verb ‘it happened/it came to pass’ introducing the event. خواب is the noun ‘sleep’ (object/state). وخورد is و + خورد (classical coordination with the past stem) here indicating a following action affecting the subject — ‘and was struck/consumed/attacked’ in context — and از سواران چین names the agent as ‘by the Chinese horsemen’ (از + NP marking the source/agent in passive-like constructions).
segments.s655-g1056: سواری is ‘rider’ (subject/agent in the following negative statement). نبرداشت is a past negative compound meaning ‘did not take’ / ‘could not remove’ (here idiomatically ‘could not pull him off’). از اسب زین is a composed prepositional phrase ‘from the horse’s saddle’ (از + اسب + زین where زین postmodifies اسب as ‘the horse’s saddle’). Together: no rider could take/remove him from the saddle.
segments.s655-g705: چودانست is an archaic fused form equivalent to چو دانست (“when he knew/realized”), a past-tense verb introducing a temporal clause; the subject خاقان follows it (verb‑before‑subject, a common poetic order). که here begins the subordinate content clause 'that ...'. او is the third‑person pronoun 'he', and تاو شاه is a nominal phrase 'kingly radiance/glory' (تاو = radiance + شاه = king).
segments.s655-g706: ندارد is third‑person negative present/persistent 'does not have' (negation with ندارد). به پیوند او جست راه is a verbal phrase where جست (here, 'sought') takes راه ('a way, means') as object and به پیوند او ('for/into an alliance with him') as its purpose; note the archaic past form جست and the preposition به marking purpose/goal.
segments.s655-g707: نباید is an impersonal modal 'one must not/should not' (negative obligation). بدین کار کردن is an infinitival nominal construction: بدین ('in/with this') modifies کار ('deed, action') and کردن is the light verb 'to do' forming 'doing this deed'. درنگ is a noun 'delay, pause' serving as the object of the modal phrase: 'must not delay in doing this'.
segments.s655-g708: که introduces the explanatory/reason clause 'for/because'. کس را uses را as the definite/indirect‑object marker ('to anyone' / 'for anyone'). ز پیوند او uses ز in the causal/relational sense 'because of / as to (an) alliance with him'. اونیست is a fused archaic form of او نیست ('it is not'), serving as the copula + negation. ننگ is the predicate noun 'shame/disgrace'.
segments.s655-g709: ز ... تا marks a geographic range 'from X to Y' (ز = poetic 'from', تا = 'to/as far as'). چین here is the place-name China, and بخارا is Bukhara; سپاه is 'army/host'. ویند is an archaic verbal/inflectional ending (‑vind) indicating third‑person plural or a copular sense in epic diction, roughly 'there were/they were'.
segments.s655-g710: همه ('all/everyone') modifies مهتران, here 'the great ones/chiefs' (مهتران = elders/great men). نیک‌خواه is an adjective/noun 'well‑wisher' or 'well‑disposed', and the same archaic ending ویند marks plural/copula 'were' — together: 'all the great chiefs were well‑wishers.'
segments.s655-g711: چو بشنید is a temporal clause 'when he/they heard' (چو = 'when', بشنید = past 'heard') with the verb preceding the implied subject as common in poetic Persian. گفتار آن بخردان is a genitive construction 'the speech/words of those wise ones' (آن = 'that', بخردان = plural 'the wise'), the object of بشنید.
segments.s655-g712: بزرگان و بیداردل موبدان is a coordinated noun phrase: بزرگان 'the great/elder men' and بیداردل 'wakeful‑hearted' (an epithet), modifying موبدان (mōbeds, Zoroastrian priests). Adjectives precede the noun in this poetic order; the phrase lists who reacted or were involved.
segments.s655-g929: A nominal phrase: شتر (noun) is followed by the past-participial بارکرده (‚laden/loaded"), so the whole unit reads 'camels laden...'. ز introduces the material/source (poetic use of 'of/from'), and دیبای چین is a genitival phrase 'brocade of China' where چین qualifies دیبای.
segments.s655-g930: An adverbial/locative phrase describing placement: بیاراسته here is a participial/adjectival word modifying an implied action (the goods/ornaments being arranged); پشت اسبان is a noun + genitive 'the backs of the horses'; به زین is a prepositional phrase 'onto/onto the saddle' showing final placement.
segments.s655-g931: چهل را uses the numeral + را (object/definiteness marker) to single out 'forty (of them)'. ز introduces material 'of/from', دیبای زربفت is a compound 'brocade (dība) woven with gold', and گون is a suffix/word meaning '-like / of the kind', qualifying the preceding cloth description.
segments.s655-g932: کشیده is a past participle 'drawn/strewn/draped' describing what was done to the cloth; زبر here functions adverbially/prepositionally as 'above/on top'; جد is an archaic item whose attested senses don't fit clearly in this phrase (so marked unknown); به introduces the target/location 'into/with', زر is 'gold', and اندرون 'inside', giving a sense 'draped above with gold inside'.
segments.s655-g933: صد اشتر is a numeral + noun phrase 'a hundred camels' serving as the (implicit) subject; ز گستردنی is a prepositional/participial modifier 'of the spreading / to be spread' qualifying بار (the load), and کرد is the past verb 'did/made' completing the clause (here 'bore/carried the load').
segments.s655-g934: This is a short clause with a noun phrase + verb: پرستنده ('attendants/devotees/followers') followed by سیصد پدیدار ('three hundred [who] appeared' — پدیدار as 'appearance/people who appear') and the verb کرد 'made/caused'. The structure means 'he caused/put three hundred attendants to appear.'
segments.s655-g935: همی‌بود is a past-habitual/auxiliary ('would be' / 'there was/used to be'); تاهرکسی is a generalising phrase 'to/for anyone' meaning 'whoever'; برنشست is the past simple 'sat upon' (the verb comes after the subject/phrase, as common in Persian clauses with auxiliary or habitual markers).
segments.s655-g936: برآیین is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'according to the custom / in the manner of'; چین here modifies that phrase as 'Chinese' (i.e., 'in the Chinese manner'); با introduces an instrument/ accompaniment 'with'; درفشی is used as 'banner/standard' in context though the listed sense alternatives don't match exactly (marked unknown); بدست is an adverbial noun phrase 'in hand'.
segments.s655-g825: از ایشان یکی را سپارم بدوی is literally 'I entrust one of them to X'. از + ایشان marks source ('from/of them'); یکی را is the definite object with را after the numeral; سپارم is 1st-person singular present/subjunctive/optative of سپردن ('to entrust'). بدوی is an archaic/contracted dative (≈ بدو/به دو ‘to him/to you’) attached after the verb, so word order is object before verb and recipient after the verb (typical poetic inversion).
segments.s655-g826: برآسایم از جنگ وز گفت و گوی is a purpose/result clause: برآسایم (1st-person 'I shall be at ease / relieve myself') + از جنگ ('from war') + وز گفت و گوی ('and from talk/dispute'). برآسایم is present/subjunctive/optative in an older poetic form meaning 'I will find ease/relief'; از introduces the things he will be freed of; the final phrase uses گفت و گوی (a noun compound 'speech/conversation/dispute').
segments.s655-g827: بدو گفت خاتون که با رای تو = 'To him said the lady that with your counsel ...'. بدو is an old dative 'to him'; گفت here is the verb 'said' (verb placement before the subject خاتون is a poetic inversion); که introduces the subordinate clause. با رای تو = 'with/by your counsel' (با + رای, رای here means 'plan/counsel').
segments.s655-g828: نگیرد کس اندر جهان جای تو = 'no one would take your place in the world.' نگیرد is present/subjunctive/optative with negative prefix ن- plus گیرد (from گرفتن) yielding 'would not take/ seize'; کس = 'anyone/no one' (here indefinite negative); اندر جهان جای تو = prepositional phrase 'in the world, your place'. The clause is dependent on که 'that'.
segments.s655-g829: برین گونه یک شب بپیمود خواب = 'In this way he spent one night asleep.' برین گونه = بر + این گونه 'upon/in this manner' (بر + the demonstrative); یک شب = 'one night'; بپیمود is an archaic past-tense verb meaning 'he traversed/spent' (بـ- prefix common in older Persian past forms); خواب = 'sleep' (he spent the night in sleep).
segments.s655-g830: چنین تا برآمد ز کوه آفتاب = 'thus until the sun rose from the mountain.' چنین reinforces 'in this way/so'; تا introduces the temporal limit 'until'; برآمد is simple past 'arose/appeared'; ز کوه آفتاب = 'from the mountain the sun' (z = poetic 'from'). Word order places the source phrase after the verb, as common in poetry.
segments.s655-g831: بیامد بدر گاه مهران ستاد = 'Came at the full-moon hour Mehrān [and] praised.' بیامد is past 'came' (poetic form). بدر گاه = 'the full-moon time' (بدر = full moon, گاه = time/occasion). مهران is the personal name (subject). ستاد is a past verb (here 'praised' or 'set up' depending on context) concordant with مهران; word order is slightly compressed as typical in verse.
segments.s655-g832: برتخت او رفت و نامه بداد = 'He went to his throne and gave the letter.' برتخت = 'onto the throne' (بر + تخت 'on the throne'); او is the pronoun 'he' (or 'she' depending on antecedent); رفت is past 'went'; و = 'and'; نامه بداد is an older verbal form for 'gave the letter' (نامه + [ب]داد); in poetic usage بداد appears as an archaic/periphrastic past, here meaning 'gave'.
segments.s655-g1337: شود is the classical third-person verb 'become' (here 'becomes'); دستگاهش = 'his court/establishment' (دستگاه + possessive -ش). چو = 'when/whenever' introduces a temporal clause, خواهد is the present tense of خواستن used as 'wishes/wants', and فراخ is an adjective 'spacious' that is completed by the verb in the next segment (i.e. 'fراخ' is the property he will cause/make).
segments.s655-g1338: کند is the third-person singular verb 'does/makes' (classical/plain present); the following nouns (گلشن، باغ، میدان، کاخ) are direct objects — a list of things he causes to exist or be enlarged. The coordinating و simply links the items in the list.
segments.s655-g1339: نهد is the classical 3rd-person form of نهادن 'to place/put' (= 'he sets/places' or 'he lays out'); here it governs گنج 'treasure' and is coordinated with the following verb phrase. گرد آورد is a light-verbal compound meaning 'gathers' or 'brings together' (گرد 'around' + آورد 'brings'), so the clause expresses that he sets out treasure and gathers offspring.
segments.s655-g1340: بسی modifies روز and means 'many/much' (classical usage); برآرزو is an adverbial phrase 'according to (his) desire/as he wishes'; بشمرد is the 3rd-person present/habitual of شمردن 'he counts' or 'he reckons'. Together: 'he counts many days to his liking.'
segments.s655-g1341: فراز here functions as an adverbial/verb-forming element 'up/on high' combined with آورد 'brought' to give a sense 'he brought up/raised' or 'he marshaled'; لشکر is the direct object 'army'. و خواسته links the action to his desire — 'and (as) he wished' or 'and what he desired.' This is a compact, poetic coordination.
segments.s655-g1342: شود is 'becomes' (passive/state), followed by the coordinated noun phrase کاخ و ایوانش 'the palace and its iwan/portico' (ایوان + possessive -ش). آراسته is a past-participle/adjective 'adorned'; together they form a passive/state clause 'the palace and porticoes become adorned.'
segments.s655-g1343: گر (here 'if') introduces a conditional; دون = 'low/ignoble' (characterizing the person). که introduces a relative clause 'who/that' and درویش‌باشد is the classical subjunctive/present phrase 'is a dervish' or 'takes the dervish's life'; به رنج means 'by/through hardship' (به used adverbially + رنج 'hardship').
segments.s655-g1344: فراز آرد is a verbal pairing meaning 'brings up/raises' or more loosely 'will obtain/achieve' (فراز as 'on high' combined with آرد 'brings'); از هر سویی = 'from every side/direction' (از 'from' + هر 'every' + سویی 'side/direction'); نام و گنج = 'name (fame) and treasure' — the things he will receive or attract from all quarters.
segments.s655-g265: شما را is the experiencer/topic marked by the object marker را (here functioning like a dative/emphatic 'to you'). بر + noun (بر آسایش و بزمگاه) names the things that are felt as burdensome (literally 'upon ease and the banquet‑hall'). The preposition بر here indicates the domain in which the predicate applies.
segments.s655-g266: گران شد is the verbal phrase 'became heavy/burdensome' (گران + شد). چنینتان is a poetic combination of چنین + تان (thus/to you) adding resultive emphasis. سر از رزمگاه literally 'head from the battlefield' is an idiomatic verbless phrase here — the verb of leaving is implied — meaning 'to withdraw/leave the battlefield'; سر is the noun 'head' used idiomatically for person/position.
segments.s655-g267: تن آسان شود is a simple predication 'the body becomes easy/comfortable' (شد is 'become'). هرک رنج آورد is a relative/generalizing clause 'whoever brings/undergoes hardship' (هرک = هر که 'whoever'); آورد is used in a classical/habitual sense 'brings/undergoes' here.
segments.s655-g268: ز رنج تَنِش ('from the hardship of his body') begins with poetic ز = 'from'. تن + ش is 'his body' (ش = possessive enclitic). باز here means 'again / in return', and گنج آورد is 'brought/produced treasure' — the clause states the result: suffering yields treasure.
segments.s655-g269: به نیروی یزدان is an agentive/instrumental prepositional phrase 'by the power of God' (نیروی یزدان = 'the power/strength of God'). سرماه را is a definite object (را) — سرماه is ambiguous but here functions as a resource/capital to be mobilized, placed as the object of بسیجیم in the next segment.
segments.s655-g270: بسیجیم is 1st‑person plural hortative/intentional 'we will muster / we shall mobilize' (verb placed up front for emphasis). یک سر همه راه را is the object: 'one head/chief for the whole road' (سر here = 'chief/leader'); همه راه را is a genitive/definitional phrase 'of the whole road' marked by را. The line thus states the plan to deploy a single commander/force for the entire route.
segments.s655-g271: به سوی خراسان is a directional phrase 'toward Khorasan' (به + سوی both indicate direction). کشم is a 1st‑person verb 'I will/shall draw/lead' (classical poetic form of کشیدن) and لشکری is the object 'an army/troops' — the verb precedes the object here for metrical/poetic word order.
segments.s655-g272: بخواهم is a 1st‑person verb 'I will want / I will request' (here 'I will summon/ask for'). سپاهی is 'soldiers/warriors' (object) and ز هرکشوری 'from every country' uses poetic ز = 'from'. Together: 'I will call soldiers from every land.'
segments.s655-g1689: بدو (be-do) is a prepositional pronoun meaning 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb for emphasis; گفت is the past tense verb 'said' (here 'Kisra said to him'). کسری is the subject 'Kisra'. کرا is an archaic interrogative/relative 'who' functioning as the object of دوست; بیش دوست literally 'more friend' = 'more beloved' ('whom do you love most').
segments.s655-g1690: که introduces a relative clause ('who'), linking to کرا. با او is 'with him'. یکی بود is a nominal phrase 'was one' (یکی = 'one, united'; بود = past 'was') meaning 'was one with him' (i.e. united in mind/body). از before a coordinated pair (مغز و پوست) here marks the constituents 'in/from brain and skin' idiomatically meaning 'in mind and body.'
segments.s655-g1691: چنین 'thus' modifies the manner of the reply. داد here is the past tense 'gave' (gave an answer). پاسخ is the noun 'answer/reply'. که begins the content clause ('that'/'that which follows'). از نیک‌دل literally 'from/of a kind‑hearted [person]' — the phrase marks the sort of person being spoken about (a kind‑hearted one).
segments.s655-g1692: جدایی 'separation' is the nominal subject; نخواهد is a negative future/auxiliary meaning 'will not' (here 'will not be separated' in context). جز 'except' introduces the only possible cause; از marks source/agent. دل‌گسل is a compound noun 'heart‑breaker' (one who breaks the heart), the agent that could cause separation.
segments.s655-g1693: دگر 'another, other' introduces a contrast. آن کسی 'that person / the one' is the noun phrase being defined. کو is an archaic relative particle equivalent to 'who'/ 'که' used in poetry (here marking the relative clause). نوازنده‌تر is the comparative of نوازنده 'musician/ player' with the comparative suffix -تر = 'more of a musician' or 'more accomplished as a player.'
segments.s655-g1694: نکوتر is the comparative of نکو ('good, fine'), here 'better' or 'more virtuous'. به کردار literally 'in/with regard to conduct' — به marks the domain ('in conduct/behavior'). و links the list of qualities. سازنده‌تر is the comparative of سازنده ('builder/maker/composer') meaning 'more of a maker/creator' or 'more constructive'; it parallels نوازنده‌تر and نکوتر as another comparative attribute.
segments.s655-g1695: بپرسید is the past tense 'asked' (they asked). دشمن here is the addressee/target of the question ('the enemy' — i.e. they put the question to the enemy). کرا is the archaic interrogative 'who/which' and بیشتر is the comparative 'more' — together introducing the question 'which one more...'. The remainder of the sentence completes that question.
segments.s655-g1696: که introduces the subordinate clause ('who/that'). باشد is the present/subjunctive form of 'to be' used here to state 'who is' (the available sense lists do not include a plain present 'be' sense for this form). بدو 'to him' marks the target of the ill intention. بر is a preposition meaning 'against/toward' in this collocation. بداندیش‌تر is the comparative adjective 'more ill‑intentioned / more hostile', completing the question 'who is more hostile toward him.'
segments.s655-g505: نخست is an initial adverb meaning 'first'. آنک is a demonstrative/relative ('that/which') introducing reported material. گفتی is the second-person past 'you said' (classical usage) introducing the report, and ز is the poetic preposition 'about/of' taking the following noun هیتالیان ('the Hitalians') as its object.
segments.s655-g506: کزان = 'from which' (relative phrase linking back to 'آنک'); گونه here means 'manner/way' so کزان گونه = 'in that manner'. بستند is the past 3rd-person plural 'they bound/placed'. بد را میان: بد ('evil') is the definite object marked by را, and میان ('between') indicates position — literally 'they set evil between (them)'.
segments.s655-g507: به بیداد is an adverbial/instrumental phrase 'with injustice / in tyranny'. بر + خیره places the action 'upon the stunned/astonished ones' (خیره = 'astonished/stunned'). خون ریختند is the verbal phrase 'they poured / shed blood' (ریختند = past of ریختن). Word order: adverbial → prepositional object → verb.
segments.s655-g508: به دام نهاده is a participial construction ('having placed into a trap' or 'into a trap having been set'); خود is emphatic 'themselves/their own', and آویختند is the past 3rd-person plural 'they hung (themselves)' or 'they were hung'. The clause implies being caught by or falling into their own snare.
segments.s655-g509: اگر is the conditional 'if'. بد کنش is a noun phrase 'evil action(s)' (adjective before noun is common in classical style). زور دارد = 'has force/strength' (دارد is present 3rd-sg 'has'). چو شیر is a simile 'like a lion' modifying زور.
segments.s655-g510: نباید is an impersonal modal meaning 'ought not / should not'. که introduces the subordinate clause. باشد is the present/subjunctive copula 'that there be' (3rd-person). به یزدان is a prepositional phrase 'by/with God' and دلیر is the predicate adjective 'brave'. Grammatically this gives 'it ought not be that (one) be brave by God' (the line is compact/classical and somewhat ambiguous in scope).
segments.s655-g511: چوایشان is a fused classical phrase meaning 'when they' (چو = when; ایشان = they). گرفتند is past 3rd-plural 'they took/seized'. راه پلنگ = 'the path/way of the leopard' (پلنگ modifies راه). The clause reads 'when they took the leopard's path'.
segments.s655-g512: تو is the subject 'you'. پیروز is the predicate adjective 'victorious'. گشتی here functions as a past/resultative (from گشتن) meaning 'you became' (classical idiom where گشتن marks change of state). برایشان 'for them/on their behalf' and به جنگ 'in battle' are adverbial/benefactive phrases — so the clause means 'you became victorious for them in battle.'
segments.s655-g817: This is an older word order where the modal 'باید' (must) combines with the verb root 'داد' (to give) to mean 'must be given'. 'یکی دختری' = 'one/a daughter' (indefinite object) precedes the verbal phrase. The final item 'بدوی' is an enclitic/old form indicating the recipient ('to him') in this clause (archaic placement and spelling).
segments.s655-g818: 'که' introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'in order that'). 'ما را' uses the object/topic marker 'را' with 'ما' = 'for us'. The verb 'فزاید' is a present/subjunctive-type form meaning 'may increase' or 'will increase'; it takes 'بدو' (= 'to him/her') as the indirect object and 'آبروی' as the thing increased (honour/reputation).
segments.s655-g819: This is an existential/possessive pattern: 'تو را ... یک دخترست' = 'There is a girl for you'. 'تو را' marks the beneficiary/possessor, 'در پس پرده' = 'behind the curtain' (compound locative), and 'دخترست' is the older fused form of 'دختر است' ('is a girl').
segments.s655-g820: 'کجا' here introduces a relative/descriptive clause meaning 'who/which' or 'where' in older usage. The phrase 'بر سر بانوان' = 'upon the heads of the ladies', and 'افسرست' is an archaic predicate meaning 'is a diadem/ornament' (literally 'is a helmet/crest' in the gloss) — i.e., 'she is a crowning ornament among women.'
segments.s655-g821: 'مرا آرزویست' is an idiomatic older construction where 'مرا' (to me) + 'آرزویست' ('there is a desire' / 'I long') expresses longing ('I desire'). 'از مهر اوی' specifies the object of desire: 'for his love' (اوی = his/her in archaic form).
segments.s655-g822: The clause begins with 'که' (that/so that) introducing the consequence. 'دیده' (eyes) is the object of 'نبردارم' — an old negative-subjunctive/present form meaning 'I do not lift / I will not raise' (ن + بَر دارم). 'از چهر اوی' = 'from his face' (i.e. 'I will not take my eyes off his face').
segments.s655-g823: 'چهارست' is an archaic fused form meaning 'there are four' (numeral + 'است' combined). 'نیز از پرستندگان' = 'also among the worshippers/devotees' — here 'از' marks group membership ('of/among').
segments.s655-g824: This gives the description of those four: 'پرستار' (caretaker/protector) and 'بیداردل بندگان' — 'بیداردل' is a compound adjective ('wakeful‑hearted', 'vigilant'), modifying 'بندگان' (servants/subjects). The two nouns are coordinated with 'و'.
segments.s655-g1185: This is a compressed, inverted question. به شهری (preposition + noun) = 'into/unto which city'; کجا is the interrogative 'where/which'; برگذشتی is the past verb 'passed by/entered' (here used with the following noun as subject); سپاه is the subject 'the host/army' placed after the verb for poetic word order.
segments.s655-g1186: نیازارد is a negative verb ('does not spare / did not spare') agreeing with the army from the previous segment; زان = 'from that / from them' introduces the agent causing harm; کشتمندی is used adjectivally/plurally as 'murderers, the murderous folk'; به راه = 'on the road' (به + راه = 'along the way'). Together: 'was not spared by those murderers on the road.'
segments.s655-g1187: نجستی is the past of جستن (here with the negative sense implied in the context) meaning 'sought/asked' used impersonally ('no one sought/asked'); کسی ... ازکسی is the correlative 'no one from anyone' (i.e. nobody asked anyone); نان و آب are the accusative objects 'bread and water.'
segments.s655-g1188: بره‌بر is the agent 'porter/carrier' (the person who would set up camp or carry baggage); بیاراستی here functions as a verbal/adjectival idea 'did not make ready / did not arrange' (the literal lexical options offered are adverbial, so the line uses an idiomatic poetic form meaning 'no porter prepared'); جای خواب = 'a place to sleep/bed.' The negative sense carries over from the previous clause: 'no porter made ready a sleeping place.'
segments.s655-g1189: برینسان (barinsān) is the subject 'these princes / noble men'; همی is a frequentative/adverb 'continually/then'; گرد + گیتی بگشت is a verbal cluster meaning 'went about the world / traversed the earth' — classic epic word order puts adverbial گرد before the object گیتی. (The exact form بگشت is an older narrative past.)
segments.s655-g1190: نگه کرد is a compound verb 'looked / kept watch' (نگه = gaze/look, کرد = do → 'made/looked'); هرجای = 'everywhere / wherever'; هامون و دشت = 'plain(s) and field(s)' — the phrase names the open places they surveyed. The verb governs the following locative phrase.
segments.s655-g1191: جهان دید = 'the world saw / beheld'; یک سر here functions idiomatically as 'all at once / entirely' (one whole); پر از = 'full of'; کشتمند = 'the slain / murderous dead' (here 'slain people' caused by the earlier violence). The clause states that everywhere was full of corpses/slain.
segments.s655-g1192: در و دشت is a fixed pairing meaning 'towns/inhabited places and plains' (در here names settlement/door/place in older usage); پرگاو و پرگوسفند is a compound adjective 'full of cattle and full of sheep' (پر- = 'full of' + گاو/گوسفند). Together: the inhabited places and the plains were teeming with cattle and sheep.
segments.s655-g305: This is a compact nominal + verb clause. 'یکی لشکری' = 'one / a certain army' (indefinite noun phrase) followed by the verb 'براند' (classical form of راندن). In Ferdowsī's diction this verbal form functions as a third-person narrative (past/simple) meaning 'sent forth / set in motion / marched'. 'از مداین' is a simple prepositional phrase 'from Madā'in' indicating origin.
segments.s655-g306: 'که' introduces a result/explanatory clause ('so that / such that'). 'روی زمین' is a locative phrase literally 'the surface/face of the earth.' 'جز' is the restrictive 'except.' 'بدریا' is the sea (here meaning 'all that remained was the sea'). 'نماند' is third-person singular perfect/past of ماندن used here to mean 'was left / remained.' The clause therefore states the outcome of the army's action.
segments.s655-g307: This is a compressed spatial description. 'زمین کوه تا کوه' (here written compactly) means 'from plain/land to mountain' or 'from hill to hill' (extent). 'یک سر سپاه' is an appositive/noun phrase meaning 'one head/wing/end of the army' (i.e. one flank or section). Word order is poetic and elliptical: the extent phrase precedes the specification of the army's one 'head/wing.'
segments.s655-g308: 'درفش جهاندار' is a noun phrase in which 'جهاندار' is an epithet/name of the banner (literally 'world-holder' — here the standard's name). 'بر' is the preposition 'on/upon,' and 'قلبگاه' is the locative noun 'heart/centre.' Together this places the Jahāndār banner 'upon the centre' (i.e. over the army's centre).
segments.s655-g309: 'یکی لشکری' again 'a certain army.' 'سوی گرگان' is a directional phrase 'toward Gorgan.' 'کشید' is the past of کشیدن and here means 'moved / marched / drew (toward)' — a narrative past describing the army's advance. Note the compact, paratactic structure common in epic narrative.
segments.s655-g310: 'که' introduces the consequent/result clause. 'گشت' (from گشتن) is third-person past meaning 'became' or 'turned out to be,' and takes the adjectival predicate 'ناپدید' ('vanished'). The structure 'گشت ... ناپدید' = 'became vanished / disappeared.' 'آفتاب از جهان' ('the sun from the world') is the subject/phrase affected: the clause says the sun was no longer visible in the world.
segments.s655-g311: 'بیاسود' (literally 'not at rest') is a past-state adjective/verb meaning 'did not rest / was uneasy.' 'چندی' here means 'for a while.' 'ز بهر شکار' is a causal/teleological phrase 'on account of / for the sake of the hunt.' Together: 'He did not rest for some time because of the hunt.'
segments.s655-g312: 'همی‌گشت' is an iterative/imperfective past form meaning 'kept going / wandered about.' The locatives 'درکوه و در مرغزار' ('in the mountain and in the meadow') specify where he roamed. The verb plus coordinated locatives create the continuous action 'he wandered in mountain and meadow.'
segments.s655-g745: نویسنده (subject) + نامه را (definite direct object marked by را) + پیش (adverb) + خواند (3rd person singular past). The verb خواند is the main past tense action 'read/recited'; پیش here means 'in the presence of / before (them)'. Word order is subject–object–adverb–verb, typical of older Persian narrative.
segments.s655-g746: ز introduces source or topic ('concerning/about' or 'from') and governs the following phrase; فراوان modifies سخنها as an adjective 'abundant/many words'; براند is the predicative verb (a poetic/narrative form meaning 'uttered / brought forth / delivered'). The implicit subject continues from the previous clause (the scribe).
segments.s655-g747: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb 'he commanded / he said (as a ruler)'; تا introduces a subordinate clause of purpose/report ('that/so that'); نامه پاسخ is the object phrase 'a reply to the letter' (no را here); نبشت is the 3rd person singular past 'wrote/inscribed' and completes the subordinate clause.
segments.s655-g748: گزیده سخنهای is a noun phrase 'chosen/selected words' with گزیده as adjective; فرخ functions here as an epithet/adjective 'auspicious / felicitous' modifying سخنها (or as a name depending on context) and نبشت is the past verb 'wrote'. The clause has an elided subject carried over from the previous command.
segments.s655-g749: نخست is an initial adverb 'first'; آفرین here functions as 'praise/acclamation' and is combined with کرد (the light verb 'did/made') to mean 'praised' or 'offered praise to'; بر + کردگار is the prepositional object 'to the Creator/God'. The verb کرد is 3rd person past.
segments.s655-g750: This segment lists appositive epithets of 'کردگار': جهاندار (sovereign/ruler of the world), پیروز (victorious), و (and), پروردگار (Lord/nurturer). These are nouns/adjectives in apposition, not separate clauses, and modify/rename 'کردگار' from the previous segment.
segments.s655-g751: به فرمان 'by the command' is an instrumential prepositional phrase; اویست is an archaic/fused copular/possessive form meaning essentially 'it is by Him' or 'it belongs to Him' (an older poetic contraction of او + است); گیتی 'the world' is the subject and به پای 'on its feet / firmly' is a locative/adverbial phrase. The clause therefore reads 'The world is (by) His command, firm on its feet.'
segments.s655-g752: همویست functions as a predicate meaning 'he is likewise / he is also so' (an older/poetic copular construction). بر نیک و بد is a prepositional phrase 'over / with respect to good and bad', and رهنمای is a predicate noun 'guide' (so the clause means 'He is likewise the guide concerning good and evil'). Note classical Persian often uses such compact predicates without an explicit auxiliary.
segments.s655-g321: همه خاک ایران به چین آوریم is a compact clause: همِه + noun (خاک ایران) = “the whole/entire soil (land) of Iran.” به چین is a prepositional phrase ‘toward/into China.’ آوریم is a first-person-plural present-subjunctive/hortative form of آوردن used here as a volitional/future action ‘let us / we will bring.’ Word order is typical Persian: topic (whole land) — adverbial (to China) — verb.
segments.s655-g322: همان تازیان را بدین آوریم: همان تازیان is the definite object ‘those same Arabs’ with را marking the direct object. آوریم is the same hortative/volitional verb ‘we will bring.’ بدین here is a contracted prepositional phrase (ب + این) meaning ‘to this (place / here / thus)’; ب is the prepositional prefix meaning ‘to/into’ and دین is the remainder of the contraction (used demonstratively here), so the whole phrase reads ‘bring those very Arabs to this (place).’
segments.s655-g323: نمانم که کس تاج دارد نه تخت: نمانم is the negative form of ماندن used in a hortative/optative or volitional sense ‘I will not leave / I will not allow (it to remain).’ که introduces the subordinate clause describing what will not remain. کس is an indefinite ‘anyone / anyone at all,’ تاج دارد نه تخت lists two things someone might possess, with دارد (to have) as the governing verb. The short clause uses the usual Persian order: verb (negated/optative) — complementizer — subject — object(s).
segments.s655-g324: نه اورنگ شاهی نه از تخت بخت uses the paired negator نه ... نه ... = ‘neither ... nor ...’. اورنگ شاهی = ‘the throne/worldly glory of kingship’ (اورنگ + شاهی). نه از تخت بخت = ‘nor (from/of) the throne of fortune’ where از functions like English ‘of’/‘from’ to mark the genitive relationship (تختِ بخت = throne of fate). The whole extends the list of things the speaker will leave no one in possession of.
segments.s655-g325: همی‌بود یک چند باگفت وگوی combines the progressive/continuative prefix همی- with بود (past/continual ‘there was / it was happening’) producing ‘there continued to be / for a while it was so.’ یک چند = ‘for a short time / a few moments.’ باگفت وگوی is the poetic concatenation of با + گفت و گو = ‘with speech/conversation’ (literally ‘with saying and saying’); it functions adverbially to characterize the mode (they were engaged in talk).
segments.s655-g326: جهانجوی با لشکری جنگجوی is a noun phrase describing the person present: جهانجوی (‘world-seeker’ = ambitious/expansionist person) followed by با + لشکری (‘with an army’) where لشکری is an adjectival/collective form meaning ‘troops.’ جنگجوی is an attributive adjective/noun ‘warrior’ describing that army. The phrase means ‘a would-be conqueror accompanied by an army of fighters.’
segments.s655-g327: چنین تا بیامد ز شاه آگهی: چنین = ‘thus/so.’ تا here functions temporally ‘until / when’ introducing the time clause. بیامد is simple past ‘came’ (news came). ز شاه آگهی = ‘news/information from the king’ (ز = poetic ‘from’). The sequence is typical classical Persian: temporal marker — verb — source phrase (news from the king).
segments.s655-g328: کز ایران بجنبید با فرهی: کز is the fused relative/conjunction (که + از) often meaning ‘that/from which’ or simply ‘that,’ introducing the content of the news. ایران is the source/subject. بجنبید is a poetic/archaic verb form meaning ‘were roused / stirred / moved’ (a plural or impersonal past sense); here it says the people of Iran were roused. با فرهی = ‘with the nobles’ (فرهی = nobles/leading men), با marking accompaniment. The clause gives the effect of the king’s news: Iran rose up with its nobles.
segments.s655-g961: شهنشاه ایران is a genitive phrase 'the Shahanshah of Iran' (noun + place-name). مرا is the clitic form 'to/for me' (here written as م + را in the data) marking the person to whom the relationship applies. افسرست is a compound of the noun افسر 'afsar' and the archaic copula -ست (old form of است); literally 'is an afsar' so the whole clause reads 'The Shahanshah of Iran is, to me, an afsar.'
segments.s655-g962: نه is simple negation 'not'. پیوند او is 'his bond/connection' (پیوند as head noun with the pronoun او following to mark possession). از پی ...ست is a prepositional phrase 'is from/by the line of ...' with پی here meaning 'after/following' or 'line/trace' and دخترست = 'is a daughter' (دختر + copula -ست); the clause means 'his tie is not by the daughter's line.'
segments.s655-g963: که is the subordinating conjunction 'that'. تامن is an older fused form = تا من 'that I / as far as I', introducing the reporter. شنیدستم is the 1st-person past 'I heard' (archaic/persianized past ending -ستم). از marks source 'from' and بخردان is the oblique plural of بخرد 'the wise ones' — together 'that I have heard from the wise.'
segments.s655-g964: This is a coordinated noun phrase: بزرگان 'the great/elders' و 'and' followed by بیدار دل موبدان. بیدار دل is an adjectival + noun compound 'wakeful/alert-hearted' modifying موبدان (mobeds, Zoroastrian priests). Word order: modifiers precede the head noun they qualify.
segments.s655-g965: ز is the poetic preposition 'from / on account of'. It governs a series of coordinated nouns: فر (the sacred royal glory, farr), بزرگی 'greatness', and اورند شاه (a third noun phrase). The three nouns are linked by و 'and'—a simple coordinate list introduced by the preposition.
segments.s655-g966: بجستم is an archaic past 1st-person form meaning 'I sought' (classical pattern ب + جُستن); the dataset's lemma list is incomplete but functionally it is the past verb 'I sought'. همی is the frequentative/continuous adverb 'always/still' placed before the object(s). رای و پیوند شاه are the coordinated objects 'counsel/advice and the king's bond/alliance' — note شاه follows پیوند as a genitive/attributive 'of the king.'
segments.s655-g967: که again introduces a clause 'that/for'. اندر جهان 'in the world' is a locative phrase; سر به سر is an idiomatic adverbial 'throughout / all over / head to head' modifying the following predicate. دادگر is an adjective 'just' (or a substantive 'just one'); together this portion states 'that throughout the world (he is) just' or sets up a general claim about justice in the world.
segments.s655-g968: جهاندار is a noun 'world-holder'/'sovereign'. چون او is the comparative/simile 'like him'. نبندد is the 3rd-person present/gnomic negative 'does not bind' (classical negative + present form), and کمر is the object 'waist / girdle' — together forming the idiom کمر بستن 'to gird one's loins'. So the clause says roughly 'a sovereign like him does not gird his waist.'
segments.s655-g401: مرا (m+را) functions as the object pronoun 'me' plus the object marker را; داد is a past‑tense 3rd‑person singular verb 'gave'. بی‌آرزو precedes دخترش in the poetic word order to qualify her as 'without desire/hope', and دخترش is the noun 'his daughter' with the possessive enclitic -ش attached.
segments.s655-g402: نجویند is a present‑tense (3rd plural) verbal form meaning 'they seek/ask'; جز introduces the exception 'except'. رای here means 'counsel/plan/opinion' and من is the possessive/agent 'my'. لشکرش is the noun 'his army' with the enclitic -ش; the phrase means 'his army seeks nothing but my counsel.'
segments.s655-g403: وزان is a fused poetic conjunction meaning 'and from that / and of that'; هدیه is 'gift' and کز (که + ز) introduces a relative clause 'which/from which'. پیش here means 'before / previously' (referring to an earlier sending) and نزدیک شاه places the action in the context of 'near/by the king' or 'sent by the king'—together: 'of that gift which earlier the king ...'.
segments.s655-g404: فرستاد is the past tense 'sent / dispatched' (3rd singular). وهیتال is an interjection 'woe!/alas' inserted for lament, and بستد is a past form meaning 'shut/closed' (here 'blocked' or 'stopped'). ز راه literally 'from the road' or 'of the way'—so the clause says that, alas, the way was closed/blocked relative to that sent gift.
segments.s655-g405: بران here introduces motive/direction ('with regard to that' / 'against them') and کینه means 'hatred/grudge'. رفتم is the past tense 'I went/left' and من is the emphatic subject 'I'. از شهر چاج literally 'from the city of Chāj' gives the departure point; the whole segment states the speaker left Chāj out of hatred.
segments.s655-g406: که introduces a purpose/result clause here ('so that / in order that'). بستانم is a subjunctive/optative or present‑tense formation with first person 'I may take' (purpose: 'to seize/obtain'). از names the source 'from Ghatfar' (غاتفر as a proper name), and گنج وتاج list the objects 'treasure and crown'.
segments.s655-g407: بدان گونه is a poetic adverbial phrase 'in that manner / thus' (built from the prefix ب- + دان + گونه), where گونه means 'manner' and the whole phrase modifies رفتم 'I went'. ز گلزریون 'from the Gulzarīān' gives the origin; note that دان here is part of the fixed phrase and does not act as an independent verb.
segments.s655-g408: که is the narrative conjunction 'that/so that/and it came to pass', شد is the past 'became/was made', and لعلگون آب جیحون ز خون is a verbless descriptive result: 'the water of the Jeyhun turned ruby‑colored from blood' (لعلگون = ruby‑colored, آب جیحون = the waters of the Jeyhun, ز خون = from blood).
